Marlborough and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; 1st class mods., 1st class lit. hum.; ast. dist. comsnr., Zanzibar Prot., 3rd Feb., 1915; seconded for mil. ser., June, 1917; temp. comm. in K.A.R.; ag. 1st ast. sec.; Zanzibar, 1919-20; ast. sec., Uganda, 1st Jan., 1921; senr. ast. sec., 22nd June, 1923; ag. ast. ch. sec., 29th July, 1924; ast. col. sec., Mauritius, Apr., 1925; ag. col. sec., Jan. to Mar., 1927; col. sec., Br. Honduras, 4th June, 1927; admtd. govt., 14th Oct., 1927.

EVANS, SIR FREDERICK, K.C.M.G. (1908), K.C.V.O. (1912), C.M.G. (1881), C.V.O. (1903), C.B.E. (1918); Comdr. of Order of Isabel la Católica (Spanish), 1912; Knight Comdr., ditto, 1914.—B. 1849; entered the civ. ser., Admiralty, Somerset house, 13th Dec., 1867; transfd. to the sec.'s dept. at Whitehall, Jan., 1872; ast. col. sec. and treas., S. Leone, May, 1878; priv. sec. to Sir S. Rowe, K.C.M.G., Feb., 1879, to Feb., 1881; ag. clk. of legis. coun., Sept., 1879; acted as col. sec., Dec., 1879, to Feb., 1880; ch. sec. and ch. of the staff to Sir S. Rowe during threatened hostilities with Ashanti, 1881; ast. col. sec., G. Coast, 1882; ag. collr. of customs and treas., Oct., 1882, to Jan., 1883; ag. col. sec., Aug. to Dec., 1883, and on other occasions; clk. of legis. coun., Feb., 1882, to Apr., 1884; dep. gov., Lagos, July to Aug., 1883, and May, 1885, to Jan., 1886; dep. gov., G. Coast, Sept., 1883, and Apr., 1885; col. sec., Lagos, Jan., 1886; ag. admnstr., July, 1886; col. sec., G. Coast, 1887; Leeward Is., 1888; Jamaica, 1896; ag. 1st cl. clk., C.O., Feb., 1900; col. sec., Gibraltar, 1901; ag. gov. on seven occasions; ret., 1914; ag. col. sec., Gibraltar, from 21st Dec., 1914 to 19th Feb., 1915 and from 30th Apr., 1917.
EVANS, H. A.—Bailiff mag.'s ct., Virgin Is., Jan., 1886; bailiff, sup. ct., Apr., 1890; clk., post office, 1890; govt. offr. and cashier, 1901.
EVANS, H. G.—Ast. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., Apr., 1913; 1st grade admstve. offr., 1926.

EVANS, HON. SIR JOHN WM., Kt. Bach. (1926), C.M.G. (1906)—B. 1855; M.L.A. for Kingborough, Tasmania, since 1996; warden and master warden of marine bd. of Hobart for many years; premier and min. of educn., Tasmania, 11th July, 1904, to 10th Oct., 1905; premier and treas., 11th Oct., 1905, to 1st May, 1906; premier and ch. sec. 1st May, 1906 to June, 1909; subsequently speaker.

EVANS, SAMUEL, Hon. LL.D. Witwatersrand Univ.—B. 1889; pvt. sec. to finan. adviser, Egyptian govt., 1883; ch. contr., Egyptian coast guard serv., 1886; inspr.-gen., Imp. Ottoman Bank, Constantinople, 1889; man., Turkish Tobacco Regie, 1892; during Anglo-Boer War served on staff of Lord Roberts from Kroonstad to Johannesburg, and on staff of mil. gov. of Johannesburg as civ. comsnr.; medal, 3 bars; pub. debt comsnr., Union of S. Africa.
EVANS, SAMUEL ALAN, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1899; ed. Mountjoy Schl. and R. Vety Coll., Dublin; war serv., R.A.F., cadet, 1918; vety. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Nov., 1921; ast. vety. pathologist, May, 1926.

EVELYN, EDWARD A.—2nd clk. to regisr., St. Kitts, 1899; govt. offr., Sandy Point, Apr., 1903; govt. offr., Dieppe Bay, St. Kitts, June, 1905; clk., regisr.'s off., May, 1906; regisr. of births and deaths, St. George's parish; sg. ch. clk., regisr.'s offr., Antigua, Dec., 1910, to July, 1911; ag. regisr. and provost marshal for several periods since 1913; regisr. and provost marshal and regisr. of titles, St. Kitts-Nevis, Dec., 1922; prov. offr., mem., leg. coun., Oct., 1923.
EVELYN, EDWARD A. (jr.)—B. 1904; ed. St. Kitts Grammar Schl.; ag. supermry. rev. offr., 1921; supermry. rev. offr., 1922.

EVELYN, MERRIT—2nd clk. to pres., St. Kitts, Dec., 1878; ch. clk., treasy., Nevis, 1881; ag. treas., Nevis, July, 1882-1883; rev. offr. in charge, Nevis treasy., 1883; prot. of imigrts., Nevis, 1883; land and house tax comsnr., 1892; ag. mag., Nevis, 1894-5, 1896-6 and 1912; water comsnr., Nevis, 1901; ohmn., quarantine bd., Nevis, 1911.

EWART, F. K.—B. 1902; ed. Boteler Sch., Warrington and Rossall Schl., Williams Mem. Exhn.; schol., Exeter Coll., Oxford, 1920; pres., O.U.S.C., 1923; B.A., 1923 (chem. pt. 1); F.C.S., 1923; 2nd chem., pt. 2., 1924; mem., Inner Temple, 1925; ast. mast., King's Coll., Hongkong, 1928; M.A., 1927.

EZCHIEL, PERCY HUBERT, C.M.G. (1923).—B. 1875; B.A. Bombay, 1889 (ctd. 14); B.A., London, 1894; major schlr., Trin. Coll., Camb., 1896; 4th wrangler and B.A., Camb., 1897; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd cl. clk., C.O., 11th Oct., 1898; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1903 sec. to crown agts. for the colonies, May, 1905; mem. of Fair Wages Advisory Comtee., 1909-1920; sec. to Colonial Economic Development Comtee., 1919-20; sec. and mem. R. African Currency bd., 1919 (resigned secretarieship 1921); crown agt. for the cols., Dec., 1920; dir. of colonial scholars, 1921; rep. of C.O. on exec. coun. of Br. Empire Exhib., 1921-26; chmn., Palestine currency bd., 1926.

EZCHIEL, VICTOR GERALD.—B. 1883; ed. King's Coll. Schl. and Caius Coll., Camb.; B.A. (Math. Tripos.) 1906; cadet, F.M.S. civ. serv., 1907; passed cadet (Tamil) 1911; ag. ast. audt., Selangor, Negri Sembilan, and Pahang, 1909; transfd. to S. Stiltns., civ. serv., 1911; ag. ast. supt. Indian imigrts., Penang, 1911; ag. 3rd mag., 1914; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, 1914; ag. dist. offr., Butterworth, 1915; ag. dist. offr., Dindings, 1915; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, 1916; ag. ast. supt., govt. monopolies, 1917; attached food control office, 1919; supermry offr., cl. III., S. Stiltns., Nov., 1919; dep. treas., 1920; dep. treas., B., Dec., 1921; mag., Ipoh, Feb., 1924.

FAFUNWA, W. K.—Cadet, Lagos civ. ser., 1896; 5th cls. clk., Jan., 1898; 4th cls. clk., Dec., 1898; 3rd cls. clk., audit off., Nov., 1902; paymaster, P.W.D., May, 1903; ag. acctnt., P.W.D., Mar.-May, 1904; 2nd cls. clk., treasy., Sept., 1906; 1st cls. clk., Jan., 1907; asst. ch. clk., Jan., 1910.

FAHEY, FRANCIS JOSEPH.—B. 1878; ed. St. Michael's Coll., Toronto, Canada; printer; mem., prov. coun., Natal, 1920-23; prov. exec., 1924; joined bd. of trade and industries, Union of S. Africa, 1st Oct., 1924; re-apptd., 31st Mar., 1927.
FAIR, ARTHUR, LL.B. (N.Z.), K.C.—B. 1886; ed. state schls., Nelson Coll. and Victoria Univ. Coll., N.Z.; crown solr., 1921; prin. law offr., 1923; solr.-gen., 1925.

FAIR, LIEUT.-COL. J. G., D.S.O.—B. 1864; entd. Army (21st Hussars—now Lancers) 1885; capt., 1892; major, 1900; staff off. to Gen. Gatacre during Athura campaign, 1898 (desps., Khedive's medal and clasp); with 21st Lancers at Omdurman (Queen's medal, clasp, 4th cls. Medjidieh); adjut., cavalry depot, Canterbury, 1899-1900; S. African war, 1901-2 (desps., D.S.O., Queen's medal, 5 clasps); commanded div. S.A.C., Eastern Transvaal, 1901-4; ditto, O.R.C., 1904-8; nom. mem. of I.C.C., 1906; res. comsrr. and comdt.-gen., Southern Rhodesia, 1908-1911; dep. asst. dir. of remounts since 1911.

FAIRBAIRN, ST GEORGE, KT. BACH. (1926).—B. 1856; ed. Geelong Grammar Schl. and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; M.L.A. for Toorak, Victoria, Oct., 1903 to Sept., 1906; M.H.R. for Fawkner, 1906-13; Commonwealth Senator for Victoria, May, 1917; agt. gen. for Victoria, 1924.

FAIRBAIRN, RICHARD PURDOM, C.E.—B. 1865; ed. at high schl., London, Ontario; served in engr.'s off., London, 1872; P.L.S., 1876; practising engrnr., 1877; P.W.D., Ontario, 1879; chief engr., pub. wks., Ontario, 1903; dep-min. of pub. wks., 1910.

FAIRCLOUGH, F.R.—B. 1895; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civil serv. and assigned to C.O., Aug., 1914; on military service from 13th Dec., 1915 to 9th March, 1919; priv. sec. to Sir James Stevenson, 12th Aug., 1922; cler. offr., higher grade, 7th July, 1923.

FAIRLEY, WILLIAM, B.A., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1891; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge (schol.) B.A., (mech. science tripos) 1913; 6th Bu. E. Surrey Regt., 1914; 1st K.A.R., 1917-19; asst. engr., P.W.D., Kenya, Dec., 1919; ag. exec. engr., Mar. to Dec., 1920; dist. engr., P.W.D., Zanzibar, Feb., 1922; ag. asst. D.P.W., Mar. to Oct., 1923 and 14th Mar., 1924; exec. engr., P.W.D., Kenya, Aug., 1925.
FAIRWEATHER, C.—Apptd. govt. printer, Gold Coast, 19th Oct., 1907.

FAIRWEATHER, EUGENE ERNEST, M.A., LL.B.—B. 1884; ed. at King's and Acadia Unv. and Dalhousie Law Schl., N.S.; asst. solr., dept. of rlys. and canals, 1912; solr., 1914; counsel, 1919; asst. gen. counsel, Can. Nat. rlys., 1922.

FAIRWEATHER, COMMDR. JOHN HERBERT, D.S.O., R.D., R.N.R.—B. 1873; sub-lieut., R.N.R., 1902; 1st cls. certf., gunnery and torpedo, 1904; R.N., 1904-5; lieut., R.N.R., 27th July, 1905; lieut.-commdr., R.N.R., July, 1913; served with No. 2 Cross River col. and Cameroon Expedy. Force, 6th Aug., 1914 to 23rd May, 1915 R.N., June to Dec., 1915 and July to Nov., 1916 seconded to army as major, R.E., and posted i. I.W.T., Mesopotamia, Nov., 1916; dep. asst. dir. I.W.T., Mesopotamia, Nov., 1917; ment. in despatches and awarded D.S.O., 7th Mar., 1918; lieut.-col., R.E. and asst. dir. I.W.T., Mar., 1918; demob. 10th Apr., 1920; commdr., R.N.R. (ret.), 31st Mar., 1919; marine offr., S. Nigeria, 31st Aug., 1907; senr. marine offr., 1st Jan., 1920; priv. marine offr., 1st Sept., 1922; ag. dep. dir., marine and harbour mast., Lagos, May, 1925.

FAIRWEATHER, WILLIAM GEMMEL.—B. 1886; ed. at Univ. of St. Andrews (B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E.; asst. survr., N.W. Rhodesia, Sep., 1910; ch. asst. survr. and examiner of diagrams, Nov., 1917; ch. survr. on various occasions, 1916 to 1920; ch. survr., 1920; dir., surveys, 31st Oct., 1924.
FAITH, JOHN ALEXANDER AELFRED, B.A., Oxford.—B. 1903; ed. Harrow and New Coll., Oxford; cadet, F.M.S., Jan., 1927; attd. to Pahang sect., Feb., 1927.

FALCONER, JOHN.—B. 1898; ed. Robert Gordon's Coll. and Aberdeen Univ.; M.A., Aberdeen (1st cls. honors); cadet, F.M.S., Apr., 1921; ag. 2nd asst. dist. offr., Ulu Selangor, Apr., 1922; passed cadet, July, 1922; ag. asst. dist. offr., Kuala Kubu, Oct., 1922; asst. collr. of land rev., Kuala Lumpur, June, 1924; dep. regisr., titles, Selangor, June, 1924; second asst. adviser, Muar, Feb., 1926.

FARFAN, JOSE MANUEL.—B. 1876; 6th cls. secretariat, Trinidad, 29th Apr., 1895; 5th cls., 16th Aug., 1897; 4th cls., 1st Feb., 1902; 3rd cls. and sec. to central road board, 13th Feb., 1906; 2nd cls. conf. cls., secretariat, 1st Aug., 1910; 2nd cls. sec. and sup't. of govt. printing, 1st Apr., 1920; ag. 1st cls. col. sec. on several occasions since 1920.

FARMER, FRANK.—B. 1899; entd. C.O., Dec., 1914; asst. clk., Apr., 1917; on milly. serv., Mar., 1917 to Feb., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; promoted, under order in council of 1910, clk. of the 2nd div., Feb., 1920; ag. cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Dec., 1924; cler. offr., higher grade, 10th Oct., 1925.
FARQUHAR, EDWARD ALLAN.—Commsr., S. Aust. harbours bd., 1914; chmn., 1924.

FARQUHAR, GEORGE NEIL, M.C.—B. 1896; on milly. serv., Dec., 1915; cadet, Ceylon, Mar., 1920; attd. to secretariat, Apr., 1920; ditto, Puttalam Kach., June, 1921; office asst. to govt. agt., S. Prov., Mar., 1922; ditto to govt. agt., N.W. Prov., 1925; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, Oct., 1926.

FARQUHARSON, ROBERT ALEXANDER, M.A. (Oxon), M.Sc., F.G.S., A.O.S.M.—B. 1883; ed. Boys' High Schl., Otago Univ., N.Z. and St. John's Coll., Oxford; s.n.r. schol., N.Z. Univ., 1905; dipl. in mining engnr., 1905; 1st cls. hons., geology and 2nd cls., physical science, N.Z., 1906; Rhodes schol., N.Z., 1906; B.A., Oxford, hons. in geology and mineralogy, 1908; M.A., 1912; govt. petrologist, mines dept., W. Australia, 1911-22; lent at request of C.O. to Somaliland govt. to furnish rep't. on geological and mineral resources of Prot., 1923; govt. geologist, Somaliland, 1923-26; rec'd. thanks of S. of S. for geol. map of Somaliland; dir., agr. and geologist, Somaliland, 1926.

FARRELL, WILLIAM JOSEPH HENRY.—Entd. post office, C. of G.H., 1889; tel. dept., S. African Repub., 1895-1902; priv. sec. to min. of... just., Union of South Africa, 1910; priv. sec. to min. of posts and tels., 1912; priv. sec. to min. of lands, 1913; dept. of lands, 1916; asst. sec. to prime min. and ast. clk. of exec. coun., 1924; ast. sec. for external affrs., and clk. exec. coun., 1927.

FARRER, EDMUND HUGH, C.M.G. (1922).—Chartered acctnt.; entd. Transvaal civil serv., 1904; local govt. inspr., 1904; provincial auditor, Transvaal, Aug., 1910; sec., financial relations comsn., Mar., 1911; chief acctnt., treas., Union of S. Africa, July, 1912; under sec. for finance, Oct., 1916; sec. for finance, 1918.

FARRER, FREDERICK ARTHUR.—B. 1866; clk. and Zulu interp., mag. office, Inanda divn., 1889; 2nd ols. clk. of the ct. and Zulu interp., mag. office, lower Tugela, 1891; transf. to Estcourt divn., 1894; 1st cls. clk., 1897; regis., Natal high ct., 1899; mag. for col. of Natal, 1903; mag., Alfred divn., 1903; mag., Ixopo divn., ag. judge, Natal high ct., 1920; judge, native high ct., Natal, 1921.

FARRER, ROBERT GRAVES BLACKBURN.—B. 1897; ed. Marlborough Coll. and Jesus Coll., Camb.; 2nd lieut., R.H.A., 1916; lieut., 40th Batt. R.F.A., 1st Divn., France, 1917-18; capt., R.A. heads., XV. Corps, France, 1918; lieut. (ag. capt.), 40th Batt. R.F.A., 1st Divn., France and Germany, 1918-19; cadet, F.M.S., Mar., 1920; ast. chmn., sany. bd., Seremban, May, 1920; ag. ast. sec. to res., Selangor, July, 1920; ag. 4th ast. sec. to govt., Sept., 1920; priv. sec. to ch. sec., Oct., 1920; ag. chmn. and sec. sany. bd., and ast. regis., sup. ct., Seremban, Apr., 1921; ag. temp. 3rd ast. col. sec., S. Stilmta., Apr., 1921; ag. ast. dist. offr., Larut, June, 1921; ag. ast. dist. offr., Gopang and Kampar, Oct., 1921; ag. ast. dist. offr., Larut, Nov., 1921; passed cadet, Jan., 1922; ag. ast. dist. offr., Bruss, June, 1922; offr., cls. V; ag. harbmr., Jumput, Feb., 1925; ag. dist. offr., Pasir Puteh, Mar., 1925; offr., cls. IV, Mar., 1927.

FARREK, ROLAND J.—Ed. at Eton and Balliol Coll., Oxon; cadet, S. Stilmta., Nov., 1896; ag. dist. offr., Nibong Tebal, Mar., 1897, to Sept., 1898, Dec., 1898, to Mar., 1900; ag. collr. land rev., Penang, Mar., 1900; ag. dist. offr., Dindings, May, 1900; ag. sup. educ., Penang, June to Aug., 1900; head of Malay Coll., Malacca, May, 1901; ag. 2nd ast. col. sec., June, 1901; ag. dist. offr., Bukit Mertajam, May, 1902; confirmed, July, 1904; ag. dep. pub. proscr., July, 1904; resumed duty as dist. offr., B. Mertajam, Feb., 1905; ag. collr., land revenue, Singapore, June, 1908; seconded as municipal assessor, Singapore, Aug., 1911; promoted to class III., 11th Sept., 1911, but remained seconded; ag. British adviser, Kelantan, 1915; offr., cls. II., 25th May, 1917; offr., cls. I., B., 1st Jan., 1919; lent to F.M.S. Govt., 7th Feb., 1919; ag. pres., munic. comsnre., Singapore, June, 1919; chmn., Muhamadan advisory bd., 1st Jan., 1920; pres., munic. comsnre., Singapore, 1st Jan., 1921.

FARRER-MANBY, PERCY ALAN.—B. 1877; ed. at Charterhouse and Trin. Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1898; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1902; S.E. circuit; seven years practice in England; stip. mag. for Georgetown, B. Guiana, Nov., 1909; judicial comsnr., F.M.S., 8th Oct., 1915; ag. senr. puisne judge, Penang, Sept., 1920; ag. ch. judl. comsnr., F.M.S., Oct., 1920 to June, 1921 and Dec., 1921 to Jan., 1923; ag. ch. just., F.M.S., Aug., 1925 and Apr., 1926; judge, F.M.S., Nov., 1925.
FAULKNER, S.N., O.B.E. (1918)—Ast. auditor, E.A.P., 8th Jan., 1909; senr. ast. auditor, Apr., 1915; dep. ch. acctnt., Uganda Rly., 1917.
FAWCETT, J. F. ST. A.—Deputy regis., high ct., E.A.P., 1910 regis., high court, Feb., 1919; mast. and regis., sup. ct., Sierra Leone, July, 1921; ch. regis. and sheriff, Gold Coast, May, 1924.
FAZAN, S. H.—Ast. dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., Nov., 1911; dist. comsnr., Kenya, Feb., 1920.

FEETHAM, RICHARD.—B. 1874; ed. Marlborough and New Coll., Oxford; mem., Inner Temple; called to bar, 1899; deputy town clk., Johannesburg, 1902-3; town clk., Johannesburg, 1903-5; admitted advoc., Transvaal, 1905; legal adviser to high comsnr. for S. Africa, 1907-10 and 1912-13; K.C., 1919; mem., leg. coun., Transvaal, 1907-10; mem., H. of A., Union of S. Africa, 1915-23; lieut., 1st battn. Cape Corps, 1916-19; mem., Southborough comtee. on Indian Reforms and chmn. functions comtee., 1918-19; mem., India Office comtee. on Govt. of India Bill, 1919; ag. pres., sup. ct. of Swaziland, 1922-23; judge, sup. ct., Transvaal Prov. Divn., 16th Aug., 1923.
FELIX-JONES, IVOR, B.A.—B. 1899; ed. Westminster and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; supt., educn. dept., N. Provs., Nigeria, 8th Mar., 1922.
FELLING, C. L. N., C.M.G.—S. African Rlys.; seconded to Kenya as gen. man., Uganda Rly., Dec., 1922; mem., leg. coun.

FENN, JOHN CYRIL DOUGLAS, C.M.G. (1924).—B. 1879; ed. at Marlborough Coll.; ast. treas., Gold Coast, Nov., 1903; resigned, Dec., 1906; re-apptd., Jan., 1908; senr. ast. treas., June, 1908; retired, Apr., 1909; acctnt., treas., Cyprus, May, 1910; ast. treas., Apr., 1913; ag. treas., June to Nov., 1913, and Apr. to Oct., 1915; chief ast. sec., Nov., 1913; ag. chief sec., from 29th Aug., 1916, to 13th May, 1917; ag. treas., Dec., 1917, to Mar., 1918; regis. gen. and dir. of surveys, 1st Sept., 1918; ag. chief sec. to govt., Nov., 1918, to Sept., 1919; apptd. Br. deleg. of evkaf., 1st Aug., 1919; ag. treas., June to Oct., 1920; ag. chief sec. to govt., Dec., 1919, to Aug., 1920; chief sec., 25th Aug., 1920; admstd. govt. 27th Oct., 1920 to 3rd Feb., 1921, 17th to 23rd Mar., 1921, 14th May to 10th June, 1922, 27th June to 3rd Nov., 1922, 27th June to 13th Oct., 1923, and 27th June to 23rd Sept., 1925; col. sec., Br. Guiana, 1925.

FENTON, JAMES STEVENSON.—B. 1891; ed. Dundee High Schl. and Edinburgh Univ., M.A. (Edin.); war service, Mesopotamia 1917-19; passed progress exam. in Mendi; ast. dist. comsnr., Sierra Leone, 28th Apr., 1915.
FERGUSON, DAVID GILBERT.—B. 1861; called to the bar, 1890; judge of sup. ct., New S. Wales, Mar., 1912.

FERGUSON, HON. GEORGE HOWARD, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1870; ed. Kemptville, Ont., High Sch., Toronto Univ. and Osgoode Hall; called to bar, 1894; el. to Ont. legis., g.e., 1905; re-el., g.e., 1908, 1911, 1914, 1919 and 1923; leader of conservative party since 1920; premier of Ont., 1923.

FERGUSON, HERBERT, C.B.E., (1924).—B. 1874; ed. Perth and Edinburgh; apppd. after compet. exam. to Imp. civ. serv., inland rev. dep., 1893; col. treas., Grenada, Nov., 1909; ag. col. sec. and admin., Apr. to July, 1914; col. sec., July 1915; ch. censor and chmn., food control comtee. during war; represented Windward Is. at Canada—W. Indies confere., Ottawa, June, 1920; admstd. govt. of Grenada on various occasions between 1914 and 1924; ag. gov. and comdr.-in-ch., Windward Is., 15th July to 30th Oct., 1926.

FERGUSON, Hon. William Nassau.—B. 1869; ed. at Upper Canada Coll. and Osgoode Hall, Toronto; called to the bar, 1894; practised his profession at Toronto; K.C., 1903; bencher of the Law Society of Upper Canada, 1915; justice of appeal, sup. ct., Ontario, 4th Dec., 1916.

FERGUSSON, Gen. Sir Charles, Bart., G.C.M.G. (1924); K.C.B. (1916); K.C.M.G. (1918); C.B. (1911); M.V.O. (1906); D.S.O. (1898), D.L., J.P.—B. 1865; ed. Eton and Sandhurst; joined Grenadier Guards, 1883; adjutant, 1890-94; joined Egyptian Army, 1895; Sudanese campaigns, 1896-98; severely wounded, Rosaires (med. with eight clasps, ment. five times in despatches); commanded 15th Sudanese, 1899 (2nd cls. Medjidie); garrison and dist., Omdurman, 1900; adjt.-gen., Egyptian Army, 1901-3; commanded 3rd Batt. Grenadier Guards, 1904-7; brig.-gen., gen. staff, Irish commd., 1907-8; inspr., infy., 1909-13; served European War, 1914-18; in command, 5th divn. and subsequently of 2nd and 17th Army Corps; mily. gov., occupied German territory, 1918-19 (seven times ment. in despatches); gov. and comdr.-in-ch., New Zealand, 20th Oct., 1924; assumed govt., 13th Dec., 1924.

FERNANDO, Sir Hilariom Marcus, Kt. Bach. (1923), M.D., B.Sc., (Lond.), Fellow of Univ. Coll. (London); J.P.—B. 1864; ed. at the Royal Coll., Colombo, and Univ. Coll., Lond.; Ceylon Govt. Univ. Scholar and Gilchrist Scholar for the East, 1882-83; 2nd medical entrance scholar, Univ. Coll., 1884; Atchison scholar, 1888, 1st cls. hons. and gold medal, physiology, medicine, and forensic medicine (Univ. of London); asst. col. surg. and regir., Ceylon med. coll., 1890; 1st physician, general hosp., and lecturer in medicine, Ceylon med. coll., 1898; reported on outbreak of malaria in Galle, 1894, and plague in Bombay, 1897, (rec. thanks of govt.); ret. from govt. serv., 1909; served on Colombo lake development, railway traffic, education, opium, prevention of tuberculosis, and several other commissions and comtees. of enquiry (1909-1918); nominated mem. of legis. coun. of Ceylon as the first low-country Sinhalese representative, Jan., 1917; nom. unoff. mem., leg. coun., 1921-1924; ditto, exec. coun., 1921-1924.

FERNANDO, Peter Oliver.—B. 1898; cadet Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1921; attd. to Kurunegalla kach., Dec., 1921; ditto, Batticaloa kach., Feb., 1922; ditto, Jaffna kach., Mar., 1923; pol. mag., Puttalam, Apr., 1924; ag. pol. mag., Dandagamuwa, Nov., 1924; attd., Ratnapura kach., Sept., 1925; office asst., ditto, Mar., 1926; pol. mag., Dec., 1926.

FIDDES, Sir George Vandeleeur, G.C.M.G. (1917), K.C.M.G. (1912), C.B. (1901), C.M.G. (1905), B.A.—B. 1858; ed. at Dulwich Coll. and late scholar of Brasenose Coll., Oxford; 2nd cls. class. moda., 1879; apptd., after compet. exam., to be a clk. in the office of the sec. of state for the cols., 25th Mar., 1881; priv. sec. to Earl of Onslow, Feb., 1887; and to Baron H. de Worms (afterwards Lord Pirbright), Feb., 1888, to Aug., 1892; priv. sec. to Sir R. Meade, 11th Mar., 1896; 1st cls. clark, 1896; imperial sec. and acctnt. to Sir A. Milner, high commr. for S. Africa, Sept., 1897; political sec. to Lord Roberts at Pretoria, June, 1900; sec. to the Transvaal administration, Dec., 1900; returned to C. O., 1902, as prin. clk.; accounting offr., 1907; ast. under-sec. of state, 20th June, 1909; ohrmn., West African currency board, 1912-16; perm. under-sea. of state for the colonies; 10th Mar., 1916; sec. to the Order of St. Michael and St. George; ret., 1921.

FIDDIAN, Alexander.—B. 1875; ed. Univ. Coll., Cardiff, and Oxford; schlr. Pembroke Coll., 1893; 1st class classical honours, 1896; Goldsmiths' co. exhib., 1896; 1st class lit. hum., 1897; R.A., 1897; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd class clk. C.O., Oct. 12th, 1897; ast. priv. sec. to Rt. Hon. Alfred Lyttelton, S. of S. for the colonies, 1st Apr., 1905; ditto to Earl of Elgin, Dec., 1905; 1st cls. clk., Jan., 1907; mem. of deptmtl. comtees. of W. African med. staff, 1909; principal clk., 1st Nov., 1917; ast. sec., 1st Apr., 1920; estabm't. offr. for C.O. and Dominions office, 15th July, 1926.

FIELD, Edgar John.—B. 1874; ed. pub. schls. and Christian Brothers' Coll., Adelaide; ent. survey dept., S. Australia, Jan., 1891; clk., office of commr. of crown lands, 1892; sh. clk. and acot., 1909; ch. immgr. offr., 1911; sec. for lands, chmn. of advances to settlers bd. and recvr. of rents, 2nd Apr., 1917, chmn., irrig. commr., S. Aust., 1923.

FIELDGATE, Alan Frederic Edmond, B.A. (Oxon.)—B. 1889; ed. Worcester Coll., Oxford; ast. dist. commr., Gold Coast Colony, 28th Apr., 1915; ag. dist. commr. on various occasions, 1918-21; dist. commr., 5th Aug., 1922; ag. pol. mag., Mar. to June, 1925.

FIELDING, The Rt. Hon. William Stevens, P.C. (1923), D.C.L., LL.D.—B. 1848; mem. for Halifax, Nova Scotia assem., 1883; declined premiership, but entered govt. without office in that year, and continued in that position till May, 1884, when he resign.; premier, provincial sec., and treas., Aug., 1884; min. of finance in Sir W. Laurier's adminstr., June, 1896; deleg. to Col. Conf., London, 1902; one of H.M.'s plenipotentiaries for negotiation of Franco-Canadian treaty, 1907; one of the Royal Commiss. to inquire into trade relations between Canada and Brit. W. Indies, 1909; del. to Washington to discuss proposed reciprocity agreement, 1911; defeated at g. e. 1911; re-elec., g. e., 1917 and 1921; min. of finance in King admatn., 29th Dec., 1921; del. to League of Nations Assem., 1922.

FIENNES, Lieut.-Col. The Hon. Sir Kustace Edward Twisleton-Wykeham-Bart.—B. 1864; Lieut.-Col., Oxforfs Imp. Yeo.; served Riel rebellion, 1885 (medal with clasp); Egypt, 1887 and 1888 (medal with clasp, bronze star); pioneer expdn. to Mashonaland, 1890; South Africa, 1900 (medal with three clasps; twice ment. in despatches); European war, 1914 (France, Belgium, Dardanelles and Home); M.P. (L.) Banbury div. of Oxfordshire, 1906-1910, and 1910-1918; gov. and comdr.-in-chief, Seychelles, 6th July, 1918; assumed govt., 8th Oct., 1918; gov., Leeward Is., 29th Oct., 1921; assumed govt., 4th Dec., 1921.
FILLEUL, P. R.—Ast. dist. commr., E.A.P., 1910; dist. commr., Kenya, June, 1918.
FINCH, A.—Ast. supt., pol., Kenya, Dec., 1922.

FINCH, F. G.—B. 1882; ast. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., 1907; ast. state engrnr., Kedah, 1910; ast. state engrnr., Kedah, 1911 and 1915; exec. engrnr., F.M.S., 1913; state engrnr., Pahang, 1923; ast. state engrnr., Selangor, 1924; ast. ch. hydraulic engrnr., F.M.S., 1925.

FINLAY, George Hugo.—B. 1888 ed.; Repton and Oriel Coll., Oxford; B.A. (Hons. Mod. Hist.); ast. dist. offr., Southern Nigeria, 26th July, 1911; 2nd cls. dist. offr., 28th Nov., 1919; ast. sec., S. Provs., 14th June, 1922; ag. res., 22nd Sept., 1923.
FINLAYSON, HON. SIR JOHN GEORGE, K.C.M.G. (1910); LL.D., K.C.—Atty.-gen. and col. sec., New Zealand, Nov., 1906; attended Imp. Conf., 1911; resigned, 1911.

FINLAYSON, GEORGE ALEXANDER.—Bacteriologist, Singapore, May, 1903; pathologist, May 1906; ag. prin., S. Stilts., and F.M.S. Govt. med. sch., Mar., 1907; mem., S. Stilts., and F.M.S. med. coun., Jan., 1908; del. to Hong Kong med. conf., Jan., 1912; seconded for sp. duty on anti-malarial work, Mar., 1912; deleg. to Saigon med. congress, Nov., 1913; capt., R.A.M.C., Egypt, Sept., 1915; ag. ch. med. offr., gen. hosp. in addition, Jan., 1917; mem., S. Stilts., and F.M.S. med. coun., Jan., 1917.

FINLAYSON, GEORGE DANIEL.—B. 1882; ed. Picton Acad. and Dalhousie Univ.; B.A., 1907; apptd. to dept. of insurance, Canada, 1907; associate of Inst. of Actuaries of Great Britain, 1911; supt. of insurance, Canada, 1914; dep. min. of insurance, 1924.

FINN, BABIL ARTHUR.—B. 1888; ed. at Alley's Sch., Dulwich and Wadham Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1912, 2nd cls. mod. hist.; ast. dist. offr., N. Provinces, 29th Apr., 1914; attached to Eastern dept., C.O., 20th Dec., 1915, to 19th Mar., 1919; ag. ast. col. sec., Gambia, 17th May, 1919; ag. col. sec. for various periods, 1919-24; ast. col. sec., 17th Feb., 1920.
FIRR, TOM FREEMAN, A.M.I.C.E.—Ed. Crystal Palace Engng. Sch.; 1st ast. survr., Nyamaland Prot., June, 1903; ast. dir. of pub. wks., 1909.

FISET, MAJOR-GEN. SIR EUGENE, KT. BACH. (1917), C.M.G. (1915), D.S.O., G.H.S., B.A., M.D.—B. 1874; apptd. staff, adjt. med. corps., Canada, 1902; dir.-gen., med. services, 1903; dep. min. of militia and defence, vice-pres. of the militia coun. since 1906; recd. D.S.O. for service in S. Africa; mentioned in despatches.

FISHER, Rt. Hon. ANDREW, P.C. (1911).—B. 1862; M.L.A. for Gympie, Queensland, 1893 and 1899; sec. for rly. and min. of pub. wks., 1899; returned to first H. of R., C. of A., 1901; re-elected 1903 and 1906; min. for trade and cust., 1904; deputy leader of Federal Labour Party; chmn. of Labour party, 1907; prime min. and treas., C. of A., 1908-9, Apr., 1910—June, 1913; represented C. of A. at the Coronation of H.M. King George V., and at Imp. Conf., 1911; prime min., C. of A., Sept., 1914, to Oct., 1916; high commr. in London for Commonwealth of Australia, Jan., 1916; mem. of comte. for settlms. of ex-service men within the Empire, 1917; ret., 1920.

FISHER, JOHN CAMPBELL, O.B.E. (1918).—B. 1880; ed. at Oakham Sch. and Sid. Suss. Coll., Cambridge; clk., col. audt. branch, exchequer and audit dept., 14th July, 1902; ast. auditor, Lagos, 26th Sept., 1903; detached for duty in N. Nigeria, 16th Nov., 1903; ast. auditor, N. Nigeria, 30th Apr., 1906; local auditor, 24th July, 1909; examnr., col. audt. branch, E. and A. dept., 1st Feb., 1910; 1st div. clk. (2nd cls.), central office, 1st May, 1910; auditor, Malta, 17th Mch., 1913; mem. of control board and head of food and commerce control office, Malta, 1917; senr. clk., central office, 1st May, 1918.

FISHER, SIR STANLEY, KT. BACH. (1922).—Ed. at Westminster and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; M.A., 1891; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple and Lincoln's Inn (called 1890); apptd. pres. of dist. ct. of Kyrenia, Cyprus, May, 1902; puisane judge, Cyprus, 1911; ch. just., Cyprus, 1919; on sp. mission to Cairo under F.O., 24th Jan. to 14th Mar., 1924; ch. just., Trinidad, 1924; ch. just., Ceylon, 1926; passed exam. in modern Greek, Dec. 1903 and in Turkish, 1918; joint commr. for compiling revised edition of Statute Laws of Cyprus (published 1907), for which recd. thanks of legis. coun. and S. of S.; joint commr. under reprint of statutes law, 1913; edition published, Feb., 1914; commr. for laws, under reprint of statutes law, 1922; edition published, 1924; compiler of "The Mukhtar's Handbook," 1911; published "Ottoman Land Law," 1919.
FISHER, V. M.—Medical offr., K.A.P., Mar., 1919; senr. med. offr., Kenya, Jan., 1926.

FISHLOCK, WALTER CHARLES.—Trained at Royal Gardens, Kew; agric. instructor, Virgin Is., Apr., 1902; mem. exec. coun., 1903; mem. quarantine bd., 1906; mem. bd. of health, 1908; J.P., 1908; ag. commr. and dep. judge, summary juris., etc., Virgin Is., 7th May to 5th July, 1917.

FITCHETT, WILLIAM HERBERT.—Ck., agric. dept., Cape Colony, 22nd Feb., 1898; elk. to C.C. and R.M., Wodehouse, 27th Sept., 1898; Port Elizabeth, 23rd Oct., 1899; Coleberg, 16th Mar., 1900; Britstown, 16th Aug., 1901; acted as C.C. and R.M., Britstown, 1902; ast. regir., high ck., and chief elk. to the sheriff of the O.R.C., 29th July, 1902; acted as regir. and sheriff in 1903 and 1904; regir. and sheriff, O.F.S., 1st Aug., 1921.
FITZGERALD, THOMAS, O.B.E. (1927).—F.M.S. postal service; dep. P.M.G., Kenya, July, 1920; P.M.G., Jan., 1922.
FITZ-GIBBON, WILLIAM GUERIN.—B.A., Royal Univ. of Ireland; B. 1885; rly. dept., Hong Kong, 1913; 1st clk., colonial secretariat, Hong Kong, 1917; J.P., 1924; sec., P.W.D., 1927.
FITZMAURICE, ALEXANDER LINDSAY—M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P. (1909); M.B., B.S. (Lond.), 1913; late out-patient offr., ast. house surg. and house physician, Guy's Hosp.; med. offr., Somaliland Prot., 1914.

FITZPATRICK, RIGHT HON. SIR CHARLES, P.C. (1908); G.C.M.G. (1911); K.C.M.G. (1907).—Born in Quebec, 1853; Dufferin medallist, law faculty, Laval Univ., 1876; one of the counsel for the defence of Louis Riel at Regina in 1885; elected to Quebec legislature, 1890; to Dominion parl., 1896, again in 1900 and 1904; solr. gen., Canada, 1896; min. of just., 1902; ch. just. of Canada, 1906-1918; mem. of Hague tribunal of arbitration, 1908; lieut.-gov. of Quebec, 1918 to 1923.

FITZPATRICK, JAMES PATRICK, L.R.C.P.I. and L.R.C.S.I.—House Surg., Jervis St. Hosp., Dublin, Jan. to June, 1914; milly. serv., R.A.M.C., 1915-17; med. offr., med. dept., F.M.S., 11th Nov., 1921; ag. health offr., Tampin, Dec., 1921; med. offr., Taiping, May, 1922.
FLANAGAN, MAURICE JOSEPH, B.A. (Cantab.).—Ast. audir., S. Nigeria, Nov., 1912; audir., Cameroons Expedy. Force, 1916; audir., Windward Is., June, 1919; audir., Nyassaland, Oct., 1925.

FLAVIN, FRANK.—B. 1888; Imp. posts and tel., July, 1905; postmstr., Nyassaland, 26th Sept., 1910; ag. ast. postmstr.-gen., Mar., 1922 to Feb., 1923; ast. postmstr.-gen., 3rd Feb., 1923; ag. P.M.G., Nov., 1923 to June, 1924 and Feb., 1927.

FLCKER, ORIEL JOYCE, B.Sc.—Ed. Cheltenham Ladies' Coll.; assoc. of Cheltenham Ladies' Coll., 1908; gold med., Société Nationale des Professeurs de Français en Angleterre, 1909; B. Sc., Lond., 1912; Somerville Coll., Oxford, 1924-5; headmistress, girls' schl., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1925; author of "A School Chemistry," 1923.

PLEMING, EDWARD DONOVAN.—B. 1898; ed. Bradford Gram, Schol. and Jesus Coll., Camb.; 2nd lieut., R.G.A., Sept., 1917; Salonika, Dec., 1917-Feb., 1919; cadet, F.M.S., Apr., 1921; sent to Canton to study Chinese, Dec., 1921; attd. Chinese prot., Mar., 1924; offr., cls. V., Apr., 1924; attd., cust. dept., Apr., 1924; ag. asst. prot., Chinese, Perak, Sept., 1925; attd. Chinese prot., Ipoh, Mar., 1926.

PLEMING, ERNEST.—B. 1891; 5th class clk., crown agts. office, Oct., 1910; apptd., after compet. exam., elk. of the 2nd division of the civ. serv. and temporarily employed in the Bd. of Trade, Jan., 1912; assigned to C.O., April, 1912; on military service from 25th May, 1915 to 9th Feb., 1919; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Apr., 1922.
PLEMING, W. P.—B. 1887; apptd. after compet. exam., S. and T. elk., G.P.O.; lent to C.O., 9th May, 1921; cler. offr., C.O., 1st Sept., 1921; on mil. serv., 4th Apr., 1916 to 4th May, 1919.

FLETCHER, ARTHUR GEORGE MURCHISON, C.M.G. (1922), C.B.E. (1919).—Ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Trin. Coll., Oxford (exhib.), cadet, Hong Kong, 1901; seconded for famine relief work in Kwang Si, May-June, 1903; ag. asst. registr. gen., 1903-4; J.P., 1904; ag. asst. col. sec. and elk. of couns., 1905-8-9; priv. sec. to O.A.G., 1907; dep. offr. recvr., 1909; asst. to atty-gen., 1909; regiar. of trade marks and letters patent, 1910; ag. assessor of rates, 1911; ag. dep. registr., 1911; priv. sec. to O.A.G., 1912; offr. recvr., 1912; ag. asst. col. sec. and elk. of couns., 1913; ag. treas., 1913; asst. col. sec. and elk. of couns., Aug., 1913; priv. sec. to O.A.G., and to gov., Aug., Dec., 1913; ag. col. sec. and mem. of exec. and leg. couns. in 1916, 1917, 1918, 1919, 1920 and 1922; chmn., shipping control comte., 1917-19; ag. col. sec. and mem., exec. and leg. couns., 1923 and 1925; col. sec., Ceylon, Jan., 1926.
FLETCHER, REGINALD H.—Joined Jamaica civ. serv., post office dept., by compet. exam., 1890; 1st cls. elk., 1908; ch. elk., 1915; asst. postmr., 1921; postmr., Jamaica, 1925.

FLETCHER, WILLIAM.—B. 1872; ed. Caesius Coll., Camb., and St. Mary's Hosp., London; B.A., 1893; M.B., B.C., 1896; M.D., 1910; Univ. Schol., St. Mary's Hosp., 1893; Craig's research prizeman, London Schol. of Trop. Med., 1909; dist. surg., F.M.S., 1903; pathologist, Inst. for Med. Research, K. Lumpur, 1909; temp. lieut., R.A.M.C., 1915; capt., 1916-18; specialist in bacteriology, R.A.M.C.; bacteriologist, Inst. for Med. Research, K. Lumpur, 1920; ag. dir., govt. laboratories, F.M.S., July, 1920, July, 1921 and Mar., 1925; author of articles on beri-beri, malaria, dysentery, enterica, leprosy, syphilis, influenza, etc.

FLEURY, CAPT. A. M.—Second lieut., 3rd Royal Irish Fusiliers, May, 1900; capt., May, 1905; lieut., W.A.F.F., July, 1900; Ashanti campaign (medal), 1900; dist. commnr., N. Territories, G. Coast, Jan., 1907; local commdt., Cyprus mil. police, Feb., 1910; commnr., Nov., 1919.

FLINN, MAJOR WILLIAM HENRY, O.B.E. (1919)—B. 1895; ed. St. Andrew's Coll. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; Royal Irish Regt. and staff, 1914-19; admtn., offr., Nigeria, Sept., 1919; asst. sec., ch. sec.'s office, Cyprus, Mar., 1921; A.D.C. and priv. sec. to H Commnr., 14th Apr., 1921; ag. clk., exec. coun., Feb.-Mar., 1922 passed exam. in mod. Greek, Apr., 1922; ag. ch. astt. sec., July, 1922; ag. commr., Sept., 1922; ag. local commdt., police, Sept., 1922; ag. ch. astt. sec., June to July, 1923; ag. registr., co-op. societies, June, 1923; regiar., co-op. societies, July, 1923; ag. registr., co-op. credit societies, June, 1923 to Apr., 1924 and from Oct., 1924; ag. ch. astt. sec., for various periods, 1924-27; ag. col. sec. in 1926 and 1927; trade commr. for Cyprus in London, July, 1927.

FLOOD, J. E. W.—B. 1886; ed. at Portora Royal Schol., Enniskillen, and Trin. Coll., Dublin, B.A. (scholar univ. student); apptd. after compet. exam., 2nd cls. elk., C.O., 10th Oct., 1910; sec., edible nuts comte., 1916; 1st cls. elk., 15th Oct., 1918; on military service from 16th Apr., 1917 to 7th Dec., 1918; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; asst. sec., Feb., 1926; vice-chmn., col. advisory med. and sanv. comte., 1924-26.

FLOWERS, HON. P.—M.L.C., New South Wales, since 1900; vice-chmn. of partly standing comte., on pub. wks., 1904; chmn., 1907; vice-pres. of exec. coun., Oct., 1910; min. of pub. health, 1914; now pres. of legis. coun.

FLYNN, HON. EDMUND JAMES.—B. 1847; ed. Quebec Seminary and Laval Univ. (L.L.L. 1873); admitted to Queb. bar, 1873; prof. of Roman law in Laval Univ., 1874-1914; LL.D., Laval 1879; dean of law faculty; practised as advce. in Queb. City; mem., Queb. legislature for Gaspe, 1878-1900 when elected for Nicolet Co.; commr., crown lands, Chapleau admtn., 1879-1882; commr. of rlys., and solr. gen., Ross admtn., 1884-87; commr., crown lands, de Boucherville govt. from 1891-96; prime min. and commr. of pub. wks., May, 1896 to May, 1897; leader of opposition, 1897 to 1904; retired from Queb. legislature 1904; Q.C. by Dom. govt., 1887 subsequently by Queb. govt.; bataillonier of Queb. bar, 1907-09; apptd. judge of superior ct., June, 1914; promoted to ct. of king's bench, July, 1920.

FOLEY, GERALD ROBERT EDWARD, O.B.E., B.A.—B. 1886; ed. Tipperary Gram. Schol. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; schol. and senr. moderator in maths., Dublin Univ.; Royal Irish Constab., 1911-22; war serv. with R. Irish Regt. in France, Palestine and Salonica (rank major); twice ment. in desps. and awarded O.B.E. (mil.); asst. commdt., Br. gendarmerie, Palestine, 1922; commdt., Police Training Schol., 1926.

FOLLOWS, CHARLES GROFFEY SHIELD.—B. 1896; ed. Wellington; 2nd lieut., The King's (Liverpool) Regt., 1914; served in France, 1915-18, latterly as adjt.; on staff, 1918 and 1919, attached to H.Q., Southern dist., Ireland, and H.Q., Aldershot command; priv. sec. to gov. of Seychelles and elk. to couns., 1920; asst. sec., Gibraltar, 1925; ch. asst. sec., 1927.

FOORD, FRANCIS LATTON.—B. 1874; served in Cape Mounted Rifles, S. African War, 1899-1902; lieut., 1st S. Staffs. regt.; sub-inspr., Basutoland Mounted Pol., 1902; inspir., 1913; asst. commr., 1914; accompanied Basuto deputations to England in 1909 and 1919; mem., poor white comm., 1918.
FORAN, WILLIAM—B. 1871; entered civ. serv., Canada, 1890; 2nd cls. elk., 1894; sec., bd. of civ. ser. examns., 1895; 1st cls. elk., 1906; sec. of civ. ser. coman., 1908.
FORBES, HARRY.—B. 1873; cust. dept., Fiji, 1911; elk. of peace, Rawa, Fiji, 1914-18; J.P., 1915; cashier, treas., 1918; ag. acct., Gilbert & Ellice Col., 20th June, 1921; ag. treas., ch.
FOSDIKE, ALFRED ERNEST.—Served in Bechuanaland Prot. pol., 1907-1917; contrlr. of stores, 1917.

FOSTER, RIGHT HON. SIR GEORGE E., P.C. (U.K.) (1916); G.C.M.G. (1918); K.C.M.G. (1914). B.A., D.C.L., LL.D.—B. 1847; ed. Superior Schls. and Univ. of New Brunswick, Edinburgh and Heidelberg; prof. of classics, Univ. N.B., 1873-1877; elec. to H. of C., Canada, 1882, for electoral dist. of Kings, N.B.; min. of marine and fisheries, 1885; re-elec., 1887 and 1891 for same constituency; 1896 for York Co., N.B.; 1904 for North Toronto, Ontario; re-elec. 1908, 1911 and 1917 for same constituency; min. of finance in Sir John A. Macdonald's govt., 1888 to 1891, and in govt. of Sir John Thompson, Sir John Abbot, Sir Mackenzie Bowell and Sir Charles Tupper from 1891-1896; min. of trade and commerce in Mr. Borden's govt., 1911; ditto in National govt., Oct., 1917; mem. of Dominions royal comm., 1912; mem. of the Peace Conf. at Versailles, 1919, as joint representative of the Dominion of Canada; del. to League of Nations assem., Nov., 1920; resig. portfolio and called to the senate of Canada, 22nd Sept., 1921.
FOSTER, LIONEL HUBERT LISLE.—B. 1889; ed. Bradford and Christ Ch. Oxford (B.A. 1912); min. of educn., Egypt, Jan., 1913; asst. res., Nyasaland, Jan., 1915; res., 1st grade, Apr., 1925.

FOSTER, R. S.—B. 1894, Apptd. after compet. exam. clk. of the 2nd div. of the civil service; temporarily employed at Nat. Hl'th. Insurance Comsn., Mar., 1913 to June, 1914; assigned to C.O., June, 1914; on military service from 4th Aug., 1914, to 6th Jan., 1919; asst. auditor, Gold Coast, Jan., 1920.

FOSTER, HON. RICHARD WITTY.—Mem. of H. of Assem., S. Australia, 1893 to 1906; Comsnr. of pub. wks., 1899; ditto, and min. of industry, 1902-1904; ditto, and min. of agric., 1st March to 26th July, 1906; mem. of H. of R., C. of A., from 1909; min. of wks. and rly., 1921-3.

FOSTER, ROBERT SPENCE, M.A. (Oxon).—B. 1891; ed. Magdalen Coll. Schl. and St. Edmund's Hall, Oxford; 2nd Lieut., K.S.L.I., 1915; K.A.R., 1917-18; head mast., educn. dept., Tanganyika Territory, 1922; ag. dir., educn., Aug. to Oct., 1924.

FOSTER, HON. WALTER EDWARD.—B. 1874; ed. St. John Pub. and Grammar Schls.; chosen leader of opposition liberal party, New Brunswick, 1916; premier, N.B., 4th Apr., 1917; sec. of state of Canada, 5th Sept., 1925.

FOSTER, WILLIAM, M.B.E.—B. 1880; served in S. African War, 1900-02; Queen's Med., 3 clasps; King's Med., 2 clasps; Br. post office, 1897-1920; serv. in Gt. War, 1914-20; ment. in desps.; major, 1920; dep. postmr.-gen., Palestine, July, 1920; ag. postmr.-gen. on several occasions.
FOULGER, HENRY CECIL.—B. 1891; ed. Malvern Coll. and Pembroke Coll., Camb.; asst. res., Nyasaland, Aug. 1914; res., 1st grade, Jan., 1924.

FOWLER, ALBERT EDWARD.—B. 1868; ed. Lawrence Dist. High Schl. and Otago Boys High Schl. (N.Z.); endl. New Zealand govt. serv., 1885; resigned, 1893; re-apptd., 1897; dep. comsnr., taxes, 1914; comsnr., taxes, 1925.

FOWLER, JAMES ROBINSON.—B. 1875; ed. Rathmines Schl. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; 1st Hebrew Sizar, 1894; 1st entrance prize Eng. hist. and mod. geog., 1894; prizeman in classics and Eng. lit., 1895; Hebrew, 1896; second of first honours in classics, 1896; scholar in classics,

FRANCIS, CYRIL GERARD BROOKE.—App'td Fiji civ. serv., 1898; lieut., armed native constab., 1901; certif., musketry and machine-gun, Hythe, 1905; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1907; inspr., Fiji constab., 1907; barrister and solr., sup. ct., Fiji, 1908; A.D.C. to ag. gov., Dec., 1906 to 1909; ag. inspr.-genl. constab., and prisons, 1909 to 1910; A.D.C. to ag. gov., Aug., 1910 to Feb., 1911; ag. commr. and stip. mag., 1911-12; ag. chief pol. mag. July to Aug., 1911; ag. inspr.-genl., constab. and prisons, 1912 to 1913; 2nd grade dist. commr., May, 1912; ag. chief pol. mag., Mar. 1913 to Dec., 1914; 2nd in comd. local forces with rank of major. 5th Aug., 1914; mentioned by Army Council for services in 1914 in connection with defence of Fiji; capt., E. Surrey Regt., Mar., 1915; coy. comdr., K.A.R., E. Africa, 1916-19; 1st grade dist. commr., Fiji, Jan., 1916; regisr.-gen., regisr., sup. ct., commr. stamps, and pub. trustee, Fiji, Jan., 1920; cust., enemy property and contr. L.C.O., Fiji and W. Pacific, Feb., 1920; ag. atty.-gen., May, 1920 to May, 1921; commr. for the revision of the laws of Fiji, 1920 (not completed); atty.-gen., B. Honduras, July 1921; ag. oh. just., July, 1921 to May, 1922 and from July to Nov., 1923; chmn., comm. to enquire into outbreak of yellow fever, 1921; ohmn., retrenchment comtee, 1922; chmn., franchise comm., 1923; revd. and consol. laws, B. Honda., 1924; atty.-gen., Zanzibar, 27th Nov., 1924.

FRANCIS, HAROLD VANSITTART, M.B.E. (1924).—B. 1883; ed. Blundell's Schl.; pvt. sec. to admstr., N.W. Rhodesia, 1908; senr. grade clk., secretariat, Oct., 1912; ch. clk., 1st Apr., 1916; ag. astt. sec. on various occasions, 1921-26; astt. sec., N. Rhodesia, Aug., 1927; clk., exec. and leg. councils.

FRANCIS, WILLIAM, F.I.C.—Ed. Glasgow Univ.; "Malcolm Kerr" burner in chemy. and astt. to prof. of chemy., Glasgow Univ., 1890; astt. to Sir C. A. Cameron, C.B., govt. analyst, Ireland, 1892-4; govt. analyst, agr. chemist and lect. in chemy. to the govt. of Cyprus, 1900-20; ag. island postmtr., astt censor, and pres. of the note currency bd., Cyprus, 1915-20; dep. govt. analyst and geologist, B. Guiana, 1920; ag. dir. of science and agr., Feb.-July, 1921 and in 1922; astt. dir., science and agr., B. Guiana, 1922; ag. dir., science and agr. and chmn., bd. of agr., Feb. to Nov., 1926; senr. editor, B. Guiana Handbook, 1922.
FRANCKLIN, B. J. F.—Ast. jun. staff survr., E. Africa Prot., 1914; astt. dist. commr., May, 1915; 2nd grade res., Nyasaland, Jan., 1925; 1st grade ditto, Apr., 1925.
FRANZ, G. H., B.A.—B. 1896; ed. Transvaal Univ. Coll., Transvaal; inspr., native educn., O.F.S., 1926.
FRASER, ALASTAIR GARDEN, B.A.—B. 1902; ed. Rugby and Queen's Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1924; 3rd cls. hons., phil., polit., and econ.; astt. mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1924.

FRASER, REV. ALEXANDER GARDEN, M.A.—B. 1873; ed. Merchiston Castle, Edin., Edin. Univ. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; deacon, 1913; priest, 1915; C.M.S., Uganda, 1900-03; prin. Trinity Coll., Kandy, Ceylon, 1904; chaplain with 3rd divn., France, 1917-18; chaplain to the forces; prin., Prince of Wales Coll., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1924.
FRASER, FREDERICK WILLIAM, C.B.E. (1922).—B. 1870; ed. Sherborne Sch.; cadet, B. N. Borneo div. serv., 1896; dist. offr., Keningau, 1899; sec. to gov., 1903; res. of the interior,
FRASER, H.—Ed. Grammar Sch., Inveraray; George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh and Edinburgh Univ.; B.Sc., 1925; asst. conservator of forests, Tanganyika Territory, 1926.

FRASER, HUGH.—B. 1890; ed. Wellington and Exeter Coll., Oxford (schol.); cadet, F.M.S., 1913; ag. priv. sec. to oh. sec., Apr. 1914-Feb., 1915; ag. supervisor of cuit., Port Dickson, May, 1915; ditto, Port Swettenham, Jan., 1916; passed cadet, Dec., 1916; offr., cls. V., Dec., 1916; 3rd asst. sec., Fed. sect., Aug., 1917, priv. sec. to oh. sec. in addn., Sept., 1917; dist. offr., Jelebu, Feb., 1918; asst. dist. offr., Ipoh, Sept., 1920; offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1920; 3rd asst. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Dec., 1921; dist. offr., Kuantan, Sept., 1924; offr., class III., Nov., 1925.

FRASER, JOHN ALEXANDER, M.C.—B. 1896; ed. Trinity Academy, Leith, and Edin. Univ.; on mily. serv., 1915-19; cadet, Hong Kong, 1919; attd. to sany. dept., 1921; 3rd asst. to sec. for Chinese affrs., 1921; 2nd pol. mag., Apr., 1922; dist. offr. (North), June, 1922; passed in Cantonese, 1922; asst. head, sany. dept. and sec. to sany. bd., 1922; ag. head, sany. dept., 1923; dist. offr., South, 1924; admitted Middle Temple, 1925; ast. supt., impta. and expts., 1925; dist. offr., North, Feb., 1926.

FRASER, SIR JOHN GEORGE, KT. BACH. (1924). C.M.G. (1913).—B. 1864; cadet Ceylon olv. ser., Oct., 1887; ag. dist. judge, &c., Badulla, Feb., 1894; astt. govt. agent, Chilaw, Dec., 1899; land settm. offr., Aug., 1901; ag. govt. agt., W. Prov., 4th Jan., 1912; ag. controller of revenue, in 1911, 1912, 1913, 1915 and 1916; govt. agt., W. Prov., July, 1914; M.L.C.; ret., 1924.

FRASER, LAURENCE, L.R.C.P., L.R.C.S. (Eng.)—Lieut., R.A.M.C., Dec., 1915; capt., Dec., 1916; M.O., 15th (R.) Batt. York and Lancaster Regt., Dec., 1915; St. George's hosp., Malta, July, 1916; 63rd Gen. Hosp. Salonika, May, 1917; 1/1st S. Midland F.A., 5th Royal Sussex Regt., 11th Gloucester Regt., N. Italy, Nov., 1917; 1/6th Gloucester Regt., Albania, Feb., 1919; 1/6th Gloucester Regt., 2/13th London Regt., mil. hosp., Raseltin, Alexandria, May, 1919; dist. med. offr., Cyprus, Mar., 1921; ag. health offr., July to Nov., 1922.

FRASER, MALCOLM, O.B.E. (1919).—B. 1873; ed. at Inverness; entd. N.Z. land and income tax dept., 1893; acct., pub. serv. superannuation fund, 1908; govt. statistician since 1911; admtd. enrolment and balloting, Expedy. Force Reserve, 1916-18; N.Z. rep. at British Empire Conf. of Statisticians, 1920.
FRASER, PERCY LOUIS ALEXANDER, M.B.E. (1918).—B. 1867; entd. civ. ser., Trinidad, 1st Dec., 1886; supt. of prisons, 1st Jan., 1907.

FRASER, WALTER MORTIMER.—B. 1865; 3rd cls. clk., P.W.D., Jamaica, 1st Feb., 1886; acctnt. and clk., lands dept., 1st Oct., 1890; ag. 1st cls. clk., record office, 1st Aug., 1900 to 20th May, 1904; 1st cls. clk., treasy., 1st Nov., 1910; acctnt., govt. savings bank, 1st Nov., 1918; ch. clk. and acctnt., treasy., 1st July, 1920; dep. treas., 1st Apr., 1921; treas., 1st Apr., 1925; nom. mem., leg. coun.
FRASER, WINIFRED BLACHE.—B. 1868; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, 1892; mag., St. Andrew, Trinidad, Jan., 1923; mag., Caroni, Nov., 1923; mag., St. George East, Dec., 1924.

FRAZER, HON. FRANCIS VERNON, M.A., LL.B.—B. 1880; ed. Nelson Coll., Otago Univ. Coll., and Canterbury Univ. Coll.; barrister and solr., stip. mag., 1911; chmn., N.Z. pub. serv. appeal bd., 1918; ast. pub. serv. commnr., 1920; judge, et. of arbitn., 1921.
FREEMAN, B. J.—Ast. chief acctnt., G.P.O., E. Africa Prot., Apl., 1918; dep. ch. acctnt., 1924.
FREEMAN, GEORGE BOVET.—B. 1886; ed. Winchester Coll.; ast. dist. commnr., Gold Coast, 29th Apl., 1914; dist. commnr., 1918.

FREEMAN, WILLIAM GEORGE, B.Sc. (Lond.), A.R.C.S., F.L.S.—B. 1874; ed. at St. Olave's Gram. Schl. and Royal Coll. of Science, Lond.; B. Sc. (1st cls. hons. in Botany), 1898; priv. ast. to dir. of Royal Bot. Gardens, Ceylon, 1896-97; demonstrator in Botany, Royal Coll. of Science, 1897-1900; scientific ast., Imp. dep. of agric., West Indies, 1900-1903; ast. of colonial collections, Imp. Inst., 1903-1911; lecturer on trop. cultivation, C.O. Trop. African Serv. Course; visited S. Nigeria on special serv., 1904; ast. dir. of agriculture and govt. botanist, Trinidad and Tobago, 1911; dir., ditto, 1st May, 1917; mem., leg. coun., 1923; vice-pres., bd. of agric.; mem. of govt. commn. on deterioration of cacao and educn.; editor, Bulletin of Dept. of Agric.; author of various books and papers on tropical agric. and botany.

FREESTON, LESLIE BRIAN.—B. 1892; ed. at Willaston Schl., Nantwich and Oxford; scholar New Coll., 1911-1919; 2nd cls., hons. mod., 1913; B.A. (war) 1919; on mil. ser., 17th Sept., 1914 to 8th Mar., 1919; temp. 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 10th Mar. to 16th July, 1919; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 16th July, 1919; ast. prin., 1st Apr., 1920; priv. sec. to perm. under-secr., 18th June, 1920; sent to Ceylon for temp. serv. as sec., salaries commn., 1921; act. princ., 6th Dec., 1922; princ., 1st April, 1924.

FRIMANTLE, MAJOR JOHN MORTON, C.M.G. (1927). M.B.E. (1918).—B. 1876; ed. at Eton and Hertford Coll., Oxford; M.A., 1902; served in S. Africa, first in the ranks (Distinguished Conduct Medal), and afterwards as capt., 15th Batt. I.Y.; is hon. capt. in the army and capt., 3rd Co. of London I.Y.; priv. sec. to S. of S. for War and ast. sec. to royal commn. on militia and volunteers, 1903-4; comptroller of the household to gov-gen. of Canada, 1910-11; 3rd cls. res. Northern Nigeria, Nov., 1904; 2nd cls. res., Jan., 1910; is now res. at Zaria.
FRENCH, JOHN KERRIE.—B. 1872; clk., poor law bd., Sandy Point, Mar., 1894; clk., P.O., St. Kitts, Nov., 1897; clk., treasy., St. Kitts, Oct., 1907.

FRENCH, SIR SOMERSET RICHARD, K.C.M.G. (1901), C.M.G. (1896).—B. 1848; postmr.-gen.; apptd. money order dept., G.P.O., Lond., 30th Aug., 1866; transf. to sec.'s off. (postal branch), Sept., 1869; in Feb., 1870, asted. in transfer to State of the undertakings of the telegraph companies in the U.K.; apptd. offr. in charge of intell. branch, telegraph, and subsequently to control of racing and special arrangements branch of impl. telegraph service; also May to Oct., 1874, supervised surveying branch, home counties dist.; in July, 1878, selected to proceed to Cyprus with expeditionary force under Lieut.-Gen. Sir Garnet Wolseley, to organise postal and telegraph service on that island. Shortly after return to U.K. was asked to undertake reorganisation of Cape Colony postal service; acted as sec. and acctnt., P.O., Cape Colony, 1st Aug., 1880, to 31st July, 1881, when appntn. confirmed; introduced P.O. savings bk. system, 1st Jan., 1884; from which date acted as controller of branch in addition to other appntnts.; P.M.G. and gen. man. of telegrapha, Cape Colony and Basutoland, 27th Jan., 1892; also P.M.G., B. Bechuanaland, 1st Apr., 1893, until 1897, when that territory annexed to Cape Colony; P.M.G., Bechuanaland Prot., 1897; organised postal and telegraph services of Rhodesia, and acted as gen. man. of Rhodesian telegraph service, 1893-7; and man. Trans-Continental telegraph co., 1893-7; mem. of tender and advisory bds.; agt. gen. for Cape Colony in Lond., 1907, retired, 1st July, 1910.

FRERE, SIR BARTLE HENRY TEMPLE, KT. BACH, (1918), LL.B.—Ed. at Charterhouse schl. and Trin. Coll., Cam.; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1887; pres. dist. court, Cyprus, 1897; transf. to Gibraltar as pol. mag. and coroner, 1902; atty.-gen., 1911; chief justice, Apl., 1914; judge of the prize ct., Aug., 1914; cable censor during the European War; ret., Aug., 1922.

FRERE, NOEL GRAY.—B. 1885; ed. Wanganui Collegiate Schl., N.Z., and Cranby Schl.; asst. dist. comsnr., S. Leone, 31st July, 1909; dist. comsnr., 7th Jan., 1915; ag. prov. comsnr., N. Prov., Sept., 1921 to Nov., 1921.

FRETTZ, WILMOT THEODORE STUART.—B.1869; ed. Framlingham Coll., Suffolk and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; Rhodesian adminstr., B.S.A.P., N. and S. Rhodesia, 1913-19; Transvaal Border Patrols, 1918; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1921; atty.-gen., St. Lucia, 1923; chmn., agrl. coman., 1924; ag. admstr., St. Lucia, Dec., 1924; ag. ch. just., St. Vincent, July, 1925.

FROST, CAPT. MEADOWS, M.C.—B. 1875; ed. Charterhouse and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; B.A., Oxon; cadet, F.M.S., 1898; passed cadet, 1901; cls. VI, 1903; cls. V, 1905; seconded as H.B.M.'s consul for west coast of Siam, 1905; cls. IV, and adviser to H.H. the Raja of Perlis, 1910; acted Br. adviser, Kedah, 1911; cls. III, 1912; joined the Army for gen. serv., 1915; discharged, 1919; cls. II, 1919; ag. dist. offr., Kinta, 1920; dist. offr., Larut, Sept., 1922; comsnr., lands and mines, Johore, Aug., 1923.

FRY, WM. HERBERT, L.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.).—B. 1868; late house surg. and house physician, N.W., Lond. hosp.; asst. electrotherapeutic physician, Charing Cross hosp.; house surg., Liverpool hosp.; house surgeon, S. Stltims., 13th Aug., 1896; supernumerary col. surg., Sing., 1st Apl., 1900; col. surg., Prov. Wellesley South, 17th Nov., 1900; ditto, Prov. Wellesley North, 1st Mar., 1901; asst. col. surg. res., Penang, 18th Mar., 1901; state surg., Pahang, F.M.S., June, 1905; ag. senr. med. offr., Penang, 1908; med. offr., grade I, 1st Oct., 1908; ditto, Perak, 7th Apl., 1909; ag. senr. med. offr. in charge, gen. hosp., Singapore, 14th Apl., 1911; ag. health offr., Perak N., 6th Oct., 1912 to 24th Sept., 1913; ag. senr. med. offr., Perak in addtn., 21st June, 1913 to 8th May, 1914; ag. health offr., Perak N., 8th June, 1914; med. offr. in charge, Pahang, 1st May, 1915; mag., Raub dist., in addition, 1st July, 1916; ch. med. offr., Penang, Aug., 1919; ag. ch. med. offr., Singapore, 27th May, 1922.

FULLER, SIR F. C. B. D., K.B.E. (1919), C.M.G. (1906).—Cadet, Fiji, 1884; res. comsnr., Rotumah, 1889; dis. comsnr., Lagos, 1892; res. of Ibadan, 1897; col. treas., Lagos, 1901; asst. sec. to govt., Malta, 1902; mem. exec. coun.; ch. comsnr., Ashanti, 1906; ret., 24th July, 1920.

FULLER, HON. SIR GEORGE WARBURTON, K.C.M.G. (1919).—Elected to first H. of R., C. of A., 1901; re-elected, 1903 and 1906; min. of home affairs, C. of A., June, 1909 to Apl., 1910; col. sec., N. S. Wales, Nov., 1916-20; premier, 1922-25.

FULTON, ADAM, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1897; lient., May, 1915; capt., 1916; on active serv. with 1st, 6th and 2nd batts., Border Regt., Gallipoli, Sept. to Nov., 1915; Egypt and Suez defences, Jan. to July, 1916; France, July to Sept., 1916 and from Jan. to Aug., 1917; wounded, 26th Sept., 1916; Italy, Deo., 1918 to Jan., 1919; demob., Jan., 1919; vety. offr., Gold Coast, 18th May, 1926.

FURKERT, FREDERICK WILLIAM, C.M.G. (1926), M.I.C.R., A.M.I.M.E.—B. 1876; ed. Hokitika High Schl., Wellington and Dunedin Tech. Schls., and Otago Univ., N.Z.; joined N.Z. pub. wks. dept., 1894; res. engrn., 1903; inspecting engrn., 1912; asst. engrn.-in-chief, 1919; engrn.-in-chief and under sec., 1920.

FURNESS, ROBERT HOWARD.—B. 1890; ed. King William's Coll., Isle of Man; solr. (hons.), 1902; practised at bar of B. Honduras, 1906-13; regar.-gen., B. Honduras, 1918; ag. atty.-gen., 1914-15; lieut.-comdg. 1st B. Honduras was contingent, 1915; served with B. W. I. Regt., 1915-19; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1919; mag., Tanganyika Territory, 9th Jan., 1920; senr. mag., Apr., 1921; ag. puisne judge, Nov., 1922 to Feb., 1923; solr. gen., Trinidad, Nov., 1923; ag. atty.-gen., Apr. to Oct., 1924 and from 15th Jan., 1925 to 13th Aug., 1926; ch. just., Barbados, Oct., 1926.

FURNES-SMITH, CECIL, Barrister-at-Law.—B. 1890; ed. Birkenhead Coll., St. John's Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1912; LL.B., 1913; 2nd lieut., gen. res. of offrs.; asst. dist. comnr., G. Coast, 29th Apr., 1914; dist. comnr., 1921; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1922; dist. crown coun., Mar., 1925; crown coun., G. Coast, 18th Mar., 1925.

FURSE, MAJOR R. D., D.S.O.—B. 1887; ed. Eton and Balliol Coll., Oxford, B.A.; asst. priv. sec. to sec. of state (Mr. Lewis [now Lord Harcourt]), Dec., 1910 to Aug., 1914; on active service in 1st King Edward's Horse, K.O.D.R., 5th Aug., 1914 to 26th Nov., 1918; served in France and Italy, Apl., 1915 to 26th Nov., 1918; promoted capt., 1914; major, 1916; wounded, 1917; D.S.O., July, 1918; Bar to D.S.O., gazetted, Mar., 1919; twice ment. in des.; asst. priv. sec. (appntts.) to sec. of state (Lord Milner), May, 1919; Mr. Winston Churchill, Feb., 1921; Duke of Devonshire, Oct., 1922; priv. sec. (appntts.) to sec. of state, Mr. Thomas, Jan., 1924; Mr. L. C. M. S. Amery, Nov., 1924; visited Canada on C.O. business, 1922, and again in 1923 as rep. at Imp. Forestry Confce; mem., bd. of govrs., Imp. Forestry Inst.

GALEA, ARTHUR.—B. 1872; ed. Malta Lyceum; ent. Malta civ. serv., 1891; 3rd cls. clk., 1st Jan., 1895; 2nd cls. clk., 1st July, 1901; asst. registr. cts. of judicial pol., 17th Dec., 1913; registr., 6th Jan., 1914; transf., secretariat, 14th May, 1918; ch. clk., lieut.-gov.'s office, Sept., 1920; ch. clk. to legislature, 20th Dec., 1921; compt., charitable institutions, 4th Apl., 1923; offl. sec. to head of ministry, 1st Mar., 1927.

GALEA, PROF. ROBERT V., L.S. & A.—B. 1882; matric. (with hons.), Malta Univ., 1900; graduated as land survr. and architect, 1903; lect. on engnr. and architecture, Malta Univ., 1905; prof., ditto, 1915; ag. rector of univ., 1923; el. for 1st divn. in leg. assem., June, 1924; re-el., Aug., 1927; min. for pub. health and charitable institutions, 16th Aug., 1927.

GALL, GEORGE LOUIS BECKLES.—B. 1880; ed. Queen's Coll., B. Guiana, 1897; ent. govt. serv., B. Guiana, 1897; J.P., 1902; 3rd grade comr. of taxation, 1904; sworn weigher and gauger, 1905; qual. in colloquial Hindi, 1909; ag. imigr. agt. in addn., 1911; sec. to rum duties comm., 1912; ag. imigr. agt. in addn., 1913; supervisor, pub. wks. in addn., 1915; 1st grade comr. of taxation, Oct. 1915; sec., sugarcomte, 1916; ag. prin. elk., col. sec.'s office, June to Dec., 1924; ag. ch. comr. in Jan. and Feb., 1925; dir. of co-op. socs., 1st Jan., 1926; ag. ch. comr., 24th July, 1926; ag. col. treas. from June, 1927.

GALLAGHER, LIEUT.-COL. ALBERT ERNEST, C.B.E. (1927), D.S.O., (1907).—Reserve of offrs.; B. 1872; ed. privately; served in S. African war, 1900; served with N. Nigeria Regt., W.A.F.F., 1901-1908; Argungu operations, 1902 (desps.); Sokoto expdn., 1903; Sokoto expdn., 1906 (wounded, deeps. D.S.O.); asst. res., N. Nigeria, 1908; local comdt. Cyprus mil. police, 1909; chief comdt., Cyprus mil. police, 1915; served with 7th Royal Munster Fus., 10th Divn., Dardanelles, 1916, and Serbia, 1915; major and 2nd in command, 11th Worcester Regt., Salonika Force, Sept., 1916; served with 7th Royal Fus. (63rd Div.) as 2nd in command, 1917, B.E.F., France; lieut.-col. comdgm. 2nd Royal Inniskilling Fusiliers, Sept., 1918, B.E.F., France; wounded, Oct., 1918; resumed duties as chief comdt., Cyprus mil. police, Aug., 1919; ag. regtr. gen. in 1921 and 1922; Br. del. of Evcaf., Aug., 1920; mem., exec. coun., Apr., 1922; mem., gen. coun., 1926.

GALLIHER, HON. WM. ALFRED.—B. 1860; ed. pub. and high schls., Walkerton, and Collegiate Inst., Collingwood; called to the bar, Manitoba, 1887; N.W.T., 1889; B. Columbia, 1897; served in the Nile expdn., 1884-5; elected to H. of C., Canada, 1900 and 1904; judge of ct. of appeal, B. Columbia, 1909.

GALT, HON. ALEXANDER CASIMIR.—B. 1853; ed. Hellmuth Coll., Toronto Univ.; practised law in Toronto, 1876-1896; Rossland, B.C., 1896-1905; Winnipeg, 1906-1912; judge of the ct. of king's bench, Manitoba, 1912; contrib. to Can. Law Journal and Can. Law Times.

GALWAY, LT.-COL. SIR HENRY LIONEL, K.C.M.G. (1910), C.M.G. (1899), D.S.O. (1896).—B. 1859; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Sandhurst; entered army, May, 1878; promoted lieut., Mar., 1881; capt., Oct., 1887; brevet-maj., May, 1897; maj., Mar., 1899; and lt.-col., Apr., 1901; apptd. dep. comsnr. and vice-consul, Oil Rivers prot., Mar., 1891; dep. comsnr. and consul, Niger Coast prot., Jan., 1897; and divn. comsnr. Niger Coast prot. (now South Nigeria), Apr., 1899; ag. consul-gen. Niger Coast prot., Aug. to Nov., 1896; Jan., 1897; Feb., 1898, to Jan., 1899; ag. high comsnr., South Nigeria, Mar. to Dec., 1900; Brass expdn., 1895 (desps., medal with clasp, D.S.O.); Benin expdn., 1897 (desps., clasp, brevet majority); Aro expdn. as chief political officer, 1901-2 (desps., medal with clasp); gov., St. Helena, 1902; gov., Gambia, 30th Sept., 1911; gov., S. Australia, 27th Feb., 1914; assumed gov't., 18th Apr., 1914, retired, 1920.

GALWAY, JOHN PATRICK FRANCIS.—Ed. St. Francis Xavier's Coll., Liverpool; elk., vety. pathological lab'y., Kabete, Aug., 1910; seconded, mly. serv., 1915; corp., E. African Vty. Corp., 1st June, 1916; lieut., ditto, 17th Oct., 1917; lieut., 3rd K.A.R.; "1914-15" Star, Gen. Serv. and Victory med.; labour inspnr. offr., E. Africa Prot., Apr., 1919; office supt., P.W.D., Dec., 1922.

GAMBAL, LAWRENCE IRVING WASHINGTON.—B. 1876; elk., gen. man., rlys., Trinidad, 5th May, 1893; elk. to engr. of surveys, 15th Mar., 1900; 2nd elk., crown lands office, 1st July, 1907; ch. elk., crown lands office, 15th Feb., 1914; warden, St. David, 16th Apr., 1920; warden, St. Patrick, 16th June, 1922.
GAMBLE, F. C.—Asst. dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., Feb., 1915; rea. mag., July, 1923.
GAMBLE, JOSEPH SAMUEL.—Ag. sub-inspnr. of police, B. Guiana, 1905; wounded in Georgetown riots, 1905; sub-inspnr. of police, 1906; Hythe musketry certif., 1908; dist. inspnr. of police, 1912.

GAMMANS, LEONARD DAVID.—B. 1892; 2nd lieut., R.F.A., 1915; B.E.F., France, 1916; lieut. and capt., 1917; seconded for serv. as instr. in gunnery and equitation, R. Mily. Coll., Canada, May, 1918; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1920; ag. 2nd asst. dist. offr., Ulu Selangor, Jan., 1921; passed cadet, Jan., 1922; ag. asst. dist. offr., Kuala Kubu, Apr., 1922; asst. supervisor, cust., Port Dickson, Oct., 1922; supervisor, cust., Parit Buntar, Mar., 1923; ag. dist. offr., Port Dickson, July, 1923; served at Malayan pavilion, Br. Empire Exhibn., Mar., 1925; offr., cls.IV, June, 1927.

GANADO, ROBERTO F., LL.D.—B. 1875; ed. Malta Lyceum; matric., Malta Univ., 1891; LL.D., Malta, 1898; bar, Malta, 1899; advoc. for poor, 1905; mag., judl. pol., 1918; examr., laws, Malta Univ., 1919; chmn., licensing bd., 1920; visitor, notarial acts Nov., 1921; ag. crown advoc., 23rd to 30th June, 1922; ag. pub. pros. and treas. coun., 1st July to 9th Oct., 1922; chmn., bd. of prison visitors, 1924; ag. judge, supr. cts., 11th June to 22nd Sept., 1924; mem., gen. coun. of Univ. of Malta, 1924; judge, supr. cts., 16th Sept., 1925.

GARDINER, FREDERICK GEORGE.—B. 1874; ed. Diocesan Coll., Rondebosch, and Keble Coll., Oxford; B.A., Cape and Oxford; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1896; admitted to Cape bar, 1897; ag. puisne judge, Natal, 1907; atty.-gen., Cape Province, 1910; puisne judge of sup. ct. of S. Africa (Cape Prov. Div.), 16th Oct., 1914; judge pres., Cape Prov. divn., 1926.

GARDNER, ALFRED.—B. 1879; ed. Saltley Teachers' Training Coll., Birmingham; schlmr., Straits govt., 19th Apr., 1903; headmnr., Gold Coast, 5th Jan., 1910; inspr., schl., 15th Apr., 1911; ag. prin., training institution, 16th Jan. to 30th Apr., 1912; ag. senr. inspr., schl., on several occasions; censor, Cape Coast, 20th Aug., 1914 to 12th Jan., 1915; prov. inspr., schl., 1st Jan., 1915.
GARDNER, H. M.—Asst. conservator of forests, E.A.P., May, 1914; senr. asst. conservator, forests, Kenya, Dec., 1923; ag. conservator, 1925.
GARDNER, JOHN HISLOP, B.Sc.—B. 1883; ed. Kelvinside Acad. and Univ., Glasgow; asst. engrnr., Basutoland, 1909; govt. engrnr., Swaziland, 1919; served with R.E., Flanders, 1916-18.

GARNETT, W. J., B.Sc. (Lond.), B. 1889.—Apptd., after compet. exam., elk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O., Jan., 1907; seconded for service in Oversea Sttlmt. Office, Jan., 1919.

GARRAN, Sir Robert Randolph, K.C.M.G. (1920), Kt., Bach. (1917), C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1867; barrister-at-law; sec. to drafting comm. of Australian Fed. Convention, 1897-8; sec. to atty.-gen.'s dept., C. of A., Jan., 1901, and parly. draftsman; solr.-gen., 1916; author of "The Coming Commonwealth," 1897, and joint author (with the Hon. Sir John Quick) of "The Annotated Constitution of the Austn. Commonwealth," 1901.

GARRAWAY, David Julian.—B. 1878; entd. pub. serv., St. Lucia, Mar., 1892, as superv. clk., treas.; ag. 4th clk., Nov., 1892; clk., P.O., June, 1893; ag. clk. to adminstr.-in-chief, Windward Is., Aug., 1893; 2nd clk. regis.'s off., June, 1895; ch. clk., Jan., 1898; ag. regis., sheriff and adminstr.-gen., on 3 occasions, 1901-2-3; rev. offr., Grenada, June, 1903; ag. pol. mag., West Dist., Feb., 1904; additional coroner, West Dist., Mar., 1904; ag. pol. mag., North Dist., for spec. purpose of hearing appeals agst. town bil. assessmts. for 1904; asst. tax offr. in connectn. with hearing appeals agst. assessmts. for land and house taxes for 1904; ag. pol. mag., West Dist., May and June, 1906; ag. comsnr., Carricau, Aug.-Sept., 1906; ag. road surv., West Dist., Oct., 1906, in addition to duties of substantive office; ch. clk., regis.'s off., and dep. regis., Oct., 1907; regis., sup. ct., Oct., 1914.

GARRAWAY, Lt.-Col. Sir E. C. F., K.C.M.G. (1922), C.M.G. (1911), L.R.C.S.I.—Dist. surg., Millwood, Kranz, Cape Colony, 1888; ditto, Kuruman, Br. Bech., 1891; surg., Boch. Bord. pol., 1892; divml. med. offr., S.A.C., with rank of major, 1901; prin. med. offr., ditto, Mar., 1906; mil. sec., S. Africa, from 1st July, 1908, with rank of major; mil. sec. to Lord Gladstone, 1910-1913; mem. of S. Rhodesia native reserves comsnr. since May, 1914; served in Matabele wars and Boer war (1899-1902); res. comsnr., Bech. Prot., 1st May, 1916; res. comsnr., Basutoland, 15th Dec., 1917; ret., 19th Apr., 1926.

GARTHORNE, Eric Henry Rudston.—B.S.A., pol. serv.; clk., govt. house, Cape Town, 1898; clk., high comsnr.'s office, 1901; permit offr., native affrs. dept., Transvaal, 1902; ch. clk., native affrs. dept., Transvaal, 1902; prin. clk., native affrs. dept., Union of S. Africa, 1910; ch. clk., native affrs. dept., Union of S. Africa, Oct., 1919; under sec., native affrs., 13th Oct., 1926.

GARTON, Harry.—B. 1889; served in R.F.A., France, Salonica, Egypt and Palestine, 4th Aug., 1914 to Apr., 1919; clk. to court martials offr., R.A.F., May to Sept., 1919; Admiralty, 21st Oct., 1919 to Oct., 1922; served on Naval Inter-Allied Comsnr. of Control in Germany, May, 1921 to Oct., 1922; cler. offr., C.O., 21st Oct., 1922.
GARVEY, Ronald Herbert, B.A.—B. 1903; ed. Trent Coll. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; cadet, Fiji and Western Pacific services, 10th Nov., 1926.

GARVIN, Thomas Forrest, K.C.—B. 1881; Crown counsel, Ceylon, Mar., 1908; ag. solr.-gen., Mar., 1913; ag. addtl. dist. judge, Colombo, Feb., 1914; solr.-gen., 1915; ag. puiane just., Nov., 1921 to Jan., 1922; ag. atty.-gen., June, 1922 to Dec., 1922; ag. puiane just., sup. ct., Jan., 1923; puiane just., June, 1924; ag. ch. just., 7th July to 19th Sept., 1926.
GASH, Vincent Gordon.—Served in Bechuana land Prot. pol., 1911-15; sub-inspr., 1915; passed 3rd grade Seawana exam., 1923.

GATFIELD, William Huket, M.C.—B. 1895; ed. Streatham Gram. Schl. and St. George's Coll., London; apptd. to the 2nd divn. of the Imp. civ. serv., 1914; joined Queen's Westminster Rifles, 1915; 2nd lieut., 1916; served in France and demob. with rank of heut., 1919; cadet, S. Stiltsm., 20th Nov., 1920; proceeded to China to study Chinese, July, 1921.
GAUNT, A. E.—B. 1897; entd. C.O., Aug., 1913; apptd. ass't clk., Oct., 1915; on mil. ser. from Dec., 1915 to Mar., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920.

GAUNT, Reginald Frederic, M.A.—B. 1885; Hastings exhbnr., Queen's Coll., Oxon.; B.A. (Hons. Mod. Hist.), 1908; F.R.G.S.; asst. dist. comsnr., S. Nigeria, 26th July, 1911; special comsnr., Arochuku mutiny cases, 1913; educa. dept., E. Africa Prot., 8th July, 1914; ag. liquidator of enemy aliens, 7th Jan., 1918; inspr. coast educn., 1st Apr., 1919; senr. inspr. of schls., Kenya, 17th Aug., 1920; ag. dir. of educn., 3rd May, 1921; dir., educn., Nyasaland, 1925.

GAUNTLETT, Hugh Gauntlett.—B. 1873; barrister-at-law; called to bar, Inner Temple, Nov., 1919; entd. civ. serv., Jamaica by compet. exam. and apptd. asst. clk. of cts., 1st Mar., 1894; dep. ditto, Apr., 1903; ag. clk. of cts., on various occasions between June, 1907 and June, 1912; clk. of cts., 1st Feb., 1915; ag. rea. mag., St. Thomas, Aug., 1926 and from Sept., 1926.

GEBERS, H. L.—Student Int. S.N.A. Dep., 1894; clk., Zulu and D. Int. mag. ct., Dundee, 1895; 3rd cls. clk., 1895; 2nd cls. clk., 1900; lent to Transvaal, 1901; clk. of ct. P. P. Vryheid, 1901; J.P., Vryheid, 1901; A. A. M., Vryheid, 1902; reverted to Natal service, 1903; transferred to mag. ct., Dundee, 1904, senior clk. and A. A. M.; ast. mag., Inanda div. at Indwedwe, 1912; mag. Emtonjaneni div., Nov., 1916; Ngotshe, Oct., 1920; Helpmakaa, Apr., 1926; Nongoma, 1st Mar., 1926.

GEERING, Sidney.—Temp. asst. to irrign. engrnr., P.W.D., B. Guiana, 1st Aug., 1916 to Oct., 1918; ag. dist. engrnr., Oct., 1918 to Aug., 1920; 1st cls. overseer, July, 1919; clk. of works, Gambia, 20th Aug., 1920.

GELDENHUYZ, Frans Eduard.—B. 1889; B.A., 1910, Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, S.A.; studied in U.S.A. at Univs. of Cornell, Wisconsin, Illinois and California; B.Sc., Cornell Univ., 1913 and Ph.D., 1917; teacher of nature study and agrl. science, Grey Coll. Schl., 1918; edr., "Die Landbou-weekblad," Bloemfontein, S.A., 1919; first ch., divn. of agrl. economies and marketing, dept. of agr., S.A., 1925; under sec. for agr., 1926.

GENEVE, Frederic Pierre.—B. 1888; ed. Royal Coll., Mauritius; called to bar, Middle Temple, Nov., 1909; ag. dist. and stip. mag. for varying periods; dist. and stip. mag., Mauritius and Rodrigues, 1st July, 1921; addnl. substitute procureur and advoc. gen., 1923; substitute procureur and advoc. gen., 1923; ag. ditto, Aug., 1924 to Feb., 1925 and Apr., 1926 to Jan., 1927; atty.-gen., Br. Honduras, 1st Aug., 1927.

GENT, G. E. J., D.S.O., M.C.—B. 1895; ed. King's Schl., Canterbury (King's Scholar, 1909-14 and Trinity Coll., Oxford (Ford Studentship, Dec., 1913); 2nd lieut., 3rd batt. D.C.L.I., Aug., 1914; capt., 1916; ag. major, Nov., 1917; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, ast. prin., C.O., 19th July, 1920; sec., advisory rubber comnc., 1922; act. prin., 19th Nov., 1923; prte. sec. to Mr. Ormaby Gore, Dec., 1924; prin., 1927; dep. sec., C.O. confco., 1927.

GENT, John Roland Phillip.—B. 1887; ed. Durham Schol. (Kings Schol.) and Worcester COLL., Oxford (exhibr.); diploma of forestry, 1911; Indian forest service, 1911-24; dep. conservator, forests, Gold Coast, 1924.

GENTLE, GEORGE EDWIN PALMER.—B. 1880; ed. Denstone Coll.; clk., gov.'s office, Grenada, 1898; warden, P.W.D., St. Vincent, 1901; road survr., Grenada, 1906; ag. supt., pub. wks., 1914, 1916, 1918, 1921, 1922; asst. supt., pub. wks., 1916; supt., pub. wks., 1922; unoff. mem., leg. counc., 1922.

GENTLE, WILLIAM TODD ATKINSON.—B. 1879; ed. George Watsons Coll., George Heriot's Schl. and Univ., Edinburgh; asst. audr. for rly., Ceylon, 1910; ag. col. audr. in addn., July and Aug., 1919; ag. ass't gen. man. and acct. rly., May, 1920 to May, 1921; asst. col. audr., 1921; ag. col. audr., Feb. to Dec., 1923; ch. acct., rly. dept., Feb., 1925.

GERAHY, CHARLES CYRIL.—B. 1888; ed. Trent Coll.; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1909; 2nd lieut., 3rd (Rev.) Batt. E. Lancs. Regt., 1910; lieut., 1911; asst. res. and oomsnr., prov. etc., N. Nigeria, 1911-14; capt., E. Lancs. Regt., 12th Nov., 1914; active serv. in France and Belgium, 1914-15; mil. (legal) rep., appeal tribunal, Feb., 1916 to Jan., 1917; travelling recruiting offr., Nigeria Regt., W.A.F.F., Jan., 1917 to Jan., 1918; capt. and adjt., 3rd Nigeria Regt., 1918; restored to civil estabm't., N. Prov., Nigeria, 11th Nov., 1918; legal asst., lands directorate, W.O., Sept., 1919 to Sept., 1920; pres., dist. ct., Laraco, Cyprus, 14th Nov., 1920; passed exam. in mod. Greek, Mar., 1922; pres., dist. ct., Limassol, Sept., 1922; ag. atty.-gen., June to Nov., 1925 and Sept. to Dec., 1926; ag. puisne judge, June to Sept., 1926; atty.-gen., 8th Dec., 1926.

GERMAN, RALPH LIONEL.—B. 1892; ed. Portsmouth Gram. Schl. and Emmanuel Coll., Camb. (class. sch.); B.A. 1914; served with Hampshire Regt., 1915-19; Tank Corps, 1919-20; cadet, F.M.S., Feb., 1921; ag. habmr., Perak, Nov., 1921; 2nd lieut., M.V.I., Perak, Feb., 1922; ag. 2nd asst. dist. offr., Larut, Oct., 1922; passed cadet, Jan., 1923; offr., cl. V., Oct., 1923; ag. asst. dist. offr., Larut, May, 1923; asst. dist. offr., Ipoh, Mar., 1924; offr., cl. IV., Oct., 1924; asst. dist. offr., Ipoh, Oct., 1924; ag. collr., land rev., Kuala Lumpur, Feb., 1926; dist. offr., Pekan, June, 1926.

GETHIN, PERCY EDWARD LOVELL, A.F.C., F.R.G.S.—B. 1884; ed. Radley Coll.; R. Geographical Soc'y. dipl. in surveying, 1908; explor. in S. Arabia, 1909; asst. supt., surveys, Ceylon, 1910; in charge, topographical surveys in the field, 1912; on mily. serv., 1914-20; major, R.F.C., 1916; asst. dir., surveys, Cyprus, 1922; passed exam. in mod. Greek, 1923; ag. reg. gen., 1923; dir. surveys, Tanganyika Territory, 1928.

GETLEY, ALFRED CURPHEIT.—B. 1897; ed. Roborough Schl., Eastbourne; acctnt. branch, Royal Navy, 1915-22; paymr.-lieut., Jan., 1920; ret. at own request, 1922; seconded as pilot to R.N.A.S. and R.A.F., 1917-20; ent. col. audit. dept., Apr., 1923; asst. audr., Uganda, June, 1923; ag. senr. asst. audr. on several occasions.
GIBB, JOHN.—B. 1881; supervisor of warehouses, govt. stores, Ceylon, Oct., 1914; col. storekeeper, Sept., 1916.
GIBBON, JOHN FRANK, M.B., C.M. (Edin.)—B. 1869; entd. med. serv., Trinidad, 11th Jan., 1899; dist. med. offr., 1st Jan., 1916.

GIBBONS, ROBERT KILLINGLY.—B. 1880; 2nd cls. supervisor of cust., Gold Coast, 6th Apr., 1910; asst. contrlr., enemy property, Dec., 1914; contrlr., enemy property, G. Coast, 13th Feb., 1920 and for Ashanti, 25th Mar., 1920; sec., bd. of Basel Mission trustees, Mar., 1920; apptd. to constitute local clearing office for purpose of treaty of peace order, 1919, 27th Mar., 1920; transf'd. from cust. to transport dept. as asst. transport offr., 30th Mar., 1918; retransf. to cust. dept. as 1st cls. supervisor, June, 1919; ag. dep. contrlr. of cust. on various occasions, 1920-22; ag. contrlr. of cust. on various occasions, 1920-22; offl. mem., Accra town coun. on various occasions, 1922-26.
GIBBS, GEORGE F. W.—Head clk. and storekeeper, Uganda Prot., 12th Mar., 1909; 2nd asst. transport offr., 1st Apr., 1913.
GIBBS, GEORGE HOWARD.—B. 1889; asst. dist. oomsnr., Gold Coast, 25th Aug., 1920.
GIBSON, CLARENCE HYNE.—B. 1876; ag. dist. offr., Gilbert and Elllice Is., Jan., 1914; confirmed in apptmt., Jan., 1916; a dep. oomsnr. for the W. Pacific, with powers in Gilbert group, June, 1915.
GIBSON, HARRISON WYATT.—Insp'r. of pub. wks., Basutoland, 1904; dir. of pub. wks., 1910.

GIBSON, SIR HENRY JAMES, K.C.B. (1912), C.B. (1902), B.A.—B. 1860; ed. Rossall and St. John's Coll., Oxford; Cashier scholar; 1st cls. class. mods.; clk. War Office, 1885; princ. clk., 1897; asst. acctnt.-gen., 1900; dep. acctnt.-gen., 1903; asst. dir. of Army finance, 1904; priv. sec. to Mr. Woodall, 1894-96, to Hon. St. John Brodrick, 1896-6; asst. comptr. and audr., 1906; mem. of comtee. to inquire into organisation of crown agents' office, 1908; hon. asst. audr. for certain Crown Colonies and Prots., ag. on behalf of the S. of S. for the Colonies; comptr. and audr.-gen., 1911.

GIBSON, COLONEL SIR JOHN MORISON, K.C.M.G. (1912), K.C., M.A., LL.D.—B. 1842; called to the bar, 1867; LL.B., Toronto Univ., 1869; mem. of senate of Toronto Univ., 1873; re-elected, 1878 and 1883; mem. of Ontario legislature, 1879; re-elected, 1883 and 1886; provi. sec., 1889; oomsnr. of crown lands, 1896; has also held office as atty.-gen.; lieut.-gov. of Ontario, 1908-1914.

GIBSON, LENNIE BERTRAM.—B. 1896; ed. King Henry VIII. Schl., Coventry; hon. LL.B. (War), London; flight sub-lieut., R.N.A.S., attnd. Grand Fleet; lieut., R.A.F.; cadet, S. Stiltns., Mar., 1920; attnd. to atty.-gen.'s office, Singapore, Apr., 1920; attnd. to food control office, Penang, May, 1920; dep. dir. of food prodn., Penang, Apr., 1921; ag. collr., income tax, Malacca, Dec., 1921; passed cadet, Mar., 1922; dep. registr. and asst. offr. assignee, Penang, June, 1922; asst. sec., rec., Selangor, July, 1925.

GIBSON, ROY ALEXANDER.—B. 1888; ed. Brandon High Schl. and Brandon Coll.; ent. fed. govt. serv. at Regina, 1908; transf'd. to Ottawa, and apptd. ch. clk. to dep. min., 1908; asst. dep. min., 1st June, 1919; oomsnr., Yukon and N.W. Territories, 1923.
GIBSON, THOMAS WILLIAM.—B. 1859; ed. pub. schl., Wroxeter Academy, Rockwood, Ontario; sec. bureau of mines, Ontario, 1891; dir., ditto, 1900; dep. min. of mines, 1906.
GIBSON, HON. WILLIAM GERRAND.—Mem. of the H. of R., Commonwealth of Australia for Corangamite, Dec., 1919; postmr.-gen., C. of A., 1923.

GIBSON, WILLIAM SUMNER, B.A., Oxon., Barrister-at-Law (Lincoln's Inn).—B. 1878; ed. Marlborough and Keble Coll., Oxford (schol.), B.A. (1st cls. mod. hist.), 1899; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1899; passed final exam., Cantonese, Mar., 1902; Malay, Jan., 1908; ag. Indian immigr. agt., Perak, Oct., 1902; ag. mag., Taiping, July, 1902; ag. 2nd asst. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, and dist. offr., Xmas Is. (alternately), May, 1904 to Oct., 1906; services lent to Johore govt., July-Nov., 1908; asst. man., monopolies dept., Penang, Oct., 1909; ag. mag., Ipoh, Jan., 1911; called to the bar, June, 1913; ag. prot. of Chinese, Perak, Nov., 1913; ag. dist. judge, Singapore, Feb., 1915; sg. regis., sup. ct., Penang, May, 1915; legal adviser, Kedah, Jan., 1916; offr., cls. III, Mar., 1916; judge of high ct., Kedah (in addn.), July, 1916; offr., cls. II, Jan., 1919; ag. legal adviser, F.M.S., Dec., 1920; pres., coman. to enquire into organisation and expend. of govt. depts., F.M.S., Feb., 1922; legal adviser, F.M.S., December, 1922.

GILBERT, JOSEPH TROUNSELL, B.A. (Oxon), M.B.E. (1927).—B. 1888; ed. Bedford and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1914; asst. collr. (now asst. dist. coman.), Zanzibar, 4th Jan., 1912; ag. mag., 15th Mar. to 21st June, 1915; 2nd asst. sec., 26th Mar., 1915; priv. sec. to res., June, 1915 to Aug., 1921; ag. first asst. sec., 21st June, 1915; ag. asst. chief sec., in 1915, 1918, 1919, 1924 and 1926; offr. examr. in the Swahili language, 1919; ag. ch. sec., 7th Sept. to 17th Oct., 1924 and from 10th Aug. to 30th Sept., 1926; Order of the Brilliant Star of Zanzibar, cls. IV., 1919; senr. asst. sec., 7th Sept., 1925.

GILCHRIST, WILLIAM JAMES.—B. 1879; ed. at Brunswick House private schl., B. Guiana; entd. H.M. Customs, B.
