Sugar Bounties Conf., 1889; priv. sec. to Mr. Buxton, M.P., Jan., 1894, to June, 1896; and to the Earl of Selborne, June, 1896; 1st class clk., 1896; specially engaged in work connected with Venezuelan bndry. arbitn., 1896-8; priv. clk., Nov., 1898; sent to Paris, 1899, in connection with the Venez. bndry. arbitn.; conducted British case in Brazilian-B. Guiana bndry. arbitn., 1901-4; mem. of comtes. to inquire into organization of Crown agents' office, 1908; ch. clk., 1st Feb., 1909; represented C.O. on advisory comtes. of comm. intell., B. of T., 1900-17; mem. of managing comtes. of Imp. Inst. 1909-16; gov. and d. in e., Newfoundland, 1st Nov., 1917; assumed gort., 17th Dec., 1917; ret., 1922; author of numerous articles in Dict. of Pol. Econ. on economic and statistical subjects, and of lives of various colonial govs. and statesmen in Dict. of Nat. Biog.

HARRIS, DAVID JOHN EDWARD.—B. 1897; apptd. after compet. exam., boy clk., G.P.O., 1912; on mily. serv. (R.E. and R.A.F.), 8th Oct., 1915 to 27th Feb., 1919; tempy. employment clk., Miny. of Labour, 1st Oct., 1919 to 20th June, 1920; permanent ditto, 21st June, 1920 to 30th Sept., 1922; cler. offr., C.O., 1st Oct., 1922.

HARRIS, HERBERT JOHN.—B. 1884; entd. serv. of Imperial Post Office, 1st Jan., 1900; transfed. to dept. of po-ts and telegraphs, Cape of Good Hope, 13th Nov., 1902; resig., Dec., 1907, and entd. S. Rhodesia telegraph serv., 1st Jan., 1908; seconded for serv. with African Trans-Continental Telegraph Co., 12th Apr., 1908; resig., 31st Jan., 1910; provincial postmaster, S. Nigeria, 1st Feb., 1910; asst. supt. of po-ts and telegraphs, Fed.Malay States, 14th June, 1913; seconded for mil. serv., Mar., 1918; 2nd lieut., Indian army res. of offr., unattached, Mar., 1918; 2nd lieut., 91st Punjabis, July, 1918; lieut., Mar., 1919; released from army serv., Oct., 1919; resumed duty, Kuala Lumpur, July, 1920; relinquished comsn., I.A.R.O., Sept., 1920; contr., poets and tels., F.M.S., Oct., 1926.
HARRIS, PHILIP CAESAR, A.R.I.B.A.—B. 1891; ed. Parkfield Schl., Liverpool, Liverpool Univ. and the Royal Acad. Schls. of Art; on war serv., 1914-19; architect, Zanzibar, Jan., 1st:22.

HARRIS, REGINALD ROBERT.—B. 1890; ed. Queen Mary's Schl., Basingstoke; clk., govt. sec.'s office, Nyasaland Prot., 18th June, 1913; editor, govt. gaz., since 1914; clk., 1st grade, Oct., 1920; ag. ch. clk., secretariat, Nov. 1920 to June, 1921; asst. treas., Apr., 1925.

HARRIS, HON. ROBERT EDWARD, D.C.L., LL.D.—B. 1830; ed. High School, Annapolis Royal; pri. tuition; D.C.L., King's Coll. Univ., Windsor, N.S.; LL.D., Dalhousie Univ., Halifax, N.S.; called to the N.S. bar, 1882; K.C., 1889; practised at Yarmouth, 1882-92; Halifax, 1892-1915; justice, sup. ct., N.S., 1915; ch. just., sup. ct., N.S., 7th Feb., 1918; selected as third arbitr. to determine value of Canadian Northern Rly. stock, on its acquisition by the Dom. Govt.; mem. of the coun. of the N.S. Barrister's Soc. and for some years pres.; chancellor, diocese of N.S., 1905; dir., schl. for blind, Halifax; gov. of King's Coll., Windsor, N.S.

HARRISON, ALBERT SEDDON, M.Sc. Manchester, 1908.—B. 1887; on staff of Manchester Univ., and asst. mast., Fielden demonstrn. schl., 1908-10; senr. mast., Halesowen comml. schl., 1910-12; on staff Manchester Univ. educn. dept., and headmst., Fielden demonstrn. schl., 1912-14; inspr. of schls., Ceylon, Apr., 1914; on active serv., 1918-19; ag. asst. dir. of educn., Ceylon, May, 1919, to Apr., 1st:20; ag. dir. of educn., Apr. to Oct., 1920; ag. asst. dir. of educn., Oct., 1920; prin., training coll., Jan., 1921.

HARRISON, CAPT. HORACE D'URFORD.—B. 1884; war serv., with D.L.I., 1914-19; France, 1915-18; twice ment. in desps.; headmst.-ct., govt. primary schls., Gold Coast, 5th June, 1920; mast., training coll., 1st Jan., 1921; inspr., schls., 1st Apr., 1926.

HARRISON, ERNEST, M.S.A. (Ivwa), B.Sc. (Agrio.), N.D.A. (Hons.).—Lecturer in agr., schl. of agr., Grootfontein, Cape, 1904; prin., schl. of agr., Cedara, Natal, 1913; dep. dir., agr., Kenya, 1921; ag. dir., agr., Feb. to Nov., 1922 and Mar.-Oct., 1924.

HARRISON-JONES, CHARLES.—B. 1884; ed. Reading and St. John's Coll., Oxford (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1907; pol. mag., Puttalam, Ja., 1909; asst. govt. agt., Colombo and Negombo dists., July, 1910; ag. ast. govt. agt., Hambantota, Apr., 1912; pol. mag., Matara, May, 1912; ag. ast. govt. agt., Mannar, Feb., 1913; on military duty, Jan., 1915; dist. judge Tangalla, Dec., 1919; ag. ast. govt. agt., Matale, June, 1920; ditto, N'Eliya, Nov., 1924; ag. govt. agt., Eastern prov., May, 1927.

HART, H. W.—B. 1897; on mily. service in France and Flanders, Mar., 1915 to Jan., 1919; service in corps of mily. accnts., 1919-21; temp. clk., C.O., 1st Mar., 1921; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 9th July 1926.

HART, RUPERT LANCELOT LEON, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1885; ed. Reading Schl. and R. Veterinary Coll., London; govt. vety. surg., N. Rhodesia, 1910-14; vety. offr., Uganda, 1914; served with E. African Forces in Great War, 1914-17 and on Western Front, 1917-19; senr. vety. offr., Uganda, 1920.

HART, WILLIAM HUME, B.A., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Dub.).—B. 1885; ed. Sedbergh, Yorks and Dub. Univ. (Trin. Coll.); med. offr., grade III, F.M.S., May, 1912; grade II, Nov., 1913; seconded for mil. serv., Dec., 1915; temp. 2nd lieut., R.A.M.C., Feb., 1916; capt., Feb., 1917; discharged, Sept., 1919; ag. med. supdt., cent. mental hosp., Tanjong Rambutan, June, 1920; ag. senr. med. offr., Perak, May, 1922; ditto, Negri Sembilan, Apr., 1923; ditto, Perak, Nov., 1925; ditto, Pahang, Dec., 1925; ditto, Selangor, Apr., 1926; senr. med. offr., Pahang, but continues ag. senr. med. offr., Selangor, Aug., 1926.

HART-DAVIS, CHARLES HENRY.—B. 1874; ed. at Eton and New Coll., Oxford, B.A.; served in 13th I.Y. in South Africa, 1900 (Queen's medal, 3 clasps); priv. sec. to Sir W. Haynes Smith, Cyprus, 1901; ast. insp. of schools, Cyprus, 1901 to 1906; passed exams. in Turkish and Greek, 1903; insp. of schools, Gold Coast, 1906; dis. comsnr., 1906 to 1910; sec. to high comsnr. for the Western Pacific, 1910; on mily. serv., 1915-19; ch. ast. sec., Cyprus, Apr., 1919; ag. ch. inspr. of schls., Aug. to Oct., 1919; ag. ch. sec., Sept. to Dec., 1919; ag. regis.gen., Dec. to May, 1920; ag. comsnr., July to Oct., 1920; ag. ch. sec., 27th Oct., 1920 to 3rd Feb., 1921, 17th to 23rd Mar., 11th May to 3rd Oct., 1921 and 14th July to 13th Oct., 1923; comsnr., 1st July, 1922; mem., leg. coun., Jan., 1924.

HARTER, JUAN DENIS EDGARD.—B. 1879; ed. R. Coll. of Mauritius; ast. mast., R. Coll., 1901; ast. mast., Victoria Schl., Seychelles, Aug., 1902; ast. mast., King's Coll., 1910; headmast., Victoria Schl., July, 1920; ag. dir. educn. and inspr. of schls., Seychelles, Jan., 1922; inspr. of schls., Seychelles, Jan., 1924; sec. to bd. of educn.; local sec., Cambridge local exam.; ag. pvt. sec., clk. to gov. and clk. to couns., from 16th Dec., 1924.
HARTNELL, G. F. W.—Chief mechanical engnr., Uganda rlyw., June, 1918, and from Apr., June, 1922; comsnr., July, 1922; ag. regis.gen., July-Sep., 1922.
HARWELLI, CHARLES HERBERT, B.A. (Can-tab).—B. 1904; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1926; attld., Kandy kach., Jan., 1927.
HARVEY, DAVID HAROLD.—B. 1874; entd. Natal civ. serv., Apr., 1898; transf. to Swaziland
HAWKINS, BRIAN CHARLES KEITH.—B. 1900; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Hertford Coll., Oxford; B.A. (Oxon.), hona., mod. hist., 1923; cadet, Hong Kong, Dec., 1924; ast. assessor, 1927.

HAWKINS, GERALD.—B. 1891; B.A., London Univ.; pte., 15th Gloucester Regt., 8th Aug., 1914; 2nd lieut., 1/5th Gloucester Regt., 7th Feb., 1915; B.E.F., 29th Mar., 1915; ment. in desp., 20th Dec., 1917; wounded, and discharged with rank of capt., 5th May, 1920; cadet, F.M.S., 20th Nov., 1920; ast. to land office, Penang, 21st Dec., 1920; dep. collr. of land rev., Penang, 14th Jan., 1921; ast. dist. offr., Balik Pulau, 21st Dec., 1921; offr., cls. V, May, 1919; cls. IV, May, 1923; ast. ast. sec., res., Selangor, Dec., 1926; sec., res., Pahang, Apr., 1926.
HAY, CLIFFORD H., C.M.G., M.V.O.—Under sec., premier's dept., New S. Wales since Mar., 1916.

HAY, MOETTNER CECIL.—B. 1891; ed. The Schl., Bishop Stortford and St. Catharine's Coll., Cambridge (exhbr.); B.A., Cantab.; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1913; ast. ast. contrir. of lab., Penang, Mar., 1915; ast. ast. dist. offr., Kuala Lipis, Mar., 1916; passed ast. July, 1916; offr., cls. V, Dec., 1916; dep. contrir. of lab., Klang, Dec., 1920; ast. ast. adviser, Endau, Johore, May, 1921; ast. ast. adviser, Batu Pahat, Mar., 1922; ast. prot. of Chinese and contrir. of lab., Johore, Oct., 1922; ast. ast. adviser, Batu Pahat, Sept., 1923; dep. contrir., lab., Kuala Lumpur, Sept., 1925; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1925; ast. ast. adviser, Kemaman, Tengganan, June, 1926.

HAYCRAFT, SIR THOS. WAGSTAFFE KT. BARC. (1919).—B.A., St. John's Coll., Oxon.; called to the bar, In. Tem., 1886; practised in S.E. circuit and Sussex sessions; examin. of the ct., 1889 to 1899; apptd. arbitrator on bd. of Lond. chain of arbitn., 1897; pres. dist. ct., Larnaca, Cyprus, 1899; pol. mag., Gibraltar, 1911; puisne judge, Mauritius, 1913; ch. just., Grenada, 1916; ch. just., Palestine, 1921; ret., Mar., 1927; author of "Executive Powers in relation to Crime and Disorder."

HAYDEN, JAMES J.—B. 1883; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Dublin; clasped in feudal and English law, Dublin Univ. (duel gold medallist); admitted solr., sup. ct. of judicature, Ireland, 1906; called to Irish bar, 1926; registr., high ct., Uganda, 21st Jan., 1921; also addnl. dist. mag., 1st cls. admin., rev. authority, regir. of companies, patents, designs, trade-marks, documents and business names, custodian of enemy property and contr. of local clearing house; mag., Uganda, Mar., 1927.

HAYDOCK-WILSON, HUGH.—B. 1897; ed. Bedford; on war serv., Artists Rifles (London R.) and Manchester Regt., 1915-19; ent. civ. serv., Br. Guiana as 4th cls. clk., C.S.O., 25th Apr., 1921; 3rd cls. clk., 16th July, 1924; capt., B.G. Militia (Art.), 1922-25.

HAYES, ROBERT EDWARD, I.S.O. (1926).—B. 1869; ed. New Zealand Univ. entd. N.Z. postal dept., 1885; acct., tourists and industries and commerce dept., 1903; registr., friendly societies, 1906; also supt., national provident fund, 1910; ast. ast. and acct. to treas., 1923; sec. treas., paym.-gen. and registr. of inscribed stock, 1925; again supt., national prov. fund, 1926.

HAYES-SADLER, CECIL WILLIAM, M.A. (Oxon.)—B. 1892; ed. Eton and Trinity Coll., Oxford; served in France with R. Engnr. (Signals), 1914-18; French Croix de Guerre, 1917; ast. dist. commr., Kenya Col., 1920; ast. senr. ast. sec., 1926.

HAYNES, ALWIN SIDNEY.—B. 1878; ed. at Haileybury Coll.; cadet, F.M.S., civ. ser., Nov., 1901; attached to dist. office, Batang Padang, Jan., 1902; ag. finan. asst., Krian, Oct., 1902, to Apr., 1903; passed final exam. in law, Oct., 1902; mag., Perak, 1902; passed final exam. in Tamil, Aug., 1903; ag. finan. asst., Krian, Sept., 1903; passed final exam. in Malay, Nov., 1906; ag. asst. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, Mar., 1906; ag. impr. schls., Negri Sembilan, Nov., 1906; ist cls. mag., Negri Sembilan, 1906; ag. asst. supt., Indian immigrts., Penang, Nov., 1906; asst. supt., ditto, Nov., 1908; examr., Tamil, Perak and Penang; ag. supt., Indian immigrts., F.M.S. and S. Stilts, Feb., 1910; ag. asst. supt., immigrts., Klang, Jan., 1911; passed final exam. in Siamese, Aug., 1912; ag. dist. offr., Pekan, Sept., 1913; ag. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, Dec., 1914; mem., bd. of examnrs. in Malay for Perak, Apr., 1915; dist. offr., Tampin, 1916; sec., high commr., F.M.S., Apr., 1920; sec. for agr., S.S. and F.M.S., and dir. food production ditto, Feb., 1922; chmn., small holdings comte., (rubber restrictions), Nov., 1923; ag. Br. res., Pahang, Feb., 1924; ag. Br. res., Perak, Nov., 1925; ag. Br. adviser, Kedah, Dec., 1925; ag. Br. res., Pahang, Sept., 1926; resumed as sec. for agr., S.S. and F.M.S., May, 1927.

HAZELTON, ERIC.—B. 1895; 2nd lieut., Monmouthshire Brig., R.F.A., June, 1915; joined B.E.F., France, Nov., 1915; lieut., R.H. and R.F.A., June, 1916; served with E.E.F., Suez Canal, Sinai, Palestine, 1916-19; cadet, F.M.S., Johore, Dec., 1919; 2nd asst. adviser to gov't. of Johore, Jan., 1921; temp. ag. asst. adviser, Endau, Feb., 1921; ag. 2nd asst. adviser, Batu Pahat, May, 1921; passed cadet, Nov., 1921; ag. 4th asst. sec. to gov't., F.M.S., Aug., 1922; 4th asst. sec. to gov't., F.M.S., Nov., 1922; ag. pvt. sec. to ch. sec., Nov., 1923; off. sec., res., Negri Sembilan, Nov., 1924; ag. asst. prin., C.O., Oct., 1926; offr. cls. IV, Nov., 1926.

HAZEN, HON. SIR JOHN DOUGLAS, K.C.M.G. (1918), K.C., LL.D.—B. 1860; ed. at Collegiate Schl., Fredericton, and Univ. of New Brunswick; called to the bar, 1883; K.C., 1894; elec. to H. of C., Canada, for St. John City and County, 1891; defeated, 1896; elec. to New Brunswick legis. for Sunbury, 1899, 1903, 1908; leader of the opposition in the New Brunswick legis. during that period; prem. and atty.-gen. of New Brunswick, 1908-1911; has been pres. of St. John law soc. and barristers soc. of New Brunswick; mem. of the P.C. for Canada, and min. of marine and fisheries in Mr. Borden's cabinet, Oct., 1911; chief jut. of New Brunswick, Nov., 1917.

HAZLERIGG, THOMAS MAYNARD, M.C.—Admitted solr., sup. ct., England, 1906; lieut., R.A.S.C., 1914-1915; capt., R.A.S.C., 1915 to 1920; asst. crown solr., Hong Kong, 1920; ag. asst. land offr. in addn., 1921 to 1922 and from 1922 to 1923; ag. crown solr., Mar. to Nov., 1924; treas'y. solr. in addn., 1926.
HEAD, W. G.—B. 1899; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., C.O., Oct., 1916; on mil. serv. from July, 1917, to Oct., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; higher cler. offr., 19th May, 1925.

HEAD-BYANS, ELLIS JAMES, M.I.M. & C.E., A.M.S.B.—Asst. engr., P.W.D., Nyassaland, Jan., 1912 to Aug., 1916; ag. asst. D.P.W., Nyassaland, Jan. to Sept., 1913; served with Nyassaland Vol. Res., 1914-15 (A.G.S. Med.); attd. as civil offr. to Nyassaland Field Force as ch. engr., road constrn. in operns. in German E. Africa, Mar., 1916 to Sept., 1917; seconded as asst. engr., P.W.D., Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1916; exec. engr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1920.
HEAPE, WILLIAM LESLIE.—B. 1896; ed. Rugby and Sandhurst; asst. sec., Somaliland, Nov., 1919.

HEARST, SIR WILLIAM HOWARD, K.C.M.G. (1917).—B. 1864; ed. pub. schls., Collingwood Coll. Inst. and Toronto Univ.; called to the bar, 1888; K.C., 1908; prominent legal practitioner at Sault Ste. Marie, Ontario; now head of firm Hearst, Rowland and Atkin; has been counsel in all more important civil and criminal cases in Algoma district in recent years; unsuccessful cand. for legis. assembly, Ontario, 1894; elec. at g.e. 1908, 1911, and 1914; min. of lands, forests and mines in Whitney administration, 1911; premier of Ontario, 1914; also min. of agric.; defeated at g.e., Oct., 1919; resig. with his govt., Nov., 1919; mem., internat. joint coman. (Can. section), 23rd Feb., 1920.

HEATH, A. P.—B. 1897; ed. Uppingham; on mil. serv., Aug., 1915; 2nd lieut., Feb., 1917 to Aug., 1917; temp. olk., Min. of Nat. Serv., Nov., 1917; transf'd. O.S.O., Mar., 1919; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., O.S.O., Nov., 1921.

HEATH, HUBERT HAROLD.—B. 1865; ent. pub. serv., Barbados, 1883; junr. clk., P.O., 1885; 1st clk., 1902; acctnt., 1908; ag. col. postmr. on several occasions, 1907-18; ch. clk., treasy., 1918; ag. col. treas., Mar. to July, 1919; ch. offr., cust., 1919; ag. comptr., cust., on several occasions, 1919-22; man., govt. savings bank, 1922; col. postmr., 1926.

HEATH, W. G.—M.B., Ch.B., Edin. Univ.; qualified 1897; med. offr., Windward dist., Dominica, 1898; med. offr., dist. 2, Montserrat, 1901; ag. med. offr., dist. "D," Dominica, 1903; distt., dist. "A," 1904; ag. sen. med. offr., Montserrat, 1905, 1912, and since 1916; pres., defence reserve, 1909; ag. mem. legis. coun., 1911; ag. med. offr., Antigua, 1911; mem. legis. coun., May, 1915; med. offr.-in-chge. of Montserrat recruits for the B.W.I. regt., Mar., 1916; surg.-lieut., defence force, 1916; provisionally mem. exec. coun., 1916; ag. commnr., Montserrat, on several occasions since Feb., 1918.

HEDDON, JOHN DONALD, B.A. (Oxon).—B. 1879; clk., cust., Port Elizabeth, 1903; prin. clk., East London, 1915; survr., Durban, 1916; ch. clk., head office, Pretoria, 1918; dep. commr., cust., and excise, 1926; del. for Union govt. at Imp. Cust. Confce., 1921; cust. adviser to deleg. to Australian govt. (flour shipments), 1921; mem., deleg. to Kenya to rept. on trade prospects, 1922; rep. of Union govt. at economic conference, Antananarivo, Madagascar, 1923; advisory mem., Imp. Confce. 1926.
HEENAN, PETER.—Elec. to Ontario legis., 1919-25; House of Commons, Canada, 1925; min. of lab., 1926.

HEIDENSTAM, OSCAR CHARLES.—B. 1877; ed. at King's Schl., Warwick; vol. clk., col. sec.'s off., Cyprus, 5th Feb., 1897; 3rd div. clk., ditto, 16th Jan., 1899; inspr. of pol., 12th Jan., 1904; sent to Egypt for training with H.M. 's troops, May, June, and July, 1904; in command of depot, 8th Aug., 1904; in command of Kyrena div., gov. of prison and dep. coroner, 15th July, 1905; in command of Papho div., gov. of prison and dep. coroner, 4th Aug., 1906; acted as commr. for Papho on several occasions; passed in mod. Greek, lower standard, Apr., 1906; ag. priv. sec. and A.D.C. to high commnr., Sir. C. A. King-Harman, and clk. of exec. coun., 4th Jan. to 15th Feb. 1907; temporarily attached to ch. sec.'s off., 16th Feb., 1907; in comd. of pol., Kyrenia div., gov. of pris., and dep. coroner, 24th June, 1907; asst. dist. supt. of pol., Uganda Prot., 12th July, 1907; in commd. of Entebbe pol., and supt. of cent. gaol, 10th Sept., 1907; inspr. of police, Cyprus, Apr., 1910; ch. of pol., Grenada, 22nd July, 1914; inspr.-gen., pol., Barbados, 1926.

HELLINGS, GEOFFREY STEWART.—B. 1888; ed. Christ's Hosp. (foundation school), and St. John's Coll., Camb.; Stewart of Rannoch Univ. schol., Camb., 1909; B.A., 1910 (cl. trip. ii. i); cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1911; seconded for mil. ser., Jan., 1915; 2nd lieut., 8th (Service) Batt. Duke of Cornwall's Light Infy., Mar., 1915; 2nd lieut., 2nd Batt. D.C.L.I., May, 1916; lieut., 2nd Batt. D.C.L.I., Sept., 1916; ag. capt., 2nd Batt. D.C.L.I., June, 1918; demob., Sept., 1919; ag. 2nd asst. dist. offr., Batu Gajah, Nov., 1919; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese, Perak, Dec., 1920; ag. asst. prot. Chinese, Selangor, Sept., 1921; contr., lab. and prot. of Chinese, Johore, Sept., 1923; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1923.

HELPS, EDMUND ARTHUR PLUNKETT.—B. 1888; ed. Marlborough and Clare Coll., Cambridge; history tripso., 2nd cls. (1909); B.A. (1909); cadet, F.M.S., Jan., 1912; ag. 2nd asst. supt. of imigr., Klang, Sept., 1912; ag. asst. contr., lab., Aug., 1913; ag. asst. sec. to res. Selangor, May, 1914; passed cadet, July, 1914; offr., cls. V., Mar., 1916; lent for service under the admn. of German E. Africa, July, 1917; offr., cls. IV., Jan., 1919; ag. dist. offr., Raub, Apr., 1920; ag. asst. dist. offr., Kuala Lipis, May, 1920; ag. dist. offr., Larut, July, 1920; dist. offr., Kuala Selangor, May, 1921; addt. to sec., rubber restriction comte., Kulim, Nov., 1923; ag. asst. adviser, Kedah, Jan., 1924; offr., cls. III., ag. Br. adviser, Kedah, Nov., 1925; ag. adviser, Kelah, Dec., 1925.

HEMMANT, GEORGE.—B. 1880; ed. Tonbridge and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; cadet, F.M.S., 1903; ag. asst. dist. treas., Kuala Lumpur, June-Dec., 1904 and Mar.-June, 1905; passed cadet (Malay and Law), May, 1905; ag. rev. audir., Negri Sembilan, July, 1905; ag. asst. dist. offr., Kuala Pilah, Aug.-Nov., 1905; ag. dist. offr., Jelebu, Mar.-July, 1908 and Jan.-Feb., 1909; ag. asst. dist. offr., Kuala Pilah, July-Sept., 1908; ag. asst. dist. offr., Tampin, Oct.-Jan., 1909 and from Mar., 1909-Oct., 1910; asst. collr., land rev., Seremban, Dec., 1910; ag. dist. offr., Temerloh, Oct., 1911-Jan., 1912; seconded, Kedah, Dec., 1914; ag. legal adviser, Kedah, Dec., 1914-Jan., 1916; seconded, Johore, Jan., 1916; ag. asst. adviser, Muar, Jan., 1916-June, 1918; collr. of land rev., Kuala Lumpur and regir. of titles, Selangor, Nov., 1919-Nov., 1920; sec. to res., Selangor, Dec., 1920-Mar., 1922; mag., Kuala Lumpur, Jan.-Apr., 1923; ag. under-sec., S. Stiltns., May, 1923-Mar., 1924 and from Apr.-July, 1924; ag. col. sec., S. Stiltns., Mar. and Aug., 1924; gov.'s dep., Mar., 1924; ag. gen. adviser, Johore, Sept., 1924.

HEMSTEAD, RUPERT WILLIAM, O.B.E. (1919).—2nd treasy asst., E.A.P., 19th Sept., 1899; ch. acctnt., Somaliland, 1st Dec., 1901, to 1st Dec., 1903; asst. collr., E.A.P., 1st Dec., 1904; collr., 17th Dec., 1906; offr.-in-charge, Massai reserve, 1912; senr. comanr. (1st cls.), Jan., 1924.

HENDERSON, FREDERICK LOUIS.—Ed. at Camb. Univ.; B.A., 1897; M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P., (Lon.); med. offr., E. Africa and Uganda Prots., 18th Oct., 1904; senr. med. offr., Apr., 1920; dist. surg., May, 1923.

HENDRIE, CAPT. HARRY GEORGE, M.C.—Foreman of Works, P.W.D., Gold Coast, 26th June, 1921; head mast., jun. trade schl., educn. dept., 1st Dec., 1921; on mil. serv., 7th Sept., 1914; 2nd lieut., Sept., 1915; coy. comdr. and ag. capt., Sept., 1916; demob. with rank of capt., Feb., 1920; serv. in Northd. Cyclists' Batt., Northd. Fus. and Machine Gun Corps; M.C. and Bar.

HENRY, WILLIAM, M.A. (Edin.)—B. 1888; ed. Daniel Stewart's Coll., Edinburgh (dux and gold medallist), and Edinburgh Univ.; Egyptian educn. serv., 1911-15 and 1919-20; serv. Royal Highlanders and Royal Irish Regt., 1915-19; ment. in deep., 1919; dir. of educn., Zanzibar, 1920; M.L.C., 1926.

HENKEL, JOHN SPURGEON.—Joined forest dept., Eastern Conservancy, Cape, 1st July, 1888; asst. to conservator, 1st Mar., 1890; dist. forest offr., King William's Town, 1st June, 1893; Stutterheim, 1st Aug., 1898; selected to proceed to Royal Indian Engineering Coll., Cooper's Hill, for scientific course of forestry; obtained coll. diploma and specially commended by pres. of coll., 5th Aug., 1902; asst. conservator of forests, Eastern Conservancy, 1st Oct., 1905; asst. conservator, Western Conservancy, 1st July, 1907; apptd. principal lecturer in forestry, S. African schl. of forestry, 29th Oct., 1906; resign lecturership, 29th Feb., 1908; chmn. of bd. of management, S. African schl. of forestry, 18th May, 1907 to 31st Jan., 1909; asst. conservator of forests, Midland Conservancy, 1st Feb., 1909; conservator of forests, Midland Conservancy, 2nd grade, 1st Apr., 1912; Conservator of Forests, Natal Conservancy, Dec., 1914; served as comte. with dist. mounted troops during S. African war, 1901-2; Queen's medal; tranfd., Rhodesian forest serv., 1st Jan., 1920.
HENLEN, GEORGE WILLIAM.—B. 1902; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., C.O., Feb., 1920; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920.

HENNESSEY, PATRICK H.—M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.); house surg., S. Stiltns., Feb., 1907; supernumerary med. offr., Penang, Feb., 1908; med. offr. gen. hosp., Singapore, 1st May, 1908; ag. med. offr., Malacca, 14th Oct., 1908; med. offr., grade II, Kuala Pilah, and med. offr., rly. construction, 1st Apr., 1909; ag. med. offr., grade I, 6th May, 1914; ag. senr. med. offr., Selangor, 2nd to 26th Nov., 1918, in addition, and again 3rd Dec., 1918 to 9th Feb., 1919, and from 9th Apr. to 23rd Dec., 1919; ophthalmologist physician in ad. division; med. offr., gen. hosp., K. Lumpur, 1st Sept., 1920; med. offr. and ophthal. surg., Ipoh, 20th Sept., 1923.

HENNIKER-HEATON, HERBERT.—B.A. (Oxon.); ed. at Eton and New Coll., Oxford, (hons. hist., 1901); cadet, Fiji, 1902; attached to col. sec.'s off., to 1905; European offr., armed constab., June, 1903; in regir. gen.'s off., Mar.-May, 1904; ag. adjut., armed constab., mun. instr., and inspr. of pol., Feb., 1905, to Feb., 1906; stip. mag., Feb., 1906, and ag. stip. mag., Lau, and inspr. of taxes, Feb. to May, 1906; ch. clk., native dept., and ag. asst. native comanr., July, 1906; apptd. asst. native comanr., Mar., 1907; ag. chief pol. mag. and offr. recvr. in bankrupt, Mar., 1910, to Feb., 1911; mem. of mining bd., Apr., 1910; comanr. of customs, Jan., 1911; mem. bd. of examiners, Feb., 1911; nom. mem. Suva schl. bd., Oct., 1911; ag. chief pol. mag., Jan., 1912; mem. native regulation bd., May, 1912; asst. col. sec., Mauritius, Oct., 1913; ag. col. sec., Mar. to Sept., 1914; ag. prot. of immigts. and poor law comsnr., Apr., 1915, to Jan., 1916; ag. col. sec., Jan. to May and from July, 1916, to May, 1917; in comd., coy., Mauritius Vol. Force, 1916-17; col. sec., Gambia, 1917; ag. gov., June to Dec., 1918, Oct. to Dec., 1919 and July, 1920 to Jan., 1921; col. sec., Falkland Is., 1921; ag. gov., June, 1923 to May, 1924; col. sec., Bermuda, Oct., 1925.

HENRY, HON. GEORGE STEWART, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1871; ed. pub. schs., Upper Can. Coll., Univ. of Toronto, and Ont. Agric. Coll.; farmer; sl. to Ont. legis. at by-el., 1913; re-el., 1914, 1919 and 1923; min. of pub. wks. and highways in Ferguson admstrn., 1923.

HERBST, JOHN FREDERICK, C.B.E. (1919).—Ck., Cape census off., May, 1891; Carnarvon, Dec., 1891; Willowmore, May, 1895; atty.-gen.l's off., Cape Town, July, 1896; held several ag. appntns.; astt. mag., Gordonia, Apr., 1897; relvng. offr., Apr., 1899; astt. mag., Rietfontein, Nov., 1905; confirmed Jan., 1906; ag. civ. oomntr. and mag., Somerset, E., May, 1908; mag., St. Marks, July, 1908; detached for special service in B.B. Prot. in connection with inroads of Hottentots ex German S.W. Africa, under Simon Kooper; received thanks of the Cape govt. and Imp. Germ. govt. upon successful accomplishments of mission; inspecting mag., S.W. Africa Prot. (with rank of Major in Union Defence Forces), Sept., 1915; dep. secy., S.W. Africa Protectorate, Mar., 1916; secy. for Protectorate, Dec., 1916; acctg. offr. in addition, Apr., 1920; ag. admstr., S.W. Africa, 2nd to 17th Oct., 1920; sec. for S.W. Africa, civ. admstr., 1921; sec. for native affrs., Union of S. Africa, Apr., 1923.

HERCHENRODER, SIR FURCY ALFRED, KT. BACH. (1914), K.C.—B. 1865; ed. Roy. Coll., Mauritius; called to bar, Mid. Term, June, 1888; ag. crown pros., Mauritius, 1893-8; leg. adviser, pol. mag., crown pros., conservr. of mortgages, and curator of vacant estates, Seychelles, May, 1898; judge, Seychelles, 1900; ch. just., 1903; proc. and advoc. gen., Mauritius, 3rd Apr., 1906; ag. chief just., 3rd Sept., 1912; ch. just., 1st Jan., 1913.

HERDMAN, HON. ALEXANDER LAWRENCE.—B. 1869; ed. at Otago and Oamaru high schls.; barrister-at-law, 1894; entd. New Zealand parlt., 1902; atty.-gen., min. of justice, 1912; atty.-gen. in National ministry, 1915; resig., 1918, and apptd. judge of N.Z. sup. ct.

HEREFORD, GEORGE A., MA., (Oxon), barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn.—B. 1875; Cadet, S. Sttlmts., 1898; ag. dist. officer, Balik Pulau, Penang, Apr., 1899; passed final exam. in Malay, Oct., 1901; supt. of educn., Penang, Nov., 1902; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, Jan., 1904; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, Mar., 1906; astt. dist. offr., Batu Gajah, Jan., 1907; ag. mag., Kuala Lumpur, July, 1908; ag. astt. dist. offr., Larut, July, 1909; ag. astt. dist. offr., Kinta, Feb., 1910; ag. dep. pub. prosecutor, Kuala Lumpur, Apr., 1910; dist. offr., Raub., continued ag. dep. pub. prosecutor, May, 1910; offr., cls. III., Jan., 1912; passed final exam. for the bar, May, 1913; ag. regier., sup. ct., F.M.S., June, 1913; ag. censor, Penang, June, 1915; ag. senr. dist. offr., Prov. of Wellesley, June, 1915; ag. 2nd mag., Singapore, May, 1917; ag. dist. offr., Butterworth in addition, Nov., 1917; ag. pres., munie. comsners., Penang, May, 1919; offr. collr., land rev., Penang, May, 1919; offr., cls. II., Jan., 1919; legal adviser, Johore, offr., cls. I.B., Sept., 1920; ag. regier. of impts. and expts., Singapore, June, 1921; ag. gen. adviser, Johore, Feb., 1924.
HERMON-HODGE, H. B.—B. 1885; ed. Winchester coll. (scholar) and Magdalen coll., Oxford; B.A., hona. in mods. and hist.; astt. res. N. Nigeria, 26th Dec., 1908.

HEROLD, THOMAS BARRY.—Cape civ. ser., 1882-1901; ch. clk. to master of sup. ct., Transvaal, Oct., 1901; astt. master, June, 1902; comntr. of protocols, Dec., 1904; master of the sup. ct., Oct., 1906; mem. of prisons advisory bd., Feb., 1907; dir. (chmn., 1907-8 and 1908-9), land and agric. bank, Oct., 1907; chmn. and gen.-man., land and agric. bank of South Africa, 1912; J.P., Union of S. Africa, 1912; man. dir., land and agric. bank, Oct., 1924.

HERSHENSOHN, J. M. N. A.—lst cls., astt., govt. schls., Natal, 1899; clk., col. sec.'s dept., 1900; sworn translator, Dutch; senr. clk., 1906; civ. ser. examr., 1902; Dutch bd. of examr., 1903; one of the secretaries at S. Africa National Convention, 1908-1909; chief clk., admstr.'s dept., Natal Prov., 1910; ag. prov. sec., Aug.-Nov., 1913, and from Aug., 1917; prov. sec., 1st Feb., 1918; dep. admstr., Natal Prov., Jan.-Apr., 1926.

HERTZOG, HON. J. B. M., B.A., LL.D., M.L.A.—B. 1866; ed. at Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, and Amsterdam Univ.; judge of O.F.S., 1895 to 1900; comd. Boer forces of S.W. div., 1899 to 1902; one of the signatories of the treaty of Vereeniging; atty.-gen., O.R.C.; min. of just., Union of S. Africa, 1910-1912; leader of opposition in Union parl., 1913-24; prime min., 30th June, 1924.

HESLIP, MAJOR ISAAC.—B. 1876; 3rd batt., R. Irish Rifles; passed course of instrn., R.I.C., Dublin; Hythe certif.; astt. comntr., pol., Sierra Leone, 8th Feb., 1911; comntr., pol. and sherif., 8th July, 1914; dist. comntr., 22nd Sept., 1920.

HEWINS, WILLIAM ALBERT SAMUEL, M.P.—B. 1866; ed. at Wolverhampton Grammarsch., and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; M.A.; dir. of London schl. of economics, 1895-1903; teacher of modern economic history, Univ. of London, 1902-3; Took professor of economic science and statistics, King's Coll., 1897-1903; mem. of senate of Univ. of London, 1900-1903; late sec. of Tariff comm.; M.P. (U) for Hereford, 1912-1918; parly. under sec. of state for the colonies, 26th Sept., 1917, to Dee., 1918; author of numerous works and articles on economics and political economy.

HEWLETT, JOHN BERTRAM.—B.1890; astt. auditor, G. Coast, 26th Feb., 1914; ag. senr. astt. auditor, 28th Jan. to 17th Mar., 1919; ag. astt. col. sec., 19th Aug. to 14th Sept., 1919; jun. clk., Col. Audit Dept., 1st July, 1920; 2nd cls. clk., 1st Apr., 1921; audr., Gibraltar, 3rd Nov., 1922; audr., Trinidad, 18th Sept., 1926.

HEWLETT-COOPER, C. T.—Ent. "Britannia," 1889; lieut., 1898; comdr., 1909; capt. (ret.), 1921; S. African Med., 1900; commanded H.M.S. Aquarius, Aegean and Adriatic operations, 1914-17; commanded H.M.S. Andes, escort to convoys, 1918; commanded H.M.S. Marshal, 1919-20; joined col. serv. as dir. of marine, Dar-es-Salam, 2nd Oct., 1920.

HEYWOOD-WADDINGTON, ALFRED.—B. 1898; ed. Oriel Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1920; lieut., 3rd Somerset L.I., 25th July, 1918; cadet, F.M.S., 15th Mar., 1921; ag. astt. contr., lab., 1st May, 1921; ditto, Seremban, Jan., 1923; passed cadet, Aug., 1923; astt. contr., lab., Seremban, Mar., 1924; astt. contr. lab., Klang, Aug., 1924; fedl. examr. in Tamil, Sept., 1924 and July, 1926; astt. contr., lab., Penang, Jan., 1926; ag. dep. contr., Mar., 1926.

HIAM, ROBERT WOOD.—M.I.C.E., M.I.M.E., M.I.S.E.—B. 1874; ed. Claremont Grammar Schl.; pupil, G.W.Rly., 1890-1893; engrg. staff, G.W.Rly., 1893-1910; engrg. staff, F.M.S.R., 1910-19; engrg. for ways and wks., F.M.S.R., 1919.
HICKMAN, HON. ALBERT E.—Elec. M.H.A., Bay de Verde, Newfoundland, 1913; M.E.C., 1917; mem. tonnage comtee., 1917; min. without portfolio, 1918.

HICKSON-MAHONY, R.S.B.M.—Civ. ser., Cape Colony and Union of S. Africa, 3rd Jan., 1903 to 19th Mar., 1917; astt. polit. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 20th Mar., 1917; war ser., Boer War, 1899-1900, Great War, 9th S. African Inf., 1915-1917.

HIGGINS, EVELYN SCOTT.—Ed. at Blundell's Schl., Tiverton, and Chatham House, Rmsgate; served on Uganda rly., 1898-1900; supt. of pol., Somaliland Prot., 22nd May, 1905; astt. supt. of pol., E. Africa Prot., Sept., 1911; astt., pol., Kenya, Oct., 1925.

HIGGINS, HON. HENRY BOURNES.—B. 1851; M.L.A., Victoria, 1894-1900; chrmn. of royal comms. on legal procedure; mem. of Austn. Fed. Convention, 1897-98, and of the first Federal parlmt.; justice of High Court, C. of A., since Oct., 1906.

HIGGINS, MAJOR ROBERT HENRY CONSTABLE, M.B.E., M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1889; ed. St. Andrew's Coll. and R. Vety. Coll., Dublin; war serv., France, Mesopotamia and Archangel, 1914-19; ment. in desps.; vety. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1923.
HIGHAM, REGINALD.—B. 1879; traffic canvasser, Gold Coast rly., 1904; ag. senr. traffic astt., May to Oct., 1910; ch. traffic offr., Aug., 1911.

HIGET, DAVID JOHN, M.I.C.E.—First astt. engrg., Ulu Selangor extension, Oct., 1890; ch. astt. engrg., Jan., 1894; ag. res. engrg. for rlyws., Mar., 1894; dist. rlyw. engrg., F.M.S.R., Jan., 1903; ag. divisional engrg., Selangor and N. Sembilan, June, 1904; divisional engrg., South, Apr., 1906; ag. gen. man., F.M.S.R., May, 1909.

HIGNETT, CHARLES FRANCIS.—Ed. Woking Coll., Surrey; served with Imp. Army, Apr., 1884, to Aug., 1889; 3rd clk., mag. office, Durban, 1st Aug., 1889; sub-inspr., Zululand pol., 27th Jan., 1891 to 31st Jan., 1896 (three years as adjutant); astt. mag., Umlalazi sub-dist. of Eshowe, 1st Feb., 1896; mag., Umlalazi dist., 30th Dec., 1897; Ngutu dist., 10th Aug., 1899; ag. mag., Eshowe dist., Apr. to Aug., 1903; Dundee dist., Sept., 1908 to Feb., 1909; mag., Alexandra div., 31st Aug., 1909; Umgeni div., Pietermaritzburg, 6th June, 1911; Eshowe, Zululand, 6th May, 1912; Verulam, 1st Nov., 1915; senr. mag., Durban, 1st Oct., 1920; judge, native high ct., Natal, 1st Oct., 1922; war services, rebellion in Zululand, 1888-9; Anglo-Boer War, 1899-1902; Natal native rebellion, 1906.

HILL, ARTHUR HEYLIGER.—B. 1876; ed. Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana, and New Coll., Eastbourne; clk., treas., Br. Guiana, 1883; 3rd grade comary. of taxation, 1902; 2nd grade, 1904; sec., excise bd. and supervising comary., treas., in rev. branch, 1905; passed exam. in Hindi, 1905; 1st cls. clk., imgrn. dept., 1907; seconded as astt. emigrn. agt., Calcutta, Mar., 1913; ag. emigrn. agt. at Calcutta for Br. Guiana, Trinidad, Fiji and Jamaica, 14th Apr. to 7th Aug., 1914, and from 20th Nov., 1915 to 6th Nov., 1916.
HILL, FRANK MACLEY.—Graduated, Lond., 1891; govt. vet. offr., Bechuanaland Prot., 1919; J.P., 1920.

HILL, JAMES REGINALD.—B. 1877; ed. Queen's Coll., B. Guiana; 8th clk., adminstr.-gen.'s off., B. Guiana, May, 1898; 5th cls. clk., police dept., Feb., 1897; sub-inspr. of police, Dec., 1900; dist. inspr. of police, Apr., 1903; ag. adjut. and musketry instr., July to Nov., 1906; ag. county inspr. of police, Nov., 1909 to Apr., 1910, and from June to Aug., 1911; ag. pay and qrtmtr., from Sept., 1911; pay and qrtmtr., 1st Jan., 1912.

HILL, REGINALD DYKES RICHARDSON.—B. 1902; ed. Cranleigh Schl.; called to bar (Gray's Inn), 1924; 1st cls. clk., col. sec.'s office, Br. Guiana, 20th Jan., 1926; ag. prin. clk. and clk. to the ct. of policy and combined ct., Sept. to Dec., 1927.

HILLYER, HERBERT KEYS, O.B.E. (1919).—B. 1881; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civil service, and assigned to the C.O., July, 1900; short'd writer and astt. clk., col. sec.'s off., Ceylon, Jan., 1908; sec., Colombo Port Coman., July, 1913; on war service, 1918-1919; resumed duties, 1919.

HIME, CHARLES FREDERICK WILLIAM.—B. 1869; ed. Hilton Coll., Natal; sec. to late ch. just. Sir Henry Connor, 1st Jan., 1888; clk. atty-gen.'s office and ag. regiar., sup. ct., 1st Mar., 1889 to 30th June, 1904; sec. to min. of just., 1st July, 1904; mag. for colony of Natal, 1st July, 1908, holding post, astt. mag., Pietermaritzburg; ag. ch. mag., Pietermaritzburg, 1st Apr., 1915; mag., Bergville, 1st Dec., 1915; addnl. mag., Durban, 1st Dec., 1918; mag., Dundee, 1st May, 1920; senr. mag., Pietermaritzburg, 1st Aug., 1921.

HINCKS, MAUNICK THORPE.—B. 1885; ed. Stonegate Schl., Leicester, and Blairlodge Schl., Polmont, N.B.; solr., sup. ct., 1908; comsnr. for oaths, 1913; on mil'y. serv., 1915 to 1920; junr. administrative offr., Gold Coast, Jan., 1920; pol. mag., Accra, Apr., 1920; ag. ch. regiar., Gold Coast, on various occasions in 1921 and 1923-27; ag. solr.-gen., Jan. to May, 1922; regiar., sup. ct., Nigeria, Aug., 1927.

HISLOP, CAPT. FRANCIS DANIEL, M.A. (hons. hist.), Edin. Univ.—B. 1891; served Territorial Force, 1909-21; on active serv., 1914-19; served Gallipoli, Egypt, France; seconded to Machine Gun Corps, 1917; instr. in machine gunnery, 1918-19; cadet, S. Stltmts., Oct., 1919; atttl., treas., Singapore, Dec., 1919; astt. asp't., govt. monopolies, Singapore, June, 1920; resig. on med. grounds, June, 1921; cadet, Kenya, Dec., 1923.

HISLOP, JAMES, C.V.O. (1927) M.V.O. (1930), O.B.E. (1919).—B. 1870; entd. New Zealand civ. serv., 1885; New Zealand govt. rlyws., 1890; astt. priv. sec. to min. for rlyws. and postmtr. gen., 1900; priv. sec. to prime min., 1907; under sec. for internal aff'rs and clk. of writs, 1912.
HITCHENS, ALFRED EDEN.—B. 1880; astt. engrg., water wks., Trinidad, 24th Dec., 1900; 1st grade dist. offr., P.W.D., Trinidad, 24th Sept., 1906; divnl. engrg., 1st Apr., 1926.

HOBDAY, WILFRED ERNEST.—B. 1889; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1912; attached to col. sec.'s office, Jan., 1913; Colombo Kachcheri, April, 1913; Trincomalee Kach., Nov., 1913; ag. offr. astt. to the govt. agt., Ratnapura, May, 1914; on mil. duty, Sept., 1914; landing surv., Colombo customs, May, 1919; 4th astt. col. sec.,

HOBSON, SAMUEL GEORGE.—B. 1872; ed. govt. schls., Victoria; asst. supt., post and tels., Selangor, July, 1902; asst. supt., posts and tels., Selangor, Negri Sembilan and Pahang, Jan., 1905; supt., posts and tels., Perak, Nov., 1912; contr., posts and tels., Perak, Oct., 1926.

HOBSON, WILLIAM EDWARD, M.B.E. (1919).—B. 1885; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. in lower (now 2nd) div. of the civ. serv., and assigned to the office of the comsrs. of H.M.'s wrks. and pub. bldgs., 1st Jan., 1885; transf. to the C.O., April, 1891; asst. regis., 1899; promoted to higher grade, Jan., 1900; staff-clk., first grade, 1911; supt., printing dept., Dec., 1924.
HOCART, ARTHUR MAURICE.—Archaeological comsnr., Ceylon, May, 1919.
HODGE, S.O.V.—Asst. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., Apr., 1914; ag. dist. comsnr., Kenya, 1926.

HODGES, ARTHUR HARRIS.—B. 1884; entd. Impl. civ. ser., and apptd. to acct.-gen's. office, G.P.O., London, 1900; ch. acct's. office, G.P.O., O.R.C., 1903; Australia, 1908-9; junr. asst. treas., N. Nigeria, 1910; senr. asst. treas., Nigeria, 1920; dep. treas., Nigeria, 1924.

HODGINS, HON. FRANK EGERTON.—B. 1854; educ. Upper Canada Coll. and Trin. Univ.; B.C.L.; called to the bar, 1879; K.O., 1902; prominent legal practitioner in Toronto for several years; pres., Ontario bar assoc., 1908-9; legal agt. for Dominion govt. at Toronto, 1890-1896; counsel for Ontario govt. on license investigation, 1907, and on other occasions; judge of high ct. of Ontario, 1912; also local judge in Admiralty, Exchequer Ct.; now a just. of appeal.
HODGSON, HARRY COURTNEY.—B. 1887; ed. Rocklington Schl. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; B.A. (Oxon.) asst. dist. comsnr., Sierra Leone, 30th Nov., 1910; dist. comsnr., 1st May, 1915.

HODKIN, CAPT. THOMAS WILLIAM, Fellow R. Econ Soc., Fellow R. Statistical Soc., Fellow Inst. of Commerce, Assoc., Chartered Inst. of Secs., Assoc., Inst. of Bankers.—B. 1886; ed. Manchester Grammar Schl., Manchester Univ. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; dipl. in educon., Oxford; served in France with E. Lancs. Regt., 1914-18; attd. to gen. staff; educon. offr., 66th divn., 1918-19; instr., schl. of educon. for offrs., Catterick, 1919; instr., schl. of instrn. for offrs., Bedford, 1919-20; instr., W.O. schl. of educon., Shorncliffe, 1920-22; brig. educon. offr., 1st Air Defence Brig., Aldershott, 1922; A.K.C., res. of offrs.; examr. for W.O. in bookkeeping and accountancy; commercial master, Zanzibar, 1922; prin., Arab schl., Mombasa, Kenya, 1927.

HODSON, ARNOLD WIENHOLT, C.M.G. (1922).—Ed. Italy, Mulgrave Castle, and Felsted; in Central Queensland, 1900-1902; joined Australian Commonwealth Horse for service in S. Africa, 1902; in Transvaal, 1902-4; sub.-inspr., Bech. Prot. pol., 1904; specially employed in connection with Damaras war (med. and clasp); mem. of Ngamiland mission, 1906; ag. asst. comsnr., Mar. to May, 1910, and Mar. to Dec., 1911; dist. comsnr. and 1st cls. mag., Somalland, 1912 H.B.M. consul. for Southern Abyssinia, 1914; ditto, S.W. Abyssinia, 1923; served part time in European War; present at coronation of Empress Zoditow, 1917; 3rd cls. Star of Ethiopia; African Gen. Ser. Med. and Clasp, Jubaland, 1917-18; attchd., Abyssinian army in expedition against Tigre, 1919 (med. and clasp); late capt., Royal Sussex Regt. (Territorials); has passed Cape Univ. mag. law exam.; Somali language, lower standard; Galla language, higher standard; gov. and commdr.-in-chief, Falkland Is. and their dependencies, 22nd Dec., 1926; joint author of "A Practical Galla Grammar."

HODSON, THOMAS ARTHUR.—B. 1882; ed. Cheltenham and Wadham Coll., Oxford (M.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1905; seconded asst. supt. of pol., Galle, Dec., 1906; asst. supt. of pol., Galle, July, 1908; ditto, Tangalla, May, 1909; ditto, Matara, Dec., 1909; office astt. to govt. ast., S. Prov., Jan., 1910; ast. pol. mag., Kandy, Feb., 1911; ast. govt. ast., Trincomalee Nov., 1911; ag. dist. judge, Batticaloa, Feb., 1914; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, May, 1914; ast. settlm. offr. and spec. offr. under Waste Lands Ordee., June, 1916; on mil. duty, 1917; addnl. astt. col. sec., Apr., 1919; astt. govt. ast., Kalutara, May, 1919; addnl. astt. dir., educn., Mar., 1922; ag. astt. dir., educn., Apr., 1922; ag. govt. ast., N.W. Prov., May, 1923; astt. govt. ast., Matale, Mar., 1924; govt. ast., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, Mar., 1925; ditto, N.W. Prov., Oct., 1926.
HOFMEYR, HON. JAN HENDRIK, M.A.—B. 1894; prof. of classics, Univ. of Witwatersrand, 1917; prin., 1919-24; admstr., Transvaal Prov., 1924.
HOGGEN, SIDNEY JOHN, B.A.—B. 1898; ed. Highgate Coll. and Oriel Coll., Oxford; supt., educon. dept., N. Provs., Nigeria, 24th Aug., 1921.

HOGG, WILLIAM EDWARD, A.R.C.S., A.M.Inst.C.R.—B. 1880; ed. City of London Schl. and Imp. Coll. of Science; Whitworth Schol., 1906; eng. inspr., C.A., 1910; astt. engrn., 1919; dep. head, engrn. insptn. dept., 1920.

HOHENKIRCH, LUDOVIC SMITH, M.B.E. (1925).—B. 1874; ed. in B. Guiana; ag. 6th cls. clk., govt. land dept., B. Guiana, 1890; ag. survr., 1896; 5th cls. clk., 1897; 3rd grade survr., 1897; 2nd cls. offr., lands and mines dept., 1904; ag. 1st cls. offr., 1901 and 1905; J.P. for the col., 1906; ag. warden, No. 3 mining dist., 1906, 1907 and 1909; 1st cls. offr., 21st Sept., 1908; ag. forestry offr., 1914-16; performed duties of prin. clk. in head offr., 1916; represented Col. at Imp. Forestry Confnee., Canada, 1923; seconded for duty with B. Guiana exhibit, Br. Empire Exhibn., 1924; supt., forest surveys, forestry dept., 1926.

HOLDBROOK, HUBERT GEORGE, B.A., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O., Trinity Coll., Dublin, L.M., Rotunda Hosp.—B. 1887; med. offr., S. Stltnta, 1912; med. offr., F.M.S., 1913; certif., London Schl. of Trop. Med., 1921; health offr., F.M.S., 1921; health offr., Perak North, Feb., 1922.

HOLDEN, GEORGE.—Astt. supt. of wks., S. Stltnta., June, 1901; supt. of wks., Prov. Wellesley, Jan., 1908; ag. supt. of wks. and surveys, Singapore, July, 1908; ditto, Malacca, May, 1909; exec. engrn., Malacca, Dec., 1909; ag. exec. engrn., Singapore, Jan., 1910; ag. exec. engrn., Prov. Wellesley, Oct., 1910; ag. exec. engrn., Singapore, June, 1913; ag. exec. engrn., Malacca, Mar., 1915; ag. exec. engrn., Singapore, Oct., 1916; ag. dep. col. engrn., Mar., 1920; exec. engrn., Malacca, 29th Dec., 1920.
HOLDING, RICHARD HENRY.—B. 1898; on mily. serv., Apr. 1917 to Mar., 1920; temp. clk., O.S.O., Apr. 1920; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., O.S.O., 6th Oct., 1921.
HOLKE, LIEUT.-COMMDE. GEORGE FRANCIS, R.N.—B. 1887; cadet, H.M.S. "Britannia,"

HOLLIDAY, ARTHUR ROBERT.—B. 1884; atty. and notary, sup. ct. of S. Africa, 1906; asst. dist. commr., Gold Coast Colony, 1st Jan., 1915; seconded for service in Togoland; dist. polit. offr., Misahoe, 12th Oct., 1917; dist. polit. offr., Lome, 1918; ag. dist. commr., N. Territories, Gold Coast, Oct., 1918; asst. admstr.-gen., Zanzibar, 19th Jan., 1925; ag. admstr.-gen., pub. trustee regis. of documents and land offr. in 1926 and 1927.

HOLLINGSWORTH, LAWRENCE WILLIAM, B.A. (Lond.).—B. 1896; ed. Sir Walter St. John's Schol., London Univ., and London Day Training Coll.; on active serv., E. Africa, 1916-19; normal master, Zanibar educn. dept., 1922.

HOLLINS, NOEL CLINTON.—B. 1886; ed. Rossall Schol. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; B.A. (Cantab.), 2nd cls. hist., tripod; qualified in exam. for gen. consular service; asst. col. sec., Sierra Leone, 31st Mar., 1910; ag. senr. asst. col. sec., 1911 and 1912; asst. dist. commr., 1st Jan., 1913; dist. commr., 1st Jan., 1915; ag. prov. commr., C. Prov., Nov., 1922.

HOLLIS, Sir ALFRED CLAUS, K.C.M.G. (1927), C.M.G. (1911), C.B.E. (1919).—B. 1874; asst. collr., E.A.P., 12th Mar., 1897; employed on transport duty during Uganda Mutiny, 1898; collr., 13th June, 1900; ag. Brit. vice-consul for German East Africa, Apr., 1900 to Feb., 1901; ag. sec. to admstr. and priv. sec. to H.M. commr., Feb., 1901 to Apr., 1903; sec. to the admstrn., 1st Apr., 1903; Jubaland expdn., 1900-1, and Nandi expdn., 1905-6 (medal and two clasps); sec. for native affairs and M.L.C., 13th June, 1907; on special service to Uganda, Oct.-Nov., 1909; ag. chief sec. to gov't., Apr. to Dec., 1911, and Feb. to Oct., 1912; Swahili examiner, 1910 to 1912; col. sec., Sierra Leone, 26th Jan., 1913; ag. gov't., Oct., 1913 to Feb., 1914, June to Oct., 1915, and Jan. to Mar., 1916; eo. to provisional admstrn., German East Africa (Tanganyika Territory), 4th Nov., 1916; ag. admstr., Feb. to Nov., 1919; chief sec., Tanganyika Territory, 1919; ag. gov', Feb. to Oct., 1922; Br. res., Zanzibar, Jan., 1924; author of works on the Masai, Nandi and other E.A. tribes.

HOLLOWAY, JOHN EDWARD.—B. 1890; B.A. (hons. hist.), 1910, Univ. C of G.H. (Victoria Coll.); res. mast., Grey Coll. Schol., Bloemfontein, 1911-13; mem., coun. of examrs., O.F.S., 1912-13; D.Sc. (Econ.) Lond. (Schl. of Economics), 1917; Hutchinson research medallist; lect., Grey Univ. Coll., 1917-19; Transvaal Univ. Coll., 1919-21; prof., economics, T.U.C., 1921-25; dir. census and statistics, Union of S. Africa, 1925.

HOLM, ALEXANDER, C.B.E. (1926), M.R.A.S.E. (Gold Medallist), F.H.A.S., S.E.A.C. (Dip.Hons.).—Gen. manager, govt. experimental farm, Potchefstroom, Transvaal, 1903; gen. manager and principal, schl. of agric., Potchefstroom, 1909; under sec. for agric. (eductn.), Union of S. Africa, Oct., 1912; dir. of agric., E. Africa Prot., 1918; mem., exec. leg. and inter-col. rly. couns.; pres., agric. and horticult. society of Kenya; Br. Empire Exhibn. commr. for Kenya, 1924.

HOLMES, HAROLD KENNARD.—Ed. All Saints, Bloxham; admitted solr., sup. ct., Hong Kong, 1900; served with K.R.R.C., Somme, 1916; wounded, Guilemont; with 20th London Regt. at Salonika, Palestine, France and Rhine, 1917-18, 2nd lieut., 1918; asst. land offr., Hong Kong, 1919; J.P., 1919; land offr., 1921 to 1922; ag. crown solr., 1922; crown solr., 1922.

HOLMES, ROBERT BLAKE WORSLEY, O.B.E., M.I.M.E., F.R.G.S.—B. 1878; ed. Aberdeen Gram. Schol. and Aberdeen Univ.; asst. engr., Lagos rly. (constrn.), Nigeria, 1900; asst. loco. supt., Nigeria rly., wks. man., dist. loco. supt., dep. ch. mech. engr., Nigerian Eastern Rly., 1906-15; formed mech. and shipyard formations, inland waterways and docks br., R.E., Richborough, 1916; major, 14th Sept., 1914; lieut.-col., 1st Mar., 1917; ment. in desps. and awarded O.B.E., 1918; col., 1st Mar., 1918; demob., Apr., 1919; recalled to colours, June, 1919, and proceeded to Damascus as asst. dir., rly. traffic, in ch. of Hejaz rly., Nov., 1919; in ch., evacuation of troops by rail from Syria, Dec., 1919; col., grade T., Mar., 1920; gen. man., Palestine mil'y. rly., Mar., 1920; transf'd to civ. gert., as gen. man., Palestine rly., 1st Oct., 1920; col., R. of O., 1st Nov., 1921.

HOLMES, STEPHEN LEWIS, M.C. (1918).—B. 1896; King's scholar of Westminster, 1909; 2nd lieut., Royal Garrison Art'y., July, 1915; served in France and Belgium, 1916-19; adjt.; ag. major; twice ment. in desps.; scholar of Christ Church, Oxford, 1919; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, asst. prin., C.O., 7th Feb., 1921; prte. sec. to perm. under sec. (Sir S. Wilson), Feb., 1926.

HOLMES, WILLIAM, B.A., LL.B. (Dublin).—B. 1901; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1923; att. to Badulla kach., Jan., 1924; ditto, N. Elira kach., Aug., 1924; ditto, secretariat, June, 1925; pol. mag., Gampola, Dec., 1926; asst. priv. sec. to gov't., Mar., 1927; ag. pol. mag., Kurunegala, May, 1927; office asst., Kurunegala kach., Sept., 1927.

HOLMES, WILLIAM FREDERICK, B.A. (Sydney), Assoc., R. Coll. of Science (London) in Geology, Assoc., R. Schl. of Mines (London) in Mining Mem., Inst. of Mining and Metallurgy, F.G.S. (1900)—Demonstr., mine surveying, R. Schl. of Mines, 1901; mining engr., Dutch E. Indies, S. Africa, Siam and Gold Coast; senr. inspr., mines, Gold Coast, 1914; sec. for mines, 1922.

HONEY, DE SYMONS MONTAGU GEORGE, C.M.G. (1919), B.B. 1872; served in B.S.A. Co.'s expedition to Mashonaland, 1891 to 1893; with B.C. Africa admtn., 1893 to 1896; cust. dept., Transvaal, 9th Apr., 1901; native affairs dept., 1902; sec. for Swaziland affairs, and acting offr., 20th Oct., 1904; gov't. sec., Swaziland admtn., 22nd Mar., 1907; res. commr., Swaziland, 1st Jan., 1917; gov', Seychelles, 1928.
HOOD, FREDERICK JAMES, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1893; ed. George Heriot's Schl. and R. Vety. Coll., Edinburgh; war serv., France, 1916-19; vety. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1922.

HOODLESS, D. W., B.Sc.—B. 1887; ed. Sheffield Univ. and King's Coll., London; B.Sc. hon., Lond., 1910; Drewgold medal, mathematics, 1910; associateship of King's coll.; mast., H.M.S. "Worcester," 1911; asst. mast., Queen Victoria schl., Fiji, 1912; head mast., Lau Schl., 1915; J.P. for the colony, 1916; ag. head mast., Queen Victoria schl., 1918; ag. sup't., schl., 1922; prov. mem. leg. coun., Nov., 1924; asst. dir., educn., 1927.

HOOKER, RICHARD SYMONDS.—B. 1885; ed. Bedford Gram. Schl. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; M.A. (Cantab.); qualified in exam. for gen. consular service; passed lower standard exam. in Mende; asst. dist. comsnr. Sierra Leone, July, 1910; dist. comsnr., Jan., 1915; ag. prov. comsnr., N. Prov., Nov., 1920 to Aug., 1921 and from Sept., 1922; prov. comsnr., 1st Jan., 1926.

HOOPS, ALBERT LAUNCELOT.—B. 1876; ed at King William's Coll. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; B.A., 1900; M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O., 1901; M.D. (stip. cond.) 1903; D.P.H., 1903; L.M., Rotunda, 1903; certif. honours, London Schi. of Trop. Med., 1904; served in S. African War as medical offir., 28th M.I., 1901-2 (severely wounded, ment. in despa., medal and four clasps); house surgeon, Penang, 1904; surg. capt., Penang vol.; ag. col. surg. res., Penang, June, 1905; seconded to organise medical and prison depts. in Kedah, Mar., 1906; state surg. and supt. of prisons in Kedah to Nov., 1920; ag. adviser to govt. of Kedah, Sept., 1906, to Apr., 1907, and from July to Oct., 1907; supt. of Indian immigts., Kedah, in addition to other duties, Oct., 1910; prot. of lab., Kedah, in addition, Nov., 1917; prin. civil med. offr., S. Sttlmts., 23rd Nov., 1920; pres. coun., coll. of med., Spore; pres., S.S. and F.M.S. med. council; mem., leg. coun., S. Sttlmts., 1st Jan., 1923; chmn., tuberculosis comttee, June to Aug., 1923; pres., 5th Congress of Far Eastern Assoc. of Trop. Med., Singapore, Sept., 1923.
HOPKINS, J.G.—Asst. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., Mar., 1917.

HOPKINSON, CAPT. EMILUS, C.M.G. (1922), D.S.O. (1900), M.A., M.B., B.Ch. (Oxon.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), F.Z.S.—B. 1869; ed. at Haileybury, Trin. Coll., Oxford, and St. Thomas's hosp., Lond.; late ophthalmic house surg., St. Thomas's hosp.; clinical asst., Royal Ophthalmic hosp., Moorfields; house surg., Radcliffe infirmary, Oxford; served in S. African war, 1900-1901, as surg.-capt., 15th batt., I.Y. (ment. in despa., Queen's Med., 4 clasps, D.S.O.); late surg.-lieut., Oxford I.Y.; med. offr., W.A.F.F., Gambia, 1901-1902; prot. med. offr., Gambia, 1903; travelling comsnr., South Bank prov., Gambia, 1912; travelling comsnr., North Bank Prov., Gambia, 1921; author of "Vocabulary of the Mandingo Language."

HOPKINSON, GEORGE FREDERICK.—B. 1894; cler. asst., post office, Br. Guiana, 1911; 6th cls. offr., cust., 1912; 5th cls. ditto., 1914; 4th cls. clk., treasy., 1920; 3rd cls. ditto., 1923; 3rd cls. clk., coll. secretariat, 1925; 2nd cls. clk., ditto, 1926.

HORE-RUTHVEN, MAJOR-GEN. HON. SIR ALEXANDER (ORE ARKWRIGHT), K.C.M.G. (1928), V.C. (1899), C.B. (1919), C.M.G. (1918), D.S.O. (1916).—B. 1872; ed. Eton; joined 3rd batt. Highland Light Infy., 1891; attd. Egyptian Army, Soudan, 1898; commanded Camel Corps detachment at battle of Gedaref and subsequent operas. (V.C., 4th cls. Osmanieh, Englis; and Egyptian medics with clasp, three times ment. in despa.); sp. serv. offr., Somaliland, 1903-04; mily. sec., Viceroy of Ireland, 1905-06; mily. sec., gov.-gen., C'wealth of Australia, 1908; served European war, France and Gallipoli, 1915-18 (severely wounded, D.S.O. and bar, C.B., C.M.G., ment. in despa. five times); commanded Welsh Guards, 1920-24; commanded 1st Infy. Brigade (Guards); 1924-28; gov., S. Australia, 1928.

HORN, ARTHUR EDWIN, C.M.G. (1923), M.D., B.Sc.(Lond.); M.R.C.S.(Eng.); L.R.C.P.(Lond.); D.T.M. and H. (Cantab.); certif., Lond. Schi. of Trop. Med. (with distinctions).—Ed. at St. Mary's Hosp. and London Univ.; civil surg., 19th brigade field hosp., S. African field force, 1900-1901; ap'td. to W. African medical staff, 1904 and has served in G. Coast, N. and S. Nigeria and Gambia; awarded Craggs research prize for original research in trop. med. (London S.T.M.), 1908; read. thanks of S. of S. for the Colonies for report on cerebro-spinal fever in Northern Territories of the G. Coast, 1908, and for report on sleeping sickness in the Volta River dist. of the G. Coast, 1910; seconded for special service at the C.O., 1910-12; personal asst. to the P.M.O., S. Nigeria, 1913; seur med. offr. ad mem., exec. and leg. couns., Gambia, 1914-16; prin. civ. med. offr., German E. Africa, 1917-19; attd. Rockefeller comn. for investigation of yellow fever in W. Africa, 1920; dir., med. and sanry. services, Malaya, 1921-22; pres. elect for 5th Congress of Far Eastern Assoc. of Trop. Med., 1923; med. sec. to Col. Advisory Med. and Sanry. Comttee. at C.O., 1923-24; junr. med. adviser to C.O., 1924; mem., exec. comttee. of Br. Empire Leprosy Relief Assoc.; fellow R. Soc'y. of Med.; R. Soc'y. Trop. Med.; author of "Health of Europeans in W. Africa," "Health of Europeans in E. Africa."

HORNBY, CAPT. HENRY EPTON, O.B.E., F.R.C.V.S., D.V.S.M.—B. 1890; ed. King's Coll. Schi. and R. Vety. Coll., London; lect. in bacteriology to S.E. Agrl. Coll. (Univ. of London), 1912-13; vety. offr., N. Rhodesia, 1913-20; war serv., E. Africa, N. Rhodesia Rifles, 1914-17; S.A.V.C., 1917-19; ment. in despa.; studied physiology and pathology, Univ. Coll. Schi. of Trop. Med., London, Ecole de Medicine, Paris, and Vetv. Research Lab., Pretoria; vety. pathologist, Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1922.

HORNBY-PORTER, CHARLES.—Apptd. dist. comsnr., Lagos, 1897; 2nd asst. col. sec., 1901; senior asst. col. sec., 1903; ag. col. treas., 1912-19; ag. col. sec., 1914; holds dormant comsnr. to sit as pol. mag., and sat as such in 1897, 1901, 1902 and 1913; on spec. serv. to Ijebu-Ode, 1901 and 1914, and to Oyo and also to Ife, 1904; pres. of pol. comsnr., 1901; mem. of rly. comsnr., 1901; pres. of cust. comsnr., 1904.

HORNE, EDWARD BUTTLER.—Ed. at Bedford Gram. Schi.; asst. collr., E. Africa Prot., 21st Apr., 1904; dist. comsnr., 1st Apr., 1909; offr.-in-charge, Masai res., May, 1923; senr. comsnr. (2nd class), Kenya, Jan., 1924; senr. comsnr., 1st grade, 1926.
HORNE, EDWIN NOEL.—B. 1902; appt. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 4th May, 1920.

HORSBURGH, HARRY, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1876; ed. Aske's Haberdasher's Company Schi.; C.A., Jan., 1918; asst. dir., min. of munitions, 1915; dep. contr., munitions disposal bd., 1919; dep. head, engnr. contracts, 1920; tech. asst., "Discovery Comttee," 1923.
HORSLEY, BERNARD HILL.—B. 1890; European War, 1914-18; major, 8th (reserve) batt., K.O.Y.L.I.; D.S.O., M.C., desps.; dist. polit. offr., Somaliland, 1919; dist. comsnr., Somaliland, 1922.

HORSLEY, RICHARD.—B. 1893; ed. Cowley Gram. Schi., St. Helens; gen. fitter, P.W.D., Gold Coast, 13th Aug., 1919; instr. of metalwk., Accra tech. schi., 10th Apr., 1920; ag. prin., 1st Dec., 1920 to 22nd Mar., 1921; house mast., 6th Sept., 1922; head mast., junr. trade schl., 28th Apr., 1926.

HORWOOD, Hon. Sir Wm. Henry, Kt. Bach. (1904).—B. 1862; ed. at Bishop Feild Coll., St. John's; called to the bar, 1886; bencher of the law society, Newfoundland, 1891; Q.C., 1896; elected to the legislature, 1894; acted as col. sec., 1894-5; delegate from the Newfoundland govt. to the Ottawa conf., Apr., 1896, on the subject of confederation with Canada; mem. of ex. coun., 1894-97; ag. atty.-gen., 1897; elected to legislature to represent Harbour Grace, 1897; apptd. by ch. just., with minister of just. to act in conjunction with judges as committee to amend rules and procedure, sup. court, 1899; minister of just. and atty.-gen., 1900; ex-officio mem. of ex. coun., and of treas. board; acted as leader of govt. during sess. of 1901; and also in 1902; apptd. ch. just., July, 1902; administered govt., 1902, 1904, 1909, 1912, 1913, 1914; deputy gov., 1907, 1910; chrmn. of royal comm. on Sealing Industry, 1913; pres., Newf. centre of St. John Ambulance Assoc.; chrmn. of comtes. for selection of non-combatants for service abroad; chrmn. of Newf. fund for hospital costs for soldiers and sailors; representative on Peace Comm. established under treaty between Great Britain and U.S.A., May, 1916.

HOSE, Edward Shaw, C.M.G. (1924).—B. 1871; junr. offr., Perak, F.M.S., Jan., 1891; ag. financial ast., Kuala Kangsar, Oct., 1891; ast. dist. mag., ditto, Nov., 1893; ag. dist. mag., Batang Padang, Oct., 1898; ast. sec. to govt., Perak, Nov., 1898; ag. dist. offr., Larut and Krian, May, 1903; offr. of cls. III, S. Settlements, Mar., 1904; ag. dep. pub. pros.; ag. 2nd mag., Singapore, July, 1904; ag. commnr., ct. of requests, Singapore, Aug., 1904; chmn., sanitary board, Kuala Lumpur, Oct., 1904; ag. commnr., trade and cust., F.M.S., Sept., 1912; ag. aud.-gen., 1912; ag. controller of labour, May, 1913; ag. dir. of agric., Oct., 1915; offr. of cls. II, F.M.S., Mar., 1916; dist. offr., Lower Perak; ag. dir. food production, F.M.S., 8th Feb., 1919; dir. food production, S. Sttlmts., 17th July, 1919; in addition; contr. of lab., F.M.S. and S. Sttlmts. (offr. cls. Ia.), but continues to act as dir. of food production, Sept., 1919; dep. food contr., Malacca, in addition, Jan., 1920; food contr., Mar., 1920; ag. Br. res., Selangor, Sept., 1920; ag. Br. res., Negri Sembilan, Nov., 1921; Br. res., Negri Sembilan, Feb., 1922; ag. ch. sec., F.M.S., May, 1923; resumed duty as Br. res., Negri Sembilan, Oct., 1923; ag. ool. sec., S.S., Apr., 1924; col. sec., July, 1924.
HOSKING, E. B.—Asst. dist. commnr. E.A.P., Dec., 1913; ag. dist. commnr., Kenya, 1926.
HOSKING, Hon. Sir John Henry, Kt. Bach. (1925).—B. 1854; barrister and solicitor; K.C., 1908; judge of sup. ct., New Zealand, 1914-25.

HOUGHTON, H. E.—B. 1892; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser. and assigned to the C.O., Apr., 1912; attached to office of high commnr. for S. Africa, Nov., 1920; transf., to Basutoland serv., Nov., 1923.

HOW, Douglas Walshaw.—B. 1889; ed. Cheltenham Coll., London Matric.; Un. of S. Africa civ. serv. lower law exam.; Basutoland 2nd grade Sesuto exam.; clerical ast., Basutoland, 5th Oct., 1910; sub-instr., Basutoland mtd. pol., 1st July, 1912; chief clk., master of ct. and regar., 1st July, 1917; served as 2nd lieutenant with Royal Welsh Fusiliers in France, 1918; instr. and staff offr., Basutoland mtd. pol., 1st July, 1919; dep. ast. commnr., 1923.

HOWARD, Hon. Eratos Edwin.—B. 1868; ed. Iroquois High Schil., Inverness Acad., and McGill Univ.; B.A., 1896; gold medallist in maths and nat. philos.; B.C.L. (gold medallist), 1898; barrister; practised in Montreal, 1899-1919; called to the bar, Quebec, July, 1899; K.C., Sept., 1913; judge, superior ct., prov. of Quebec, 26th Sept., 1919; judge of ct. of King's Bench, 9th Aug., 1920; prof., civ. law, McGill Univ. and elective fellow of law faculty.

HOWARD, Frederick James, O.B.E.—B. 1883; ed. at Bedford; 2nd divn. clk., W.O., June, 1901; 2nd cls. ast. acctn., army acctn. dept., May, 1905; selected for advancement to higher grade of scale, 1st Nov., 1911; ag. 1st cls. ast. acctn., Oct., 1914; ag. acctn., Nov., 1916; 1st cls. ast. acctn., 15th Dec., 1919; personal supernumy, appt. as acctn., 1st Jan., 1921; serv. with min. of munitions as dir. from 1915, and with disposal and liquidation commn. treas.—as ast. contr. of deptl. finance until 1921; O.B.E., 1st Jan., 1918; ag. prin., old style, 1st Jan., 1920; ag. prin. C.O., 15th Sept., 1921.

HOWARD, John Curtis.—B. 1887; ed. Uppingham and Clare Coll., Camb. (exhib.); called to the bar, Inner Temple, Jan., 1913; 2nd lieut., 4th Lincoln Regt., Mar., 1915; served in France, Jan., 1917 to July, 1920; legal adviser, G.H.Q., France, May, 1919 to July, 19.0; prov. dist. ct., Cyprus, Aug., 1920; passed exam. in mod. Greek, Aug., 1921; ag. king's advoc., Nov., 1922 to Mar. 1923 and Aug., 1923; king's advoc., July, 1924; title altered to atty.-gen., May, 1925; joint compilers, 2nd vol., statute laws of Cypru., 1923; solr.-gen., Nigeria, 1926.

HOWARTH, Samuel Edward James.—Ed. at Buxton Coll., London; served in S. African War, Queen's and King's medals with five clasps; ast. Colony manager, field force canteens, O.R.C., Feb., 1902, to Mar., 1903; ast. acctn., P.W.D., E. Africa Prot., 6th May, 1905; ch. acct., P.W.D., 1916.
HOWATSON, Charles William, M.B., C.M. (Edin.).—B. 1861; dist. med. offr., Trinidad, 15th May, 1905.

HOWELL, Alleyn Graham.—B. 1871; 5th clk. corresp. branch, C.S.O., Barbados, 1890; 4th clk., 1892; 3rd clk., Jan., 1898; ch. clk. commnr. of agriculture, 1899; sec. to W. Indian agric. conferences, 1900, 1901, 1902, 1903, and 1907.

HOWELL, E. H., M.B.E., (1927).—B. 1879; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser. and assigned to the C.O., Mar., 1898; promoted, with gratuity, Dec., 1907; conf. clk., 1911; staff clk., 1st grade, 8th Sept., 1917; deptl. ast., 16th Jan., 1923; clk. for legal instru-ments, 26th Feb., 1923.

HOWELL, Frederick Rouse.—B. 1886; sup. clk., P.O., Barbados, Feb., 1907; jun. clk., P.O., Nov., 1907; clk. to master in chancery, Dec., 1907; 4th clk., C.S.O., May, 1909; 3rd clk., C.S.O., 1919; 3rd grade offr., clerk, exec. coun. and exec. comtees, 1925.

HOWELL, John Bruce, O.B.E. (1927).—B. 1867; 3rd clk., treas., Barbados, July, 1886; 3rd clk., col. sec.'s office, Sept., 1891; 2nd clk., C.S.O., and clk. of exec. coun. and comtees, C.S.O., 1898; ag. priv. sec. and A.D.C. to the gov. of Barbados in 1902, 1904, 1906, 1907, 1910 and 1911; J.P., 1918; ch. clk., col. secy.'s off., Aug., 1918; ag. col. sec. on various occasions 1922-25; has held various other ag. apptmts.; man., govt. savings bank, 1926.
HOWELL, WILFRED IRVING.—Cadet, botanical and experiment statn., St. Kitts, July, 1907; foreman, Mar., 1909; agri. instr., Nevis, Apr., 1912.

HOWES, ARTHUR BURNABY.—B. 1879; ed. at Dulwich Coll.; called to the bar, Middle Temple, June, 1906; Western circuit; conveyancer, Uganda Prot., 22nd May, 1916; custodian of enemy property, 20th Mar., 1917; dist. mag., dist. judge, and admstr.-gen., Entebbe, Uganda, 1st Apr., 1907; dist. mag., dist. judge, and offr. rec. in bankruptcy, Kampala, Uganda, 18th July, 1917; ag. judge of high ct., Jan.-June, 1920; ag. atty.-gen., Feb. to Dec., 1921; senr. mag., 1st Jan., 1922; ag. judge of high ct., Apr. to Oct., 1922 and June, 1923; puiane judge, Gold Coast, Dec., 1924.

HOWITT, CAPT. CHARLES ROBERTS.—B. 1894; ed. Carlisle and Queen's Coll., Oxford; R. Fusiliers, 1914-16; Border Regt., 1916-19; Malayan civ. serv., Nov., 1920; attd., land office, Johore, Dec., 1920; 2nd cls. mag., Sept., 1921; ag. 2nd cls. mag., Johore, May, 1922; 1st cls. mag., May, 1922; passed cadet, June, 1922; asst. collr. of land rev., Johore Bahru, Sept., 1922; offr. cls. V, June, 1922; collr. land rev., Johore Bahru, Oct., 1922; 2nd lieut., J.V.R., Aug., 1923; asst. adviser, Kota Tinggi, May, 1924; lieut., J.V.R., Aug., 1924; attd., pol. ots., Singapore, Oct., 1925; offr. 3rd mag., Singapore, Oct., 1925; offr., cls. IV, June, 1926.

HOY, SIR WILLIAM WILSON, K.C.B. (1922), KT. BACH (1916); C.B. (1818).—B. 1868; entd. serv. of Cape Govt. rlys., 1889; representative of C.G.R. in Johannesburg, 1896; ag. asst. traffic manager, Bulawayo, 1897-8; Kimberley, 1898; asst. traffic manager, Port Elizabeth, 1899; traffic manager, Bloemfontein, Johannesburg, 1900-02; chief traffic manager, C.S.A.R., 1902; represented C.S.A.R. at rly. confere. at Washington; ag. gen. manager in 1905 and 1907; asst. gen. manager, 1909; gen. manager of rlys. and harbours, Union of S. Africa, 31st May, 1910; ret., 1927; chmn., Rhodesia rly. comm., 1927.

HUBBALL, JOHN GORST.—Clt., control and audit office, Cape, 1895; examr., 1895; asst. acctnt., 1903; asst. acctnt., contrlr. and auditor-gen.'s office, Union of S. Africa, 1910; prin. clk., 1912; chief acctnt., treas., Union of S.Africa, 1916.
HUDDEN, EDWARD LANPHIER.—B. 1902; ed. Radley Coll.; lieut., 4th Battn. Gloucestershire Regt., T.A., 1925; cadet, Uganda Prot., 1926.

HUDSON, HAROLD.—Book-keeper, treas. dept., Uganda Prot., Mar., 1911; cashier, treas. dept., Aug., 1911; asst. treas., Mar., 1914; 1st grade asst. treas., Jan., 1922; ag. deputy treas., June to Dec., 1919, Dec., 1921 to Feb., 1922, Sept., 1922 to Apr., 1923, May, 1923 to Mar., 1924, Feb. to Oct., 1925, Mar. to Dec., 1926, and from Mar. to July, 1927.

HUDSON, PHILIP JAMES.—B. 1897; on mil. serv., June, 1916; cadet, Ceylon, Mar., 1920; attd. to Colombo Kachcheri, Apr., 1920; pol. mag., Balapitoya, Mat., 1921; asst. settlement offr., 1922; office asst., Jaffna kach., Dec., 1924; asst. stiml. offr., May, 1925.

HUDSON, LIEUT.-COL. WILLIAM, O.B.E., M.C.—B. 1880; ed. privately and King's Coll., London; S. African War, 1900-02; Queen's Med., with 2 clasps; King's Med., 2 clasps; contr.'s staff, cant. telegraphs, London, 1902; dep., P.M.G., N. Nigeria, 1912; ag. P.M.G., Apr. to Aug., 1912; R.E. (signals), 1914-18; offr. in charge, civ. tels., occupied enemy territory admstr., Palestine, 1919; P.M.G., Palestine, July, 1920.

HUGGARD, WALTER CLARENCE, K.C., B.A., LL.D.—B. 1884; ed. at Dublin Univ.; 1st honour-man and senr. mod. in legal and polit. science; called to the bar, King's Inns, Dublin, 1907; station mag., Nigeria, Apr., 1914; ag. puiane judge, July to Sept., 1914; pol. mag., Jan., 1917; solr.-gen., Trinidad, Nov., 1920; ag. puiane judge, June-Oct., 1921; K.C., 1921; ag. atty.-gen., Jan.-Oct., 1922; atty.-gen., 1st Nov., 1922; prepared revd. edn., Laws of Trinidad and Tobago, 1925; atty.-gen., Kenya, Feb., 1926; ag. col. sec. and gov't. dep., Aug., 1927.

HUGGINS, JOHN, M.C. (1916).—B. 1891; ed. Bridlington Gram. Schol. and Leeds Univ., B.A.; 2nd lieut., Yorks Regt., Nov., 1914; Gallipoli, 1915; Egypt, 1916; France, July 1st, 1916; capt., Sept., 1916; ment. in desp.; cadet, F.M.S., 27th Mar., 1920; govt. vice agt., Klang, 24th May, 1920; ag. asst. dist. offr., Kuala Lipis, 7th July, 1920; agt. to food contr., Selangor and Pahang, 7th Oct., 1920; passed exam. in law, Mar., 1921; 4th asst. sec. to govt., 9th Mar., 1921; passed final exam., Malay, June, 1921; dist. offr., Kajang, 24th Aug., 1921; offr., cls. IV., Aug., 1923; recvr., Alsagoff concession, Johore, Feb., 1925; ag. 2nd asst. sec. (A), S.S., Feb., 1926; pvtc. sec. to high commnr., May, 1926.

HUGHES, JOHN WHITEHOUSE WARD.—B. 1883; ed. Oakham and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1906; M.A., 1919; cadet, F.M.S., 1906; ag. supt., inland rev., Kelantan, Dec., 1910; mag., Kota Bharu, Sept., 1911; ag. dist. offr., Temerloh, Oct., 1914; ag. asst. adviser, Kelantan, Jan., 1919; ag. collr., land rev., Penang, Oct., 1921; ag. dist. offr., Kuantan, Oct., 1922; ag. Br. adviser, Perlis, June, 1923; offr., cls. II, Dec., 1923; 1st mag., Kuala Lumpur, Apr., 1926; ag. dist. offr., Klang, May, 1927; 1st mag., Kuala Lumpur, June, 1927; ag. comsnr., lands, S.S., Aug., 1927.

HUGHES, LESLIE DONALD CHARLTON, M.C.—B. 1890; B.A., Oxon; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1914; attached to Colombo Kachcheri, Dec., 1914; attached to chief censor's office, Apr., 1915; attached to Badulla Kachcheri, Oct., 1915; ag. office asst. to govt. agt., Prov. of Uva, March, 1916; on military duty, 1917-19; asst. dir. of food prodn., Apr., 1920; pol. mag., Jaffna, Mar., 1921; asst. govt. agt., Batticaloa, Nov., 1921; ag. dist. judge, Badulla, Mar., 1923; asst. attmlt. offr., Nov., 1923.

HUGHES, BRIG.-GEN. ROBERT HERBERT WILFORD, C.B. (1921), C.S.I. (1918), C.M.G. (1917), D.S.O. (1916), Officer Legion of Honour (1917)—B. 1872; cadet, H.M.S. "Conway," 1887-88; merchant service, 1887-1898; Bd. of T. master mariner's certif.; sub-lieut., R.N.R., 1896; lieut., 1904; comdr., 1914; capt., 1919; served in S. African war, in S.A. Light Horse, Bathune's M.I., and Royal Navy, from 12th Dec. 1899 to 1st May, 1902; marine offr., S. Nigeria, 27th March, 1903; qualified in surveying at R.G.S. in 1906; elected a younger Brother of Trinity House, 1909; senr. marine offr., 1st Jan., 1909; ag. marine supt. and harbr.-mast., Lagos, 1st July to 16th Dec., 1906; commanded Nigerian govt. yacht "Ivy" as armed auxiliary under the R.N. in the Cameroon Expdn., Aug., 1914-Jan., 1916; Trinity House pilot in N. Sea, Apr.-June, 1916; seconded to Army as Major, R.E., 19th June, 1916; lieut.-col., Sept., 1916; brig.-gen., May, 1917; dir. Inland Water Transport, Mesopotamia, till Apr. 1921; demob., 13th Apr. 1921; dep. dir. of marine, Nigeria, Aug., 1921; dir. of marine, Oct., 1921.

HUGHES, Rt. Hon. WILLIAM MORRIS, P.C. (1916).—B. 1864; M.L.A., New South Wales, 1894-1901; elect. to 1st H. of R., C. of A., 1901, and member since; min. for external affairs, 1904; representative of C. of A. at Navigation Conference, London, 1907; atty.-gen., C. of A., Apr., 1910-1913, and from Sept., 1914, to Oct., 1915; prime min., and atty.-gen., 27th Oct., 1915; ditto, 14th Nov., 1916; ditto, 17th Feb., 1917; ditto, 10th Jan., 1918; resigned, Feb., 1923.
HUMPHERSON, LESLIE HERBERT.—B. 1902; ed. King Edward's Schl., Birmingham and Worcester Colls., Oxford; asst. dist. commr., Sierra Leone, 7th Jan., 1925.

HUMPHREYS, JOHN LISSETTER, C.B.E. (1926).—B. 1881; ed. at Bromsgrove Schl. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford (senior class scholar, 1900); 1st cls. class, mod. 1902; B.A., 1904; cadet, S. Stitts, Oct., 1905; dep. coll. of land rev., and reg. of deeds, Singapore, May, 1906; ag. dist. off., Alor Gajah, Malacca, Nov., 1906; passed final exam. in Malay, passed cadet, July, 1907; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, Dec., 1909; ag. dist. off., Dindings, Mar., 1910; seconded for serv. in Johore as coll. and mag., Batu Pahat, Feb., 1911; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, Nov., 1912; ag. dep. registrar, sup. ct., Singapore, Mar., 1913; also asst. supt. of Indian immigrants; seconded for serv. in Johore as asst. advsr., Muar, Feb., 1915; ag. Brit. agt., Trengganu, Nov., 1915; British Adviser, Trengganu, May, 1919; staff, July, 1924 (reco. thanks of S. of S. for services in connection with abolition of debt slavery); ag. Br. adviser, Kedah, May, 1925; gov., Br. N. Borneo, Sept., 1926.

HUMPHREYS, H. L.—Ed. at gram. schl.; Antigua; senior Camb. certif. 3rd div. honours, cashier, treasy., Antigua, Jan., 1892; 2nd indoor offr., 1896; ag. 1st indoor offr. on several occasions; ag. ch. audit clk., Leeward Is., Dec., 1897, to Jan., 1898; ag. audit clk., St. Kitts-Nevia, Dec., 1903, to Mar., 1904; 1st indoor offr. of treasy., Antigua, Apr., 1904; ag. ch. audit clk. and federal acctnt., Leeward Is., May to Oct., 1909, Aug., 1910, and from Apr., 1911 to July, 1912; ag. treasur. of Antigua, and federal treasur. from Dec., 1912 to Apr., 1913.

HUNT, ATLEE ARTHUR, C.M.G. (1910).—B. 1864; ed. at Balmain pub. schl. and Sydney; Gram. schl.; entd. lands dept., New S. Wales, 1879; resigned in 1887 to study for the bar; admitted to the bar, 1892, and practised until 1900; priv. sec. to first prime min. of C. of A., Jan., 1901; sec. and permanent head of dept. of external affairs, C. of A., since 1901; attended Imperial Conference, 1907 and 1911; now arbitr., federal pub. service.

HUNT, BERTIE LEONARD.—B. 1883; ed. privately and Queen's Coll., London; trooper, 5th batt. Australian Commonwealth Horse, S. African War, 1901-02; Ca. e Mounted Rifles, 1902-03; B.S.A. Co.'s mounted pol., 1904; various clkships, N. Rhodesia, 1907-10; acc. nt. P.W.D., July, 1910; acctnt., London office, B.S.A. Co., May, 1913; seconded to B.S.A. Co.'s comm. branch, N. Rhodesia, 1914-21; acctnt., N. Rhodesia, Sept., 1921; s.c., d.p.t. of lands, Apr., 1924.
HUNT, HENRY AMBROSE.—B. 1866; F.R. Met. Soc.; meteorologist, C. of A., 1906.

HUNTER, Lieut.-Gen. Sir ARCHIBALD, K.C.B. (1898), D.S.O. (1886).—Ed. at Glasgow Acad. and Sandhurst; 4th King's Own Royal Lancaster, 1874; capt., 1882; lieut.-col., 1888; col., 1894; maj.-gen., 1896; served in Egypt, and twice wounded; gov. of Dongola Prov. and commdt. Frontier Field Force, 1896-99; gov. of Omdurman, 1899; Egyptian medal and her orders; in command of 1st cls. dist. in India; lieut.-gen. on staff, commanded 10th div. S. Africa, 1900-01 (twice ment. in desps.); commanded in Scotland, 1901-3; Western Army Corps, India, 1904-7; commanded Southern Army, India, 1907-9; gov. and commr.-in-chief, Gibraltar, 1910-1913.
HUNTER, SIR GEORGE, K.T. BACH. (1921).—B. 1860; mem., N.Z. parlmt., 1896-99, and since 1911.
HUNTER, HON. GORDON.—B. 1863; ed. Bradford Coll. Inst. and Toronto Univ.; called to the bar, Ontario, 1888; removed to B. Columbia, 1891; ch. just., sup. ct., B. Columbia, 1902.

HUNTER, JAMES BLAKE, B.A. (University of Toronto).—B. 1876; clk., priv. coun. off., Canada, 1899; transf. to P.O. dept., 1900; marine and fisheries, 1901; pub. wks., 1902 ch. clk., 1907; deputy min. of pub. wks., 1908.
HUNTER, JOHN, B. B. 1878; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. and assigned to the C.O., Apr., 1899; clk. to patronage comttee., Nov., 1907.

HUNTER, JOHN ADAMS.—B. 1890; ed. at Royal Grammar Schl., Newcastle-on-Tyne and St. John's Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1913 (hist. tripus ola, 2, I.); cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1914; ag. supervisor, cust., Port Dickson, Dec., 1915; ditto, Port Swettenham, Aug.-Dec., 1917; in India studying Tamil, 1918; offr., cl. V., 26th Sept., 1918; asst. dist. offr., Klang, 5th Oct., 1918; and to chief sec.'s office, Jan.-Mar., 1919; seconded for serv. in food control dept., 26th Mar., 1919; ag. 3rd asst. sec. to Govt., F.M.S., 7th Oct., 1920; 4th asst. sec. to Govt., 3rd Oct., 1921; offr., cl. IV., offr. dep. contr., lab., Seremban, Nov., 1921; ditto, Kuala Lumpur, July, 1922; on spl. duty, rubber census, July, 1923; offr. in ch. govt. toddy shops in addn., July, 1923; 3rd asst. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Nov., 1923; dist. offr., Ulu Langat, Oct., 1926; offr., Class III., Nov., 1926; ag. sec. to res., Selangor, July, 1927.
HUNTER, K. L.—Asst. dist. commr., E. Africa Prot., Apr., 1919.

HUNTER, LOUIS LUCIEN.—B. 1889; cadet, local div., Ceylon civ. ser., Sept., 1914; attached to Colombo Kachcheri, Sept., 1914; addtl. commr. of requests and pol. mag., Ratnapura, Oct., 1915; addtl. commr. of requests and pol. mag., Panadura, Sept., 1916; office asst. to govt. agt., N.C. Prov., Aug., 1918; pol. mag., Chilaw, Sept., 1919; addnl. asst. govt. agt., Colombo, Feb., 1922; asst. stltm. offr., Jan., 1923.
HUNTER, THOMAS GILBERT, R.S.M.—B.A., 1873; inspr. of mines, Ceylon, June, 1900; ag. prin. min. survr. in addn., Mar., 1921 to Jan., 1922.

HUTCHINGS, HUGH H.—Inspr. of govt. schls., Turks and Caicos Is., 1898-1906; J.P., 1901; ch. offr., fire brigade, 1901-19; clk. to commr. and inspr. of schls., 1907; asst. commr., Grand Turk, 1908-19; ag. audr., 1908-13; audr., 1913-19; ag. commr. and ag. judge of sup. ct. five occasions, 1911-19; ag. commr. of Cayman Is., 1912-13; commr., Cayman Is., June, 1919.

HUTCHINS, ERNEST ELWIN.—B. 1876; ed. Exeter Schl.; forestry course, Edinburgh Univ., 1903-04; forest dept., S. Africa, 1904; forest de-marcator, Kenya, 1909; asst. co-servator, forests, Kenya, 1911; E. Africa Mounted Rifles, 1915-6; asst. polit. offr., German E. Africa, 1916-21; admstive. offr., 1st grade, Tanganyika Territory, 1921.

HUTCHINSON, GEORGE STUART MENTETH.—B. 1883; ed. United Services Coll., Westward Ho! and Royal Mily. Coll., Sandhurst; 2nd lieut., unattd. list, 1902; Indian Army, 1903; capt., 1911; major, 1917; Burma Mily. Pol., Southern Shan States, 1910-14; Indian Expedy. Force, France, 1915; ment. in desps., 1915; invalided, 1917; examining offr., Miny. of Pensions, 1917; regional wounds offr. for Scotland, 1919; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, asst. prin. C.O., 1st Apr., 1924.

HUTCHISON, DONALD MAXWELL WRIGHT, B.Sc. (Eng.), M.I.C.E., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1882; ed. Wellington Coll. and Faraday House, London; asst. elec. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., 1911; ch. inspr., electy. enact., F.M.S., 1915; dep. ch. engrnr., electl. bd., P.W.D., Jan., 1925; ag. ch. elec. engrnr. and inspr., electl. bd., P.W.D., F.M.S., June, 1926.

HUTSON, SIR EYRE, K.C.M.G. (1922), C.M.G. (1911), B.A.—B. 1864; 2nd clk. to gov. Leeward Is., Oct., 1888; asst. priv. sec. to gov. Barbados, Nov., 1888; priv. sec. to gov. Barbados, Mar., 1887; ditto at Mauritius, Jan., 1890; at Br. Guiana, July, 1893; Jamaica, 1893; apptd. col. sec. and regisr.-gen., Bermuda, July, 1901; chrmn., schools comsn., 1902; chrmn., St. George's harbour coman., 1903; col. sec., Fiji, July, 1908; chrmn., educn. coman., Apr., 1909; represented Fiji and Western Pacific high coman., at wireless tel. conf., Melbourne, Dec., 1909; dep.-gov., May and Oct., 1909, Sept., 1910, Mar., Sept., Oct., 1911, and June, 1912; comanr. of currency, Dec., 1914; ag. gov. of Fiji and high comanr. for Western Pacific, 13th Aug., 1915, to 25th May, 1916, and from 19th June to 9th Oct., 1918; gov., B. Honduras, 26th Nov., 1918; gov., Fiji and high comanr., W. Pacific, 1925.

HUTTON, FREDERICK AUGUSTUS.—B. 1862; ed. Bedford Public Schl., S. African Coll. and Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; B.A., 1883; LL.B., 1892; called to the bar, supreme ct., Cape Colony, 1892; prosecutor, special treason ct., 1901-3; judge, supreme ct., assigned to E.D. local divn., 1913; mem., special treason ct., 1915-16; mem. of rebellion enquiry coman., 1916-17; judge, Griqualand West Local Divn., Feb., 1923.

HYAMSON, ALBERT MONTEFIORE, F.R.Hist.S.—B. 1875; ed. Oxford High Schl. and Swansea Gram. Schl.; savings bank dept., G.P.O., London, 1895-98; supplementary estab., secr.'s dept., G.P.O., 1898 to Jan., 1921; dept. of infrn., F.O., Dec., 1917 to Mar., 1918; in charge of sec., miny. of infrn., Apr., 1918 to Nov., 1918; Br. war mission, Nov., 1918 to Mar., 1919; asst. dir., immigrn. and travel, Palestine, Jan., 1921; contrlr. of lab., Dec., 1921; in charge of dept. of immigrn. and travel, Dec., 1923 to Mar., 1924; contrlr., permits, Apr., 1924; sec., cent. town planning coman., Sept., 1922; mem., economy comttee., chmn., comttee. on rates of wages for govt. employees; author, "A History of the Jews in England," "Palestine—the Rebirth of an Ancient People," etc.
