Indies, Dec., 1896; 1st class clk., June, 1897; sent to Washington on special service in connection with W. Indian reciprocity negotiations, 1898; col. sec., Jamaica, 1900-1904; ag. gov., 1900, 1902, and 1904; returned to C.O. as a prin. clk., Oct., 1904; gov. of Jamaica, 20th Apr., 1907; assumed govt., 16th May, 1907; represented West Indian Colonies at the Coronation of H.M. King George V., 1911; perm't. sec., bd. of agriculture and fisheries, 1913; mem. of comtee. for settm. of ex-service men within the Empire, 1917; asst. comptroller and auditor, exchequer and audit dept., 1917; S. of S. for India, 23rd Jan. to 7th Nov., 1924.

O'MALLEY, SIR EDWARD LOUGHLIN, KT. BACH. (1891).—B. 1842; ed. Trin. Coll., Camb. grad. B.A., 1864; M.A., 1868; called to the bar (Mid. Tem.), 1866; joined Norfolk circuit; atty.gen. of Jamaica, 1876; atty.-gen., Hong Kong, Nov., 1879; ch. just., S. Sttlmts., 1889; ret., 1892; ch. just., Br. Guiana, 1895; judge of H.M. sup. consular ct. for the Ottoman empire, 1897; ret. 1904; mem. of Mauritius royal comm., 1909.

OMAN, DAVID JAMES, O.B.E.(1924), V.D.(1923).—Headmaster, Accra govt. schl., Gold Coast, 22nd Mar., 1902; acted frequently as inspr. of schls., 1902-1906; inspr. of schls., 1st Jan., 1907; acted as dir. of educn. on several occasions, 1907-1911; dir. of educn. and sec. to bd. of educn., 15th Apr., 1911; capt., Gold Coast Volunteers, Oct., 1914; Col. Aux. Offr.'s Decoration, Nov., 1919; offr. commdg. Gold Coast Volunteers, 1st May, 1922; sent by govt. to visit schls. and insts. in U.S.A., Sept.-Dec., 1921; extraordinary mem., leg. coun., 27th Feb., 1922 and in 1924-25; recd. thanks of S. of S. and gov. for work in connection with gen. scheme of educn. in Gold Coast, Ashanti and N. Territories, 1925; offl. mem., leg. coun., 8th Apr., 1926.

O'MORCHOE, CAPT. ARTHUR DONEL MACMURROGH.—B. 1892; ed. St. Andrew's Coll. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined Leinster Regt. (Royal Meaths), Aug., 1914; served in the great war; regtl. signalling offr., 1915; astt. adjt., 1916-17; A.D.C., maj. gen. Hon. E. J. Montague, Stuart-Wortley and camp commod., 65th divn., 1917-18; provost marshall's dept., 1918-22; junr. pol. offr., Zanzibar, Jan., 1924; suplt., pol., Feb., 1926; ag. supt., C.I.D., Nov., 1925 to May, 1926.

ONGLEY, FREDERICK OLIVER JONES.—Cadet, R.N., 1875; clk. Cyprus high ct. just. and commr.'s office, 1879; ch. sec.'a, 1884; aud.'a, 1886; rec-gen.'a, 1886; passed higher standard Turkish, 1886; transld. to high commr., 1889; acct. imp. bank of Persia, 1890; ch. clk. custs., 1890; transld. Ott. land code, 1892; astt. to dir. of surv. and prin. for. offr., 1892 (now styled astt. reg.-genl. and inspr.); ag. commr., Kyrenaica, 1893; again, and loc. comdt. and cornr., 1898; ag. astt. to loc. comdt., Nicosia, 1897; ag. reg.-genl., 1899; registr.-gen., Cyprus, 10th June, 1910; dir., land registries, Palestine, 29th Jan., 1921.

ONRAET, R. H. de S.—Pol. probationer, S. Sttlmts., Dec., 1907; passed final exam. in Hokkien, July, 1910; ag. astt. supt. of pol., Singapore, Aug., 1910; passed final exam. in Malay, Aug., 1911; astt. supt. of pol., July, 1912; ag. supt. of pol., Singapore, Jan., 1919; astt. supt. of pol., Singapore, Mar., 1919; ag. supt. of pol., Malacca, Nov., 1919; ag. dir., criminal intelligence, Apr., 1921.

OR'RIEY, BASIL VICTOR.—B. 1870; clk., customs dept., Port Elizabeth, 1889; ch. clk., Port Elizabeth, 1908; prin. clk., Cape Town, 1912; ch. clk., Cape Town, 1914; ag. colr. of customs and ag. shipping master, Cape Town, 1914; ch. clk., head office, Pretoria, 1915; dep'y-commr. of customs and excise, Pretoria, 1918; del. to the Imp. Cust. confec., London, 1921; commr., cust. and excise, 17th Aug., 1926.

ORMSBY-GORE, RT. HON. WILLIAM GEORGE AKTUR, P.C. (1927).—B. 1886; ed. Eton and New Coll., Oxford (B.A.); late capt., Shropshire Yeomanry; intell. officer Arab bureau, 1916-17; astt. sec., War Cabinet, 1917-18; astt. polit. offr., Palestine, 1918; British mem., Permanent Mandates Comsn., League of Nations, 1921-22; M.P., Stafford since Jan., 1910; accompanied C.O. mission to British West Indies, 1921-22; parly. under sec. of state for Colonies, 1st Nov., 1922 to 23rd Jan., 1924 and since Nov., 1924; chmn. parly. comm. to E. Africa, 1924; apptd. by S. of S. to visit and rept. on W. African dependencies, Jan., 1926.

ORR, MAJOR SIR CHARLES WILLIAM JAMES, K.C.M.G. (1928), C.M.G. (1921).—B.1870; ed. Bath Coll. and Woolwich Acad.; 1st comm. Royal Artillery, Feb., 1889; capt., Sept., 1899; maj., 1904; ret., Sept., 1908; served with Chitral relief force, 1895 (medal with clasp); Indian frontier, 1897 (clasp); Tirah expeditn. force, 1897-99 (clasp); China war, 1900-01 (medal); S. African war, 1900-02 (medal, 2 clasps); 3rd cls. res., N. Nigeria, Feb., 1903; 2nd cls. res., 1904; 1st cls. res., 1908; ag. sec. to admtnr., N. Nigeria, May to Sept., 1907; ch. sec., Cyprus, Feb., 1911; admtnr., govt., July to Oct., 1911, and Apr. to Aug., 1913; ret. on pension on account of ill health, Feb., 1917; attd. W.O., Int. branch, 1918-19; col. sec., Gibraltar, June, 1919; ag. gov., on several occasions; gov. and commdr.-in-ch. of the Bahamas, 8th Dec., 1926.
ORR, JAMES RUSSELL, O.B.E.(1924).—Director of educn., E.A.F., Mch., 1911.
ORR, WILLIAM CHARLES LOVE, B.Sc., M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1899; enlisted, Aug., 1918; demob., Feb., 1919; vety. offr., Gold Coast, 3rd Feb., 1926.

OSBORNE, MAJOR D. R.—B. 1879; ed. Prince Alfred Coll., Adelaide, S. Australia, Adelaide Univ. (mental and moral phil.) and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; B.A., law; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1909; practiced at bar until 1914; joined Inns of Court O.T.C., Aug., 1914; 2nd lieut., 1914; served in France with Northumberland Fus. as major and coy. commdr., 1915-17; senr. offr.' schl., Aldershot, Oct. Dec., 1917; 2nd in commd., 9th Northumberland Fus., Dec. 1917-Apr., 1918; commdg., 15th R. Scots until the 34th divn. was disbanded; ct. marital offr., Nov., 1918 to May, 1920; in Ireland as A.L.O. until Apr., 1922; stip. and circuit mag., Bahamas, 25th June, 1923; ag. ch. just., 19th Aug. to 15th Sept., 1923 and from 29th July to 10th Nov., 1924; ag. reg.-gen. in addn. 16th June to 28th July, 1924.

OSBORNE, GEORGE HAROLD.—Ed. at Leatherhead and Pembroke Coll., Camb. (scholar); Goldsmith's exhib.; B.A. (honours) in classics, 1898; S. African War, 1901-1902; medal and 5 clasps; astt. collr., E. Africa Prot., 14th Feb., 1906; dist. comsnr., 1910; senr. comsnr. (2nd cls.), Kenya, Jan., 1924.

OSTLER, HON. HENRY HUBERT, K.C. (1925), LL.B. (1904).—B. Coll.; ed. Christ's Hosp. and Victoria Univ. Coll.; assoc. to New Zealand ch. just., 1903-06; edr., N.Z. Law Repts., 1906-10; 1st astt. crown law office and crown pros., 1910; ag. solr.-gen., 1913; in private practice, 1915-25; judge, N.Z. sup. ct., 1926; examr. in law for N.Z. Univ. for many years; fellow N.Z. Univ. senate, 1916-20.

OSWELL, HENRY THEODORE WARREN.—B. 1896; ed. Dulwich Coll. and Worcester Coll., Oxford; 2nd lieut., 13th batt. Cheshire Regt., Feb., 1915; flying offr. (pilot), R.F.C., June, 1917; lieut., R.F.C., July, 1917; lieut., R.A.F., Apr., 1918; cadet, Malayan civ. serv., 1st Apr., 1920; ag. astt. contr. of lab., Klang, 1st Sept., 1920; ag. astt. dist. offr., Krian, May, 1923; astt. dist. offr., Tanjong Malim, Feb., 1925; astt. contr. lab., Seremban, Aug., 1925; offg. sec. res., in addn., Apr., 1926; offg. astt. contr., lab., Malacca, in addn., June, 1926; offr. els. IV, Apr., 1927; ag. contr., lab., Johore, July, 1927.

OTWAY, J. H.—Ck., treasy., Grenada, 1897; 2nd audit ck., 1899; audit ck., St. Vincent, 1901; audit ck., St. Lucia, Jan., 1916; sub collector and dist. offr., Soufriere, J.P. for the colony; cable censor, St. Vincent, 1918; chief ck., govt. office, and ck. of councils, St. Vincent, 918; has held numerous acting appts.

OTWAY, RUPERT COLERIDGE, O.B.E. (1925), R.E., Res. of Offrs., M.I.S.E. (1924), F.S.I. (1924).—B. 1884; ck., P.W.D., Grenada, 1903; land offr., land stlmt. scheme, and pub. wks. offr., Carriacou, 5th Oct., 1906; ward. of the Windward dist., St. Vincent, in charge of pub. wks., 1st July, 1908; offr. in charge of inauguration of land stlmt. scheme, Union Is., St. Vincent Grenadines, 1st June, 1910 to 30th Sept., 1911; ag. ward., Leewards dist., St. Vincent, and local head of the P.W.D., St. Vincent; joined Imp. army, 1st July, 1917; lieut., Royal Engnr., 15th Dec., 1917 to 23rd Oct., 1919; inspr., wks. and roads, Montserrat, 26th Feb., 1920; mem., exec. coun., Montserrat, 2nd May, 1922; mem., leg. coun., Montserrat, Dec., 1922; ag. comanr., 14th Aug. to 5th Sept., 1924.

OWEN, A. H., B.A. (Camb.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M. and H. (Camb.), Cert. of S.T.M. (Lond.)—Med. offr., Uganda, Oct., 1912; sp. serv., (venereal diseases), Uganda, 1912; active serv., E.A. Campaign, and tempy. capt., U.M.S., Jan., 1915 to May, 1918; ment. in desps.; senr. med. offr., Uganda, Jan., 1921; ch. sanitation offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1922.

OWEN, GEORGE DOUGLAS.—B. 1887; ed. Highgate Schol. (Eng.); entd. civ. ser., Trinidad, 16th Jan., 1907; 3rd ck., col. sec.'s office, Aug., 1915; asst. sec. local defense comttee, 1914-1915; ag. sec., ditto, 1915-1916; see. to educn. comanr., June, 1914 to Nov., 1916; sec. West Indian Court of Appeal Confece, 1916; priv. sec. to Sir S. W. Knaggs in 1910, 1912 and 1916; to Sir G. R. Le Hunte, 1913-1915; to Sir J. R. Chancellor, 1916, 1920 and 1921; served with H.M. Forces, 1916-19; ch. and conf. ck., col. secretariat, and ck. of legis. coun., 1st May, 1920; priv. sec. to Sir T. A. V. Best, 1921 and to Sir S. Wilson, 1922-24; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec. for several periods since 1921; asst. col. sec., ck. to exec. coun., combined ct. and ct. of policy, Br. Guiana, Feb., 1925; ag. col. sec., Oct. to Nov., 1925, Apr. to July, 1926 and from Sept., 1927.
OWEN, GEORGE HUMPHREY RICHARD ST. JOHN.—Cadet, Tanganyika Territory, 1920; 2nd grade admstve. offr., 10th July, 1922.

OWEN, HAROLD ALBERT.—B. 1892; entd. crown agt.'s office, Dec., 1911; seconded as 3rd ck., col. sec.'s office, Antigua, July, 1916; sec. to comanr. of enquiry into condition of St. John's fire brigade, Nov., 1916; returned to crown agt.'s office, Sept., 1919.

OWEN, JOHN HENRY, D.S.O. (1917).—Younger brother, Trinity House, London; master mariner; ed. Univ. Coll. Schol., London and H.M.S. "Worcester"; served in war, 1914-18; ieut., naval reserve; port mine-sweeping offr., Jamaica, Oct., 1918; habmr. and naval agt., Kingston, Jamaica, Jan., 1919.

PAGE, HON. EARLE CHRISTMAS GRAFTON, M.B., Ch.M. (Syd.).—B. 1880; ed. Sydney High Schol. and Sydney Univ. (George Allen Maths. scholarship and John Harris Scholarship for anatomy and physiology); served in European War with A.I.F. as surg. specialist to No. 3 Australian casualty clearing station, France, and No. 3 Australian gen. hosp., Egypt; mayor of S. Grafton, N.S.W., 1918; pres. N. Coast Development League, 1918; el. mem., H. of R., C. of A., for Cowper, Dec., 1919; treas., C. of A., 1923.

PAGE, CAPT. JOHN CAMPBELL.—B. 1893; ed. King's Schol., Canterbury, and Keble Coll., Oxford; B.A. (Oxon.); 2nd lieut., Buffs, Aug., 1914; on active serv. in France, 1915-19; M.C., Oct., 1916; passed progress exam. in Mendi; asst. dist. comanr., S. Leone, 27th Aug., 1916; ag. dist. comanr., Moyamba dist., 1922.
PAGE, SIDNEY MAYNARD.—Transvaal constab., Oct., 1900; ck., mgte. off., July, 1902; asst. mgte., Mar., 1913; mgte. Mar., 1918; pub. serv. inspr., Nov., 1924.

PAILTHORPE, WULFRED ARTHUR BOYNTON, B. 1875; ed. King Edward VI Schol., Chelmsford, and Gray's Coll., Essex; serv., S. African War, 1899-1902 (Queen's med. and 6 claps, King's med. and 2 claps); registrn. ck., E.A.P., 1904; registr. of docs., 1910; collr. under Stamp Act, 1910; ex-officio agt. for admstr.-gen., 1911; ag. prin. registr. of docs., crown lands, etc., 1915-19; ch. controlling rev. authority, 1918; dep. registr.-zen. of titles, Kenya, 1919; prin. registr. of docs., crown lands and land titles, 1920.

PALETHORPE, RONALD FLINT.—B. 1891; ed. Hereford Schol. and Keble Coll., Oxford; asst. mast., St. George's Schol., Windsor Castle, 1913; asst. dist. comanr., E.A.P., 5th Aug., 1914; ag. dist. comanr., Kenya, 1926.

PALMER, ARTHUR, B.A.I., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1881; ed. St. Columba's Coll. and Dublin Univ.; asst. engr., Cape gov't rly., Mar., 1903; ditto, Br. N. Borneo state rly., Feb., 1906; ditto, F.M.S. rly., Oct., 1907; dist. engr., F.M.S. rly., Dec., 1912; senr. dist. engr., F.M.S. rly., July, 1921; sec. to gen. man., F.M.S. rly., Nov., 1923.

PALMER, HAROLD.—B. 1895; apptd., after compet. exam., ck. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser.; apptd. to national health insurance audit dept., 13th Jan., 1913; on military service, 2nd May, 1918, to 26th Feb., 1920; assigned to C.O., 8th Mar., 1920.

PALMER, HAROLD THORNBURY, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M. (Liverpool); Guy's Hospital; asst. med. supt., Indo-European telegraph dept., Persia, and residency surg., Mar., 1903; med. offr., W.A.M.S., Gold Coast, 18th Aug., 1906; tempy. capt., Cameroons expeditionary force, Sep., 1915.

PALMER, HERBERT RICHMOND, C.M.G., C.B.E.—Ed. Oundle Schol. and Trinity Hall, Camb. (class. schol.), M.A., LL.B.; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1904; asst. res., N. Nigeria, 1st Oct., 1904; comanr., native rev., N. Nigeria, 1911; rev. mission to Anglo-Egyptian Sudan, 1912; supervisor, native rev., Nigeria, 1914; ag. res., Kano Prov., Nigeria, 1916-16; res., Bornu Prov., Nigeria, 1917; visited Anglo-Egyptian Sudan from Bornu via Wadai and Darfur in connection with educn. proposals, 1918; ag. ieut.-gov., N. Provs., Nigeria, May-Dec., 1921; ag. res., Sokoto Prov., Apr. to May, 1922; ieut.-gov., N. Provs., Nigeria, May, 1925.
PAMPELLONE, ARMAND JOSEPH, M.B., Ch.B. Edin.—B. 1879; ent. med. serv., Trinidad, 1st Feb., 1906; dist. med. offr., 1st Aug., 1913.

PANET, LIEUT.-COL. CHARLES LOUIS.—B. 1870; ed. Ottawa Univ.; ent. civ. serv., Canada, 1889; sec., Canadian defence comttee, 1898; priv. sec. to late Sir F. W. Borden, min. of militia and defence, 1904-12; sec., dept. of militia and defence from 1907; pres., bd. of enquiry, re claims of applicants for the fenian raid volunteer bounty, 1912-15; pres. of pensions and claims bd.; ment. in desp. for serv. rendered in Can., 1914-1918.

PARISH, CAPT. GODFREY CHARLES BRIAN.—B. 1897; ed. Church House Sch., Windsor; served in R.G.A. (S.R.), 1915-19; asst. dist. commnr., Gold Coast, 1919; ag. asst. col. sec., 1920; pte. sec., 1920; ag. dist. commnr., 1921-24; ag. pte. sec. and A.D.C., 1921; dist. commnr., 1924; asst. col. sec., 1926.

PARK, ALEXANDER DALLAS.—B. 1882; ed. Waitaki Pub. Schl., Waimate High Schl. and privately; N.Z. pub. wks. dept., 1900-18; agr. dept., 1919; inspr., pub. serv. commr.'s office, 1920; sec. of marine, 1922; asst. pub. serv. commr., 1923; asst. sec., treas., 1925; served with N.Z. forces in S. Africa.
PARK, CAPT. EDWARD NIGEL, M.C.—B. 1889; ed. Aldenham Schl.; Somaliland Camel Corps, 1917-20; dist. polit. offr., Somaliland, 1920; dist. commnr., Somaliland, Feb., 1922.

PARK, MAITLAND ELPHINSTONE, D.S.O.—B. 1894; capt., reg. army R. of O.; ed., S.A. Coll. Schl. and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; univ. commr., mily. serv., 1914-25; D.S.O., 1915; capt., 1916; Italian silver med., 1917; twice ment. in desps.; cadet, admv. dept., Tanganyika Territory, 17th Aug., 1926.

PARKER, JAMES T.—Cert. teacher, Leeward Is.; dist. postmr. Bethesda, 1901; schmnr., govt. boys' reformatory schl., 1903; clk. and storekeeper, P.W.D., and clk. to road bd., Antigua, 1906; 1st govt. offr., Virgin Is., 1915; inspr. of weights and measures, Virgin Is., 1917.

PARKER, WILLIAM ALSTEIN.—B. 1865; ed. Queen's coll., B. Guiana; entd. B. Guiana govt. serv., 1883; qualified as notary public and apptd., 1892; admitted solr., 1897; chief clk., and asct., adminstr.-gen.'s oice, 1897; off. recvr. and pub. trustee, 1905; commr. of affidavit; has acted at various times as crown solr., stip. mag., adminstr.-gen., and regiar. of sup. ct.; served on comm. of enquiry into working of regir.'s office, 1905.

PARKIN, HENRY CLARENCE.—B. 1874; clk. in secretariat, N.E. Rhodesia, Apr., 1899; pte. sec. to adminstr., July, 1901; clk., transport and supplies, June, 1902; contr., transport and supplies, Apr., 1905; resigned, June, 1906; reappt., N.W.R., govt. agt., Broken Hill, Mar., 1908; cust. dept., N.W.R., Jan., 1910; collr., cust., N.R., May, 1912; has acted as contr., cust., on various occasions between Nov., 1915 and Sept., 1923; ag. ch. clk., secretariat, Feb. to May, 1918; contr., cust., Sept., 1923; mem., leg. coun., J.P.

PARKINSON, A. C. C., M.A. (1910), O.B.E.—B. 1884; ed. at Epsom and Oxford; class demy, Magdalen, 1903-7; 1st cls. mods., 1905; 1st cls. lit. hum., 1907; apptd. after compet. exam., 1st cls. clk., acctnt.-gen.'s dept., Admiralty, Feb., 1908; transfed. as 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 19th Apr., 1909; sec., man. comtee, of imperial bureau of entomology; sec. to comtee. apptd. to investigate relationship between wild animals and trypanosomiasis, 1913-1914; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Lewis Harcourt, 5th Oct., 1914, to Mr. Bonar Law, 27th May, 1915, to Mr. Walter Long, 11th Dec., 1916; 1st cls. clk., 8th Dec., 1917; on military service from 16th May, 1915 to 26th May, 1919; prtn., 1st Apr., 1920; priv. sec. to Lord Milner, 1st Oct., 1920; asst. sec., 1st Oct., 1926.

PARKINSON, N. F., M. Sc., A.M.E.I.C.—B. 1890; ed., Toronto Pub. and Tech. High Schls. and Univ. of Toronto; civ. engrnr.; asst. to prov. sec. engrnr., Toronto; enlisted in Canadian engnr., Dec., 1914; transfed. as gunner to 13th batt., Canadian Field Arty; commnd. in France, Aug., 1916; wounded, May, 1917; invalided to Canada; returned overseas, Dec., 1917; discharged July, 1918; ent. dept. of soldier's civ. re-estab.; vocational br., Toronto; asst. to dir. of vocational training, Ottawa, Nov., 1918; dir. of vocational training, Jan., 1919; dep. min. of soldier's civ., re-estab., Apr., 1920.

PARNALL, JOHN.—Solr.; asst. aud., E. Africa Prot., 1915; asst. aud. in charge, Zanzibar, Nov., 1915; ag. treas., Zanzibar, 14th Sept., 1919 to 8th July, 1920; regiar. and offr. assignee, H.B.M. et., Zanzibar; regiar. of patents, designs, and trade marks, 1st Feb., 1920; ag. admstr.-gen. and regiar. of docs., Apr. to Oct., 1922; asst. admstr.-gen., Tanganyika Territory, 7th Sept., 1923; ag. admstr.-gen., 18th Oct., 1923 to 3rd Jan., 1924; admstr.-gen., pub. trustee, regiar. of docs. and land offr., Zanzibar, 31st May, 1924.

PARNS, ALFRED WILLIAM, O.B.E. (1919). LL.D.—B. 1860; graduated in law, Malta Univ. and called to bar, 1880; prof., comml. and maritime laws, Malta Univ., Nov., 1905; judge, Malta, Dec., 1906; judge of prize ct. and chmn. compensation bd. during war; el. for 5th div. in leg. assem., Aug., 1927; min. for just., 16th Aug., 1927.

PARR, HON. SIR CHRISTOPHER JAMES, K.C.M.G. (1925), C.M.G. (1914).—B. 1869; ed. Auckland Gram. Schl. and Auckland Univ. Coll.; barrister-at-law; mem., N.Z. parlmt., 1914-36; min. of educn., 1920-26; min. of health, 1926-32; min. of just., 1923-26; P.M.G. and min. of tels., 1925-26; high commnr. for N.Z. in England, 1926.
PARRY, J. H., B.A. (Cantab.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.)—Tempy. commn. in Indian med. serv., Mar., 1915 to Mar., 1919; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Mar., 1919.
PARRY, REGINALD FRANCIS SCARLETT.—Ed., St. John's Schl., Leatherhead, and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; jnr. supt., educn. dept., N. Nigeria, 28th Jan., 1920.
PARSONS, HON. HERBERT ANGAS, LL.R.—Judge of sup. ct., S. Australia, 1921.

PARSONS, LAURANCE DUDLEY, M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.).—B. 1873; res. surg. and supt., New Providence hosp., Nassau, Bahamas, Sep., 1901, to Dec., 1904; port surg., etc., Gibraltar, Jan., 1905, to Aug., 1912; med. supt., lunatic asylum, Ceylon, Aug., 1912; ag. regiar., Ceylon med. coll., in addition, Apr., 1917 to July, 1919; ag. asst. prin. civ. med. offr., Aug., 1922; ag. med. supt., gen. hosp., Colombo, Sept. to Dec., 1922.

PARTIDGE, GEORGE JAMES, B.A. (1915). M.A. (1921), Barrister-at-Law, Middle Temple (1927).—B. 1893; ed. Christ's Hospital and Cambridge; open exhib., (maths.), and Essex County Scholar, Queens' Coll., 1912-14; 2nd cls. maths. tripos, part I., 1913, B.A. (Warl., 1915; temp. comm., 1914-18; on active serv. in France and German E. Africa; 1914-15 Star; ment. in desp.; asst. polit. offr., 1st grade, provisional admstr., German E. Africa, Dec. 1918; priv. sec. to ag. admstr., Aug. to Dec., 1919; asst. sec., Oct., 1919; ag. senr. asst. sec., Apr. to Dec., 1920 and Aug., 1921 to Sept., 1923; clk. to exec. coun., Sept., 1922 to Sept., 1923; senr.-asst. sec., Aug., 1925; ag. asst. ch. sec., June, 1927.
PASCHALIS, NEOPTOLEMOS.—B. 1890; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1902; solr.-gen., Cyprus, Mar., 1927; ag. atty.-gen., Apr. to May, 1927.

PASHLEY, EDWARD REYNOLD.—B. 1869; storekeeper, petroleum warehouse, customs, Bahamas, 1895; landing waiter, 1896; senr. elk. of check staff, 1897; customs off., excise supervisor, boarding off., Tobago, 1901; landing waiter, customs, Trinidad, 1902; 5th elk. and paymr., 1907; 4th elk., 1912; 3rd elk., 1913; comptroller of customs, regis. of shipping, J.P., Bahamas, 1914; ag. rec.-gen., 7th Nov. to 16th Feb., 1919, from 19th Sep. to 12th Nov., 1919, and from 15th Mar. to 8th Apr., 1920; ch. rev. inspr., Trinidad, Mar., 1921.

PASKIN, JESSE JOHN, B.A., M.C.—B. 1892 ed., King Edwards Schl., Stourbridge and St John's Coll., Cambridge; 1st cls., maths. tripes, pt. I., 1913; on mily. serv., Aug., 1914 to Mar., 1919 (Worcestershire Regt., T.F. and Machine Gun Corps); Croix de Guerre, 1918; transf. to T.F. res. with rank of major, Jan., 1920; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, junr. higher divn. elk., miny. of transport, Jan., 1920; asst. prin., C.O., 29th Mar., 1921.

PASLEY, CLAUDE BURGOYNE.—B. 1877; ed. Clonmel Grammar Schl., Rathmines Schl., Dublin, R. Coll. of Surgeons, Ireland, Oct., 1894; prizeman, materia medica, 1896; prizeman in surgery, 1897; passed promary fellowship, R.C.S.I., 1897; licentiate of midwifery, Rotunda Hosp., 1898; licentiate of R. Colls., 1899; civ. surg., H.M. Forces, S. African War, 1900; F.R.C.S.I., 1910; med. offr., Malay States med. serv., 1912; ch. surg., F.M.S., 1921; examr. in surgery, King Edward VII Coll. of Med., Singapore, 1923; lieut., M.V.I., 1923.

PASSINGHAM, FRANK SELWOOD.—B. 1883; ed. Goldsmiths' and Morley Colls., Lond.; hons. medallist in typography, City and Guilds of Lond. Inst.; govt. printer, Mauritius, Feb., 1914; also supt., bookbinding works and stat. stores, Apr., 1914; supt. of printing, Jamaica, Mar., 1924.

PATERNERSON, HENRY SIBBALD.—B. 1890; ed. Winchester Coll. (schol.) and Magdalen Coll., Oxford (exhib.), B.A., 1913; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1913; ag. asst. collr. of land rev., K. Lumpur, June, 1914; seconded for war serv., Mar., 1915; active serv. in France and England, 1915-19; ment. in desp.; demob. with rank of major, Aug., 1919; asst. dist. offr., Kuantan, Aug., 1919; seconded as asst. adviser, Trengganu, July, 1920; supernym. offr., cl. IV., Nov., 1920; dist. offr., Kuala Lipis, Dec., 1923; ag. collr., land rev., K. Lumpur and regis., titles, Selangor, Nov., 1925; offr., cl. III., Nov., 1925.
PATRICKSON, NICHOLAS JULIAN, B.A., K.C.—B. 1867; atty.-gen., Grenada, 1st Jan., 1912; ag. ch. just., 10th Jan., 1921; ag. col. sec., 27th Sept., 1921; admstr., Grenada, 10th Oct., 1921.

PATTERSON, FRANCIS TUCKER, Dip. civ. Engng. (Bristol), M. Inst. C.E.—B. 1883; Axminster and Lyme Regis rly., 1901-03; Cape Col. water supply investgn., 1903-06; pub. wks. constrn., England, 1907-13; circlc engr., irrign. dept., Union of S. Africa, 1914-19; engr., Kamanassie irrig. bd., 1919-24; suptg. engr., irrign. dept., 1924-25; asst. dir., irrign., 1925; ag. dir., irrign., 27th Aug., 1925.

PATTERSON, JOHN, D.S.O.(1916).—B.1878; ed. at George Watson's Coll., Edin.; Scottish Horse, S. African War; asst. treas., E. Africa Prot., 1st Apr., 1905; chief paymaster, E. Africa Pay Corps (ment. in desp., 1915), finan. adviser, Br. Salvage Comsn. for E. Africa, 28th Dec., 1912; staff offr., Volunteers, 16th Feb., 1920; staff offr., war records, 1st Apr., 1921.

PATTULLO, HON. THOMAS D.—B. 1873; journalist; editor of "The Galt Reformer"; apptd. secy. to Comsnr. of the Yukon, 1897; resigned to enter business in Dawson; later, removed to Prince Rupert, where he took an active interest in municipal affairs, at one time holding the office of mayor; elec. to legis. ass., Brit. Columbia, g.e., 1916; min. of lands in Brit. Columbia cabinet, 1918.

PAUL, A. H. D.—Ed. at Bradford and Bath colls.; trooper in Matabeland mtd. pol., 1896; took part in the Jameson raid, 1896; planting in Ceylon, 1896 to 1904; trooper in Ceylon M.I.; dist. supt. of pol., N. Nigeria, 1905; asst. res. 1st Nov., 1908; ag. supt. of agric., Aug., 1911.

PAUL, ROBERT JAMES.—B. 1888; sub.-inspr., constab., Trinidad, 9th Sept., 1912; 2nd lieut., Trinidad local forces, 29th Jan., 1913; pay and quartermaster, constab., and senr. supply and transport offr., Trinidad local forces, 21st Nov., 1914, to 9th Oct., 1916, and from 10th Apr. to 31st May, 1917; ag. inspr. of constab. and capt. local forces, 15th Aug., 1915; asst. supt., fire brigade, 24th Nov., 1914, to 14th Nov., 1915, and from 1st Feb. to 31st Mar., 1919; ag. supt., fire brigade, 16th Nov., 1915, to 30th Sep., 1916; J.P. for the colony; ag. chief of police, constab. local forces, supt. of prison, St. Vincent, 4th Aug., 1919; J.P. for the colony; supt. of pol., Nyassaland, 11th Mar., 1923; ag. contr., pol. and prisons, May, 1925 to Sept., 1926; ag. ch. comsnr., pol. and ch. inspr., prisons, Sept., 1926.

PAULIN, GEORGE.—B. 1885; ed. Canterbury Coll., Univ. of New Zealand, B.Sc., 1906; B.Eng., 1908; pupilage with govt. rlys., New Zealand, with city engnr., Christchurch, and with Selwyn county engnr. and engnr.-in-chief, city of Dunedin drainage; engnr.-survr., P.W.D., N. S. Wales, 1910; dist. engnr., Fiji, 1912; res. engnr., Suva harbr. wks., Fiji, 1915.
PAWAN, JAMES LENOX, M.B., Ch.B., Edin.—B. 1887; ent. med. serv., Trinidad, 1st Jan., 1913; dist. med. offr., 8th Mar., 1916; bacteriologist and pathological regisr., 11th Nov., 1923.

PEACOCK, HON. SIR ALEXANDER JAMES, K.C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1861; mem. of Austn. Federal Convention, 1897-8; premier of Victoria, 1901-2; later chief sec. and min. for labour, Victoria; min. for educn. and labour, Dec., 1913; premier, treas., and min. of justice, 1914; min., for educn., forests and labour, Nov., 1920-27.
PEACOCK, F.—Ast. supt., pol., Kenya, Jan., 1923.

PEARCE, DR. ALEXANDER HUGH BLAXELL.—B. 1882; ed. George Watson's Coll., Edin. Univ. Trin. Coll., Dublin; L.R.C.P. & S., Edin.; L.R.F.P. & S., Glasgow (1904); L.M., Dublin (1906); D.P.H., Dublin Univ., 1918; spec. certif., London Schl. of Trop. Med.; F.R.I. Pub. Health; Fellow of Tropical Society; ag. R.M.O., Seamen's Hosps., Albert Docks; house surg., Lincoln County Hosps.; O.C., Coombe lying-in hosp., Dublin; junr. med. offr., Montserrat, 1907; D.M.O., St. Kitts; res. med. supt., Charitable Institts., Antigua, and health offr., Antigua, 1908; ag. C.G.M.O., Antigua, 1911; col. surg., Falkland Is., 1912; M.L.C. and M.E.C., Falkland Is., dormant comm. to administer govt. of Falklands; pres., bd. of health, Falklands; seconded to R.A.M.C., 1916-1918; capt., R.A.M.C.; served in France and Far East; chief med. offr., Bahamas, 1918; M.L.C.; chmn., hosp. bd., Bahamas Genl. Hosp.

PEARCE, RT. HON. SIR GEORGE FOSTER, K.C.V.O. (1927), P.C. (1921)—Senator for Western Australia in commonwealth parl.; sec. of trades coun. at Perth, 1896; pres., 1899; returned to first Commonwealth Senate, 1901; re-elected, 1906; and elected chmn. of comtees; min. of defence, C. of A., Apr., 1910 to June, 1913; at Imp. Conf., 1911; min. of defence since Sept., 1914; rep. Australia at the Washington Disarmament Conf., 1921; min. for home and territories in Bruce ministry, 1923; vice-prs., exec. coun., June, 1926.
PEARSE, ARTHUR VERNON ETTON.—B. 1891; ed. Haileybury and Clare Coll., Cambridge; war service, E. Africa, 1917-18; asst. dist. commr., Sierra Leone, 18th Aug., 1914; dist. commr., 1st May, 1926.

PEARSON, FRANCIS MEREDITH.—B. 1878; ed. Highgate Schol. and Weymouth Coll.; temp. clk., Royal Comn. on Local Taxation, 1900-01; ent. Crown Agent's office, 2nd Dec., 1901; on active service with 9th London Regt., Sept., 1914; lieut., R.A.S.C., June, 1915; capt., June, 1916; O.C., R.A.S.C., Cyprus, Nov., 1916 to July, 1917; O.C., R.A.S.C., Zagarig, Egypt, Oct., 1918 to Feb., 1919; asst. head, appts. dept., C.A., 26th Oct., 1917; dep. head, appts. dept., 1st July, 1921; dep. chmn., overseas panel for employment of ex-service men, ministry of lab., 1927.

PEARSON, JAMES RAE, C.I.E.—B. 1871; schol., St. Paul's Schol. and Clare Coll., Cambridge; ent. I. C. S., 1892; held various Indian appts., 1892-1926; ret., I. C. S., Aug., 1926; sec. for Indian affrs., Fiji, 1927.

PEARSON, JOSEPH, D.Sc. (Liverpool), D.Sc. (Manchester), F.R.S.E., F.L.S.—B. 1881; ed. Liverpool Coll. and Univ. Coll., Liverpool; B.Sc., Victoria Univ. 1st cls. hona., 1902; B.Sc., Liverpool, 1904; M.Sc., 1905; D.Sc., 1908; M.Sc., Manchester, 1920; D.Sc., 1922; dir., Colombo Museum, and marine biologist to Ceylon Govt., 11th Aug., 1910; offr. in charge, pearl fisheries; lecturer in zoology, Univ. Coll., Colombo; editor, "Spolia Zeylanica," "Bulletins of the Ceylon Fisheries," "Memoirs of the Colombo Museum," and author of numerous scientific papers on zoology and oceanography.
PEASE, J. W. K.—Asst. dist. commr., E.A.P., Apr., 1914; ag. dist. commr., Kenya, 1926.
PECK, ERIC FINCH, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.)—B. 1891; on war serv. in R.A.M.C., 1914-15; lieut., R.F.A., 1915-18; certif., L.S.T.M., 1922; dist. med. offr., Cyprus, 1922.

PEDLOW, JOSEPH HOWARD, B.A. (N.U.I.)—B. 1889; ed. Queen's Coll., Galway; cadet, S. Stltmts., Dec., 1912; attached Chinese Prot., Penang, Jan., 1913; at Amoy to study Chinese (Hok-kein), Sep., 1913; attached Chinese Prot., Singapore, Mar., 1916; attached treasy. for custodian enemy property and war loan work, May, 1916; collr. of war tax, Singapore, in addition, Mar. to June, 1918; pol. mag., Singapore, Aug., 1919; superny. offr., cls. IV., Dec., 1919; asst. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, Sept., 1920; ag. dep. treas., Singapore, Oct., 1920; ag. as ast. treas. and commr. of stamps, Penang, Nov., 1920; asst. to atty-gen., Singapore, Dec., 1920; ag. dep. treas., S. Stltmts., Jan., 1921; offg. ast. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, June, 1921; ag. ast. prot. of Chinese, Penang, 24th Oct., 1921; pol. mag., Penang, 31st Oct., 1921; mem., rent assessment bd., Penang and munici. commr., George Town, 4th Nov., 1921.
PEDRAZA, R.—Asst. dist. commr., E.A.P., Aug., 1914.

PEEBLES, MAJOR H.W., O.B.E. (1917), D.S.O. (1917).—Joined Canadian M.I., Dec., 1899; 2nd lieut., A.S.C., Sept., 1900; lieut., Nov., 1901; S. African War, Jan., 1900, to May, 1902 (severely wounded, Queen's medal and four clasps, King's medal and two clasps); res. of offrs., June, 1902; A.D.C. to Brig.-Gen. Sir W. H. Manning, K.C.M.G., C.B., Feb., 1903, to June, 1904; Somaliland campaign, Jan., 1903, to June, 1904 (twice men in desp., medal and two clasps); A.D.C. and priv. sec. to Sir B. Sweet-Escott, K.C.M.G., gov'r. of B. Hond., Oct., 1904; local capt., Nov., 1904; commdng. Belize L.I. volks., Dec., 1904; local major, 1905; asst. res., N. Nigeria, Dec., 1905 to Jan., 1907; ag. priv. sec. to Sir E. B. Sweet-Escott, K.C.M.G., gov'r. of Leeward Is., Oct., 1907; asst. res., N. Nigeria, Mar., 1908; 3rd class res., N. Nigeria, Jan., 1909; resigned on account of ill-health, June, 1909; clk. and priv. sec. to administrator, Dominica, Mar., 1913; raised and commanded Dom. Defence For., Aug., 1914; served with B.E.F. in France from Oct., 1914; ment. in desp., Jan., 1916, Jan., 1917, and May, 1917; awarded D.S.O., June, 1917; ment. in desp., June, 1919; brevet major, June, 1919; D.A.D.O.S., 3rd Army, B.E.F., June, 1916, to Oct., 1917; D.A.D.O.S., Aldershot Command, May, 1918, to 31st May, 1919; commr., Virgin Islands, July, 1919; commr., Montserrat, 25th Feb., 1922; assumed govt., 4th Mar., 1922.

PEEL, W., C.M.G. (1926), B.A.—B. 1875; ed. Queens' Coll., Cambridge, 11th wrangler, math. tripose, 1896; cadet, S. Stltmts., Oct., 1897; ag. dist. offr., Prov. Wellesley, Aug. to Dec., 1898; and from Mar., 1899; passed fin. exam. in Malay, Dec., 1899; dist. offr., Prov. Wellesley, Dec., 1901; ag. 2nd ast. col. sec., May, 1902; ag. ast. col. sec. and clk. of couns., Nov., 1902 to Jan., 1903 and in Aug., 1903; ag. ast. supt., Indian immigr., Sing., in addn., Aug., 1903; ag. 2nd ast. col. sec., Sept., 1903; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, Feb., 1905; ag. collr., land rev., Penang, Jan., 1906; dist. offr., Tampin, F.M.S., June, 1908; ag. sec. to res., Selangor, 22nd Oct., 1909; ag. dist. offr., Lower Perak, 21st Feb., 1910; ag. supt., Indian immigr., S.S. and F.M.S., 2nd May, 1910; ag. chief ast. dist. offr., Kinta and regis. of titles, South Perak, 21st Oct., 1910; seconded as pres., mun. commrs., Penang, 1st Jan., 1911; offr., Class III., but remained seconded, 1st Jan., 1912; ag. res. councillor, Penang, in addn., Feb. to Oct., 1917; pres., mun. commrs., Singapore, July, 1918; offr., Class II., F.M.S., 1st Jan., 1919; joint passage contrlr., Malaya, in addn., Feb., 1919; seconded as food contrlr., Malaya and chmn., shipping ctee., 26th May, 1919; ag. contrlr., lab., F.M.S. and S.S., 10th Nov., 1920; chmn., European unemployment commte. in addn., Feb., 1921; contr., lab., F.M.S. and S. Stltmts., 4th Feb., 1922; ag. Br. adviser, Kedah, 27th Apr., 1922; Br. adviser, Kedah, 4th Oct., 1922; res. coun., Penang, 9th July, 1925; ag. ch. sec. to govt., F.M.S., May, 1926; ch. sec., F.M.S., June, 1926; offr. administering govt., S.S. and high commrs., Malay States, May to June, 1927.

PEET, JAMES HERBERT.—B. 1882; clk., audit office, Bahamas, 1904; 4th clk., P.O., 1907; 2nd clk., C.S.O., 1909; 3rd clk., treasy. dept., 1910; ch. clk., P.O., 1913; ch. clk. and ast. examg. offr., customs, 1914; comptir. of cust., 4th Apr., 1921; ag. recvr.gen., treasy. and temp. mem., exec. coun., 20th Sept. to 8th Nov., 1925 and from 19th Apr., 1926; has held numerous other ag. appts.

PELLETIER, PHILIPPE.—B. 1849; called to the bar, Quebec, 1875; mem. of the coun. of the bar of Montreal, and sec., 1878; a fire commr. for city of Montreal, 1879 to 1887; unsuccessfully contested St. John's in provincial elections, 1886, and Beauharnois in Dominion elections, 1887; ch. clk., sec. of state dept., Canada, 1888; asst.-under-sec. of state of Canada, 1909.
PELLISSIER, S. H., B.A.—B. 1887; ed. Grey Univ. Coll.; vice prin., Boshof, 1914; prin., Ficksburg, 1917; dir., eduon., O.F.S., Dec., 1926.

PEMBERTON, ERNEST WILFRED, J.P.—B. 1874; ent. traffic dept., Cape Govt. rly., 1896; stores dept., 1899; treasy., 1903; audr.-gena. dept., 1906; inland rev. dep., 1910; ag. rec. of rev., Kimberley, 1915; rec. of rev., E. London, 1917; lieut., Cape Peninsula Regt., 1902; res. of offrs., 1913; apptd. to Pretoria Regt. from res. and served in German S.W. Africa campaign, Sept., 1914 to Aug., 1915.

PENGILLEY, ERNEST EDGAR.—B. 1897; ed. Christ's Hosp. (exhib.) and Exeter Coll., Oxford (class. schol.); lieut., 23rd Batt., Royal Fusiliers, 1917-19 (wounded and prisoner of war); cadet, F.M.S., 12th Jan., 1921; ag. asst. sec. to res., Selangor, 7th Mar., 1921; passed cadet, July, 1922; addnl. mag., Kuala Lumpur in addn., Aug., 1922; sec. Selangor rubber restric. comte., in addn., Oct., 1922; passport offr. in addn., Dec., 1922; offr., cls. V., Jan., 1924; asst. dist. offr., Rembau, Feb., 1924; attd. to C.O., Nov., 1925 to May, 1926; asst. collr., land rev., Kuala Lumpur and dep. regiar., titles, Selangor, 18th June, 1926; dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, Apr., 1927.

PENNINGFATHER, CHARLES EDWARD DE FONBLANQUE.—B. 1883; clk., Gilbert and Ellice Is. Prot., 1908; clk. of peace, 1910; sub-inspr. of constab., Fiji, 1910; inspr., ditto, 1912; dist. comsnr., 1914; ag. dist. comsnr., Rewa, and gov. comsnr., Naitasiri, 1914-15; ag. dist. comsnr., Colo East, 1917-18; ag. oh. inspr., Fiji constab., ag. ch. pol. mag., regist.-gen., regist. of sup. ct., regist. of titles, pub. trustee, regist. of companies, cust. of enemy property, and cur. of intestate and vacant estates, 1918; dist. comsnr., 1922.
PENNINGTON, ARTHUR LAWRENCE, B.A.—B. 1901; ed. Newbury Gram. Schl. and Keble Coll., Oxford; asst. dist. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 4th Sept., 1925.

PENTELOW, WILLIAM CYRIL DOUGHTY, B.A. (Cantab).—B. 1903; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1925; attd., Matara kach., Jan., 1926; ditto Nuwara Eliya kach., June, 1926; ag. office asst., Kegalle kach., Dec., 1926.

PEPPYS, WALTER EVELYN.—B. 1888; ed. at Malvern and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1907; M.A., 1910; cadet, F.M.S. civ. serv., 1908; ag. asst. dist. offr., Pekan, Apr., 1910; ag. asst. sec. to res., Perak, Jan., 1911; passed cadet, June 1911; cls. V., Jan., 1912; dist. offr., Jelebu, Apr., 1912; ag. dist. offr., Kuala Langat, June, 1913; ag. mag. cent. ct., Kelantan, and state auditor, Mar., 1914; on duty with Kelantan detachmt., Malay States Guides, 15th to 31st May, 1915; dist. offr., Pasir Puteh, 1st June, 1915; ag. upt. of lands, Kelantan, and chmn., conservancy bd., Aug. to Dec., 1917; dist. offr., Pasir Puteh, Jan., 1918; offr., cls. IV., F.M.S., 1st Jan., 1918; ag. upt. of lands, Kelantan and chmn., conservancy bd. in addition, May, 1918; ag. Br. adviser Trengganu, Nov., 1919 to Jan., 1920; ag. 2nd mag., Singapore, July, 1920; offr., class III., F.M.S., Nov., 1920; federal secretariat for sp. duty, Aug., 1921; ag. comsnr. lands, Trengganu, Mar., 1922; ag. asst. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Sept., 1922; ag. sec., res., Selangor, May, 1925; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1925; asst. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Nov., 1926.

PERCEVAL, SIR WESTBY BROOK, K.C.M.G. (1894).—B. 1864; was mem. for Christchurch City in legis. assem., of N. Zealand; chmn. of comtes., in 1890; agt.-gen. for the col. in England, 1891-96; called to bar, Mid. Tem., 1878; Roy. commr. for Chicago Exhib., 1893; agt.-gen. for Tasmania, 1896 to 1899.

PERCY, H. VANE (formerly Baumgartner).—B. 1883; ed., Bedford Grammar Schl.; clk., land settlt. dept., O.R.C., 16th Oct., 1902; clk. and typist audit dept., 2nd Dec., 1902; clk., audit dept., 1st July, 1904; examr. of acts., audit dept., 1st Jan., 1908; jnr. asst. treas., G. Coast, 1st Jan., 1914; asst. treas., 25th Apr., 1917.

PERDUE, CECIL GRAHAM.—B. 1891; ed. Trin. Coll., Dublin; police probationer, Hong Kong, Nov., 1912; passed in Chinese, 1915; attached to Punjab police, 1915-16; passed in Urdu, 1916; temp. 2nd lieut., Indian army reserve, 1916-19; ag. asst. supt. police, Hong Kong, 1919; ag. deputy supt., police, 1919 and in 1926; J.P., 1920; asst. supt., pol., 1920; asst. census offr., 1921; ag. dir., criminal intelligence, 1924; ag. dep. supt., pol., 1927.

PERDUE, HON. WM. EGERTON, B.A.—B. 1850; ed. Brampton high schl. and Toronto Univ.; called to the bar, Ontario, 1879; called to the bar, Manitoba, 1882; judge, ct. of King's Bench, Manitoba, 1903; judge, ct. of appeal, Manitoba, 1906; ch. just. of Manitoba, 1918.
PEREIRA, ROBERT JAMES.—B. 1869; apptd. to cls. V. Ceylon civ. ser., May, 1920; extra office asst., Colombo Kachcheri, May, 1920.
PERERA, BENJAMIN FRANKLIN.—B. 1901; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1925; attd., Colombo kach., Jan., 1926; ditto, Anuradhapura kach., Apr., 1926.
PERERA, CONRAD BONIFACE PETER, B.Sc. (Lond.).—B. 1903; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Feb., 1927; attd., Badulla kach., Mar., 1927.

PERERA, THEODORE DUNCAN.—B. 1895; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., local divn., Aug., 1919; attached to Kurunegala Kach., Aug., 1919; Jaffna Kach., Dec., 1919; ag. pol. mag., Puttalam, June, 1920; pol. mag., Avissawella, July, 1921; ag. pol. mag., W. Prov., Feb., 1923; asst. comsnr. of stamps, July, 1923; pol. mag., Pt. Pedro, Oct., 1926; asst. comsnr., stamps, Nov., 1926.
PEREZ, JOSEPH LEON MATHEU, LL.B.—B. 1896; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1917; dep. regiar.-gen., Trinidad, Oct., 1927.

PERKINS, ARTHUR JAMES.—B. 1871; Diplômé de l'école Nationale d'Agriculture de Montpellier, 1890; management of farm estates, Tunis, 1890-2; prof. of viticulture and oenology, S. Aust., 1892; sec. for agriculture, 1902-1904; prin. of agric. coll. and prof. of viticulture and oenology, 1904; dir. of agric., 1914.

PERKINS, CHARLES JAMES, I.S.O. (1927).—B. 1872; ed. Westbury and Melksham Grammar Schls.; dipl. in surveying, Queensland, 1896; govt. survr., Queensland, 1899-1904; survr., F.M.S., Feb., 1904; staff appt., Sept., 1904; asst. supt., 1906; supt., 1919; ag. asst. survr.-gen., F.M.S. and S.S., 1920-21; ag. survr.-gen., F.M.S. and S.S., 1920-21 and Jan., 1924; asst. survr.-gen., F.M.S. and S.S., 1922; served R. Fusiliers, Mar., 1915 to Aug., 1916; B.E.F., France; M.S.V.R., 1906-15; M.V.I., 1916; capt. and 2nd in comm., Selangor Unit; ag. offr., commdg., 1923-24; ag. survr.-gen., F.M.S. and S.S., Jan., 1924; asst. survr.-gen., F.M.S. and S.S., Oct., 1924.

PERKINS, HUGH WHARTON, C.B.E.—B. 1896; ed. Berkhamsted Sch.; on active serv., Nov., 1914; D.A.R.T., June, 1915; temp. major, July, 1916; temp. lieut.-col. and asst. dir., rly. transport, Nov., 1917; C.B.E. (mil.), June, 1918; demob., Nov., 1919; ag. traf. man., F.M.S. rly., Feb., 1925; traf. man., F.M.S. rly., Oct., 1925.

PERKINS, LEICESTER GRANT.—B. 1890; ed. privately and Harrison Coll., Barbados; clk., post office, Barbados, Jan., 1909; 5th clk., audit office, Mar., 1911; 2nd clk., registn. office, Nov., 1913; clk. to master in chancery, May, 1914; 4th clk., savings bank, Jan., 1915; 2nd-lieut., Imperial Army, 30th Mar., 1917, served during the war in Egypt and Palestine with 2nd B.W.I. Regt.; 1st-lieut., 1st Oct., 1918; 1st-clk., Barbados savings bank, Aug., 1919; 2nd grade supvr. of customs, Nigeria, Feb., 1920.

PERKINS, PHILIP HAROLD.—B. 1891; ed. Dudley Schl.; apptd. after compet. exam., elk., G.P.O., 1907; on mily. serv., 4th Aug., 1914 to 22nd Oct., 1920; comdt., German prisoners of war camp, France, 1917; dep. asst. prov. marshal, 1919; capt., reserve of officers; loaned to C.O., 2nd May, 1921; apptd. cler. offr. and assigned to C.O., 1st Aug., 1921.

PERLEY, HON. SIR GEORGE HALSEY, K.C.M.G. (1915).—B. 1857; ed. Ottawa gram. schl., St. Paul's schl., Concord, N.H., and Harvard Univ.; B.A., 1878; elec. to H. of C., Canada (Argenteuil) 1904; re-elec., 1908 and 1911; chief conservative whip, 1911; mem. of privy coun. for Canada and min. without portfolio in Mr. Borden's cabinet, Oct., 1911; ag. high commr. in London, 1914; min. of overseas mil. forces, 1916; high commr., 1917; joint commr. and plenipotentiary of Canada to sign Peace Treaties, 1919; Canadian del. to League of Nations Assembly, 1921; resigned high commrship, 1922.

PERRY, SIR ALLAN, KT. BACH. (1904); M.D. (Durham); D.P.H.(Lon.); M.R.C.S. Eng.; L.S.A. Lond.—B.1860; prin. civ. med.offr.and inspr.gen. of hosps., Ceylon; prin. of Ceylon med. coll., 10th Dec., 1897; major R.A.M.C. (retired); mem. municipal coun., Colombo; late pres. Ceylon branch Br. med. assoc.; del. for Ceylon to Internat. Leprosy Conf., Bergen, 1909; ret., 1915.
PERRY, B. C. G.—B.1886; apptd. after compet. exam., elk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. serv., and assigned to C.O., 9th Apr., 1906; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Apr., 1922.

PERRY, GEORGE McREDDIE, M.B., C.M., Glasgow, and St. Mary's hosp., London, F.R.G.S.—Asst. col. surg., Lagos, Oct., 1893; ag. col. surg., Feb. to Mar., 1894; ag. asst. col. sec., May, 1894; med. offr. to exped. to Idanai mountains; sen. asst. col. surg., Aug., 1895; ag. col. surg., 1895.

PERRYMAN, PERCY WILBERHAM, O.B.E. (1926), M.B.E. (1919), F.R.G.S.—B. 1883; ed. Eton and Balliol Coll., Oxford; asst. dist. commr., Uganda, Dec., 1908; dist. commr., Sept., 1916; senr. asst. sec., Mar., 1920; asst., ch. sec., Uganda, June, 1923.
PERSSE, CAPT. EDMUND MATURIN, M.C. (1918), F.R.G.S., R. of O., W. Yorks. Regt.—B. 1890; ed. Bedford Mod. Schl.; asst. dist. commr., Uganda, Oct., 1919; dist. commr., Apr., 1925.
PERTH, ARCHBISHOP OF, MOST REV. CHAS. OWEN LEAVER RILEY, D.D., Cantab.—B. 1864; vicar of St. Paul's, Preston, Lancs., 1885-1894; bishop of Perth, W. Aust., 1894; created archbishop, 1914.
PETERS, BENARD R., M.B.E., A.M.I.C.E.—Asst. engrnr., E.A.P., 1914-15; exec. engrnr., Tanganyika, 1919; ag. dep. D.P.W. in 1926 and 1927.

PETRIDES, PHILIP BERTIE.—B. 1881; ed. Dulwich Coll.; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1906; joined Midland Circ. and Birmingham and cent. crim. court sess., 1906; legal adviser and crown prosecutor, Seychelles, 22nd Jan., 1916; ex-officio mem. exec. and legis. couns.; ag. chief just., 23rd July, 1916, to 20th Mar., 1917, 13th May, 1918 to 8th Oct., 1918, and from 23rd Dec., 1918 to 11th Nov., 1919; temp. empd., C.O., Feb. to Apr., 1920; ch. just., Seychelles, 10th June, 1920; admstd. gov't., 13th Mar., 1921 to Sept., 1922; atty.-gen., Nyasaland Prot., 1924; ag judge, May to Dec., 1925; puisne judge, Nigeria, 1926.
PETRIE, GEORGE MELVIN.—Joined Bech. Prot. pol., June, 1910; treasy. clk., 1922; acct., 1924; ag. finan. sec., master and regisr., deeds, 1926.

PHELIPS, HUGH RICHARD.—Ed. Weymouth Coll. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; local auditor, Niger Coast Prot., 27th Oct., 1894; asst. auditor, E. Africa Prot., 9th Dec., 1896; local auditor, Uganda, 29th Apr., 1897; served in Uganda mutiny (medal and clasp); local auditor, E. Africa and Uganda rly., 26th Dec., 1901; auditor, Hong Kong, 1st Nov., 1904; J.P., 1905; hon. auditor, Hong Kong Univ., 2nd May, 1911.

PHILIPPS, JAMES ERASMUS TRACY, Capt. The Rifle Brigade (Reg. Army R. of O.), M.C. (1916), Ordre de la Couronne of Belgium (1921), B.A., B. Litt., F.R.G.S., F.R.A.I.—B. 1888; ed. Marlborough Coll., Oxford and Durham Univ., of which was under-graduate representative at the Congress of Unis. of the Empire, London, 1912; editor of Durham Univ. Journal; hon. sec., D.U.B.C.; pres. of the Union; lieut. 1912; asst. dist. commr. and addnl. dist. magis., Uganda, 1912; asst. intelligence offr., E. Africa Expedy. Force, from 8th Aug., 1914, attached K.A.R.; commanding 2,000 armed levies and scouts, from 9th Sept., 1914 (wounded and ment. in despa.); admstd. Kifumbiro (Bukoba) area of occupied territory from 5th Sept., 1914, under martial law; polit. offr. on staff of Major-Gen. J. M. Stewart, C.B., Bukoba Expedy. Forces, from 16th June, 1915 (ment. in desps.); captin., Kagera Command Staff, Jan., 1916; chief polit. offr., Uganda area and N.W. Provs., Tanganyika during operations, 17th Jan., 1916; chief intelligence offr. (G.S.O.3), on staff of the Lake Command, E.A.E.F., to Tabora, from 4th Aug., 1916; Mily. Cross, 1916; in charge of polit. mission in British and Belgian areas of conquered territory, Nov., 1916, to Mar., 1917, when invalided; emp'l. intell. duties, W.O., June to Aug., 1917; attd. Admy. War Staff, intell. div., Aug. to Oct., 1917; employed by F.O. to Abyssinia, 1918; on special duty in Sudan, 1918; raised two companies Somalias and Abyssinians for K.A.R. in French and Abyssinian territory, Apr., 1918; ag. dist. commr., Kigesi, British Ruanda, and dist. mag. Kabale, Feb., 1919; made expedit. across Africa from E. to W. via Lake Kivu through Belgian, French and Portuguese territories; Br. commr. for Russian Red Cross in Russia, 1921; Br. relief commr., S. Russia, under the L. of N., 1922; mem. of Br. comttee. for Moslem refugees, Turkey, 1922; "Times" war corres. in Greco-Turkish war, 1922; liaison offr. with French troops and with Greek forces of occupation in Turkey, 1923; mem. Greco-Bulgarian frontier enquiry, 1923; seconded to Sudan govt. as asst. dir. of intelligence, Khartoum, 1924; comnr., Tembura dist., French frontier, 1925; publications, "Pan-Islam in Africa," "Cape to Cairo developments"; "Ruanda" (1919); "The Nabingi" (1919); "A Kinya-Ruanda Vocabulary" (1920); "Ethiopianism" (1921); "The Turkish Emigration from Thrace" (1922); "The Religion of the Niam-Niam (Zande) People" (1926).
PHILLIPS, C. H., L.M.S.S.A. (Lond.).—Tempy, lieut., R.A.M.C., Jan., 1915 (General List); med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1920.

PHILLIPS, HAROLD ERNEST.—B. 1877; clk. to auditor, B. Honduras, Oct., 1892; 5th clk., treasy., customs and P.O. depts., June, 1893; 4th clk., Feb., 1894; 3rd clk., treasy. and cust. dept., 16th May, 1897; 2nd clk., June, 1898; 2nd clk., C.S.O., Sept., 1900; ag. dist. comnr., Stann Creek, 1905; ch. clk. and clk. of couns., Jan., 1906; priv. sec. to gov. Sir E. Swayne, 1909-10; to gov. Collett, 1911-13; to ag. gov., Walter, 1916; ag. comnr. of currency, 18th Feb., 1911; ag. treasr., collr. of cust., 1911-14 and 1917-20; ag. col. sec., 1920; Br. Honduras del. to Canada-W. Indies Confco., Ottawa, June, 1920; asst. col. sec., Apr., 1921; comnr. and judge, Turks and Caicos Is., 25th June, 1923.
PHILLIPS, TREVOR STANLEY, B.A.—Ed., Llandovery Coll., and Jesus Coll., Oxford; jur. supt., educn. dept., N. Nigeria, 5th Sept., 1914; attached, Nigeria Regt., 1917-18.

PHILLIPS, WILLIAM AUSTIN, M.B.E. (M.), 1919.—B. 1873; ent. C.A., 1890; ch. inspr. of stamps, 1908; dep. head, pay office, 1908; capt., 13th London Regt., 1914; major, 1917; dep. regir., C.A., 1920; dep. head, finance dept., 1921; acctnt., Palestine currency bd., 1926; head, pay dept., 1927.

PHILLIPSON, SYDNEY.—B. 1892; on mil. serv., May, 1915; cadet Ceylon civil serv., Nov., 1919; attached to Kandy Kach., Dec., 1919; ag. office asst. to gov't. agt., N.C. Prov., Apr., 1921; pol. mag., Matara, Sept., 1922; office asst., Kurunegala kach., July, 1924; asst. contr., rev., custodian, enemy property and contr., local clearing office, in addn., Jan., 1925; sec., salaries comtec., Nov., 1926.
PHOEBUS, GEORGE JUSTUS BRANDT.—B.1869; apptd., cls. V. Ceylon civ. serv., June, 1925; extra office asst. to col. sec., June, 1925.
PICKERING, G. H.—Town mag., E.A.P., 1910; ag. 2nd puisne judge, 1915; puisne judge, 1917.

PICKWOAD, HOWELL.—Ed. at Bedford Mod. schl.; clk., atty.-gen.'s off., Br. Hond., Nov., 1895; 3rd cust. offr., 12th Aug., 1898; 4th clk., treasy. and cust., and clk. to comnsrs. of currency, 27th Dec., 1901; 2nd grade cust. and excise offr., Aug., 1903; col. branch of exchequer and audit dept., 21st Oct., 1904; asst. treas., E. Africa Prot., 26th June, 1905; dep. treas., Kenya, Jan., 1922; recr. gen., Mauritius, Oct., 1923.
PICKWOAD, HUGH FREDERICK.—B. 1878; comnr. (4th div.), Rahamaa, 1909; ditto (3rd div.), 1914; ditto (2nd div.), 11th Sept., 1923.

PIENAAR, ANDRIES GUSTAF EBELAAN.—Clk. of ct., Lichtenburg, July, 1902; ag. pub. prosecutor, Feb., 1903; pub. prosecutor, June, 1903; ag. ast. res. mag., Feb., 1904; ch. clk. and ag. ast. res. mag., Nov., 1905; addnl. ast. res. mag., Krugersdorp, Sept., 1906; res. mag. and native comnr., Waterberg, Nov., 1910; res. mag., Ermelo, Apr., 1912; senr. mag., Germiston, May, 1920; pub. serv. inspr., Sept., 1921; mag., Louis Trichardt dept. of just., Aug., 1924; sec. for int., Dec., 1924; chmn., pub. serv. coman., 2nd Aug., 1926.

PIENAAR, CHARLES ISAAC.—B. 1878; ed. Gill Coll., Cape Prov.; served with Boer forces in Anglo-Boer War, 1899-1902; admitted solr., etc., 1904; ent. Transvaal law dept. as head, litigation branch, 1909; after Union became the offl. govt. atty., Union of S. Africa, 1910; seconded as S. African comnr. of commerce for continent of Europe, 1924.

PIERIS, PAULUS EDWARD, Samarasingha Sriwardhana, M.A., Trin. Coll., Camb., 1895, L.L.M., D.Litt. (Cantab.)—Barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, 1896; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., 1896; ast. to govt. agt., Colombo, 1898; pol. mag., Panadura, 1899; dist. judge, Ratnapura, 1899; ast. to govt. agt., Galle, 1901; ag. dist. judge, Matara, Mar., 1903; office ast. to govt. agt., Galle, June, 1903; ast. comnr., St. Louis exhibn., Feb., 1904; dist. judge, Kegalle, Mar., 1906; ditto, Kalutara, Mar., 1906; ag. registr.-gen., Dec., 1910; ag. dist. judge, Kurunegala, May, 1912; comnr. of requests, Colombo, Nov., 1912; ag. dist. judge, Kandy, Nov., 1913; dist. judge, Galle, June, 1914; ditto, Jaffna, Dec., 1915; ag. dist. judge, Colombo, Mar., 1919; ditto, Kandy, Apr., 1920; dist. judge, Kandy, July, 1921.
PIERS, FRANCIS A.—B. 1882; sub-inspr., Basutoland mounted pol., 1910; inspr., 1923.

PIETERSZ, JOSEPH LUCKERT.—Ent. pub. serv., Jamaica, 10th Dec., 1885; 3rd cls. clk., 1st June, 1888; 1st cls. clk., 1st Feb., 1909; senr. clk. and inspr. offr., 1st July, 1918; ch. clk., 1st Aug., 1920; dep. audr.-gen., 1st Aug., 1921.
PIGGOTT, CHARLES FRANCIS LOVELL, M.A.—B. 1890; ed. Dean Close Schl. and Univ. Coll., Oxford; ast. sec., Tanganyika Territory, 1919-22; sup't. educn., dept., N. Prov., Nigeria, 17th May, 1922.

PIGGOTT, SIR FRANCIS TAYLOR, KT. BACH. (1905).—Barrister-at-law; employed by F.O. on special service in 1887; legal adv. to prim. min., Japan, Nov., 1887; sec. to att.-gen. Sir C. Russell (afterwards Lord Russell of Killowen) during Behring Sea arbitrn., 1893-4; proc.-gen., Mauritius, 1894; ag. ch. just., 1896 to 1897; ch. just., Hong Kong, 27th Apr., 1903; ret., 1912; recd. thanks of coun. of govt. for ser. in connection with planters' loans, 1902; author of "Nationality and Naturalization," "Exterritoriality," "Extradition," "Foreign Judgments and Jurisdiction," "Law of Torts," and "Imperial Statutes applicable to the Colonies."

PILBEAM, S. A.—B. 1899; entd. C.O., Jan., 1916; passed ast. clk.'s exam., July, 1916; on military service from 18th July, 1917, to 10th Mar., 1919; assigned to C.O. as ast. clk., Apr., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan. 1920; ag. cler. offr., higher grade, 10th Oct., 1925.

PILLING, HENRY GUY.—B. 1886; ed. King's Schl., Ely, and Keble Coll., Oxford; M.A., 1911; cadet, Fiji, 1907; passed cadet, 1910; 2nd grade dist. comnr., 1914; mem., Indian Franchise Coman., 1920; ag. ast. col. sec., 1920; ast. col. sec., 1st Jan., 1921; ag. sec., W. Pacific high coman., 21st Mar., 1924; sec., W. Pacific high coman., 1926.

PINHORN, R. H., M.A., O.B.E.—B. 1871; ed. Wolverhampton Schl. and Keble Coll., Oxford; 2nd cls. hon., mod. hist., 1895; Oxf. Univ. dipl. in educn., 1911; ast. mast., R. Masonic Schl., Bushey, 1896-1904; headmaster, Free Schl., Penang, 1904; J.P. for Penang; O.B.E. "for services to education," 1923.

PINK, HERBERT JOHN.—Paymr. lieut., R.N.R.; fellow, Royal Economic Soc.; fellow, Royal Statistical Soc.; ed. Portsmouth Coll.; asst. treas., Kenya, and in charge income tax dept., 1920-23; col. treas., St. Helena, 1923; J.P., St. Helena, Feb., 1924.

PINNEY, CAPT. CHAS. ROBERT, M.C. (1917).—B. 1883; ed. Essendon Grammar Schl.; draughtsman, lands dept., Papua, 1906; on war serv., A.I.F., 1914-20; wounded; transf'd. R. of O., C.M.F., 1920; ch. draughtsman, Papua, 1920; staff survr. in addn., 1924; ag. comsnr., lands, ag. dir., mines, ag. dir., agr. and prov. mem., land bd., 1927.
PIERIE, D. A.—B. 1886; clk. Burgher camps, Transvaal, 1902; treasy., 1903; prin. clk. treasy., Union of S. Africa, 1920; sec. mily. pensions bd., treasy., 1920.

PIROW, HANS, D.Sc., M.Inst.M.M., D.I.C., vice-pres., Geol. Soc. of S.A.; mem., coun., Chem. Met. and Min. Soc., and S.A. Assoc. for the Adv. of Science.—B. 1892; ed. S. Africa and on continent; grad., S.A. and London; various offl. capacities on Witwatersrand gold mines, 1911-1919; govt. research schol., 1919-20; asst. inspr., mines, 1920; dominion sci. schol., London, 1924; G.M.E., Dec., 1926; is also chmn., mining leases bd. and elec. control bd., assessor mem., research grant bd. and statistical coun.; ag. sec., mines and industries, 1927.

PITOT, LÉON EMILE.—B. 1866; govt. survr. and supt. of pub. wks., Seychelles, 11th Apr., 1889; govt. engnr. and architect, Mauritius, Apr., 1891; gen. man. of rlyws. and engineer, Mauritius, 14th Sept., 1896.

PITT, WALTER JAMES, B.A.—B. 1875; ed. Downing Coll., Cambridge (modern and medieval language trips); ent. Middle Temple, Jan., 1919; passed Bar final exam., Dec., 1920; headmr., Cape Coast Govt. Schl., 1910; inspr. of schl., 1912; prov. inspr. of schl., 1914; attd. to polit. serv., Gold Coast, 4th Aug., 1914; asst. dist. comsnr., 25th July, 1917; dist. comsnr., 1st Jan., 1921; ag. prov. comsnr., W. Prov., Ashanti, Feb.-Nov., 1920.

PITTMAN, WILLIAM.—B. 1878; ed. S. African Coll. and Wadham Coll., Oxon; B.A. (Univ. S.A.), M.A. (Oxon); called to bar, Middle Temple, 1903; admitted to Cape (1904) and Transvaal (1905) bars; dean of faculty of law, Univ. S.A., 1918-25; K.C., 1924; judge, sup. ct., S. Africa eastern dists. local divn., 1925; mem., delimitation comsnr., 19th July, 1927.

PLACE, C. GODFREY M., D.S.O. (1917), M.C. (1916), B.A., LL.B.—B. 1886; ed. St. Columba's Coll. and Dublin Univ. (modr. and silver medallist, legal and polit. science, gold medallist, coll. hist. soc.); called to Irish Bar, 1909; asst. examr. of titles, land regisy., 1910-14; served European War, E. Surrey Regt. (ment. in des.); major, res. of offrs.; examr. of titles, land regisy. of Ireland, 1919; dist. mag., Uganda, 1921; asst. to atty.-gen., Dec., 1924; asst. atty.-gen., N. Rhodesia, 1926.

PLANT, GEORGE FREDERIC C.B.E. (1927).—B. 1877; ed. Manchester Gram. Schl. and Brasenose Coll. (scholar, 1896); 1st cl. class, moda., 1898; B.A., 1900; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., 1901; off. ast., Kurunegala, 1902; off. ast., Galle, 1904; pol. mag., Avissawella, 1905; dist. judge, Ratnapura, Apr., 1906; ditto, Matara, Dec., 1906; asst. govt. agt., Kalutara, May, 1909; temporarily employed in C.O. London, May, 1915, to Dec., 1918; sec., overseas settlement comte., Jan., 1919; permanently transferred to Home civ. serv., Sept., 1919; mem., overseas settlement delegation to Canada, 1924.

PLATTIS, HAROLD CLAYTON, M.C., B.Sc. (Eng.), Assoc.M.Inst.C.E.—B. 1899; ed. London Univ., 1916-17 and 1919-21; served H.M. Forces, 1917-19; lieut., Royal Engineers, 1918; on active serv., N. Russia; M.C., 1919; B.Sc. (Enggr.) hon., London, 1921; asst. engnr., Zanzibar Prot., 21st Feb., 1922; mem., Inst. of R. Engnr.; 1923; assoc. mem., Inst. of Civ. Engnr., 1925; ag. dist. engnr., 17th Oct., 1926; ag. A.D.P.W., 17th July, 1925 and 15th Feb., 1926; dist. engnr., 20th Aug., 1925; exec. engnr., P.W.D., Tanganyika Territory, 1927.

PLATTIS, SHIRLEY ARDEN.—B. 1899; ed. Bedford Schl.; passed army entrance exam.; "King's India cadetship," 1917; gazetted 2/9th Gurka Rifles; 3rd Afghan War, 1919; attd. 2/11th Gurka Rifles and 4/70th Burma Rifles, Burma, 1921; cadet, admstive service, Tanganyika Territory, 21st May, 1926.
PLATTIS, W. A. F.—Asst. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., 15th Feb., 1907; dist. comsnr., Apr., 1913.

PLOWMAN, CLIFFORD HENRY FITZGERALD, B.A. (class. tripes), 1911, F.R.G.S.—B. 1889; ed. at King's Schl., Ely, and Trin. Coll., Camb.; asst. dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., Mar., 1912; pol. offr., Turkana Expd., 1915 (A.G.S. Med. with clasps); ag. offr. in charge, Northern Frontier Dist., 1919-20; seconded to F.O. as H.R.M. Vice-Consul, Harar, Abyssinia, 1920; H.R.M. Consul on transfer Harar Consulate to C.O., 1922; transf'd., Somaliland, 1922; H.R.M. chargé d'affaires, Addis Ababa, 26th Dec., 1925 to 24th Jan., 1926; 2nd cls., Star of Ethiopia; has passed E. Africa law exam. and Swahili and Boran Galla languages.

PLOWMAN, HON. SIR GEORGE THOMAS ET. BACH, (1923), C.M.G. (1908).—Cik. and shorthand writer, col. sec.'s office, Natal, 1890; 1st cik., 1891; ch. olk., 1894; sec. to civ. ser. bd.; asst. under-sec., 1898; sec. to prime min., July, 1901; sec., lands and works, Nov., 1901; mem., civ. serv. bd., 1901; sec. to prime min.; acctg. offr., rly. and harbrs., 1st July, 1905; prov. sec., Natal Prov., 1910; dep. admstr., Natal Prov., Aug.-Nov., 1913, and from Aug., 1917; mem. prov. admin. comsnr., 1915; admstr., Natal, 1918.

PLUMER, FIELD-MARSHAL RT. HON. BARON, SIR HERBERT CHARLES ONSLOW PLUMER, G.C.B. (1918), G.C.M.G. (1916), G.C.V.O. (1917), G.B.E. (1924), A.D.C.—B. 1857; served in Soudan expdtn., 1894, 4th cls. Medjide, operations in S. Africa, 1896; S. African War, 1899-1902; A.D.C. to King Edward VII., 1901-2; Q.M.G. to the forces; 1904-5; comdg. 5th divn., Irish command, 1906-9, G.O.C.-in-C., Northern command, 1911-14, comdg. 2nd Army, British exped. force, France, 1915-17; G.O.C.-in-C., British forces in Italy, 1917; gov., Malta, 10th June, 1919; high comsnr., Palestine, 1925; gen., 1915; A.D.C. Gen. to the King, 1917; col., York and Lancs. Regt., 1917; Grand Officer, Legion of Honour; Grand Officer, ss. Maur. and Laz. (Italy); Grand Officer, Order of Leopold, and Croix de Guerre (Belgium).

POLE-EVANS, ILLTD BULLER, C.M.G. (1921), M.A. (Cantab.), D.Sc. (Wales), F.L.S.—B. 1879; ed. Cowbridge Gram. Schl. and Univs. of Wales and Cambridge; mycologist, Transvaal, 1905; ch., divn. of mycology and plant pathology, 1911; ch., divn. of botany and plant pathology, 1912; dir., botanical survey of S. Africa, 1918; pres., S. African Assocn. for Advancement of Science, 1920; ch., div. of botany, horticulture and entomology, 1927; author of many scientific papers.

POLLARD, ARNOLD.—B. 1893; ed. Heles Sch., Exeter; active serv., R.N.A.S. and R.N.R., 1915-19; col. audt dept., 2nd Feb., 1920; asst. audr., Tanganyika Territory, 1st Apr., 1920; asst. audr., Kenya, 30th May, 1924; ag. senr. asst. audr. on several occasions in Tanganyika and Kenya; audr., Windward Is., 2nd Jan., 1926.

POLLONAIIS, CHARLES ADRIEN, I.S.O. (1925).—B. 1862; entered civ. serv., Trinidad, 27th Mar., 1882; asst. rec-gen., 1st Apr., 1907; asst. treas., July, 1923; has acted as recr.-gen. and treas. on several occasions.

POMARE, HON. SIR MAUI, M.D., Chicago, U.S.A., K.B.E. (1922), C.M.G. (1920).—B. 1876; educ. Boys' High Sch., Christchurch; govt. health offr. to Maoris, 1911; entd. New Zealand parlmt., 1911; mem. of exec. coun., representing native race, 1912; also min. of Cook Islands since 1916; min. of health, 1923-26.

POOLE, HENRY BRADISH.—Joined Bechuanaland Prot. pol., 1914; asb.-inspr., 1914; sp. J.P., 1916; ag. asst. res. mag., 1920; ag. res. mag., 1920; ag. regis. of brands and ag. ch. clk. and regis., 1924; passed 3rd grade Seowana exam., 1925.
POOLEY, CHARLES EDWARD.—B. 1903; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 22nd Oct., 1920.

POPE, FREDERICK SIDNEY.—B. 1869; educ. Wellington Boys' Coll., New Zealand; served in govt. rly. dept., N. Zealand, 1886-1900; dept. of agric., 1900-1909; sec. for agriculture, industries and commerce, 1909-1918; asst. director-gen. of agric., 1918.

POPHAM, HENRY BRADSHAW, M.B.E. (1918).—B. 1881; ed. Tonbridge Sch.; comanr., 13th Light Infy., 1900; served, S. African War (2 meda.); seconded, G. Coast regt., 1906; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to ag. gov., G. Coast, Mar. to Aug., 1909; col. sec.'s office, Jan., 1910; priv. sec. to ag. gov., Feb. to June, 1911; ag. ch. asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., July to Aug., 1913; ag. senr. asst. col. sec., Oct., 1913; dist. comanr., Jan., 1914; seconded to Togoland polit. admin., Nov., 1914; senr. asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., Jan., 1920; ag. ch. asst. col. sec., Sept., 1920 to Feb., 1921; dep. prov. comanr. and ag. comanr., W. Prov., Feb., 1921; prov. comanr., Apr., 1922; ret'd. (invalided) May, 1923; comanr., Cyprus, Oct., 1925.

POTTS, JOHN WILFRED.—Served in Bechuanaland Prot. pol., 1912-15; clk., 1915; ag. asst. res. mag., 1920; ag. res. mag., 1920 and 1922-23; passed civ. ser. lower law exam., 1919; passed 2nd grade Seowana exam., 1922; sub. inspr., Bechuanaland Prot. pol., 1924.

POU, WILLIAM OSCAR, M.D.Mich., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (London).—B. 1875; C.M.O., Natal, 1901-02; regis. and senr. res. med. offr., Seaman's Hosp., London; Schol of Trop. Med., 1904-06; health offr., Pahang, Apr., 1921.

POUGNET, EDOUARD DESBARBIERES, A.C.G.I., A.M.I.C.E.—Pub. wks. dept., Mauritius, Jan., 1888; asst. water authority, Meh., 1898; inspr. of water wks., July, 1903; govt. survr., Moh., 1911; aspt. of pub. wks. and survr. of roads, St. Kitts, Apr., 1914; in service of French govt., in France during the war.

POULIER, REGINALD SYDNEY VERNON.—B. 1894; B.A., Lond.; cadet, Ceylon civ serv., local divn., Aug., 1919; attached to Galle Kach., Aug., 1919; pol. mag., Matale, Feb., 1922; ag. dist. judge, Tangalla, Mar., 1923; addnl. dist. judge, Mannar and Mullaitivu, Oct., 1924; addnl. asst. govt. agt., Colombo, Dec., 1925.

POUNTNEY, ARTHUR MEIK, C.M.G. (1923).—B. 1873; ed. Univ. Coll., Oxford; B.A.; cadet, F.M.S., 1896; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, 1903; ag. prot. of Chinese, Perak, 1904; ag. offl. assignee, 1905; ag. prot. of Chinese, Selangor, 1906; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese, Penang, 1909; sup't. of census, F.M.S., 1910; ag. prot. of Chinese, Selangor, 1912; treas., S. Settmts., 1st Feb., 1914; on spl. duty in connection with war tax, Jan., 1917; offg. collr.-gen., war tax, Mar., 1917; war services recog. by sec. of state, Nov., 1918; treas., Dec., 1918; custodian of enemy property in addition, Dec., 1918; pres., Straits mercantile marine service comanr., May, 1919; finan. adviser to govt. of Straits Settmts. and F.M.S. in addition, 27th Oct., 1921.

POUSSETTE, HENRY RIVINGTON.—B. 1872; ed. Sarnia Pub. Sch., Upper Can. Coll., Royal Mily. Coll., Kingston, and Sch. of Prac. Science, Toronto; capt., S. African Constab., 1901, and thereafter in same corps throughout S. African war; dist. comdr., and pub. pros., S. Africa; ent. serv., dept. of trade and commerce, Can., as trade comanr., Durban, S. Africa, 1909; trade comanr., Buenos Aires, 1910; enlisted for activeserv., 1914; returned, Can., 1919; inspr., trade comanr. serv.; sp. trade comanr., India, 1919; dir., comm'l. intelligence serv., Ottawa, 1921; trade comanr., Trinidad, 1924.

POWELL, HENRY.—Student, Royal Gardens, Kew, June, 1888, to Apr., 1890; curator, Botanic Gardens, St. Vincent, May, 1890, to Nov., 1903; asst. to dir. of agric., E. Africa Prot., 7th Nov., 1903; ch. of economic plants div., Apr., 1907.

POWELL, HENRY A., D.C.L., K.C., M.A.—B. 1855; ed. Richibucto Gram. Sch., Mt. Allison Univ. (B.A., 1875; M.A., 1890; D.C.L., 1914); atty., 1879; called to New Brunswick bar, 1880; K.C. (Earl of Aberdeen), 1894; K.C., New Brunswick, 1894; councillor, N.B. Barristers' Assocon.; prof., law faculty, King's Coll., Windsor, N.S.; el. to N.B. legislature for Westmorland, 1890, 1891 and 1892; el. to H. of C. for Westmorland, 1895 and 1896; mem., Bd. of Regents, senator, and mem., exec. comtee., Mt. Allison Univ.; mem., International Law Assocon. of Washington, D.C.; mem. of International Joint Coman.
POWER, GERALD, B.A.—B. 1891; ed. Denstone Coll., and Queen's Coll., Oxford; jnr. supt., educ. dept., N. Nigeria, 14th Jan., 1914.
POWERS, HON. CHARLES.—Justice of the high ct., of Australia, 1913.

POWTR, JOHN, C.B.E. (1927).—Traffic asst., Baro-Kano rly., N. Nigeria, Mar., 1911; asst. traffic supt., Nigeria rly., Apr., 1913; dist. traffic supt., 1920; gen. man., Trinidad govt. rly., Mar., 1922; dir., Jamaica govt. rly., Aug., 1926.

POYSER, KENNETH ELLISTON.—B. 1882; ed. Shrewsbury and Merton Coll., Oxford; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1906; European War, 1914-20, Yorks. L.I., and commanded 9th Batt., Loyal N. Lanc. Regt., (severely wounded, deeps, thrice, D.S.O., 1917); puisane judge, Leeward Islands, Sept., 1920; atty.-gen., Barbados, June, 1925; ag. ch. just., Barbados, Aug., 1925 and Aug., 1926.

PRAIN, LT.-COL. SIR DAVID, KT. B.ACH. (1912); C.M.G. (1912); C.I.E. (1906); M.A., M.B., LL.D., F.R.S.E., F.L.S., F.R.S.—B. 1857; ed. Aberdeen gram. schl. and Univ. of Aberdeen and Edinburgh; demonstrator of anatomy, coll. of surgeons, Edin., 1882-3; and univ. of Aberdeen, 1883-1884; entd. Indian med. serv., 1884; curator, Calcutta herbarium, 1887-1898; prof. of botany, med. coll., Calcutta, 1896-1908; director, botanical survey of India, and supt. of royal botanic garden, Calcutta, 1898-1905; trustee of the Indian Museum, 1898-1904; sec. bd. of scientific advice for India, 1903-1904; mem. of coun. of various scientific societies; director of royal botanic gardens, Kew, 1905; ret., 1922.

PRANKERD, HORACE ARTHUR.—B. 1883; ed. at Ipswich and Queen's Coll., Cambridge (scholar, 2nd class Tripos), B.A., 1906; asst. master, Loretto, to July, 1907; then 2½ years at Lausanne and Freiburg-in-Baden Unive.; asst. sec., N. Nigeria, Oct., 1910; asst. ree., 1911; ag. supt., schls., July, 1912 to Apr., 1913; asst. dist. offr., 1st Jan., 1914; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 20th Apr., 1921; ag. ree., 30th June to 3rd July, 1921; ag. station mag. in 1922 and 1924.

PRASAD, MURARI.—B. 1888; Cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Dec., 1912; attached to Galle Kachcheri, Jan., 1913; ag. pol. mag., Point Pedro, Sep., 1913; addtl. office asst. to gov't. agt., N.C. Prov., Aug., 1914; addtl. dist. judge, Anuradhapura, Aug., 1915; office asst. to gov't. agt., N.C. Prov., Dec., 1915; pol. mag., Chilaw, Apr., 1917; ditto, Panadure, Apl., 1919; office asst. to gov't. agt., N. Prov., Mar., 1920; dist. judge, Anuradhapura, May, 1921; pol. mag., Kalutara, May, 1922; ditto, Gampola, Mar., 1923; dist. judge, Tangalla, Oct., 1924; dep. fiscal, Colombo, Feb., 1926; ag. dist. judge, Kalutara, May, 1926; dist. judge, Matara, June, 1927.

PRATT, CAPT. EDWARD.—Ed. at Shrewsbury; cadet, F.M.S., Oct., 1898; ag. asst. dist. offr., Raub, May, 1899; ag. collr. of land rev., Kuala Lumpur, Feb., 1901; passed cadet, May, 1902; asst. dist. offr., Lower Perak, Jan., 1903; ag. asst. dist. offr., Tanjong Malim, Sept., 1903; ag. dist. treas., Batu Gajah, Jan., 1904; ag. asst. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, Nov., 1904; ditto, Tanjong Malim, Dec., 1904; ditto, Kuala Lipis, Aug., 1907; ag. dist. offr., Temerloh, Oct., 1908; ag. mag., Taiping, Oct., 1910; asst. registrar, supreme ct., Kinta, May, 1911; ag. revenue auditor, Perak, 23rd Oct., 1912; ag. dist. offr., Krian, 7th July, 1913; offr. of cls. III, S. Sttlmts., 21st Dec., 1914; joined army, Aug., 1916; attd. R.A.S.C., "Syren" force, Dec., 1918; offr. cls. II., S. Sttlmts., 1st June, 1919; demob., May, 1919; ag. int. mag., Ipoh, May, 1920; ag. dep. pub. pros., F.M.S., Sept., 1920; ag. legal adviser and judge, Johore, Dec., 1920; dep. pub. pros., F.M.S., July, 1921; offr. dist. judge, Malacca, Sept., 1922.

PRATT, F. G., A.M.I.C.E.; asst. engr., Lagos govt. rly., Mar., 1907; asst. engr., P.W.D., Uganda, Sept., 1908; ditto, P.W.D., N. Nigeria, Dec., 1911; represented N. Nigeria at 3rd International Road Congress held in London in June, 1913; dir. of pub.wks., Nyassaland, 1917; D.P.W., Tanganyika Territory, 1927.

PRENDERGAST, HON. JAMES EMILE PIERRE.—Ed. at Seminary of Quebec, and Laval Univ., Quebec; B.A., 1878; LL.B., 1881; returned for La Verendrye, Manitoba, Aug., 1886; again Dec., 1886; on formation of cabinet by Hon. Thomas Greenway, Jan., 1888, was given portfolio of prov. sec.; re-elected in said constituency; elected for Woodlands, 1888; judge of sup. ct., N.W.T., 1902; ditto, Saskatchewan, 1907; puisne judge, court of King's Bench of Manitoba, 1910.
PRESCOTT, ARTHUR STANLEY.—Apptd. after compet. exam., supplementary clk., supreme ct., Gibraltar, 1896; 3rd cls. clk., 1899; asst. regis. and deputy clk. of arraigna, 1911.

PRETTY, ERIC ERNEST FALK.—B. 1891; ed. Harrow, and Demy of Magdalen Coll., Oxford; cadet, S. Sttlmts., Nov., 1914; passed final exam. in Malay, Jan., 1916; ag. collr. of land revenue, Johore Bahru, Sept., 1917; offr., cls. V., S. Sttlmts., Dec., 1917; 2nd lieut. and asst. adjt., Johore Volr. Rifles, May, 1918; ag. asst. adviser, Endau, Johore, June, 1920; supernumerary offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1921; ag. 2nd asst. sec., "B," 21st Feb., 1922; ag. pvt. sec. to O.A.G., 28th Apr., 1922.

PREVOST, EDWARD WILLIAM DE TEISSIER.—B. 1882; ed. Wellington Coll., Berks; served in S. Africa as 2nd lieut., 3rd Royal Lancaster regt., 1901-2 (Queen's medal with two clasps); wing offr. of constab., and supt. of civil pol., B. N. Borneo, 1904-1911; passed higher standard exam. in Malay language; passed course of instruction at R.I.C. depot, Dublin; asst. commr. of pol., Gold Coast, Apl., 1912; passed lower standard exam. in Fanti language; commr. of pol., Jan., 1915; dep. inspr.-gen. of pol., Apl., 1917; ag. inspr.-gen. of pol., Mar.-Oct., 1919; June-Oct., 1920, Nov.-Dec., 1921, Apr.-Oct., 1922 and Apr.-Aug., 1924.

PRICE, CHARLES ROY.—B. 1893; ed. Wellington Schl. and University Coll., London; B.A. (war), 1919; unattached list, Territorial Force, Jan. to Aug., 1915; Royal Garrison Arty., Sept., 1915 to Dec., 1918; served in France, June, 1916 to Sept., 1918; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, asst. prin., C.O., 1st Mar., 1921.

PRICE, G. F.—Dist. acnt., S. African govt. rly.s. (construction), Natal, 1904-1907; acctnt., Baro-Kano rly., N. Nigeria, Feb. to July, 1908; acctnt., P.W.D., 1st Aug., 1908; asst. treas., N. Nigeria, Oct., 1910; ag. chief asst. treas., Aug. to Nov., 1912.

PRICE, HERBERT HENRY.—2nd clk. to asst. commr., Northern Div., Bech. Prot., 1910; 2nd clk., res. commr.'s office, 1st May, 1912; passed Cape civ. ser. lower law exam., 1910; ag. asst. res. mag., 1911-12; regis. of brands, 1913; ch. clk. and regis., 1921.
PRICE, STANLEY LEWIS, B.A.—B. 1891; ed. Lampeter Coll., and Jesus Coll., Oxford; jnr. supt., educn. dept., N. Nigeria, 26th Sept., 1914; attached, Nigeria Regt., 1917-18.

PRICE JONES, CAPT. WILLIAM.—Served during Great War in France; awarded M.C., 1917; asst. dist. commr., Gold Coast, 27th Aug., 1919; dist. commr., 1st Apr., 1924; called to bar, Gray's Inn, Nov., 1926.

PRICHARD, HERBERT WILLIAM, K.C. (1923)—B. 1873; ed. at Keble Coll., Oxford (open class scholar, 1892); called to the bar, Gray's Inn, Jan., 1902; practised in London, 1902-1910; stip. mag., Trinidad, 19th Aug., 1910; puisne judge, Mauritius, 19th Feb., 1915; 1st puisne judge, 20th Oct., 1916; ag. procureur and advoc.-gen., 30th Oct., 1916, to 2nd July, 1917; chrmn. of comm. on Royal College, 1916-1917; chrmn., sugar sales comte., 1st Sept., 1918 to 31st Aug., 1919; ag. col. sec., 6th Sept. to 13th Dec., 1920 and 2nd Feb. to 3rd Apr., 1921; ag. procureur and advoc.-gen., 15th April, 1921; confirmed in aprt., 20th Jan., 1922; ag. col. sec. from 16th Aug., 1924 to Feb., 1925; puisne judge, S. Sttlmts., June, 1927.

PRINCE, EDWARD ERNEST, F.R.S.C., &c.—B. 1888; ed. Modern Schl., Leeds, and St. Andrews, Camb., and Edin. Univs.; apptd. demonstr., zool., Edin. Univ., 1885; naturalist, Scotch fishery bd. laboratory, St. Ands.; prof. of zool. and comp. anat., Royal Infirmary Med. Coll., Glasgow, 1890; fishery expert, Irish Deep Sea Survey, 1893; vice-pres. Internat. Fisheries Congress, Washington, Paris, and St. Petersburg, 1903-06-09; vice-pres., biol. sect., Brit. Assoc., 1909; ditto, Royal Soc. of Canada, 1908; mem. of internat. relations coun., American fish. soc., 1910; Canad. mem. of internat. fish. congress, Rome, 1911; life mem. of British Science Guild; mem. of internat. fisheries comsn.; repres. Canada, under Fisheries Treaty, 1908; chmn. of many fishery comns.: lobster, 1898; Pacific salmon, 1907; Bay of Fundy Shad, 1908; Manitoba fisheries, 1909; Alberta and Sask. fisheries, 1910-1911; chmn. of biol. bd. of Canada; comsnr. of fisheries for Canada, apptd. 1892.

PRIOR, A. C. VINCENT.—B. 1881; ed. King's Coll., Cambridge; M.A., LL.B.; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1905; atty.-gen., St. Vincent, 5th Jan., 1915; ag. ch. just., Jan. to June, 1915; atty.-gen., Leeward Is., 8th Apr., 1918; solr.-gen., Sierra Leone, 5th Sept., 1920; ag. atty.-gen. on several occasions; solr.-gen., Gold Coast, 1925; ag. atty.-gen., May to Nov., 1926; atty.-gen., Sierra Leone, 1927.
PRITCHARD, MAJOR C. G.—1st coman. R.A., 23rd Nov., 1891; promoted capt., 4th Dec., 1899; adj., Hong Kong volunteers, Jan. to July, 1901; comdt. and adj. (local major), 2nd Apr., 1902.

PRITCHETT, CECIL JOHN SUCKLING, B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1888; cadet, Ceylon civil serv., Dec., 1912; attached to Jaffna Kachcheri, Jan., 1913; ag. pol. mag., Trincomalee, July, 1913; ag. ass't. settlmt. offr., Nov., 1913; office ass't. to gov't. agt., N. Prov., Nov., 1914; ass't. collr. of customs and landing surv., Jaffna, Nov., 1914; ag. ass't. gov't. agt., Mannar, Dec., 1916; office ass't. to gov't. agt., N. Prov., Feb., 1916; pol. mag., Avissawella, Oct., 1916; ditto, Panadure, Dec., 1917; ditto, Chilaw, Apl., 1918; ag. ditto, Galle, Sept., 1919; ass't. land settlmt. offr., Nov., 1921; dist. judge, Anuradhapura, Feb., 1922; dep. rubber contr., Nov., 1924; ass't. gov't. agt., Batticaloa, Dec., 1926.

PROBYN, SIR LESLIE, K.C.M.G. (1909); C.M.G. (1903).—Ed. at Charterhouse, and in France and Germany, called to the bar, Mid. Tem., 1884; joint author of "The Jurisdiction and Practice of the Mayor's Court," and other law books; atty.-gen., Br. Honduras, 1893; atty.-gen. of Grenada, May, 1896; also ag. col. sec. in 1897-8; ag. admstr. on several occasions; gov. secretary, S. Nigeria, 12th Apr., 1901; gov., Sierra Leone, 4th July, 1904; gov., Barbados, 5th Nov., 1910; assumed gov't., 13th Feb., 1911; gov., Jamaica, 31st May, 1918; assumed gov't., 11th June, 1918; ret., 1924.
PROFTING, HON. W. B., C.M.G. (1918).—M.L.C., Tasmania; atty.-gen. and min. for rly., Apr., 1916; pres., leg. coun., 1926.

PROUT, SIR WILLIAM THOMAS KT. BACH. (1924), C.M.G. (1905), O.B.E., M.B., C.M. (Edin.)—Asst. poor law med. offr., Mauritius, 1885; surg. supt., immigrant ship "Dundee," 1897; ass't. col. surg., Gold Coast, 1888; dist. comsnr., Gold Coast, 1890; col. surg., Gambia, 1893; ag. chief mag., Gambia, 1893; med. offr. in charge of base hosp. during campaign against Foday Sila; col. surg., S. Leone, 1895, title altered to prin. med. offr., 1901; retired, 1906; fellow of roy. med. soc., Edin., and correspndg. mem. of the epidemiological soc. of Lond.; lect., schl. of trop. med., Univ. of Liverpool; med. adviser to C.O.; mem. of advisory, med. and sanitary comtee. for Tropical Africa, C.O.; senr. med. adviser to C.O., 1924; author of article "Yawo" in "Diseases of Warm Climates" (Davidson); Fil. Volouloides (Archives de Parasitologie, May, 1901); "Filariasis in S. Leone" (Brit. Med. Journal, 1902); and other papers.

PRYDE, WILLIAM.—B. 1880; ed. Royal High Schl., and Univ., Edinburgh; M.A., Edinburgh Univ. (1st cls. hon. in class.); Pitt Club schol. and Guthrie fellow, Edinburgh Univ.; cadet, F.M.S., 27th Nov., 1903; passed cadet, May, 1905; offr., cls. V, Oct., 1910; offr., cls. IV, July, 1915; offr., cls. III, July, 1918; offr., cls. II, Nov., 1920; barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, Jan., 1923; mag., Kuala Lumpur, Mar., 1923; ag. dist. judge and 1st mag., Singapore, Mar., 1924; att'd. legal adviser's office, Dec. 1924; offr. assignee and pub. trustee, Jan., 1925; legal adviser, Johore, Sept., 1924.

PUCKRIDGE, GEOFFREY MARTIN.—B. 1895; ed. Exeter Schl.; 2nd lieut., Devon Regt., T.F., Dec., 1913; capt., Dec., 1914; served in France with 5th West Yorks regt.; seconded to Royal Flying Corps, July, 1917; seconded to Inter Allied Aeronautical Comsn. of Control, Germany, 1920; re-signed coman., Sept., 1921; asst. dist. comsnr., Gold Coast, 1921.

PUDSEY, FAWCETT.—B. 1882; Assoc. M.I.M.E.; Assoc. M.I.C.E.; ed. Hymer's Coll., Hull, and Repton Schl.; apprentice with Messrs. Rose, Downs and Thompson, Ltd., Hull, 1900-1905; engineering asst., dock engrn.'s staff, N.E. Rly., 1905-1911; engaged on survey work, Catalonia, Spain, for the Pearson Engineering Corporation, New York, 1911; in charge of opening of the Hessle quarry and crushing plant of the British Portland Cement Manufacturers, Ltd., 1912-1914; asst. dir. of pub. wks., B. Guiana, 1914; ag. D.P.W., Feb., 1916.
PUGH, J.—Med. offr., E.A.P., Jan., 1910.

PUGHE, S. R., M.B.E. (1926).—B. 1877; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civil ser., and assigned to the C.O., Aug., 1897; promoted, with gratuity, Aug., 1904; conf. clk., Dec., 1907; minor staff clerk, 1911; staff clk., 1st grade, 1st Mar., 1916; staff offr., 1st Apr., 1922; on service in Sierra Leone, Oct., 1926 to Feb., 1927; sec., Discovery comtee., 9th Jan., 1928.

PURCELL, SIR GILBERT KENHELM TREFFRY, KT. BACH. (1916).—Ed. Exeter Coll., Oxford; B.A. 1888; M.A. 1893; called to bar, Linc.'s Inn, Nov., 1890; mem. of western circ., also practised at cent. crim. ct.; apptd. asst. to atty.-gen. Leeward Islas.
