Hum. (B.A., 1910); cadet, Hong Kong, 24th Oct., 1910; passed in Cantonese, Nov., 1912; ag. asst. dist. offr. (South), 1912 to 1913; ag. asst. supt. of police, 1913 to 1914; ag. dep. offl. recvr., 1914 to 1915; ag. chief asst. sec. for Chinese affairs 1914 and 1915, in addition to other duties; priv. sec. to gov., in addition to other duties, 1915; attached to crown solr.'s off., 1915; passed final exam. in Hakka, July, 1915; ag. chief asst. sec. for Chinese affairs, 1916; on milly. serv., 1915-19; dep. regisr. and appraiser, Hong Kong, 1920; head of sany. dept., 1920; passed in Mandarin, May, 1921; attd. to treasy., 1924; asst. treas. and comsnr. of estate duties, Apr. 1925; supt., impts. and expts. dept., June, 1927.

SAYERS, Eldred Frank.—B. 1889; ed. King's Coll. Schl., Wimbledon, St. John's Coll., Cambridge, and Grenoble Univ., B.A., LL.B. (Cantab.); barrister-at-law (Inner Temple), Apr., 1913; passed lower and higher stand. exams. in Temne, and lower stand. exam. in Koranko; war service, E. Africa, 1918; asst. dist. comsnr., Sierra Leone, 1st Jan., 1915; dist. comsnr., 21st Aug., 1922.

SAYERS, Frederick Richardson.—M.D., D.P.H., Dublin Univ.; ed. Rathmines Schl., Dublin, The Meath Hosp., Dublin, and Trin. from 20th May, 1889; acted as dep. regir. or as sheriff and dep. regir., Singapore, on several occasions since 1893; confirmed as sheriff and dep. regir., 1st Jan., 1902; ag. offr. assignee in addition to own duties, Sept. to Oct., 1907; dep. regir. sup. ct., and sheriff in addition, Dec., 1908, and again Apr., 1914; ag. regir., sup. ct., Jan., 1909; hon. gen. sec., agri-horticultural shows in addition, May to Aug., 1910; asst. supt. Indian immigr., May, 1912; ag. marshal, prize ct. in addition, Aug., 1914; in charge of regir. and sheriff's office, Dec., 1917.

ROWE, MAJOR R. H., D.S.O., M.C.—B. 1883; ed. Wellington Coll. and Royal Mil. Acad., Woolwich; Royal Arty., 1902-25; Bermuda and Jamaica, 1902-6; served with native infantry, Nigeria, 1907-10; Northern Hinterland expdn., 1907-8; Niger Cross River expdn., 1908-9; operations against Abini, 1909, and against Munchois, 1910; dep. dir., surveys, Nigeria, 1911; Nigeria-Dahomey bndry. comsnr., 1912; Great war (Cameroons, France and Russia), 1914-18; survr.-gen., Gold Coast, 1920; comsnr., lands, in addn., 1924; survr.-gen., Sierra Leone, in addn., 1925; Gold Coast rep. with staff of H.R.H. the Prince of Wales on his visit to W. Africa, 1925; chmn., comtees, on pub. wks. accts., rly. constrn., munie. finance and mil’y. defence; mem., leg. coun., 1926; comsnr., lands, Nigeria, 27th Oct., 1926. Coll., Dublin; res. med. offr., Monkstown Hosp., Dublin, 1909-12; med. offr., S. Settmts., 23rd Mar., 1912; med. offr., gen. hosp., Penang, 27th Apr., 1912; med. offr., dist. hosp., Penang, 2nd June, 1912; port health offr. and health offr., rural areas, Penang, cor., Penang, and med. offr., in charge of leper asylum, Pulau Jerokaj, Penang, 3rd Sept., 1912; ag. ast. port health offr., Singapore, 10th Sept., 1912; resumed duties in Penang, 21st Oct., 1912; ast. censor, Penang, in addition, Sept., 1915, to Apr., 1916; ag. sen. health offr., S. Settmts., in addition, 1st Mar., 1917; ag. ast. health offr., rural areas, Singapore, 20th Mar., 1919; ag. chief health offr., S. Sttmts., in addition, 4th Apr., 1919; lecturer in hygiene, King Edward VII. medical schl., Singapore, 10th June, 1919; ag. ch. health offr., Singapore, Oct., 1920 and in 1923 and 1925; senr. health offr., Penang, 28th Feb., 1921; promoted cls. B., 1st Jan., 1925; mom., Br. delegation to internat. sany. conf., Paris, 8th May to 21st June, 1926.

SAYERS, GERALD FLEMING.—B. 1893; ed. Westminster and Christ Church, Oxford; B.A. (hons.) 1914; ast. dist. comsrr., E. Africa Prot., Feb., 1915; ag. senr. ast. sec., Nov., 1919 to July, 1920; ast. sec., Tanganyika territory, 21st July, 1920; ag. senr. ast. sec., for various periods 1920-24; clk. to exec. coun., Sept., 1923 to Jan., 1924; add. to C.O., Aug., 1924.

SAYLE, LIEUT. COMMDR., ARTHUR REGINALD WILLIS, Barrister-at-Law (Gray's Inn).—Ed. Merchant Taylors Schol. and cadet H.M.S. "Worcester"; Royal Navy, May, 1914; R.I.C., 1920; admistr. offr., S. Nigeria, May, 1921; station mag., Ibadan, S. Nigeria, Aug., 1925.
SCADDAN, HON. JOHN, C.M.G. (1924), M.L.A.—B. 1876; leader of Parly. Lab. party in W. Aust. since Aug., 1910; elected for Ivanhoe, June, 1904; premier of W. Australia, 1911-1916.

SCAMMELL, ERNEST HENRY.—B. 1873; ed. privately; sec. various business concerns, 1891-1907; visited Australia, engaged in business and journalism in Canada, 1907-1913; organizing sec., Canadian Peace Centenary Association, under the auspices of the govt. of Canada, 1913; ast. cable censor, dept. of militia and defence, Canada, 1915; sec., mil. hosps. coman., Canada, 1915; ast. dep. min. and sec., dept. of soldiers' civil re-establt., Canada, 1918; negotiated reciprocal agreements between Canadian, British and United States Govts., regarding treatment and training of Naval and Military Forces resident in these countries, 1919; Fellow of the Chartered Institute of Secretaries (London); Fellow of the Royal Colonial Institute (London); mem. of the Exec. of the Canadian Peace Centenary Assoc.

SCHMIDT, CARL FRIEDRICH, B.A.—Civ. serv. law clk., law dept., Cape, May, 1893; control and audit office, July, 1893; ast. examnr. of accts., Jan., 1894; examr., May, 1898; ch. examr., July, 1907; inspr. of accts., Jan., 1909; prin. clk., Apr., 1912; sec., educn. dept., Bloemfontein, 16th Aug., 1915; ag. dir. of educn., O.F.S., July, 1918; dir. of educn., O.F.S., June, 1920; sec. for interior, 13th Sept., 1926.
SCHNEIDER, GUALTERUS STEWART, K.C.—B. 1864; ag. pulse just., Ceylon, Jan., 1921; comsrr. of assize, Nov., 1921; pulse just., Dec., 1921; ag. ch. just., 20th Sept., 1926.

SCHOCH, AUGUST ARNOLD.—B. 1883; ed. Berne, Switzerland; graduated, Leiden, LL.D. and called to bar, Middle Temple, 1910; ent. Union pub. serv., 1911; priv. sec. to min. of justice, 1913-16; professional ast. to atty.-gen. (Transvaal), 1916-24; solr.-gen., Cape Eastern Dist., Aug., 1924; law adviser and Dutch draughtsman, Sept., 1926.

SCHOFIELD, WALTER.—B. 1888; ed. Univ. of Liverpool, M.A.; non-coll. student, Oxford, 1908-1911, M.A.; cadet, Hong Kong, 17th Nov., 1911; attached to secretariat for Chinese affairs, 1914; ast. cable censor, in addition to other duties, 1914 to 1915; passed cadet, 12th Feb., 1915; ag. 3rd ast. to sec. for Chinese affairs, 1915; ag. 2nd ditto, 1916; passed law exam., 1916; seconded to H. Kong and Shanghai Bank, 1918; ag. 2nd ast. to sec. for Chinese affairs, 1918 to 1919, ag. dep. registr. and appraiser, in addn. to other duties, 1919; ag. chief ast. to sec. for Chinese affairs, 1919; ag. 2nd ast. to ditto, 1919, 1921, 1922 and 1923; ag. ch. ast., Aug., 1920 to Nov., 1921; dist. offr., South in addn., 1922; dist. offr., South, 1923; mag., Hong Kong, 1926; mag., Kowloon, 1927.

SCHOLEFIELD, GUY HABDY, D.Sc. (Lond.), O.B.E. (1919).—Ed. Tokomairiro High Schl. and Univ. of N.Z. (Bowen and Macmillan Brown prizeman) and Univ. of London; ch. librarian, gen. assembly liby. and contrlr., dominion archives, N.Z., 1926.

SCHOLEFIELD, WILLIAM.—Dep. clk., res. mag.'s. ct., Jamaica, 12th Mar., 1913; clk., res. mag.'s. ct., 26th Feb., 1917; ast. comsrr. and mag., Turks and Caicos Islands, 19th July, 1919; ag. comsrr., ditto, 1st Sept. to 12th Oct., 1920; ag. res. mag., Jamaica, on various occasions, 1922-26.

SCHRADER, LOUIS WILLIAM CONRAD.—B. 1873; ed. Highgate School and Lincoln Coll., Oxford (M.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Oct., 1896; off. ast. to govt. agt., Prov. of Uva, April, 1897; pol. mag., Galagedara, July, 1899; off. ast. to govt. agt., S. Prov., Nov., 1899; N. C. Prov., Mar., 1901; ast. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Nov., 1901; dist. judge, Ratnapura, Jan., 1903; ast. coll. of cust., Trincomalee, Dec., 1904; dist. judge, Tangalla, Feb., 1906; ast. govt. agt., Hambantota, April, 1907; dist. judge, Badulla, Oct., 1909; ag. dist. judge, Galle, Aug., 1912; dist. judge, Matara, May, 1913; comsrr. of requests, Colombo, Oct., 1913; ag. dist. judge, Galle, Feb., 1914; dist. judge, Kalutara, June, 1914; ditto, Negombo, Oct., 1915; ditto, Galle, Dec., 1915; ag. govt. agt., E. Prov., Sept., 1918; ditto, Prov. of Uva, June to Sept., 1919; govt. agt., N. Prov., Nov., 1921; ditto, E. Prov., Oct., 1922; regisr. gen., Mar., 1923; govt. agt., N. Prov., July, 1923; dist. judge, Galle, Aug., 1924; govt. agt., S. Prov., 15th Mar., 1926.

SCHWARZ, ERNEST HUBERT LEWIS, A.R.C.S. Hons., London.—B. 1873; ed. Westminster and Royal Coll. of Science; ed., "Scientific African," 1895; joined Cape geol. survey, 1896; professor, Rhodes University Coll., Grahamstown, 1905; author of "South African Geology," "South African Geography," "Causal Geology," "The Kalahari, or Thirstland Redemption."

SCICLUNA, HANNIBAL P., L.P.—B. 1880; ed. St. Ignatius' Coll., Malta; matric., Malta Univ., 1897; ent. Malta div. serv., 1902; legal procurator, 1905; sec. and regisr., Malta Univ., 1913-20; rep. of govt. emigr. dept. in devastated regions, France, 1920; add. ministry of justice, 1921; esquire, Order of St. John of Jerusalem (Rome), 1910; Officier d'Academie (France), 1921; mem. of Societe Academique d'Histoire Internationale de Paris, 1923; libr., Malta public library, July, 1923.

SCIORTINO, J. C.—B. 1875; ed. at Lyceum Univ., Malta; in service of Eastern and S. African telegraph companies, 1891-1903; rev. offr., N. Nigeria, 26th Sept., 1903; ast. res., 4th cl. res., 29th Apr., 1907; 2nd cl. res., 15th June, 1910; senr. res., 12th June, 1921.

SCOTLAND, DOUGLAS WILLIAM, F.S.I., N.D.A., N.D.D., H.A.A.C. Dip.—Ed. at Musorrie Schol., India and Alleyn's Schol., Stone, Staffs.; Agric. Coll., Newport, Salop, 1904-1907; Salop County Scholar, 1906-1907; Prof. Assoc. of Surv. Inst., 1909; agric. instr., Tyrone (Board of Agric. Ireland), 1907-1908; lect. at Harper-Adams agric. coll., 1908-11; ast. agric. dept., Sierra Leone, 1911-1915; dir. of agric. on several occasions; dir. of agric., Sierra Leone, 3rd Dec., 1915; visited W. Indies and Br. Guiana on behalf of Sierra Leone govt., Nov., 1913 to Feb., 1914; del. to oil palm conf., at C.O., Sept., 1919; ag. commnr., lands and forests, 20th Feb., 1924 to 31st Jan., 1925; M.L.C., Nov., 1924 to 31st Jan., 1925; del. to 2nd Imp. Entomological conf., 9th to 18th June, 1925.
SCOTLAND, ERICA ELIZABETH.—Ent. C.O. as typist, July, 1913; shorthand writer, Aug., 1918; cler. offr., 9th Mar., 1922.

SCOTT, DUNCAN CAMPBELL, F.R.S.C.—B. 1862; apptd. to Indian commn.'s off., Canada, 1879; jun. 2nd cl. clk., dept. of Indian affairs, 1880; promoted to sen. 2nd cl., 1883; 1st cl., 1889; ch. clkship, 1893; ch. acctnt. and supt. of Indian educn., 1908; deputy supt.-gen. of Indian affairs, 1913; hon. sec., Royal Soc. of Canada.

SCOTT, HENRY HABOLD, M.D., London Univ.; M.R.C.P., London; F.R.S., Edin.; D.P.H.; M.R.C.S., Eng.; L.R.C.P., London; Fell. of Royal Soc. of medicine; Fell. of Royal Inst. of preventive medicine; Fell. of Soc. of tropical medicine and hygiene.—Ed. at St. Bart's and St. Thomas's hosps.; Univ. Colls. London and Copenhagen; protector, 1894; astt. demonstrator in Materia Medica, 1895; astt. house physician, 1897; house physician, 1898, all at St. Thomas's hosp.; res. med. offr., Teignmouth hosp., 1898; serv. in S. African War (Queen's medal with 5 clasps); med. offr. in charge bacteriological and X-Ray dep., milt. hosp., Maritzburg, 1903; M.O. in charge Fort Napier hosp. for women and children, 1904; res. med. offr., Kingsbury and Neasden isolation hosp., 1910; bacteriologist to Willesden Dist. Coun., 1910; gvt. bacteriologist and fermentation chemist, Jamaica, 1910; is also pathologist to Kingston gen. hosp.; mem. of med. coun., board of examiners, quarantine board, and cent. board of health, Jamaica; during 1915-1917, seconded for war service; bacteriologist at Command Laboratory, Aldershot, 1915; in charge No. XIII. mobile laboratory, 1916; pathologist and med. specialist, Cambridge hosp., Aldershot, 1916.

SCOTT, HERBERT SEPTIMUS.—B. 1873; ed. at Eton and Hartford Coll., Oxford; M.A.; inspr. of schools, Transvaal, 1902; examr., 1908; sec. to educnt. dept., Transvaal, 1911; dir. of educn., Transvaal, 1924.

SCOTT, JOHN, C.M.G. (1923).—B. 1878; ed. Bath Coll. and King's Coll., Cambridge (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1901; priv. sec. to H.E. the offr. admng. the govt., Nov., 1903; offr. ast. to govt. agt., N. Prov., Jan., 1904; ast. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Jan., 1905; Mannar, Apr., 1905; pol. mag., Hatton-Nuwara Eliya, Apr., 1907; ast. govt. agt., Mannar, Feb., 1910; ditto, Hambantota, May, 1911; dist. judge, Negombo, Nov., 1911; dist. judge, Chilaw and Puttalam, Mar., 1913; ast. govt. agt., Chilaw and Puttalam, Sept., 1913; addl. ast. col. sec., Aug., 1914; attached to censor's off., Apr., 1915; censor, Sept., 1915; prin. ast. col. sec., Apr., 1917; dep. ch. sec. to govt., Nigeria, 23rd Feb., 1921; ch. sec., Tanganyika Territory, 30th Jan., 1924.
SCOTT, MARGARET.—Ed. Sherbourne Schol. for Girls; V.A.D. nurse, Brit. R. Cross, 1915-19; housekeeper's dipl., Edin. Sch. of Domestic Econ., 1920; matron, Achimota, 1925.

SCOTT, RALPH.—B. 1874; B.A. Lond. Univ.; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1895; ag. collr. of land rev., Singapore, Mar., 1897; passed final exam. in Malay, Sept., 1897; ag. dist. offr., Malacca, Sept., 1897; confirmed, Aug., 1898; apptd. to cl. IV., Jan., 1903; ag. dist. offr., Dindings, Apr., 1903; attached to land off., Sing., on spec. duty, July, 1905; ag. collr. of land rev., Singapore, Jan., 1906; ag. 2nd mag., Singapore, Mar., 1906; ag. commnr. of ct. of requests, Singapore, July, 1906 to Feb., 1907; dist. offr., Dindings, Aug., 1906; collr. of land rev., Penang, Feb., 1907; ag. commnr., ct. of requests, Singapore, Dec., 1907; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, Jan., 1908; ag. collr. of land rev., Malacca, Oct., 1908; in charge of sttlmt., Feb. to May, 1909, and from Feb. to July, 1910; assumed duties as collr. of land rev., Penang, July, 1910; ag. senr. dist. offr., Prov. Wellesley, Feb., 1911; promoted to cls. ii., Dec., 1913; ag. dist. judge and 1st mag., Penang, Apr., 1915; ag. temp. 2nd mag., Penang, Dec., 1915; censor, Penang, Jan., 1916; ag. dist. judge and 1st mag., Jan., 1916; ag. dist. judge (civil), May, 1916; ag. res. and pres. munici. commrs., Malacca, Mar., 1920; dist. judge and 1st mag., Penang, Jan., 1921.

SCOTT, R. R., M.C., M.B., B.S. (Durham), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Lond.).—R.A.M.C. (S.R.), Aug., 1914; ment. in despa., 1919; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Mar., 1919; senr. med. offr. of health, Apr., 1924.

SCOTT, THOMAS.—Ck., aud. office, Transvaal, 18th Feb., 1901; inspr., 1st Jan., 1904; prin. ck., revenue div., treasur., 1st Oct., 1904; rev. inspr., 1st Mar., 1905; prin. ck., audit office, 1st Dec., 1908; chief inspr. of expenditure audit, control and audit office, Union of S. Africa, 1st Apr., 1912; ag. ast. contr. and audr.-gen., Feb. to Aug., 1921 and May to Oct., 1922.
SCOTT, WILLIAM DUNCAN.—B. 1861; commnr. for Canada to the Paris Exhibn., 1900; supt. of immigr., 1903-1919; ast. dep. min. of immigr. and colonization, 1919.
SCOTT, WM. ERNEST.—Ed. at Bradford and Giggleswick gram. schls., England; dep. min. of agric. and supt. of farmers' institutions, B. Columbia, 1909.
SCRAGG, JOHN, B.A.—B. 1902; ed. Manchest. Gram. Schol. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford.; schol., 2nd cl., philos., politics and economics, 1925; ast. mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1926.

SCRIVENER, RUPERT FREDERICK.—B. 1890; assoc., R. Schol. of Mines, dipl., Imp. Coll. Science, assoc., I.M.M., assoc., Inst. of Mining and Metallurgy; survey work, England and Alaska, 1911-14; commd. serv., 1914-20; capt., 1918; served in Gallipoli, Egypt, Mesopotamia, India and Palestine; commanded, 29th Rly. Coy., Sappers and Miners, I.A.; ast. ch. engr., Palestine rlys., 1920; engnr., ways and wks., Palestine rlys., 1924.

SCRIVENOR, JOHN BROCK.—B. 1876; ed. King's Schol., Canterbury, and Hertford Coll., Oxford; served on geol. surv. of the U.K. in 1902-03; geologist, F.M.S., 14th Aug., 1903; served in the Army in England and France from 8th Dec., 1916 to end of war as 2nd lieut., R.E. signal service; on sp. duty for state of Kelantan, May, 1924.

SEAGOE, ERNEST GOLDFINCH, M.B.E.—B. 1886; clk. to res. comsnr., New Hebrides, 1902; inspr. of native labourers, Br. residency, New Hebrides, 1909; ag. astt. to res. comsnr., 1911-12 and 1915-16; joint ag. native advocate, Oct., 1915 to Dec., 1922; ag. astt. to res., Nov., 1919 to Feb., 1920 and Sept. to Dec., 1921; commdt., constab., Aug., 1922; dep. comsnr., W. Pacific (New Hebrides), Nov., 1922; in ch. of accts., May to Aug., 1923; ag. astt. to res. comsnr., May, 1923 to Aug., 1924 and from 18th Aug., 1927; dist. agt. and lab. inspr., Apr., 1923; agt., Efate dist. and commdt., pol. (Br. divn.) 1st Apr., 1925.

SEALY, GORDON COURLAND MILLER.—B. 1863; clk. to the atty.-gen., Tobago, Jan., 1882; clk. in col. sec.'s dept., 1883; acted as confid. clk. to the admstr. and clk. of couns., 1883 to 1886; 3rd clk., surg-gen.'s dept., Br. Guiana, 1887; 2nd clk., 1887; has acted as ch. clk.; 4th class clk., treas'y. dept., 1st June, 1901; 3rd class clk., ditto, 14th May, 1906.

SEALY, JAMES ERNEST CAMERON.—B. 1871; 2nd clk., audit and registry dept., Tobago, Feb., 1888; clk. to comsrs. and financial bd., Nov., 1890, and Oct., 1893; sec. to bd. of educn., Sept., 1892; ch. clk. treas'y., June, 1893; postmr. of Tobago, Dec., 1895; clk. to sub-rev't., Tobago, Jan., 1899; clk. to warden, Tobago, Sept., 1899; ch. clk. and cashier, treas'y., Tobago, Feb., 1904.
SEATON, G. L.—B. 1868; apptd., after exam., 3rd cl. mess., C.O., 17th May, 1899; 2nd cl., 18th Feb., 1900; King's mess., 10th June, 1904.

SEDGWICK, RICHARD ROMNEY.—B. 1894; ed. Westminster and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; schol., Trinity Coll., 1912; 1st cls. hist. tripas, pt. I, 1914 and pt. II, 1915; Gladstone prize, 1914; elected fellow of Trinity Coll., 1919; M.A., 1919; apptd. under reconstruction scheme astt. prin., Min. of Labour, 1919; astt. prin., C.O., 1922.

SEDWICK, N. A.—B. 1890; ed. at Univ. Coll. Schl., London, and St. Peter's Coll., Camb.; B.A., London and Camb.; cadet, S. Stltlms; civ. serv., Dec., 1913; dep. to supt., govt. monopolies dept., Jan., 1914; in charge of govt. opium factory, May to Aug., 1914; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, Jan., 1915; ag. astt. supt., govt. monopolies dept., 1916; passed cadet (Malay), Mar., 1917; attached to col. sec.'s office, Apl., 1917; ag. dist. offr., Jasim, Malacca, Sept., 1917; collr. of war tax, Singapore, July, 1918; superm. offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1920; ag. astt. treas. and comsnr. of stamps, Penang, Dec., 1920.

SEEL, GEORGE FREDERICK.—B. 1895; ed. Macolesfield Grammar Schl. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford (schol.); on mil'y. serv. with Cheshire Regt. from 20th Nov., 1914 to 23rd Jan., 1919; served in Gallipoli, Egypt and Palestine; higher divn. clk. under reconstruction scheme, and apptd. to air miny., 28th July, 1919; seconded for serv. in C.O., as astt. prin., 9th Jan., 1922; sec., managing comtce., imp. bureau of entomology.

SEELY, MAJOR-GEN. RT. HON. JOHN EDWARD BERNARD, P.C. (1909), C.B. (1916), C.M.G. (1918), D.S.O. (1900).—Ed. at Harrow and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1897; lieut.-col. commdg. Hampshire Carabiniers; served with Imperial Yeomanry in S. Africa, 1900-1901 (desps., Queen's medal, five clasps, D.S.O.); M.P. for Isle of Wight, 1900-06; M.P. for Abercromby div., Liverpool, 1906-1910; M.P., Ilkeston div., Derbyshire, 1910; reed. gold medal (French govt. for saving life at sea, 1891; partly, under-sec. of state for the colonies, 1908; chmn. of comtce. to inquire into organisation of crown agents' office, 1908; under-sec. of state for war, 1911; sec. of state for war, 1912-14; joint partly, sec. to ministry of munitions, 1918; under-sec. of state for air, Jan., 1919.

SEHEULT, JOSEPH EMMANUEL.—B. 1881; entd. civ. serv., Trinidad, 12th Mar., 1902; ch. clk., dept. of agric., 1st Jan., 1916; astt. censor, 1914; censor, 18th Apr., 1918; ag. man., agrl. bank, 1st Nov., 1925.

SEHEULT, LEO GABRIEL, M.I.C.E., A.C.G.I.—B. 1886; astt. dist. offr., pub. works, Trinidad, 1st Mar., 1911; 2nd grade dist. offr., 1st Sept., 1916; 1st grade dist. offr., 1st June, 1917; gen. astt. to astt. D.P.W., 1st Jan., 1925.

SEIGNORET, CLARENCE AUGUSTUS.—B. 1867; supernumerary treas., Dominica, 1890; sec. to special yaws coman., Mar., 1891, to Sept., 1892; ch. clk., regist'r's office and clk. pub. market, 1892; astt. sec. to Sir R. Hamilton during his inquiry in Dominica, 1893; govt. offr., treas'y., 1895; sec. bd. of health and quarantine, 1895; ag. clk., adminstr.'s office and exec. coun., 1895; ag. treas., Dominica, July, 1901; harmr., 1906; survr. of shipping, 1907; ag. treas., Sept., 1910, to Sept., 1111, and from Apr. to June, 1918.
SEIGNORET, G. B.—Clk. to postmr., Dominica, 1889; govt. offr., treas'y., 1893; ch. clk., regist'r's off., 1895; ag. postmr., 1896-7; clk. govt. offr., Roseau, 1897; govt. offr., treas'y., 1901.

SELBORNE, 2ND EARL OF (creat. 1882), VISCOUNT WOLMER, BARON SELBORNE (U.K.), K.G. (1909), P.C. (1900), G.C.M.G. (1905), THE Rt. HON. WM. WALDGRAVE PALMER.—B. 1859; ed. at Winchester and at Univ. Coll., Oxford; B.A. (lit. cls. hist.), 1882; astt. priv. sec. to Mr. Childers, Chane. of Excheq., 1882-85; was mem. of parlmt. for E. Hants, 1886-92, and for W. Edinburgh City, 1892-96; succeeded his father, 1895; Under S. of S. for Cola., June, 1895; chmn., Pacific cable comtce., 1896; mem. of Pacific cable comtce., 1899; first lord of the admiralty, Nov., 12th, 1900; high comsnr. for S. Africa, and govt. and commr-in-chief of Transvaal and O.R.C., 1905 to 1910; ceased to be gov. of O.R.C. on grant of resp. govt., June, 1907; pres. of bd. of agric. and fisheries, 1915-16.

SELBY, PHILIP HAMILTON.—B. 1870; B.S.A. Pol., 1890-91; collr., N. E. Rhodesia, 1st July, 1898; civ. comsnr. and mag., 1900; judge of administrator's ct., N.W.R., 1905; mag., N.R., 17th Aug., 1911; ag. sec. for mines on various occasions, 1921 to 1924; ag. sec. for agr. and mines, 4th Apr., 1924; mem., leg. coun., J.P.
SELDON, A. A.—Astt. dist. comsnr., K.A.P., Dec., 1913; 1st grade admstr. offr., Kenya, 1926.

SELLS, H. C., B.A.(Oxon).—Cadet, S. Stltlms, Oct., 1897; ag. priv. sec. to gov., Nov., 1898, to Nov., 1899; ag. 4th mag., Singapore, Jan., 1900; passed fin. exam. in Malay, Apr., 1900; ag. dist. offr., prov. Well., May, 1901; supt., money order branch and govt. savings bank, Sing., May 1902; head of Malay coll., Malacca, July, 1904; ag. dist. offr., Nibong Tebal, Apr., 1905; ag. astt. postmr.gen., Penang, Nov., 1906; confirmed, May, 1907; ag. postmr.gen., Apr. to Dec., 1909; offr. of Cls. III., F.M.S., 1st Jan., 1912; ag. collr. of land rev. and regiar. of deeds and regiar. of Muhammad marriages, Penang, 27th Mar., 1912; astt. censor in addtn. to other duties, 1st Jan., 1915; chmn., saniry. bd., Kinta, 7th Aug., 1919; offtg. registr. of titles, Perak, ag. supt., convict estab., and inspr. of prisons, F.M.S., in addn., 5th Jan., 1920; ag. P.M.G., cls. I.A.S. Sttlmts., 1st Oct., 1920; ag. collr., land rev., and registr. of deeds and bills of sale, Singapore, in addn., cls. I.B, 14th Feb., 1921; ag. collr.-gen., income tax and collr.-gen., war tax, S. Sttlmts., ols. I.B, 1st Apr., 1921; ag. P.M.G., S. Sttlmts., ols. I.A, 1st Nov., 1921; offr., cls. I.A, June, 1925; sec., postal affrs., S.S. and F.M.S., June, 1925.

SELLS, MARTIN PERSONET, O.B.E., A.M.I.M.E. M.I.Loco. E.—B. 1887; ed. Trent Coll. and Univ. Coll., London; junr. astt. loco. supt., Lancs and Yorks. Rly.; temp. major R.E., 1917; supt., light rlys., France, 1917-1919 (twice ment. in desps.); dist. loco. supt., Tanganyika Terry. rlys., 1919; ag. ch. mech. engrnr., Tanganyika Terry. rlys., 1920; ch. mech. engrnr., Gold Coast rlys., 1922; ch. mech. engrnr., Nigerian Rly., 1926.

SEMPER, D. H.—Clt. to atty.-gen., Leeward Islands, 1893; dep. coroner for dist. B, Oct., 1898; ag. Federal treas., Oct., 1902; fed. treas., Mar., 1903; acted as clk., leg. coun., Antigua, Dec., 1902; ditto, ex. coun., Montserrat, Mar., 1903; ag. ch. regir., Leeward Is., and regir. and prov. marshal, admstr. of estates, offl. recvr. in bank, regir. of titles, regir. of friendly societies, and regir.-gen. of births, deaths and marriages, Antigua, Feb. to July, 1905; appkt. regir. and prov.-marshal, admstr. of estates, offl. recvr. in bank, and regir. of titles, St. Kitts-Nevis, July, 1905; J.P., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1905; ag. mag., dist. C., Feb., 1911; M.L.C., St. Kitts-Nevis, May, 1911; escheator-gen., 1911; offr. in charge of prison discipline, St. Kitts-Nevis, 1911; addtl. mag., Dist. C., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1916; mag. and coroner, dist. D., 30th Nov., 1921.
SEMPILL, CHARLES IGNATIUS, M.M.—B. 1825; Australian Forces, 1914-19; astt. supt., pol., Kenya, Nov., 1920.
SEMPILL, J. HOWARD, M.B.E. (1925)—B. 1877; ed. Stirling High Schl.; ch. of pol. and provost marshal, 1916; ditto and marshal, sup. ct. in admry., 1920; astt. pol., Br. Honduras, 1927.

SIMPLE, J. M., B.A., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O., T.C.D., L.M. (Rotunda), Sandhurst, 1915; lieut., R. Irish Rifles, 1915-18; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Mar., 1922; med. offr., Zanzibar, Oct., 1924; ag. S.S.O., July, 1925 to Oct., 1926.

SENARATNE, CORNELIUS.—B. 1870; cls. V., Ceylon civ. serv., Jan., 1921; extra office astt. to gov't. agt., S. Prov., Feb., 1921; office astt. to astt. gov't. agt., Matale, Feb., 1922; ditto, Hambantota, June, 1926.

SENEVIRATNE, DIONYSIUS BARTHOLOMEW, M.M.—B. 1895; on mily. serv., Nov., 1915; cadet, Ceylon, Oct., 1920; astd. to B'caloa Kachcheri, Oct., 1920; ditto, Kurunegala Kach., Nov., 1921; ag. pol. mag., Dandagamuwa, June, 1922; office astt., Batticaloa kach., Dec., 1923; addnl. astt. gov't. agt., Colombo, Dec., 1926.

SENEVIRATNE, LEOPOLD JAMES DE SILVA, LL.B., Barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn.—B. 1899; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1923; astt., Matara kach., Dec., 1923; ditto, Jaffna kach., July, 1924; ditto, Kegalla kach., Apr., 1925; pol. mag., Puttalam, Mar., 1926; ditto, Pt. Pedro, May, 1927.

SENNETT, CEDRIC WILLIAM ARTHUR.—B. 1890; ed. Plymouth Coll., Edinburgh Univ. and non-collegiate, Oxford (B.A., 1913); cadet, S. Sttlmts., 1914; ag. 4th mag., Singapore, 1915; astt. censor, 1916; astt. regir., impts. and expts., 1917; astt. supt., govt. monopolies, 1920; ag. priv. sec. to ch. sec., F.M.S., 1920; astt. dist. offr., Kuala Lipis, 1920; ag. dist. offr., Raub, 1921; supernumy. offr., cls. IV., 28th Nov., 1921.
SERGEANT, JOHN.—Ed. at Athelhampton schl., Birkdale; chief acnt., pub. wks. dept., E. Africa Prot., 30th May, 1905.

SERRET, EVARISTE JOSEPH EUGENE.—B. 1864; ed. Royal Coll., Mauritius; English schol., 1884; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1887; crown pros., Mauritius, 1892; addnl. substitute procurer and advoc. gen., 1901; ag.ch. just., Seychelles, 1911-12; substitute procureur and advoc. gen., Mauritius, 1913; ag. proc. and advoc. gen., 1913-14; K.C., 1919; puisne judge, sup. ct., 1922.

SERTSIOS, B. D.—B. 1876; ed., Athens; Ph.D., University of Athens; B.A., Greek College, Serres; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1906; judge, dist. ct., Cyprus, 1909; astt. king's advoc., June, 1924; title changed to astt. atty.-gen., May, 1925; ag. solr.-gen., Apr. to May, 1927; puisne judge, Oct., 1927.

SETH, G. G., B.A., Barrister-at-Law (Certif. of Hon.), 1910), K.C.—Calcutta; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1901; ag. sherif, Singapore, Jan., 1903; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, 5th Jan., 1904; ag. dep. regir., sup. ct., Singapore, July, 1904; ag. dist. offr., N. Tebal, June, 1906; ag. dep. regir., sup. ct., Penang, Sept., 1906; astt. to atty.-gen., Apr., 1907; astt. to atty.-gen., 15th Dec., 1910; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn (cert. of hon.), 1910; offr. of Cls. IV., F.M.S., 6th Dec., 1912; collr., war tax, Singapore, 26th Mar., 1917; supernumerary offr. of Cls. III., F.M.S., 1st Jan., 1918; offg. astt. to atty.-gen., Singapore, Nov., 1918; ag. dep. pub. prosecutor in addition, May, 1919; has held numerous acting appts.; solr.-gen., S. Sttlmts., Mar., 1920; ag. puisne judge, sup. ct., civil and criminal assizes, Malacca, Apr., June and Oct., 1920; ag. atty.-gen. and offl. mem., select comtees of leg. coun. on the constitution of the leg. coun. of the S. Sttlmts., 28th Oct., 1920 to 14th May, 1921; rep. of cols. and prots. at Empire patent confce., London, June, 1922.

SETON, CLAUD RAMSAY WILMOT.—B. 1888; ed. Framlingham Coll., Suffolk, Laleham, Margate and Univ. Coll., London; admitted solr., sup. ct., England, 1910; on mily. serv., 1914-19; M.C., twice ment. in desps.; pres., dist. ct., Jaffa, Palestine, 1919.

SEVERN, SYR CLAUN, K.B.E. (1923), C.M.G. (1917), M.A. (Cantab.), LL.D., Hong Kong (1920).—B. 1869; temp. olk. in librarian's dept., Foreign Office, Mar., 1891, to June, 1892; priv. sec. to gov', S. Sttlmts., Feb., 1894; junr. offr., Selangor, Dec., 1895; passed in Malay and law, 1898; astt. dist. offr., Ulu Langat, Selangor, June, 1897; astt. dist. offr., Serendah, May, 1899; ag. mag., Kuala Lumpur, 1900; astt. sec. to regir., F.M.S., Aug., 1903; ag. fed. sec., F.M.S., Apr. to Nov., 1905; ag. dist. offr., Ulu Langat, Dec., 1906, to Apr., 1907; ag. sec. to high commnr., F.M.S. and priv. sec. to gov', S. Sttlmts., May, 1907; sec. to high commnr., Malay States, Feb., 1911; col. sec., Hong Kong, Feb., 1912; adminstr. gov't. 16th Mar. to 3rd July, 1912, 21st Aug. to 24th Dec., 1913, 6th Nov. to 8th Dec., 1916, 10th Nov. to 16th Dec., 1917, 12th Sept., 1918 to 29th Sept., 1919, 8th Apr. to 11th May, 1921 and from 15th June to Oct., 1922; ret., 1926.

SEVIGNY, HON. ALBERT, K.C.—B. 1881; ed. Nicolet and Valleyfield (Quebec) Colls. and Laval Univ., B.A., L.L.L.; advoc., city of Quebec; unsuccessful candidate, legis. assem., Quebec, bye-elec., 1907; elec. to H. of C., Canada, g.e. 1911; dep. speaker, 1915; speaker, 1916; min. of inland rev., 1917; defeated at g.e., 1917; mem. of the P.C. for Canada; judge, superior ct., Quebec, 3rd Mar., 1921.

SEYMOUR, ALFRED WALLACE.—B. 1881; ed. Warwick Schol. and St. Andrew's Univ.; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1905; off. astt. to govt. agt., N.W. Prov., Mar., 1906; N.C. Prov., Apr., 1907; W. Prov., Nov., 1908; astt. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Aug., 1910; ditto, Trincomalee, June, 1911; astt. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Dec., 1912; ditto, Nuwara Eliya, Jan., 1913; ditto, Matale, Sept., 1915; on mil. duty, June, 1916; dist. judge, Chilaw, Aug., 1919; astt. govt. agt. Kegalla, Mar., 1920; govt. agt., N.C. Prov., May, 1921; registr. gen. Dec., 1924; col. sec., Fiji, Dec., 1926.

SEYMOUR-HALL, CHARLES CYRIL WILLIAM, F.R.S.A., F.R.G.S., F.R. Met. Soc., F.R.C.I., F.Z.S.—B. 1897; prob. flight offr., R.N.A.S., 1917; flight sub-lieut., R.N., 1917; lieut. R.A.F., 1918; ment. in des., 1919; capt. H.M.A.Z., 31, 1919; demob., 1919; astt. dist. comsnt., Zanzibar, 1921; ag. 2nd astt. sec. and priv. sec. to ag. Br. rea., 1923-4; ag. astt. admstr.-gen., 1926-27.

SHARP, GEORGE FREDERICK.—B. 1888; ed. Univ. Coll. Schol. and King's Coll., London; comptr. and acct.-gen.'s dept., G.P.O., 1907-12; acct., postal dept., S. Nigeria, 1912-14; acct., posts and tels. dept., N. and S. Provs. of Nigeria, 1914-19; ch. acct., posts and tels., Nigeria, 1920; on mil. serv., London Rifle Brig., 1908-12; Nigerian Land Contgt., 1914-18.

SHARPE, SIR ALFRED, K.C.M.G. (1903), C.B. (1897).—B. 1853; H.M.'s vice-consul, Nyasaland, 14th Feb., 1891; ditto, for territories under British influence North of the Zambesi, 18th Feb., 1893; consul for same territories, 1st Feb., 1894; ag. comsnt. and consul-gen. at Zomba, 7th May, 1894, to 3rd May, 1895; given rank of deputy comsnt., 1st Apr., 1896; and again acted as comsnt. and consul-gen., 16th Apr., 1896, to 14th July, 1897; comsnt. and consul-gen., for territories under Brit. infl. N. of Zambezi, 15th July, 1897; recd. Jubilee medal, 1897; comsnt., commdr.-in-chief, and consul-gen., Br. Cent. Africa Prot., 1st Jan., 1902; recd. Coronation medal, 1902; gov. of Nyasaland (late B.C.A. Prot.), 6th Sept., 1907; ret., 1910; represented E. African Prots. at the Coronation of H.M. King George V., 1911.
SHARPE, H. B.—Astt. dist. comsnt., Kenya Colony, Apr., 1919; ag. dist. comsnt., 1926.

SHARPE, RICHARD.—B. 1882; ed. Sexey's Schol., Somt. (Somt. county jnr. and inter. scholarships and hon. mention senr. scholarship); ent. Impl. cust. serv. by open compet. exam., and apptd. to London, 1903; London Univ. cert., polit. economy with sp. distinc. in exchange, finance and taxation, 1907; seconded to govt. of Liberia as sub-inspr., cust., 1908-10; ch. inspr., cust. and finan. adviser, 1911-12; Br. receiver of Liberian cust., 1911-19; offr. recr., liquidating German property in Liberia, 1917-18; knight comdtr., Order of African Redemption, 1913; treas., Dominica, Feb., 1920; mem. of exec. and legia. couns., collr. of cust. and excise, reg. of shipping, chmn., quarantine bd., Impl. trade corresp.; dep. contr., cust., Gold Coast, 3rd Jan., 1923; ag. contr., cust. on various occasions, 1923-25; contr., cust., 24th Sept., 1926.

SHAW, GEORGE ERNEST, O.B.E. (1919).—B. 1877; ed. Trin. Coll., Dublin; scholar and senr. moderator in classics; vice-chancellor's prize-man in English prose; B.A., LL.B.; cadet, F.M.S. div. ser., 1900; collr., land rev., Krian, 1901; mag., 1st cln., Perak, 1903; offr., Cls. V., F.M.S., 1st Jan., 1906; collr., land rev., Lower Perak, 1906; adviser, land office, Kedah, and supt. of mines, 1911; offr., Cls. IV., F.M.S., 1st Jan., 1912; supernmry. offr., Cls. III., F.M.S., 1st Jan., 1918; lent to W.O., directorate of prisoners of war, Aug., 1916; adviser, land off., Kedah, 19th July, 1919; ag. comsnt., lands and mines, Johora, May, 1923; ag. sec., agr., S.S. and F.M.S., Feb., 1924; on sp. duty, Burma, Oct., 1925; ag. sec., agr., S.S. and F.M.S., Nov., 1925; ag. Br. adviser, Kelantan, Dec., 1925; ag. gen. adviser, Johore, Sept., 1927.
SHAW, JOHN HENRY DE BUSCH, B.L.—B. 1887; ed. Clifton Schol., Dalkey, Dublin and Corrig Schol., Kingstown; astt. dist. comsnt., Sierra Leone, 25th May, 1921.

SHAW, SIR WALTER SIDNEY, KT. BACH. (1921).—B. 1863; ed. Brighton Coll. and Trin. Coll., Camb.; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1888; pol. mag. and ag. chief just., St. Vincent, 1906; mem. exec. and legis. couns.; confirmed as chief just., 1907; ag. chief just., Grenada, Feb., 1908, to June, 1909; revised and consolidated laws of St. Vincent, 1912; admstd. govt. of St. Vincent, June, 11 to Jan., 1912, and for various shorter periods; chief just., B. Honduras, 1912; puisne justice, Ceylon, Aug., 1914; ag. chief just., June to Oct., 1916; ch. just., S. Stlitma, 23rd May, 1921; ret., 1926.

SHEEDY, CAPT. FREDERICK JOHN, M.B.E., R.Sc., M.R.C.V.S.—Ed. Clongowes, Queen's Coll., Cork; late capt., K.A.P. forces; ent. army, A.V.C. 1911; vety. offr., E.A.P., July, 1914; served throughout Great War, 5th Aug., 1914 to 22nd Sept., 1919 (E. Africa Mounted Rifles and E.A.V.C.); adjutant, E.A.V.C., 9th Jan., 1916; M.B.E. (mil.), 1918, "1914-15" Star, Brit. War med., Victory med.; dep. ch. vety. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 31st Oct., 1919; ag. ch. vety. offr., on various occasions 1920-21, 1923-24 and 1926; dep. dir., vety. services, 1st Apr., 1927.

SHEEDY, ANDREW JOSEPH.—B. 1879; joined S. Stlitma's pol., Jan., 1902; ag. 3rd cls. inspr., Singapore, Aug., 1904; 3rd cls. inspr., Penang, Mar., 1906; 2nd cls. inspr., Penang, Aug., 1907; 1st cls. inspr., Penang, July, 1908; ag. ch. inspr., Singapore, Aug., 1909; ag. ch. detect. inspr., Singapore, Apr., 1910; ch. detect. inspr., Singapore, July, 1910; ag. astt. supt. pol., Singapore, Feb., 1911; astt. supt. pol., Singapore, Feb., 1911; ag. supt. pol., Singapore, Oct. and Dec., 1920; senr. astt. supt. pol., Singapore, Feb., 1925; dep. comsnt., F.M.S., July, 1926.

SHEEHAN, JOHN JOSEPH.—B. 1896; ed. Christian schls., Dublin Univ. and Univ. Coll., Dublin.—Pte., Artists Rifles, Dec., 1916; 2nd lieut., R.F.A., Sept., 1917; demob., May, 1919; cadet, F.M.S., Mar., 1920; ag. 2nd astt. adviser, Muar, Mar., 1921; ag. P.M.G., Johore, Oct., 1921; passed cadet, Nov., 1921; ag. collr., land rev., Johore Bharu, Jan., 1922; sec., rubber restriction, Johore, Oct., 1922; supernmry. offr., Cls. V., Mar., 1923; dep. contr., rubber expts., Johore, in addn., Aug., 1923; recr., Alasgoff concession, Kukub, in addn., June, 1924; attd. dist. office, Kinta, Mar., 1925; ag. dist. offr., Ipoh, Apr., 1925; off., cls. IV., Mar., 1927.

SHEFFIELD, JOHN NEWTON.—B. 1873; ed. Godolphin Sch., Lon.; civ. engnr. wks. and surveys, 1892-96; survr., trig. survey, Perak, 1896; on rly. locn. and surveys, Perak, 1896-98; on trig. surveys, F.M.S., 1899-1908; astt. supdt., topo. surveys, F.M.S., 1909; dep. supdt., ditto, 1917; sup't., ditto, 1920; asst. survr-gen., F.M.S., 1923.

SHELDON, ROBERT PAUL.—B. 1880; ed. Harrow and in Germany; ag. vice-consul, Ghent, 1905; ag. vice-consul, Havre, 1906-7; ag. consul, Bordeaux, 1907; asst. collr., Zanzibar, 27th Dec., 1907; sec. to first minister, 1909-10; collr. (now dist. commr.), Penma, 16th Oct., 1912; ag. mag., 30th Aug., 1915; ag. oh. sec., 5th Mar. to 17th Nov., 1919; senr. commr., Zanzibar, 20th Sept., 1923; 4th cls., Order of the Aliyeh and 3rd cls., Order of the Brilliant Star of Zanzibar; M.L.C.

SHELLEY, MALCOLM BOND.—B. 1879; ed. Dulwich Coll. and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1901; cadet, F.M.S. civ. serv., 1902; ag. asst. sup't., immigrts., 1906; ag. 2nd asst. sec. to res.-gen., 1907; ag. mag., Kuala Lumpur, 1910; ag. dist. offr., Batang Padang, 1911; commn. in M.S. Vol. Rifles, 1911; ag. asst. reg'nr., sup ct., Ipoh, 1912; ag. dist. offr., Larut, 1914; ag. inspr. of prisons, F.M.S., 1914; sept., M.S.V.R., 1915; ag. dep. pub. pros., Perak, 1916; ag. mag., Ipoh, 1920; off. assigned and pub. trustees, 1922; ag. dep. treas., Singapore, 1924; ag. treas., S.S., 1924; ag. dep. treas., Singapore, 1925; ag. dir., educn., S.S. and F.M.S., Dec., 1926; chmn., trade statistics comttee., Aug., 1926; contr., rubber, S.S. and F.M.S., Nov., 1926.

SHELWELL-WHITE, GEOFFREY HENRY.—B. 1897; ed. Whitgift Schl.; served with R.F.A. in India, Mesopotamia and Persia, 1916-18; asst. pol. offr., Mesopotamia, 1918-20; personal asst. to Sir Arnold Wilson, ag. civ. commn., 1918; Iraq rebellion, 1920; A.D.C., Zanzibar, 1921; priv. sec. to res. and 2nd asst. sec., secretariat, in 1921 to 1924; clk. of coun., 1924; ag. 1st asst. sec., 10th Sept. to 29th Dec., 1924.
SHELTON, C. F., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.D. (Lond.), B.S.—Civil surgeon, Serbian red cross, 1913; surgeon lieut., 1914-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1921.

SHEPPARD, WALTER SYDNEY, B.A., M.B., B. Ch. (Cantab.).—B. 1871; civil surg. in charge of H.M.'s troops, N.E. dist., Eng., May, 1898, to May, 1899; spec. plague med. offr., India, May, 1899, to Nov., 1900; house surg., gen. hosp., Singapore, 19th Nov., 1900; super. col. surg., S. Stltmtns, 1st Mar., 1901; spec. mission to Brunei, Aug., 1904; col. surg., Prov. Wellesley, Aug., 1906; senior med. offr., Singapore, July, 1911.
SHERGOLD, F. V.—B. 1900; apptd. after compet. exam. asst. clk., C.O., Apr., 1918; on mily. serv., 1918 to 1920; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920.

SHERIDAN, JOSEPH.—Ed. at Castleknock Coll. and Trin. Coll., Dublin; called to the bar, Ireland, 1907, went Connaught circuit; judcl. clk., Nyassaland Prot., July, 1908; ag. atty.-gen., Nov., 1909, to Jan., 1910; ag. judge of high ct., Dec., 1909, to Jan., 1910; ag. atty.-gen., July, 1911, to Mar., 1912; apptd. asst. to atty.-gen., Apr., 1912; ag. atty.-gen., Aug., 1912; town mag., E.A.P., 1913; ag. puisme judge, July, 1919; 3rd puisme judge, Kenya, Apr., 1920.

SHERWOOD, MONTAGUE EARLE, M.B.E.—B. 1884; cadet, S. Stltmtns, 21st Oct., 1907; attached to col. sec.'s office, 1st Jan., 1908; attached to land office, Singapore, 23rd Jan., 1908; dep. collr. of land rev. and dep. reg'nr. of deeds, Singapore, 31st Jan., 1908; ag. dist. offr., Labuan, 29th Jan. to 16th Nov., 1909; attached to dist. office, Alor Gajah, 28th Dec., 1910; passed final exam. in Malay, Jan., 1911; passed cadet, 25th Jan., 1911; attached to land office, Malacca, 1st Mar., 1911; ag. dist. offr., Jasin, 7th Apr., 1911; offr. of Cls. V., 13th May, 1911; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, 15th Oct., 1913; ag. dist. offr., Alor Gajah, 18th Feb., 1914; ag. ast. to res., Malacca, 1st Sept., 1915; ag. harbr.-mast., Malacca, in addition to other duties, 26th Jan., 1916; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to gov., 1st Aug., 1916; offr. of Cls. III., 16th Nov., 1919; ag. priv. sec. to H.E. the gov., Feb., 1920; ag. sec. to high commn., March, 1921.
SHIELD, CHARLES GEOFFREY.—B. 1896; on mily. service (France) 1914-18; on staff, 1918-19; priv. sec'y. to gov., and clk. to couns., Seychelles, 1920.
SHIELDS, W. P.—Labour inspection offr., E. Africa Prot., Apl., 1919.
SHIPWAY, WALTER ROBERT, M.B.E. (1924).—B. 1879; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd div. clk., and assigned to C.O., Dec., 1908; minor staff clk., 1911; reg'nr. of col. laws, 1st Apr., 1922.

SHIRCORE, JOHN OWEN, C.M.G. (1926), M.B., Ch.B.—Med. offr., Nyassaland, 1908; transf'd. to Uganda, 1909; re-transf'd. as med. offr., Nyassaland, 1910; med. offr., E.A.P., 1912; dep. prin. med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 15th Oct., 1919; ag. P.M.O., Mar.-Dec., 1924; P.M.O., 22nd Dec., 1924.

SHORTT, ADAM, C.M.G. (1911); M.A., F.R.S.C.—B. 1869; ed. at Walkerton High Schl. and Queen's Univ., Canada (B.A., 1883, M.A., 1886) and Glasgow and Edin. Univ.; asst. prof. of philosophy, Queen's Univ., 1885; lect. and prof. of polit. science, 1889-1908; civ. ser. commn., Canada, 1908-1917; chmn. of historical manuscripts publication board, Oct., 1917; writer on history, banking and economics; del. to internat. congress of history of America, Rio de Janeiro, 1922; mem., proprietar. coman. for econ. confere., L. of N., Geneva, 1926.

SHREWSBURY, HERBERT SUTCLIFFE, F.I.C., F.C.S.—B. 1878; ed. Newchurch Grammar Schl., Nottingham Univ.; asst. govt. analyst, Trinidad, 1910; prin. asst. govt. analyst, 1912; govt. analyst, 1920; author of various papers on orig. and analytical processes, and on agrl. and forensic chemistry.

SHUCKBURGH, SIR JOHN EVELYN, K.C.M.G. (1922), C.B. (1918).—B. 1877; ed. Eton and King's Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1899; M.A., 1906; apptd. jnr. clk., store dept., I. O., Oct., 1900; transf'd. to judcl. and pub. dept., Mar., 1901; joint editor, "India Office List," Oct., 1901; priv. sec. to perm'nt. under-secr. of state, July, 1902; senr. clk., polit. dept., Mar., 1906; ast. sec., polit. dept., Oct., 1912; sec., polit. dept., Mar., 1917; ag. ast. under sec. of state, C. O., 1st Mar., 1921; ast. under sec. of state, 1st Apr., 1924.

SIBBALD, SAMUEL KAY, A.M.I.C.E., M.T.P.I.—B. 1878; ed. Dumfries Acad. and West Scotland Tech. Coll.; ast. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., 1901; ast. to govt. town planner, F.M.S., 1921; ag. govt. town planner, F.M.S., 1925.

SIDEBOTHAM, JOHN BIDDULPH, B.A. (1914), M.A. (1920).—B. 1891; ed. King's Schl., Canterbury, and Gonville and Caius Coll., Cambridge (Stanhope exhibr.; open class exhibr., 1911, schol., 1912); 1st cls., theolog. tripes, pt. I., 1914; 2nd lieut., Home Counties R.E. (T.F.), 1914; lieut., 13th June, 1916; serv. in France, 1914-15; wounded, Apr., 1915; demob., 1919; ent. inland rev., Somerset House, 7th Feb., 1920; transf'd. to C.O. as ast. prim. under reconstruction scheme, Dec., 1922; sec., managing comttee., bureau of hygiene and trop. diseases, 1925; sec., East African guaranteed loan advisory comttee., 1927.

SIDLEY, WILLIAM HENRY FRANCIS, B.A. (Trinity Coll., Dublin).—Cadet, R.I.C., Aug., 1908; 3rd cls. dist. inspr., Oct., 1908; 2nd cls., Feb., 1910; 1st cls., Aug., 1919; county inspr., Nov., 1920; served in R.I.C. until disbandment of that force, July, 1922; depot inspr. (1st cls.), Jamaica constab., Dec., 1922; ag. dep. inspr., gen., Aug., 1923; dep. inspr.-gen., Dec., 1923; ag. inspr.-gen., 1st June to 14th Oct., 1925.

SIFTON, HON. SIR CLIFFORD, K.C.M.G. (1915), K.C.—B. 1861, Co. Middlesex, Ont.; ed. London, Ont., High Schl. and Victoria Univ. (Prince of Wales Medallist, 1880); called to the bar of Manitoba, 1882; elec. to legis. assem., Man., 1888; atty.-genl. and min. of educn., 1891-96; codified laws relating to civil procedure in Man.; had charge of negotiations with federal govt. respecting the Manitoba Schl. Law; Q.C. (Dominion), 1896; called to federal cabinet as min. of the interior, 1896; elec. to H. of C. by accl. for Brandon; British agt. before the Alaska Bdny. Tribunal, 1903; re-elec. to H. of C. 1900 and 1904; resig. from the cabinet, 1905; one of Canadian representatives at internat. confce. at Washington on preservation of natural resources, 1908; re-elec. to H. of C., 1908; chmn. Canadian comsn. of conservation 1909-1918; did not seek re-elec. at g.e., 1911.

SIKES, HOWARD LECY, B.A., B.E. (hons. and ext.), R.U.I., M.I.C.E., F.G.S.—B. 1881; ed. Bootham Schl., York; astt. engrnr., P.W.D., E. Africa Prot., 1907; res. engrnr. Mombasa water wks., 1911; engrnr. in ch., Magadi water wks. constrn., 1913; res. engrnr., Magadi rly., 1915; lieut., E.A. Pioneers, 1915; capt., 1916; gov't. hydraulic engrnr., P.W.D., Kenya, Jan., 1919; dep. dir., pub. wks., Kenya, 1921; D.P.W. and offl. mem., leg. coun., 1923.
SILVA, PADUWEE OSUND SAMUEL EDERSINGHE, B.A. (Lond.).—B. 1904; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Feb., 1927; attd., Puttalam kach., Mar., 1927.
SILVESTER, J. M.—Ast. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., Dec., 1913; ag. dist. comsnr., Kenya, 1926.
SIM, HON. SIR WM. ALEXANDER, Kt. Bach. (1924).—B. 1858; judge of sup. ct. of New Zealand, 16th Jan., 1911.

SIMMONS, JAMES WILLIAM.—B. 1877; ed. at Malvern and Oxford; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1900; in charge of treasy., Seremban, 29th May, 1901; ag. astt. dist. offr., Tampin, 1st Aug., 1901; ag. sec. sany. bd., Seremban, 1st Apr., 1902; ag. inspr. of schls. in addn., 1904; 1st astt. collr. of land rev., Seremban, 1st Jan., 1905; ag. astt. collr. of land rev., Kuala Lumpur, 13th Nov., 1907; ag. astt. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, 6th May, 1909; comsnr. of cust., Johore, 21st Dec., 1910; comsnr. of lands and mines, Johore, 9th Oct., 1914 to 4th June, 1920; sec. to comsnr. of enquiry, Trengganu, 5th Sept., 1918; 1st mag., Kuala Lumpur, 10th Mar., 1921; attd. to col. secretariat, Singapore, 9th June, 1921; 1st astt. sec. A., S. Sttlmts., 9th June, 1921; ag. finan. comsnr., Johore, 6th Dec., 1921; ag. collr., land rev., S. Sttlmts., 8th May, 1922; dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, Sept., 1922; ag. dist. offr., Kinta, July, 1923; ag. chmn., sany. bd., Kuala Lumpur, Nov., 1924; ag. Br. advisor, Trengganu, May, 1925.

SIMMONS, HON. WM. CHAR.—B. 1865; ed. at Collingwood and Owen Sound High Schls.; B.A., Toronto Univ., 1896; called to the bar, N.W.T., 1900; elec. to legis. assem. of Alberta for Lethbridge dist., 1906; resig., Oct., 1908; puisne judge of sup. ct., Alberta, 12th Oct., 1910.
SIMMS, HENRY, M.V.O. (1917), A.M.I.C.R. (1913).—B. 1881; sany. engrnr., Gold Coast, 1908; sany. engrnr., Sierra Leone, 1912; D.P.W., Cyprus, 1919; D.P.W., Jamaica, 1926.
SIMPSON, JOHN.—Imp. tel. serv., Jan., 1892; transf., post office, Cape of Good Hope, Nov., 1895; survr., posts and tels., Mar., 1920; pub. serv. inspr., Aug., 1921.

SIMPSON, SAMUEL C.M.G. (1926).—B. 1876; ed. at Owen's Coll., and Edin. Univ.; B.Sc.; Stevens scholar; sec. to the Union; sen. pres. of students' representative coun.; Highland and agric. soc'y.'s prizeman; life mem. and silver medallist of roy. agric. soc'y.; holder of nat. diploma in agric.; sen. lecturer in agric. to govt. agric. coll., Ghizeh, Egypt; cotton expert to B.C.A. Prot., Mar., 1905; dir. of agric., Uganda, 1912; mem., leg. and exec. couns.; commr. for Uganda at Br. Emp. Exhbln.; author of "Report on Cotton Growing Industry in B.C.A. Prot."; reported on the agric. resources of Angola, Trinidad, Tobago, B. Guiana and Surinam.
SIMS, JAMES HUGH.—B. 1878; sub. inspr., Basutoland Mounted Pol., 1905; inspr., 1913; astt. commr., 1918.

SINCLAIR, HON. JOHN EWEN.—B. 1879; ed. local common schls.; mem. of swine coman. which visited Europe in 1909; unsucc. cand., legis. ass., P.E.I., 1908; el. to H.C., g.e., 1917; re-el., 1921; min. without portfolio in King admstn., 29th Dec., 1921.
SINCLAIR, HON. SIR J. R., KT. BACH. (1918).—B. 1851; ed. Dunedin High Schl., N.Z.; barrister; M.L.C., N.Z., 1907-14 and since 1918; rep. N.Z. on Dominion Royal Comsn., 1912.

SINCLAIR, ST. CLAIR OVERBEKE, M.A., F.I.C., D.Sc.—Clt., atty.-gen's. office, 1893; clk., col. sec.'s office, 1894; analyst, 1897; transf., dept. of agr., 1907; transf., dept. of int., 1911; govt. analyst, Cape Town, 1918; transf., dept. of agr., 1st Apr., 1923; chief of divn. of chemistry, 3rd Mar., 1925.

SINCLAIR, WILLIAM FRANCIS.—B. 1879; ed. Oxenford House, Jersey; served in S. African War as lieut., 1st Kitchener's Fighting Scouts; Queen's Med., 5 bars; served in Gt. War as lieut., 5th D.G.'s Imp. Camel Corps and sp. list, in Flanders, Egypt and Palestine, 1914-20; comdt., grade II, Palestine pol., July, 1920; comdt., grade I, Nov., 1920; dist. supt., Palestine pol., Apr., 1926.

SINGAPORE, LORD BISHOP OF, RIGHT REV. CHARLES JAMES FERGUSON-DAVIE, B.A., M.A., D.D.—Formerly curate of St. Paul, Preston, 1896-99; dom. chap. to Bishop of Lahore, 1899-1902; S.P.G. mission at Rewari, 1902-1907; S.P.G. mission at Rawal Pindi, 1907-09; consecrated Lord Bishop of Singapore, 24th Aug., 1909.

SIRCOM, HABOLD SEBASTIAN.—B. 1878; cadet, F.M.S., 1902; passed cadet, May, 1904; ag. astt. sec. to res., Selangor, Aug., 1904; ag. astt. dist. offr., Ulu Langat, May, 1906; ag. dist. offr., Temerloh, May, 1907; ag. astt. dist. offr., Pekan, Nov., 1907; cls. V, Jan., 1910; ag. dist. offr., Pekan, Apr., 1910; seconded, comsnr. of cust., Johore, 1913; cls. IV, Nov., 1913; ag. dist. offr., Butterworth, Dec., 1917; cls. III, Jan., 1918; seconded, astt. advisor, Muar, Johore, June, 1918; cls. II, Nov., 1919; ag. comsnr. of lands and mines, Johore, June, 1920; astt. advisor, Muar, Apr., 1921; mag., Kuala Lumpur, Oct., 1922; dist. offr., Ulu Selangor, Jan., 1923; chmn., sany. bd., Apr., 1923; sec. to res., Selangor, May, 1923; ag. Br. res., Selangor in addition, Oct., 1923; contr., rubber, S.S. and F.M.S., May, 1925.

SISNETT, SIR HERBERT KORTRIGHT McDONNELL, KT. BACH. (1927).—Ed. at Harrison Coll., Barbados; called to the bar, Inner Temp., May, 1896; practised at bar, Barbados, June, 1897, to Feb., 1898; ag. junior pol. mag., Bridgetown, Barbados, Sept. to Nov., 1897; practised at bar, Jamaica, Mar., 1898, to Apr., 1903, during which period acted as res. mag. for St. Ann's, June to Dec., 1901; as priv. sec. to Sir A. Hemming, May to June, 1902, and priv. sec. to Mr. Olivier, the ag. gov., June to Nov., 1902; olk. of cts., St. James, Jamaica, Apr., 1903; ag. res. mag., St. James and Trelawny, Sept., 1903, as res. mag., St. Elizabeth, Mar., 1904, as res. mag., Manchester, Apr. to June, 1904, as res. mag. St. James and Trelawny, Sept., 1904, for two and a half months in 1906, and from June to July, 1906; registr.-gen., Br. Hond.; and dist. comsrr. of Belize, Apr., 1907; ag. atty.-gen. and ag. ch. just., Br. Hond., on several occasions; J.P. for Br. Hond., 1907; stip. mag., B. Guiana, 8th Mar., 1913; held inquiry into Rose Hall coolie riots, 1913, and was highly commended by S. of S.; ch. just., B. Hond., 1923.

SITTA PALAM, CATHIVAVELU, B.A. (Cantab.), Barrister-at-law (Middle Temple).—B. 1893; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Feb., 1923; attd. to Kalutara kach., Mar., 1923; ditto, Kandy kach., Mar., 1925; office asst., Puttalam kach., Aug., 1926.

SKEEN, DONALD TRENCH, M.B., Ch.B.—B. 1883; ed. Aberdeen Grammar Schl. and Aberdeen Univ.; med. offr., F.M.S., grade III, May, 1912; med. offr., grade II, Dec., 1912; war service, 1915-19; med. offr., Batu Gajah, Sept., 1919; senr. med. offr., Negri Sembilan, May, 1921; ag. senr. med. offr., Perak, Feb., 1924; senr. med. offr., Negri Sembilan, Oct., 1924.

SKELTON, OSCAR DOUGLAS, M.A., Ph.D.—B. 1878; ed. Orangeville and Cornwall High Schls., Queen's Univ. and Univ. of Chicago; counsellor, dept. of external affrs., 15th July, 1924; under-sec. of state for external affrs., 30th Mar., 1925.

SKENE, CHARLES EDWARD.—B. 1889; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Wren's; asst. dist. comsrr., Gold Coast, Nov., 1912; seconded for serv. in col. sec.'s office, 1912-13; dist. comsrr., G. Coast, 27th Nov., 1912; col. sec.'s office, 1912-15; priv. sec. to ag. gov., 1914; ag. dist. comsrr., Ashanti, 1916-18; dist. comsrr., 6th Oct., 1918.
SKERETT, HON. SIR CHARLES PERRIN, K.C.M.G. (1927), K.C.—B. 1863; ed. privately; called to bar, 1884; created K.C., 1907; ch. just., N.Z., 1926; for many years pres., N.Z. law soc'y.

SKINNER, ARTHUR DONALD WILLIAM.—B. 1890; ed. Bedford Schl.; 4th clk., col. sec.'s office, Antigua, Aug., 1914; sub-inspr. of schools, St. Kitts-Nevis, Apr., 1915; o/c cadet corps, St. Kitts-Nevis, 1917; ag. inspr. of schl., Leeward Is., Apr., 1918 to Feb. 1920; clk. to comsrr., clk. of couns. and educnl. dist. offr., Montserrat, Feb.-Oct., 1920; 1st clk. col. sec.'s office, clk. of couns. Antigua and supt., govt. printing office, Leeward Is., Nov., 1920; ag. astl. col. sec. and ag. clk. of couns. Leeward Is. on various occasions 1921-24; senr. bursar, Achimota Coll., Gold Coast, 1927.

SKINNER, HARVEY.—B. 1871; ed. Hurstpierpoint and Ardingly Colls.; asst. acctnt., treasy. N. W. Rhodesia, 5th June, 1907; ag. acctnt., N. Rhodesia, on various occasions, 1917-21; asst. audr., 17th Feb., 1921; ag. audr., on various occasions, 1921-26.

SKINNER, LESLIE ERNEST.—Gloucestershire R.F.A., 3rd Apr., 1908 to 9th July, 1910; B.S.A. Pol., 22nd Aug., 1910 to 21st Aug., 1913; E.A.P. Police, 17th Sept., 1913; asst. supt., 1st Apr., 1915; staff offr. to inspr.-gen. of pol. and prisons, Kenya Colony and Uganda Pol., 1st May, 1920 to 28th Feb., 1922; qualified in senior captain's course, Schl. of Musketry, Hythe, Sept., 1921; supt. pol., Nyassaland, 11th Nov., 1922; ag. ch. comsrr., pol. and ch. inspr., prisons, 17th May, 1923 to 31st Jan., 1924; asst. commsdt., pol. and gov. prisons, Oct., 1925 to June, 1926.

SKINNER, ROBERT BURNELL.—B. 1893; ed. Bedford Modern Schl.; held various minor appts., Leeward Is., 1910-20; ch. clk. to admr., and clk., couns., St. Kitts-Nevis, Nov., 1920; ch. clk. to admr. and clk., couns., Dominica, Nov., 1924; sec., loan bd., May, 1926; mem., Roseau town bd., Apr., 1927; comsrr., income tax, May, 1927.

SKINNER, WILLIAM ALEXANDER GEORGE.—B. 1865; joined N.Z. printing and stationery dept., 1879; offr.-in-charge, Lyttelton prison printing office, 1889; overseer, govt. printing office, 1905; supt., 1909; govt. printer, 1922.

SKIRVING, JOHN MACKENZIE.—B. 1874; ed. at George Watson's Coll., Edin.; served nine years in 91st Highrs.; 1st cls. certif. of eductn., Group I, 1894; certif. of mil. eng., 1896; on Boer prisoners of war staff as warrant offr., S. Africa and St. Helena, Nov., 1899, to Oct., 1902 (Queen's medal and clasp); inspr. of pol., gaoler and mag.'s clk., St. Helena, Mar., 1903; served in various offl. capacities in St. Helena, Antigua, Montserrat and Dominica, 1906-14; ex-officio J.P. for Leeward Islands; ag. mag., Dist. F., May-Aug., 1914; capt. (2nd in command), Dominica defence force, 4th Aug., 1914; comdg. local forces, Dominica, 13th Sept., 1914; in command of 3rd (Leeward Is.) contingent for B.W.I.R., at Barbados, June-July, 1917; also paying offr. and offr.-in-charge records and dispersal, B.W.I. Regt., Dominica, from 1st Nov., 1917 to 29th Oct., 1920; inspr. pol., offr. instr. to local forces and in charge of prison, fire brig. and weights and measures depot, Montserrat, Oct., 1920; ag. in charge of P.W.D., and mem. exec. and leg. couns., Apr., 1925; re-transf'd. to Antigua as ch. keeper of prisons, supt., St. John's Training Schl. and supt., pauper cemetery, Leeward Is., 1st Nov., 1924; dep. coroner, 1926.
SLACK, ERIC M.B., L.R.C.P. (Lond.), certif., L.S.T.M.—B. 1872; med. offr., Basutoland leper asylum, 1919; war serv., R.A.M.C., 1915-17.

SLACK, KENNETH HUGH.—B. 1885; ag. clk., mag., dist. "C," St. Kitts, in 1905, 1906 and 1907; clk. and inspr. wks., P.W.D., Jan., 1909; rev. offr., Dieppe Bay, Apr., 1910; govt. offr. and harbmr., Sandy Point, July, 1914; 3rd rev. offr., Apr., 1915; ag. 1st rev. offr. and harbmr., 1916; 2nd rev. offr., Jan., 1917; ag. 1st rev. offr. and harbmr., 1919; dep. harbmr., Apr., 1920; ag. cashier, 1920; ag. 1st rev. offr. and harbmr., 1922; lieut., St. Kitts-Nevis Defence Force.
SLADE-HAWKINS, W.—Asst. dist. comsrr., E. Africa Prot., Apr., 1919.

SLADER, C. H. YORKE.—Ent. Jamaica Govt. serv., 1883; 1st cls. clk., sup. ct., 1894; ag. regis., 1895-7; lieut., Jamaica militia, 1895; offr. with Jubilee contingent (medal), 1897; capt., 1899; passed exam. equal to solrs. final, 1899; clk. of cts., Clarendon, 1900; ag. comsrr. and judge of grand court, Cayman Islands, 1906; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, 1907; ag. judge, sup. ct., Turks Island, 1909; J.P. and stip. mag., Kingston, Oct., 1909; res. mag., Portland, Jan., 1910; res. mag., Clarendon, 1912; ditto, St. Ann, 1914; major, res. regt., 1914; res. mag., St. Elizabeth, 1922; ditto, St. Catherine, 1926; compiled 3rd edit. "Index to Laws of Jamaica," 1911; ed. 5th edit., "Sub-Officer's Guide," 1924.

SLATER, Sir ALEXANDER RANSFORD, K.C.M.G. (1924); C.M.G. (1916); C.B.E. (1918).—B. 1874; ed. at King Ed. schl., Birmingham and Emm. Coll., Camb. (scholar); B.A. 1897 (30th wrangler); cadet, Ceylon, Nov., 1898; extra offr. ast. govt. agt., W. P., Aug., 1899; 2nd astt., P.M.G., Mar., 1900; ag. 2nd astt. col. sec., Dec., 1901; on spec. duty at camp for prisoners of war Diyatatalawa, July, 1902; ag. 2nd astt. col. sec., Jan., 1903; clk. legis. coun., May, 1904; dist. judge, Badulla, Apr., 1906; additional astt. col. sec. and clk., legis. couns., Apr., 1907; on special deptn. to Straits and F.M.S., Jan., 1908; 2nd astt. col. sec., Feb., 1908; ag. prin. astt. col. sec., Dec., 1909; dep. collr. of cust., Colombo, Sept., 1910; joint comsnr. to report on salaries of pub. serv., Nov., 1911; prin. astt. col. sec., Sept., 1912, and clk. to exec. coun.; col. sec., G. Coast, 18th Aug., 1914; on special duty in Togoland, 1st to 28th Sept., 1915; ag. govr., G. Coast on several occasions; gov. and comdr.-in-chief, Sierra Leone, 4th Apr., 1922; gov. and comdr-in-chief, Gold Coast, 1927.

SLEEP, Capt. ARTHUR.—B. 1894; cadet, F.M.S., Feb., 1920; astt. supt., lands, Kelantan, Mar., 1923; attd. dist. office, Lower Perak, Dec., 1924; astt. dist. offr., Brusas, Mar., 1925; offr., cl. IV, Mar., 1926; offr., cls.IV, Mar., 1926; dist. offr., Kualas Selangor, Aug., 1926.

SLOAN, Hon. WILLIAM.—B. 1866; took up residence in British Columbia in 1888, residing at Vancouver and Nanaimo; unsuccessful cand. for H. of C., 1900; elec. to H. of C., 1904, for Comox-Atlin; re-elec., 1908, but resigned in favour of Hon. William Templeman, min. of in. rev., who was defeated in Victoria; elec. to legis. ass., B.C., 1916; re-elec., g.e., 1920; min. of mines in Brewster cabinet, 1916.

SLOLEY, Sir HERBERT CECIL, K.C.M.G. (1911); C.M.G. (1905).—Served in C.M.R. in campaigns against Griquas and Moirosi (medal with clasp, 1877-8-9); capt. in native contingent, Basuto war, 1880-1; mentioned in despatches; sub-inspr., Cape police, 1883; ditto, Basutoland, 1884; inspr., 1886; astt. comnr., Basutoland, 1889; ag. res. comnr., July to Dec., 1895; govt. sec., 1898; ag. res. comnr., Dec., 1900; res. comnr., Sept., 1901; ret., 1926.

SMALL, Alexander SYM.—B. 1887; ed. Glasgow Univ.; cadet, F.M.S., Jan., 1911; ag. astt. sec. to res., Selangor, Aug., 1911; ag. dist. offr., Kuala Langat, Apr., 1912; ag. sec. to res., Negri Sembilan, Jan., 1913; ag. astt. dist. offr., Krian, Jan., 1916; ag. dist. offr., Raub, May, 1916; ag. astt. adviser, Endau, Johore, Sept., 1918; ditto Muar, Johore, June, 1920; ag. astt. dir., educn., S.S. and F.M.S., Apr., 1922; ag. astt. treas., F.M.S. and state treas., Selangor, Nov., 1923; town-planning admstr., Kuala Lumpur, Dec., 1926; finan. comnr. and audr-gen., Johore, Mar., 1927.

SMALLEY, James THORNTON, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.).—B. 1882; late clinical astt., astt. house surg., out-pat. offr. and house surg., Guy's hosp.; clinical astt., Evelina hosp., London; R.M.O., Colony hosp., Suva, Fiji; D.M.O., Ra; D.M.O. and M.O.H., and port med. offr., Levuka; stip. mag., Levuka, 1910-1913; med. offr., Kowloon and New Territories, Hong Kong, med. offr., Kowloon-Canton rly., and med. offr. in charge, pub. mortuary, Kowloon, 1913; ag. astt. M.O.H., 1916; ditto, in addition to other duties, 1916; lecturer in Pharmacology and Therapeutics, and examiner in Physiology, Hong Kong Univ.; J.P., 1919; visiting med. offr., Kwong Wah hospital; med. offr. in charge, Tai Chi Kok branch prison; surg. lieut., Hong Kong Volunteer Corps; med. offr. govt. civil hosp., 1923; med. offr., lunatic asylum, 1923; ag. port med. offr., 1924; med. offr., Kowloon and New Territories, med. offr. in charge pub. mortuary, Kowloon and Kowloon pub. hosp., 1925; med. offr. in charge, Victoria hosp. for women and children, 1927.
SMART, L. M.—B. 1889; astt. supt., Kenya and Uganda Rly., Apr., 1919 to Mar., 1927; dep. traffic man., Tanganyika rly., 26th Mar., 1927.
SMARTT, FitzPATRICK FORBES Percy.—Ed. Denstone Coll.; probationer, col. audit dept., July, 1921; astt. audr., Br. Honduras, Oct., 1921.

SMARTT, Rt. Hon Sir THOS. WM., P.C. (1921), K.C.M.G. (1911).—L.R.C.S., Ireland, 1878; L.K.Q.C.P.I., 1880; M.L.A., Cape Colony, since 1894; col. sec., 1898; comnr. of pub. wka., 1900-02, and 1904-1908; ag. prime minister during periods of 1904, 1906 and 1906; attended Impl. confce., 1907; mem. of S. African Nat. Conventn. in connection with S. Africa Bill, 1909; holds Cape Colony general service medal, 1879, with clasp, "Transaik," having served as surg. with P.A.O.C.V.A.; also holds medal and clasp for Kimberley siege, 1899-1900; M.L.A. for Fort Beaufort; min. for agr., 1921-24.

SMIT, Harold PRINGLE.—B. 1883; joined Cape civ. serv., Jan., 1901; office of dir. of mil. intell., May-Aug., 1902; Transvaal law dept., 1st Sept., 1902; ch. clk. to atty.-gen., Transvaal, July, 1908; addl. mag., Benoni, Sept., 1914; war stores coman., 1915; mag., Bloemhof dist., Dec., 1915; ch. clk., dept. of just., Dec., 1917; pub. serv. inspr., Feb., 1922; sec. for S.W. Africa, Apr., 1923; dep. admstr., 19th May-31st Oct., 1924.

SMIT, Jacobus Stephanus.—B. 1878; D.T.D. (Dekoratie Trouwe Dienst) Anglo-Boer War; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1905; prte. sec. to Gen. Smuts as col. sec., Transvaal, 1907; to Gen. Botha as premier, Transvaal, 1907 and to Hon. J. de Villiers as atty.-gen., Transvaal, 1908; astt. mag., Transvaal, 1909; astt. mag., Union civ. serv., 1910; resig., 1915; el. mem. for Klarsdorp, Union of S. Africa legis. assem., 1920, 1921 and 1924; high comnr. for Union of S. Africa, London, 1925.

SMITH, Allan F., I.S.O. (1906).—Clk in P.O., Bermuda, 1879; clk. col. sec.'s office, 1886; astt. col. sec., 1895; col. postm., 1896; ag. col. sec., July and Aug., 1899, and from May, 1900 to July, 1901; recvr.-gen., 1908.

SMITH, Lieut. Arthur Bertram, F.R.G.S.—B. 1889; ed. R. Masonic Schl., Bushey; sub. lieut., R.N.R., Oct., 1914; ag. lieut., Jan., 1915; served in 8th submarine flotilla, 1914-15; Baltic submarine flotilla, 1915-17; Russian Order of St. Anne, 3rd cls. (with swords), 1915; Russian Order of St. Vladimir, 4th cls. (with swords), 1916; commanded submarine C 14 (N. Sea), Mar. to Nov., 1918, and various surrendered German submarines, Nov., 1918 to Mar., 1919; port offr., Dar-es-Salaam, Mar., 1919; harmr. and supt. of lighthouses, Trinidad, 27th Feb., 1926.

SMITH, B. O.—Cler. asst., post. dept., Br. Guiana, Aug., 1896; sec., leave and pensions comm., 1920; sec. deptl. salaries comm., 1921; gradually promoted to 1st cls. clk. and sec. currency comm., July, 1923; transf. to col. secretariat, Apr., 1925; acctnt., sup. ct., Oct., 1925.

SMITH, CHARLES BERNARD, B.A.—B. 1887; ed. Pembroke Coll., Oxford; jnr. supt., educn., Dept., N. Nigeria, 9th July, 1913; attached, Nigeria Regt., 1917-18; asst. dir., educn., S. Provs., Nigeria, 1926.

SMITH, CHRISTOPHER PATRICK, B.A.—B. 1894; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Dublin and National Univ. of Ireland; Inn of Court O.T.C., Oct., 1915; cadet, R. Mily. Coll., Sandhurst, 1916; 2nd lieut., R. Irish Regt., attd. Machine Gun Corps, 1917; demob., 1919; dep. supt., merc. marine branch, B. of T. 1920; cadet, F.M.S., Apr., 1921; attd., Chinese prot., Perak, May, 1921; ag. asst. prot., Chinese, Selangor and Pahang, Feb., 1924; passed cadet, Feb., 1924; ag. prot., Chinese, Negri Sembilan, May, 1924, ditto, Taiping, Dec., 1924; offr., cls. IV, Sept., 1925; attd., cust. dept., F.M.S., Mar., 1926; ag. 3rd mag., S'pore, May, 1926.
SMITH, E. A.—B. 1896; ent. E.I.O., Dec., 1911; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., Dec., 1913; on mily. serv., Dec., 1915 to Jan., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920.
SMITH, EMILE HAMEL, L.R.C.P., L.R.C.S., L.F.P. and S., Glas.—B. 1879; ent. med. serv., Trinidad, 16th Sept., 1909; dist. med. offr., 21st Dec., 1912.

SMITH, ERNEST GARDINER, M.A., LL.B.—Advoc.; univ. prizeman in constit. law and hist.; hons. certifs. in civ. law, pub. internat. law and med. jurisprudence; asst. dist. comm., S. Nigeria, 29th Aug., 1908; asst. pol. mag., 23rd Jan., 1911; 2nd grade mag., Nigeria, 1st Jan., 1914; 1st grade mag., 29th Jan., 1914; crown proscr., 10th Sept., 1914; ag. legal adviser, and mem., leg. coun. for various periods, 1914-19; crown counsel, 1st Jan., 1920; ag. solr.-gen., Aug., 1920 to Apr., 1921, and Aug., 1921 to Mar., 1922; circuit judge, Ashanti and N. Territories, G. Coast, 9th Aug., 1922; ag. puisne judge, G. Coast, 1st Apr. to 4th July, 1924; puisne judge, G. Coast, 12th June, 1925.

SMITH, FRANCIS JACOB.—M.A. (Oxon); ed. Merchant Taylors schl.; scholar, St. John's Coll., Oxford, 1892; 2nd cls. class. mods., 1894; 2nd math. mods., 1894; 2nd lit. hum., 1896; B.A., 1896; M.A., 1902; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Oct., 1896; attached to col. sec. offr., 1896; office asst. to gov't. agt., and deputy fiscal, Cent. Prov., Apr., 1898; commr. of requests and pol. mag., Balapitiya, Aug., 1900; ditto, Galle, June, 1901; landing survr., customs, Colombo, Dec., 1901; 2nd ast. col. sec., Sept., 1905; dep. collr. of cust., Colombo, Feb., 1908; dist. judge, Galle, Dec., 1911; gov't. agt., Prov. of Uva, July, 1912; on special duty, visiting post offices in Ceylon, Straits and F.M.S., May, 1913; ag. dist. judge, Galle, Aug., 1913; postm.-gen., Oct., 1913; dir. of food production, Nov., 1920; P.M.G., Apr., 1921; ag. sol. treas., July to Sept., 1923; gov't. agt., N. Prov., July, 1924; ag. col. treas., 12th July, 1927.

SMITH, FREDERIC GORDON—B. 1886; ed. Broomgrove Schl.; solr., Jan., 1909; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, July, 1921; legal dept., N. Rhodesia, 1913; on active serv. in East Africa with N. R. Police, July, 1915 to July, 1918; held various appts. in legal dept., N. Rhodesia during B.S.A.Co.'s adminstr. including registr., high ct. etc., and asst. legal adviser and pub. pros.; asst. atty.-gen., Oct., 1924; solr.-gen., Kenya, Sept., 1926; atty.-gen., N. Rhodesia, Apr., 1927.

SMITH, F. M. URLING.—B. 1876; ed. at St. John's, Leatherhead, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; schoolmaster in England, Scotland and Wales from 1900 to 1906; served in ed. dept., Egypt, 1906 to 1910; supt. of Nassarawa schools, N. Nigeria, 28th Sept., 1910; senr. supt., 1st Jan., 1913; dir. of educn., Northern Provs., 24th Aug., 1919.

SMITH, SIR GEORGE, K.C.M.G. (1914), C.M.G. (1911).—B. 1868; entd. war office, 10th Apr., 1878; olk., ch. sec.'s office, Cyprus, 24th Nov., 1879; ch. clk., ditto, 1881; asst. to ch. sec., 15th Dec., 1883; ag. dir. of survey, 10th Aug., 1886, to 14th Feb., 1887; clk., legis. coun., 1st Mar., 1886, to 24th Nov., 1891; ag. ch. sec., and mem. of exec. coun., 16th May, to 10th Oct., 1888, and on other occasions; commr., Papho, 26th Nov., 1891; registr.-gen. and offi. mem. of legis. coun., 7th June, 1895; has served also as mem. of comtee. of management, Cyprus museum, 1886; sec., Col. and Ind. Exhib. comtee., 1886; sec. to Queen's Jubilee Memorial comtee., 1887; mem. of land registn. comm., 1891; pres. mun. commn., Papho, 1892; mem. of ecclesiastical corp. commn., 1896; mem. of mun. commn., Nicosia, 1895; mem. of gen. hosp. bd., Nicosia, 1896; mem. of bd. of agric., 1896; ag. British delegate of Evcaf, under conven. with Turkey, 20th Mar., to 25th Nov., 1903; ag. prin. forest offr., 1903, and 1907; ag. dir. of agric., 1904 and 1905; British delegate of Evcaf, 20th July, 1905; ag. recr.-gen., and ch. collr. of cust., 1905, 1906, 1907 and 1909; col. sec., Mauritius, 10th June, 1910; administered govt., Apl. to Nov., 1911; gov', Nyasaland, 17th May, 1913; assumed govt., 23rd Sept., 1913; ret., Dec., 1923.

SMITH, CAPT. GEORGE ECHLIN.—B. 1871; ed. Rathmines Schl. and Trin. Coll., Dublin; joined 5th batt. R. Irish Regt., 1891; capt., 1896; hon. capt. in army, 1900; asst. inspr., Hausa force (G. Coast batt.), Jan., 1897; sub.-inspr., Br. Guiana pol., Feb., 1898; dist. inspr., Oct., 1900; A.D.C. to Sir J. A. Swettenham, 1902-04; A.D.C. to Sir F. Hodgson, 1904-06; asst. dist. supt., pol., E. Africa Prot., Oct., 1906; supt., pol., Apr., 1914; comdt. pol. and gov. of prisons, Zanzibar, May, 1920-25; pte. sec. and A.D.C. to Sir D. Cameron, gov., Tanganyika Territory, 1925; 1914-15 Star, Gen. Serv. and Victory med.; King's pol. med., 1924.

SMITH, HERBERT DENSHAM.—B. 1882; entd. Impl. civ. serv. and apptd. to acct. genl's. dept., Admy., 1901; asst. acct., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1910; acct., P.W.D., Sierra Leone, 1914; transf. to treasy., Sierra Leone, asst. treas., Jan., 1916; ag. senr. asst. treas. for various periods in 1917, 1918 and 1919; ag. ch. acct., rly., May to Aug., 1922; ag. treas. and currency offr., 1919 and 1923; recr. gen. and currency offr., Gambia, Jan., 1925; mem., exec. and leg. couns.

SMITH, HERBERT FRANCIS, O.B.E. (1920).—B. 1873; ed. at Malvern coll. (class. scholar); entd. Crown Agents' office, Feb., 1893; asst. cashier, 1900; head of gen. stores dept., August, 1904; sec., Dec., 1920 to Nov., 1921; mem. fair wages advisory comtee., 1921; sec., E. African Currency Bd., 1922; head of stores dept., 1922; head of shipping dept., 1924; head, finance dept., 1927.

SMITH, HUGH MIDDLETON BRICE, M.A.—B. 1884; ed. at Pocklington Schl. and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; asst. res., N. Nigeria 16th Jan., 1909; 2nd cls. dist. offr., 17th Oct., 1917; ag. mag. in 1921 and 1922.
SMITH, JAMES.—B. 1882; stock inspr., Basutoland, 1911; sub-insp., Basutoland mounted pol., 1917; inspr., 1926.

SMITH, JAMES ALFRED, M.B.E. (1918).—B. 1871; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O., Apr., 1891; promoted to higher grade, 1902; ast. sec. to col. regns. comtce., 1907-8; staff clk., 1st grade, 1915; staff offr., 1st Apr., 1922.

SMITH, JAMES DAVID MAXWELL, M.A., Aberdeen.—B. 1896; cadet, F.M.S., July, 1920; ast. collr., land rev., Lower Perak, Oct., 1920; offr. in charge, treasy., Telok Anson, Oct., 1920; ag. 2nd ast. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, Aug., 1921; ag. ast. dist. offr., Gopeng and Kampar, Nov., 1921; ag. ast. dist. offr., Sitianwan, Dec., 1922; ag. dist. offr., Temerloh, Nov., 1923; ast. sec. to res., Perak, July, 1925; ag. sec., res., Perak, in addn., Nov., 1925; ag. state treas., Perak, July, 1926; offr., cls IV, Sept., 1926; ag. ast. sec. to res., Perak in addn., Oct., 1926; ag. chmn., sany. bd., Larut and Matang, Mar., 1927.

SMITH, JAMES LAMBERT.—B. 1870; draftsman, P.W.D., St. Lucia, 13th July, 1891; draftsman, survey off., Sept., 1893; warden, and supt. of water-wks. under St. George's town bd., Apr., 1896, to Aug., 1901; chief overseer of roads and works, Grenada, 19th Aug., 1901; ast. supt. of wks., 1st Apr., 1902; comsrr. of crown lands, 28th June, 1904; suvr. of crown lands, 1904; ag. supt. of wks., 14th June, 1906, to 16th Jan., 1907; supervisor, govt. cotton ginnery, 1st Oct., 1912; ag. supt. pub. wks., St. Vincent, 4th Oct., 1913; coll. engrnr., Oct., 1918 to Aug., 1920; supt. pub. wks., St. Kitts-Nevia, Sept., 1920.

SMITH, JOHN.—B. 1883; ed. New Vtly. Coll., Edinburgh (M.R.C.V.S.), and Liverpool Univ. (D.V.H.) vety. offr., N. Rhodesia, June, 1913; resigned, Apr., 1916; active serv., 1916-19; caps.; reappt., Oct., 1921; ag. ch. vety. offr., Nov., 1921-May, 1922; ch. vety. offr., May, 1922; mem., leg. coun.; ag. sec. for agr., Apr., 1925; J.P.

SMITH, JOHN NOEL.—B. 1886; ed. at Summer Fields, Oxford, Radley Coll. and Hertford Coll., Oxford; 2nd lieut., 3rd (Res.) Batt., Oxford and Bucks. Light Infantry, 1906; seconded for serv. as ast. req., N. Nigeria, 30th Nov., 1910; attd., Nigeria Regt., Cameroons, 24th Mar., 1915; station mag., N. Provs., 22nd Mar., 1919; dist. offr., 17th Jan., 1921; ag. senr. res., 7th Sept., 1923.
SMITH, HARRY ALSTON.—B. 1886; stock inspr., Basutoland, 1913; sub-inspr. Basutoland mounted pol., 1919; war serv., 1914-18.

SMITH, LESLIE STEPHEN.—B. 1898; entd. reglay. of friendly societies, 16th Jan., 1914; apptd. after compet. exam., ast. clk., 27th Apr., 1916; on mily. serv., Jan., 1917 to Feb., 1919; trans. to savings bank dept., G.P.O., May, 1919; trans. to C.O., June, 1923.

SMITH, LINDSAY LEA.—B. 1870; ag. clk. to comsrr., Turks Is., 1894, 1896, 1900, and 1903; confirmed 1st July, 1903; ag. acctnt. and clk. (collr. of customs, postmr., cashier and regir. of shipping) comsrr.'s office, June to Sept., 1904, and May to Aug., 1906; acctnt. and clk. (collr. of cust., postmr., cashier, and regir. of shipping), Jan., 1907; gen. man., savings bank, 1st July, 1907; ag. ast. comsrr., Dec., 1910 to Jan., 1911, and from Sept., 1912 to Apl., 1913.

SMITH, NORMAN LOCKHART.—B. 1887; ed. at Sedbergh and Queen's Coll., Oxford (Hastings scholar); B.A. 1910; cadet, Hong Kong, 20th Oct., 1910; ag. ast. dist. offr., (north), 1912; ag. 2nd A.R.G., 1913; J.P. 1913; ag. ast. col. sec. and clk. of couns., 1913; ag. 1st ast. to sec. for Chinese affairs, 1913; ag. ast. postmstr-gen., 1914; seconded for serv. as British postmaster at Shanghai, 1914; ag. postmtr.-gen., 1915; seconded for military service 1916-19; ag. deputy registr. and appraiser, 1919; ag. supt. Victoria Gaol, 1919; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1919, 1920 and 1923; recd. Chinese order of Won-hu, 5th cls., 1920; qualified in Pekinese, 31st Mar., 1920; supt., impts. and expts., 1921; attd., Geneva opium confce., 1923; head of sany. dep., 1924.

SMITH, P. C., M.I. Muncl.E. (Lond.).—B. 1879; ed. at Wyggeston boys' schl., Eng.; pupil with Messrs. Keites and Fosbrooks, archs. and surveys., Leicester, for five years, and remained for two years as ast.; joined staff of Messrs. Johnson and Langley, contra., Lond.; ast. to Mr. C. F. Wike, M.I.C.E., city engrnr., Sheffield, 1902; town engrnr., Rainy River, Ont., Can., 1910; town engrnr., Souris, Manitoba, 1911; dist. engrnr., Berbice, Br. Guiana, Aug., 1916.
SMITH, PETER HAMBLIN, B.A. (Oxon.)—B. 1900; ed. Denstone coll. and Lincoln coll., Oxford; ast. dist. comsrr., Sierra Leone, 5th Sept., 1923.

SMITH, REGINALD HOPKINSON.—B. 1896; ed. Chatham House, Ramsgate; served in the Royal Naval Divn., Oct. to Dec., 1914; H.A.C., Oct., 1915 to Oct., 1917; Indian Army R. of O., Nov., 1917 to Aug., 1919; Aden Field Force, July, 1918 to July, 1919; dist. pol. offr., Somaliland, July, 1919.
SMITH, REGINALD MONTAGU BOSWORTH.—Sub.-inspr., Basutoland mtd. pol., 1896; inspr., 1906; ast. comsrr., 1909; govt. sec., 1920; ag. dep. res. comsrr. for various periods in 1921, 1923 and 1924.
SMITH, SIDNEY W.—B. 1902; apptd., after compet. exam., ast. clk., C.O., Oct., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920.

SMITH, STEPHEN HENRY, F. R. Aus. H.S.—B.1865; ed. Grafton Superior Pub. Sch.; inspr., continuation schls., N.S. Wales, 13th June, 1912 to 29th Feb., 1920; ast. under sec., educn., dept., N.S.W., June, 1920; dir., educn., N.S.W., 1923.

SMITH, SYDNEY RICHARD, O.B.E. (Mil.), 1919.—B. 1875; ast. engrnr., P.W.D., Selangor, Apr., 1900; dist. engrnr., Ulu Selangor, Jan., 1901; ag. ex. engrnr., grade III, Sept., 1902; ag. ex. engrnr., grade II, May, 1904; ex. engrnr., grade III, Perak, Dec., 1909; ex. engrnr., grade II, Jan., 1913; adjt., M.S.V.R., Apr., 1915; ment. in despatches, July, 1918; sen. ex. engrnr., P.W.D., Jan., 1919; ag. state engrnr., Selangor, May, 1921; awarded Col. Aux. Forces Long Serv. med., June, 1922; ag. state engrnr., Pahang, Jan., 1923; state engrnr., Negri Sembilan, Aug., 1923; ag. ast. dir., pub. wks., F.M.S., Aug., 1923; ag. dir., pub. wks., F.M.S., Feb., 1925; ag. state engrnr., Selangor, Apr., 1925; ag. state engrnr., Perak, Dec., 1926; state engrnr., Selangor, Dec., 1926; ditto, Perak, Mar., 1927.

SMITH, WALTER BUCHANAN, M.C. (1918).—Ed. Repton Schl.; cadet, Br. N. Borneo civ. serv., 1903; dist. offr., 1904; 1st cls. mag., 1907; ast. dist. comsrr., S. Nigeria, 1909; ast. comsrr., lands, 1912; ag. comsrr., lands, 1912 and 1914; attd., Nigeria Regt. for service in Cameroons, 1914-15 and in German E. Africa, 1916-18; 1st cls. dist. offr., 1918; res., 1921; ag. sec., S. Provs., Nigeria, 1922 and 1925; senr. res., 1926.
SMITH, WALTER JOHN.—B. 1884; ast. supt. wks., P.W.D., S. Stiltnste., Jan., 1908; ast. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Sept., 1909; ex. engrnr., Kedah, Apr., 1917; ex. engrnr., grade III, July,

SMITH, WILLIAM HUMPHREY.—B. 1879; ed. Uppingham and Jesus Coll., Camb., Rustatschol., 1897; Abbott Univ., schol., 1899; B.A., 1st cls., class. tripes, pt. I, 1901; 2nd cls. pt. II, history, 1902; clk., col. audit branch, exchequer and audit dept., 1st Dec., 1903; astt. audr., Nyasaland, 1st Oct., 1904; audr., N. Nigeria, 1st Feb., 1910; examr., exchequer and audit dept., 17th Aug., 1910; audr., Seychelles, 23rd Mar., 1911; 1st divn. clk. (2nd cls.), col. audit dept., 1st Oct., 1912; on mil. serv., 5th Aug., 1914; capt., K.O.Y.L.I., 29th Jan., 1915; wounded, 8th Mar., 1917; relinquished coman. owing to wounds, and returned to col. audit dept., 29th Sept., 1918; audr., Uganda, 29th Dec., 1923; audr., Kenya, 1927.

SMITH, WILLIAM RAMSAY.—D.Sc., M.D., M.S., Edin.; chmn. cent. bd. of health, coroner, vaccination offr., and inspr. of anatomy, S. Australia, 1899-1903; chrmn., cent. bd. of health and coroner, 1903; author of several medical and scientific works.

SMITH-DORRIEN, GEN. SIR HORACE LOCKWOOD, G.C.B. (1913), G.C.M.G. (1916), D.S.O. (1886); grand off., legion of honour; A.D.C. gen. to the King, 1910-14.—B. 1858; served in Zulu war, 1879; Egyptian war, 1882; Soudan campaign, 1885; Soudan field force, 1886-6, 4th cls. Medjidie; 4th cls. Oamanieh, Chitral expdn., 1895; N.W. Frontier, India, 1897-98; Tirah expdn. force; Nile expdn., 1898; S. African war, 1900-01; European war, 1914-15; adjt.-gen. in India, 1901-3; commdg. Quetta div. of army in India, 1903-7; commdg.-in-chief, Aldershot, 1907-12; commdg.-in-chief southern commd., 1912-14; commdg. 2nd army corps and 2nd British expdn. force in France, 1914-15; commdg. E. African expdn. force, 1916; gov., Gibraltar, 9th July, 1918; assumed govt., 7th Sept., 1918, ret., 1923.
SMITHERS, ROST. FRANCIS.—Ck., atty. gens. office, Apr., 1902; senr. clk., Jan., 1910; pub. serv. inspr., Sept., 1921.

SMUTS, GEN. THE RIGHT HON. J. C., P.C. (1917), C.H., K.C.—B. 1870; ed. at Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; B.A., Cape Univ., 1891; Ebben scholar at Christ's Coll., Camb.; double first in law tripes, 1894; barrister, Cape Col.; state-attorney, S. African Republic, 1898; served with Boer forces during S. African war, and was in commd. of Boer forces in Cape Col. during the latter part of the campaign; mem. of head comtse. of Het Volk; col. sec., Transvaal, 1907; mem. of Imp. Conf. on Naval and Military Defence, 1909; min. of finance and defence, Union of S. Africa, 1912; min. of defence, 1915; in command of troops in E.
