Africa, 16th Jan., 1908; dist. commr., 1st Apr., 1914; seconded for Coast Land arbitrn., Sept., 1913 to Aug., 1914; seconded as intell. offr. to organise carrier transport D.A.D.T., E. African Forces, 1915; dir., mil. labour, 1916; ag. ch. native comsnr., Aug., 1920 to Aug., 1921; offl. mem., leg. coun., Aug., 1920 to Feb., 1922; mem., land tenure comsnr., 1920; mem., central bd. of health, 1921; mem., native punishments comsnr., 1921; chmn., labour bureau comsnr., 1921.

WATSON, ANDREW D.—B. 1882; ed. common schls., Baddeck Acad., Dalhousie Univ., B.A., 1906; ent. civ. serv., Canada, dept. of insurance, Feb., 1906; actuary, 1914; assoc., Inst. of Actuaries, Lond., 1909; Assoc., Actuarial Soc. of America, 1913; Fellow, 1919; Contrib. to the jnl. of the Inst. of Actuaries, and the transactions of the Actuarial Soc.

WATSON, DONALD FRANCIS, O.B.E. (1926).—B. 1883; 2nd clk., admstr.'s office, Seychelles, 1899; dispenser and clk., med. dept., 1900; clk. to local bd. of health, quarantine and cemetery comtece., 1900; man., govt. pharmacy, 1901; ag. supt., lunatic asylum, May to Oct., 1920; ag. treas., collr. of customs, chmn., marine bd., and chmn., profiteering comtece., July, 1920 to Apr., 1921; contr., local clearing office, June, 1921; treas., postmr., collr. of cust., comsnr. of income tax, man. of savings bank, regiser. of shipping, Apr., 1923.

WATSON, ERNEST CHARLES.—B. 1874; ed. at Shrewsbury; called to the bar, Middle Temp., 1897; 3rd cls. res., N. Nigeria, July, 1902; ag. atty.-gen., Sept. to Nov., 1904; atty.-gen., Nov., 1905; ag. ch. just., Mar. to Oct., 1906, May to Aug., 1907, Feb. to Apr., 1908, Mar. to Sept., 1909; puisne judge, Gold Coast, Jan., 1912; ag. ch. just., Sept., to Dec., 1915 and Mar. to June, 1917; senr. puisne judge and ag. ch. just., May, 1920; puisne judge, S. Stilimta, and judicial comsnr., F.M.S., June, 1920.
WATSON, ERNEST SIBBLES, B.Sc. Tech.—B., 1902; ed. Cent. High Schl., Manchester and Coll. of Technology, Manch.; B.Sc. Tech., 1923; asst. mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1925.

WATSON, J., M.A., B.Sc.—B. 1883; ed. George Heriot's Schl. and Edinburgh Univ., M.A., 1902, B.Sc., 1904; European mast., Raffles Institution, Singapore, 1906; science mast., 1910; inspr. of schls., Pahang, F.M.S., 1915; headmast., Anderson schl., Ipoh, 1920; inspr., of schls., Penang, 1921; ch. inspr. of English schls., S.S. and F.M.S., 1923.
WATSON, P. C. M.—Treasy. asst., E.A.P., May, 1914; senr. asst. treas., Kenya, Jan., 1922; ag. dep. treas., Nov., 1922 to Mar., 1924.

WATSON, HON. ROBERT.—B. 1868; M.E.C. and M.H.A., Newfoundland; ed. at St. John's and Rugby; elected for Trinity, 1897, 1902, 1908 and 1909; mem. exec. coun.; col. sec., and parly. head of depts. of post office, telegraphs, educn., rlys., pub. health and pub. charities, 1909; mem. exec. comtece., Diocesan Synod, 1910; ag. premier, 1910; attended Imp. Conf. and the Coronation, 1911; cashier, govt. savings bank, 1913; hon. priv. sec. to gov., 1916; asst. priv. sec. to gov., 1918; hon. corres. sec., Royal Col. Inat.

WATSON, WILLIAM LAW, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1883; ed. Grammar Schl. and Robert Gordon's Coll. (Abdn.); loco. dept., G.N.S.R., 1899; loco. dept., G.W.R., 1907; engnr. design dept., C.A., 1912; min. of munitions, 1915; dep. head, engnr. design dept., C.A., 1919.

WATT, ALEXANDER STUART.—B. 1884; ed. Edin. Acad., Edin. Instit., Blair Lodge and Edin. Univ.; writer to the signet, 1909; capt., 5th Batt. Royal Scots; asst. dist. comsnr., G. Coast, 29th Apr., 1914; ag. dist. comsnr., Dec., 1915; attached G. Coast Regt., 1915; with E. Africa Exped. Force, July, 1916.

WATT, SIR THOMAS, K.C.M.G. (1912); C.M.G. (1906).—B. 1857; ed. at priv. schls. and Glasgow Univ.; admtd. a solr. to Scottish cts.; practised in Natal; served as subaltern in Imperial L.I., 1900; apptd. capt. and offr. commdg. Newcastle Town Guard, and ment. in desps.; M.L.A., Natal, 1901-1910; formerly min. of just. and educn., Natal; min. of posts and telegraphs and pub. wks., Union of S. Africa, 1912; min. of pub. wks. and interior, 1916; min. of pub. health, 1919; min. of posts and telegraphs and of public wks., 1921-24.

WATT, Rt. Hon. WM. ALEXANDER.—P.C. (1920); M.H.A.; treas., of Victoria Jan., 1909; premier and treas., 1912, and again in Dec., 1913; resig. premiership to enter H. of R., C. of A., to which he was elected in Sep., 1914; min. for war and rlys., 1917; ditto, from 10th Jan., 1918; treas., 27th Mar., 1918; min. for trade and cust., 13th Dec., 1918; acting prime min., 1919; resig. office, 1920; speaker, H. of R., 1923.

WATTS, BERTRAM T.—B. 1883; ed. Wellingborough Schl. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge, B.A., F.R.G.S.; inspr., Egyptian govt. survey, 1905-08; inspr. Egyptian govt. survey, 1912; on sp. surveys in the Sudan for irrign. wks.; dir.-sp. irrign. surveys, Mesopotamia, for Turkish govt., 1908-11; asst. dir., surveys, Cyprus, 1912-20; ag. regiser. gen., 1913, 1918, 1919 and 1920; ag. Br. del. of Evcaf., 1919, 1923 and 1926; regiser. gen., Cyprus, 1920; passed in mod. Gk. and Turkish; title altered to dir., land registn. and surveys, July, 1927.

WATTS, CLIVE.—2nd lieut., Northumberland Fusiliers, Sept., 1915; lieut., July, 1917; capt., June, 1918; ment. in desps., and wounded, European War; acctnt., treasy., Cyprus, 1920; acctnt. to pub. custodian and Cyprus contr. in addition, Apr., 1921 to May, 1923; passed exam. in Greek, 1921; ag. asst. treas., for various periods in 1924, 1925 and 1927; ag. asst. sec., col. sec's. office in 1926 and 1927; ag. dep. treas., July to Nov., 1927.

WATTS, SIR FRANCIS, K.C.M.G. (1917), C.M.G. (1904), D.Sc., F.C.S., F.I.C.—B. 1859; analytical chemist, Antigua, Jan., 1889; mem. legis. couns., 1897; analytical and agricultural chemist, Jamaica, May, 1898; Leeward Is., May, 1899 (in connection with the Imperial dept. of agriculture for the W. Indies); office subsequently made that of govt. chemist and supt. of agric. for the Leeward Is.; mem. exec. couns., Leeward Is., Oct., 1899; mem. exec. couns., Antigua, Dec., 1903; Imperial comsnr. of agric. in W. Indies, 1909; prim., W. Indian Agrl. Coll., 1921; prim., emeritus, 1924; author of numerous reports and papers on W. Indian agriculture, etc.
WAYNE, RICHARD ST. JOHN ORMEROD.—B. 1904; ed. Trinity Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1926; asst. sec., col. sec's. office, Cyprus, May, 1927.

WEATHERHEAD, ARTHUR EVREYN.—B. 1880; served in S. African constab., 1900-1907; S. African war, Queen's medal with 5 claps.; dist. supt., Uganda police, 1908; asst. dist. comsnr., Uganda Prot., 4th Apr., 1910; ag. dist. comsnr., 1914; dist. comsnr., 1919; ag. prov. comsnr., 1924; lab. comsnr., Uganda, 1st Jan., 1925.
WEAVING, L. A.—Asst. dist. comsnr., Kenya, June, 1921.
WEBB, CECIL RICHARD, O.B.E. (1926), M.C.—Aat. traffic manager, Uganda rly., 1914; traffic man., Tanganyika Territory rly., 1st Oct., 1919; gen. man., govt. rly., Sierra Leone, 20th June, 1923.

WEBB, AMBROSE HENRY.—B. 1882; ed. Clifton Coll., Bath Coll. and Trinity Coll., Oxford, 1899 (class. schol.); 1st Victoria prizeman and John Brooke schol., King's Inns, Dublin, 1908; called to bar, 1909; K.C., 1920; pres., dist. ct., Samaria, 1921.

WEBBER, ARTHUR FREDERICK CLARENCE.—B. 1873; ed. Merton Coll., Oxon., B.A., 1896; called to bar, Inn. Temp., Feb., 1896; ag. stip. mag., 1898, and March to Oct., 1900, Br. Guiana; stip. mag., Esseguibo, Oct., 1900; ditto, Whim Corentyne, July, 1902; ditto, Berbice judicial dist., 1904; ag. solr.-gen., Nov., 1906, to Jan., 1907, and in July, 1909; puisne judge, S. Nigeria, Sept., 1909.

WEBBER, OSCAR WM.—B. 1871; clk. asst., prisons office, B. Guiana, 1887; 5th cls. clk., P.O., 1889; transf. to secretariat, 1896; 4th cls. clk., 1896; 3rd ditto, 1899; 2nd ditto, 1901; 1st cl. clk., 1910; prin. clk., 1917; 2nd asst. col. sec., 1920; ag. comnr., Montserrat, Oct., 1919 to May, 1920; sec. to bd. of agric., 1901-1910; mem. of bd. of agric., 1910; priv. sec. to gov., Feb.-Aug., 1911, and from Sept., 1914 to Aug., 1915; ag. asst. col. sec., May, 1920 to Oct., 1921 and from June to Dec., 1924; asst. P.M.G., Jan., 1925; ag. P.M.G., Jan. to Sept., 1925.

WEBSTER, GEORGE FREDERICK.—B. 1889; asst. polit. offr., German E. Africa provisional admnstr., 20th Oct., 1916; administr. offr., 1st grade, Tanganyika Territory, 1st Feb., 1922; ag. senr. comnr., Mwanza dist., Feb. to Nov., 1923; ag. senr. comnr., Dar-es-Salaam dist., Jan. to Dec., 1925; dep. prov. comnr., 1st Jan., 1926; ag. prov. comnr., E. Prov. 2nd Mar., 1926; offl. mem., leg. coun., Dec., 1926.
WEBSTER, G. W., M.B.E. (1913).—Ed. at Rossall; served with Yeomanry in S. Africa, 1900-1901; asst. res., N. Nigeria, 16th Nov., 1901; 2nd cls. res., 23rd Aug., 1909; 1st cls. res., 1918.
WEBSTER, H. G.—Aat. treas., Kenya, July, 1920; asst. audr., Kenya, Apr., 1923.

WEDDERSBURN, MAXWELL MACLAGAN.—B. 1883; ed. George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh, and Edinburgh Univ. (M.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Dec., 1906; pol. mag., Point Pedro and Chavakachcheri, Apr., 1908; Matara, Aug., 1909; Kurunegala, June, 1909; asst. land settlt. offr., Jan., 1911; ag. addtl. asst. col. sec. in addition to own duties, July, 1912; ag. asst. col. sec., July, 1912; asst. land settlt. offr. and special offr. under "Waste Lands Ordo.", Aug., 1912; asst. censor, Aug., 1914; asst. land settlt. offr. and special offr. under "Waste Lands Ordo." Oct., 1914; asst. censor, Nov., 1914; asst. govt. agt., Nuwara Eliya, Sept., 1915; asst. settlt. offr., July, 1920; ag. settlt. offr., Mar. to Nov., 1921 and from Apr., 1922 to June, 1924; rubber contr., Mar., 1925; govt. ag., N.C. Prov., Sept., 1925.

WEDGE, CAPT. CYRIL NORMAN.—B. 1896; schol., mod. hist., St. Catherine's Coll., Cambridge, Mar., 1915; 2nd lieut., Worcestershire Regt., Mar., 1915; served in Gallipoli (Suvla Bay), 1915; "1914-15" Star, Br. War and Victory Meds.; seconded 1st K.A.R., 1918; served in Portuguese E. Africa and Nyassaland, 1918-20; capt. and adjt., 2/1st K.A.R., Tanganyika Territory, 1920-23; pay and qtrmr. pol. and prisons, Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1923.

WEERAKOON, WILLIAM AUGUSTUS.—B. 1878; cadet, local div., Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1899; off. asst. to govt. agt., Ratnapura, Sept., 1900; Batticaloa, Nov., 1901; Kurunegala, May, 1902; Jaffna, Mar., 1904; Ratnapura, May, 1904; pol. mag., Matara, Jan., 1908; Point Pedro, Aug., 1908; addtnl. pol. mag., Kurunegala, N.W. Prov., Jan., 1911; pol. mag., Panadure, Nov., 1913; pol. mag., Negombo, June, 1915; pol. mag., Jaffna, Aug., 1915; office asst. to govt. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, Dec., 1916; pol. mag., Puttalam, Sept., 1918; office asst. to govt. agt., W. Prov., July, 1920.

WEIGALL, LIEUT.-COL. SIR WILLIAM ERNEST GEORGE ARCHIBALD, K.C.M.G. (1920).—B. 1874; ed. Wellington Coll., Royal Agric. Coll. (gold medallist); major (ret.), 3rd Batt. Northants Regt.; served in S. Africa, 1902; M.P. (U.), Horncastle Div., Linco., 1911-1920; capt. Lincolnshire Yeomanry, and lieut.-col. and inspr. Q.M.G. services, Eastern Comd., 1914-17; mem. of national salvage council; dep. chmn., surplus govt. property disposal bd.; mem. of select comte. on national expenditure; mem., national agric. coun.; pres. agric. organisation soc.; dep. chmn., federation of county agric. comtes.; J.P., Kent and Linco.; gov., S. Australia, 27th Mar., 1920; assumed govt., 9th June, 1920; resign., 1922.

WISBECKER, ARNOLD.—C.I.C., census dept., Cape Prov., 17th Apr., 1891; P.W.D., 29th Feb., 1892; admstr.'s office (Cape Prov.), 1st July, 1910; ch. clk., 1st Apr., 1911; ch. local govt. inspr. and ch. clk., 1st Apr., 1919; prov. secy. and ch. local govt. inspr., 1st Sept., 1922.

WEISBERG, HYMAN, B.A. Cantab.—B. 1890; 1st cls. math. tripus.; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1914; passed final exam. in Malay, June, 1917; ag. 2nd asst. dist. offr., Batang Padang, June, 1917; ag. asst. dist. offr., Kinta, May, 1919; asst. dist. offr., Klang, Nov., 1919; dist. offr., Jelebu, May, 1920; ditto, Kuala Langat, May, 1922; mag., Seremban, July, 1922; av. state treas., Perak; Dec., 1922; ag. dist. offr., Upper Perak, July, 1924; ag. state treas., Perak, Aug., 1924; temp. ag. chmn., sany. bd., Larut and Matang, in addn., May, 1926; ag. dist. offr., Larut and regisr. titles, Perak, 1927.
WELBY, H. E.—Asst. dist. comnr., E. Africa Prov., Nov., 1911; dist. comnr., Kenya, Jan., 1920.
WELCH, THOMAS BURGES, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., M.R.B.S., D.T.M. & H.—B. 1881; med. supt., Chacachacare leper settlement, Trinidad, 23rd Sept., 1925.

WELLINGTON, BISHOP OF, RIGHT REV. THOMAS HENRY SPROTT, M.A., D.D. Ed. Trin. Coll., Dublin; Hebrew prize, 1877; B.A., 1878; (Respondent) 1887; Hebrew Testamur, 1879; Divinity Testimonial (1st cls.) and Church Formularies Prize, 1879; M.A., 1882; B.D. and D.D. (jure dignitatis), 1911; deacon, 1879; priest, 1880; curate of Holy Trinity, Kingston-on-Hill, 1879-1882; St. John the Evangelist, Waterloo Road, 1882-1886; vicar of St. Barnabas, Auckland, 1886-1891; examining chaplain to Bp. of Auckland, 1888-1891; vicar of St. Paul's, Wellington, 1892-1911; examining chaplain to Bp. of Wellington, 1892-1911; consecrated Bp. of Wellington, 6th June, 1911.

WELLINGTON, ARTHUR ROBARTES, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M. and H. and D.P.H. (Camb.).—B. 1877; med. offr., Sarawak, Sept., 1905; med. offr., port health office and med. offr., quarantine station, Singapore, 10th Sept., 1908; health offr., Selangor, Negri Sembilan and Pahang, 13th May, 1909; health offr., F.M.S., 1st Jan., 1911; senr. health offr., F.M.S., 5th Mar., 1919; malaria res. offr. in addn., May, 1925; ag. prin. med. offr., F.M.S., Apr., 1926.

WELMAN, CHARLES WELLESLEY.—B. 1878; ed. Christ's Hosp.; open scholarship, Pemb. Coll., Oxon., 1897; 1st cl. hon., class. moda., 1899; 2nd cl. hon., lit. hum., 1901; M.A. Oxon., 1907; bar final exam., 1909; called to the bar, Middle Temple, Jan., 1911; enlisted in 130th co. I.Y. and served in S. Africa (Queen's medal, 2 clasps), 1902; educn. dept., Transvaal, Nov., 1902, to 1905; sec., Transvaal Resp. Govt. Assoc. and Transvaal National Assoc., 1905-1907; in office of agt.-gen. for the Transvaal in London, 1907-1909; ast. dist. comsnr., Gold Coast, 1909; ast. col. sec., 1913; dist. comsnr., 1917; senr. ast. col. sec., 1917; ag. comsnr., Eastern Prov., Jan. to Apr., 1918; dep. pol. mag., Accra, Aug. to Sep., 1918; ag. chief ast. col. sec., May to Dec., 1919; ag. comsnr., Central Prov., from Dec., 1919; dep. sec. for native affairs, 1920.

Territories, G. Coast, 1912; released for service in European War, 1915-1919 (D.S.O. and one bar, French Croix de Guerre, 1914-15 Star); dist. comsrr., N. Territories, G. Coast, 1918; dep. prov. comsrr., 1927.
twin_text: WHITTALL, Lieut.-Col. Percival Frederick.—B. 1877; ed. Felsted; joined army, 1896; lieut., Lincolnshire Regt., 1902; served in S. African war, 1899-1900 (Queen's medal and three clasps); lieut., G. Coast Regt., 1907; rec'd thanks of S. of S. for services during outbreak of yellow fever at Secondee, 1910; asst. dist. commr., N. Territories, G. Coast, 1912; released for service in European War, 1915-1919 (D.S.O. and one bar, French Croix de Guerre, 1914-16 Star); dist. commr., N. Territories, G. Coast, 1918; dep. prov. commr., 1927.

raw_text: WHITTING, CHARLES EDWARD JEWEL.—B. 1900; ed. Haileybury and St. John's Coll., Oxford; supt., educn. dept., N. Provs., Nigeria, 26th July, 1922.
twin_text: WHITTING, Charles Edward Jewel.—B. 1900; ed. Haileybury and St. John's Coll., Oxford; supt., educn. dept., N. Provs., Nigeria, 26th July, 1922.

raw_text: WHYBRO, CHARLES, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1902; ed. Berkhamsted Schl., London Univ., Schl. of Economics, and Christ's Coll., Cambridge, 1920-23; economic tripes, pt. I, 3rd cls., 1922; law tripes, pt. II, 3rd cls., 1923; supt., educn., Tanganyika Territory, 1926.
twin_text: WHYBRO, Charles, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1902; ed. Berkhamsted Schl., London Univ., Schl. of Economics, and Christ's Coll., Cambridge, 1920-23; economic tripos, pt. I, 3rd cls., 1922; law tripos, pt. II, 3rd cls., 1923; supt., educn., Tanganyika Territory, 1926.

raw_text: WICKRAMASINGHA, SKNA PANDUKABHAYA, B.A., LL.B. (Cantab.)—B. 1899; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1922; attd. to Matare kach., Dec., 1922; ditto, Batticaloa kach., Apr., 1923; extra office asst., Anuradhapura kach., Jan., 1925; office asst., ditto, June, 1925; pol. mag., Dandagamuwa, Feb., 1926; addl. dist. judge, Mannar and Mullaitivu, June, 1927.
twin_text: WICKRAMASINGHA, Sena Pandukabhata, B.A., LL.B. ("Cantab.")—B. 1899; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1922; stdt. to Matara kach., Dec., 1922; ditto, Batticaloa kach., Apr., 1923; extra office asst., Anuradhapura kach., Jan., 1925; office asst., ditto, June, 1925; pol. mag., Dandagamuwa, Feb., 1926; addnl. dist. judge, Mannar and Mullaitivu, June, 1927.

raw_text: WICKREMESINGHE, CYRIL LEONARD.—B. 1890; cadet, local div., Ceylon civ. ser., Apr., 1912; attached to Badulla Kachcheri, Apr., 1912; Matare Kachcheri, Oct., 1912; office asst. to govt. agt., Uva, July, 1913; ag. pol. mag., Dandagamuwa, Nov., 1913; addl. pol. mag., Colombo, Negombo and Avissawella, June, 1916; pol. mag., Kalutara, Mar., 1917; pol. mag., Jaffna, Mar., 1920; ag. addnl. pol. mag., Colombo, Negombo, and Avissawella, Mar., 1921; addnl. asst. govt. agt., Colombo, May, 1921; asst. govt. agt., Mannar, Mar., 1923; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, Nov., 1925; asst. govt. agt., Puttalam, Oct., 1926. bilan and Pahang, 13th May, 1909, health offr., F.M.S., 1st Jan., 1911; senr. health offr., F.M.S., 5th Mar., 1919; malaria res. offr. in addn., May, 1925; ag. prin. med. offr., F.M.S., Apr., 1926.

WICKREMESINGHE, Cyril Leonard.—B. 1890; cadet, local div., Ceylon civ. ser., Apr., 1912; attached to Badulla Kachcheri, Apr., 1912; Matara Kachcheri, Oct., 1912; office asst. to govt. agt., Uva, July, 1913; ag. pol. mag., Dandagamuwa, Nov., 1913; addtl. pol. mag., Colombo, Negombo and Avissawella, June, 1916; pol. mag., Kalutara, Mar., 1917; pol. mag., Jaffna, Mar., 1920; ag. addnl. pol. mag., Colombo, Negombo, and Avissawella, Mar., 1921; addnl. asst. govt. agt., Colombo, May, 1921; asst. govt. agt., Mannar, Mar., 1923; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, Nov., 1925; asst. govt. agt., Puttalam, Oct., 1926.

WIDDUP, CHARLES PONSONBY.—Clerical asst., treas., B. Guiana, 1902; 6th cls. clk., lands and mines dept., 1903; ag. sub-inspr. of police, 1903; sub-inspr. of police, 1904; qualified as musketry instr., Hythe, 1905; dist. inspr. of police, 1908; passed Hindustan exam., 1909; county inspr. of police, 1914; dep. inspr.-gen., B. Guiana, 16th Dec., 1920; ag. inspr.-gen., pol. and commut., local forces, 6th Mar. to 10th Oct., 1924 and from 16th Aug., 1926 to 31st Jan., 1927.

WIGGINS, CLARE AVELING, C.M.G. (1923), M.R.C.S.(Eng.), L.R.C.P.(Lond.), Fellow, Entom. Soc.—Med. offr., E. Africa Prot., 4th Apr., 1901; med. offr., Uganda Prot., 4th Apr., 1909; dep. P.M.O., 1st Apr., 1911; ag. P.M.O., 18th May to 29th Nov., 1910, and 19th May to 26th Dec., 1913; D.A.D.M.S. (Mob.), 24th Oct., 1915, with rank of major; O.C. Uganda Native Med. Corps, and O.C. Uganda Bearer Corps, 17th Nov., 1915; asst. censor, 2nd Feb., 1915; offl. censor, 12th Mar., 1916; P.M.O., Uganda.
WIGHTMAN, J. W. E.—Asst. dist. comsrr., Kenyas, Apr., 1914; ag. res. comsrr., 1926.

WIGHTON, ALEXANDER RITCHIE, Mem., Transvaal Socy. of Accnts.—Joined Impl. military rlys., S. Africa, June, 1902; audit inspr., ch. accnt.'s dept., 23rd June, 1903; accnt., engrnr.'s dept., 1st Feb., 1904; ch. clk., ditto, 1st July, 1908; inspr., dept. of audtr., S. African rlys. and harbs., 1st Apr., 1910; ch. inspr., asst. controller and audr.-gen.'s dept., Union of S. Africa, 1st Apr., 1912; ch. inspr. (rlys. and harbs.), cont. and audit office, 1st July, 1916.

WIGHTWICK, CLAUDE.—B. 1881; ed. at Tonbridge schl. and Pembroke Coll., Camb., 3rd cls. class. tripus; asst. res., N. Nigeria, 18th Aug., 1906; 3rd cls. res., 1st Jan., 1913; title changed to 2nd cls. dist. offr., 1st Jan., 1914; 1st cls. ditto, 22nd Mar., 1919; res. 16th Oct., 1921; ag. senr. res., 10th Jan., 1923.

WIGLEY, WILFRID MURRAY.—Ed. Derby schl.; held various clerical appmts. under gov't., St. Kitts-Nevis, Feb., 1985, to Apr., 1898; called to the bar, Mid. Temp., Jan., 1901; practised at bar of Leeward Is. for some years; ag. admstr., St. Kitts, puisne judge, Leeward Is. and atty.-gen., on various occasions; crown atty., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1918.

WILCOCKS, CARL THEODORUS MULLER.—B. 1861; ed. Godbolt's Commi. Acad., Graaff Reinet; represented Fauresmuth including Jagersfontein and Koffyfontein in first Union parl.; M.L.A. for Winburg (O.F.S.), 1915-24; mem., rlys. and harbs. bd., 1924; pub. debt. comsrr., 1925.

WILKIE, DAVID.—Ed. at Christ's Hosp., London; S. African campaign, 1900-1; pay and acctg. branch, S.A.C., 1902; clk. to lieut.-gov., O.R.C., 1902; ag. priv. sec. to lieut.-gov., O.R.C., 1906; ch. clk., gov.'s off., 1907; clk. of exec. coun., 1909; gov.-gen.'s off., Union of S. Africa, 1910; sec. to tender bd., 1911; ag. contr. of supplies, 1916; gov't. buyer, 1920.

WILKINSON, ALAN FREDERICK LAWDER (Lieut., R.N.V.R.).—B. 1892; ed. Royal Naval Colls., Osborne and Dartmouth, and Trin. Coll., Cambridge; war service (Navy), 27th Aug., 1914, to 7th June, 1919; gunnery offr. from July, 1918; asst. dist. comsrr., G. Coast, 9th July, 1919; ag. dist. comsrr., 10th Sept. to 8th Nov., 1919.

WILKINSON, ERNEST EDWARD, M.B.E. (1922).—B. 1874; apptd., after compet. exam., clk., 2nd div., civ. ser., and assigned to the bd. of agriculture, May, 1894; transf'd. to C.O., June, 1896; minor staff offr., ch. clk.'s dept., 1902; clk. to finance and concessions comte., 1916; clk. for legal instruments, Sept., 1921; staff offr., 1st April, 1922; librarian, 26th Feb., 1923.

WILKINSON, H.S.—Clk., high commr.'s office, S. Africa, 1899; clk., polit. sec.'s office, Transvaal, 1900; accnt. to sec., Transvaal adminstr., 1901; clk., col. sec.'s office, 1902; controller of stores, educn. dept., 1902; accnt., health dept., 1904; sec., civ. serv. bd., 1906; distributor of stamps, treas., 1908; distributor of stamps, dept. of finance, Union of S. Africa, 1910; prin. clk., treas., 1916; ch. clk., treas., 1925.

WILLAN, HAROLD CURWEN, M.C., B.A., OXON.—B. 1896; ed. Kendal Grammar Schl. and Jesus Coll., Oxford; open exhib., Jesus Coll., Oxford; lieut., R.G.A.; M.C., 7th June, 1917; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1920; ag. 2nd asst. dist. offr., Tapah, May, 1921; ag. asst. dist. offr., Batang Padang, Jan., 1923; passed cadet, Jan., 1923; offr., cls. V., Oct., 1923; dist. offr., Jelebu, Dec., 1923; asst. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, Aug., 1925; ag. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, Oct., 1925 and in admn., Dec., 1926; offr. asst. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, Nov., 1926; ag. asst. registr., sup. ct., Ipoh, Apr., 1927; offr., cls. IV., Oct., 1927.
WILLAN, REGINALD, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P.—Ed. Oakham Schl. and Guy's Hospital; med. offr. to Baro-Kano rly., Nigeria, 1909-1911; joined West African med. staff, 10th Jan., 1912.
WILLDON, MARGARET ISABEL, M.B.E. (1925).—B. 1877; matron, Basutoland leper asylum, 1913.

WILLETT, THOMAS GRAHAM.—B. 1883; ed. Merchant Taylors and St. John's Coll., Oxford (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1906; off. asst. to govt. agt., N.W. Prov., Aug., 1908; pol. mag., Chilaw, Dec., 1909; addl. pol. mag., Galle, Matara, Tangalla, Hambantota and Balapitiya, July, 1910; pol. mag., Jaffna, Jan., 1911; pol. mag., Kurunegala, Jan., 1913; ag. dist. judge, Kegalla, Aug., 1913; asst. land settm. offr., and special offr. under waste land ordce., June, 1914; spec. commr. in connection with the riots, June, 1915; asst. land settm. offr., Sept., 1915; on military duty, 1918; asst. govt. agt., Kandy, Feb., 1920; pol. mag., Point Pedro, May, 1923; asst. govt. agt., Kurunegala, Jan., 1923; ditto, Colombo, Dec., 1924; ag. asst. govt. agt., Kaltura, 16th to 27th, Jan., 1926.
WILLIAMS, A. D. J. B.—Med. offr., E.A.P., Aug., 1912; senr. med. offr., Kenya, Sept., 1923; ag. dep. dir., med. services, 1926.

WILLIAMS, CHARLES EDWARD, O.B.R. (1918), M.I.Mech.E.—B. 1873; ed. Llandovery Coll.; asst. wks. man., Vulcan loco. works, Lancs.; inspr., N.Z. and W. Australian rlys., 1898; Crown Agent's office, 1900; asst. man., Robt. Stephenson's wks., Darlington, 1901; dep. ch. inspgr. engr., Crown Agts., 1904; staff capt.W.O., 1915; section dir., Miny.of Munitions, 1916; major, R.E., D.A.D., W.O., 1918; tech. head of engrg. inspection dept., C.A., 1919; visited Nigeria, 1920; Palestine and Cyprus, 1921; ch. inspgr. engr., 1922; mem. of coun. Inst. of Loco. Engrs.
WILLIAMS, D. L. GWINNE.—B. 1886; solr., sup. ot., Eng., 1914; served European War, 1915-18; ct. martial offr., 1918; staff capt. (legall 1919; conveyancer and reglar. of titles, Uganda, 1923.

WILLIAMS, EDWARD BRIDGEWATER.—B.1885; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1908; asst. dist. offr., Bruas, Mar., 1910; ag. asst. dist. offr., Ipoh, Apr., 1910; ag. asst. dist. offr., Pekan, July, 1911; ag. dist. offr., Dinding, S.S., June, 1912; ag. asst. dist. offr., Larut, July, 1914; 2nd asst. dist. offr., Larut, Dec., 1914; asst. dist. offr., Kuantan, Aug., 1917; asst. dist. offr., Lower Perak, Sept., 1918; mag., Seremban, Jan., 1920; dist. offr., Taiping, Mar., 1920; dist. offr., Coast, Nov., 1920; ag. dist. offr., Kuala Pilah, Nov., 1921; collr., land rev., Seremban, Mar., 1922; ag. Br. res., Negri Sembilan, in addn., Aug., 1925; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1925; ag. collr., land rev., Seremban, Nov., 1925; ag. collr., estate duty, F.M.S., Dec., 1926; pub. trustee, F.M.S., in addn., Mar., 1927; offr., assignee, F.M.S., in addn., May, 1927.

WILLIAMS, EDWARD LLOYD.—B. 1876; ed. at Brecon and Victoria Univ. (Univ. Coll., Liverpool); indentured for three years to the water engrnr. of Liverpool; student of the institn. of civ. engrnr.; contractor's engrnr. on rly. construction, G.W.R.; res. engrnr. on waterworks construction, N. Wales county asylum; contractor's sub-agt. and engrnr. on rly. construction, G.W.R., served with 29th I.Y. (Denbighshire Hussars), South Africa, 1900-1; asst. engrnr. on rly. survey, Gold Coast and Ashanti, 1901-2; asst. surveyor of mines, G. Coast, 1902-3; ch. surveyor, 1904-5; contractor's agt., waterworks construction for Liverpool corporation, 1905-6; exec. engrnr., P.W.D., Northern Nigeria, 9th June, 1906; ag. dep. D.P.W., in 1909, 1910, 1911 and 1912.

WILLIAMS, ERIC TREGEAR.—B. 1890; ed. Blundell's schl. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; cadet, S. Sttlnts. civ. ser., 23rd Nov., 1913; attached to col. sec., 26th Dec., 1913; ag. supt., govt. printing office, 12th Mar., 1914; ag. dist. offr., Jasim, 1st Sept., 1915; dist. offr., Alor Gajah, 19th Oct., 1917; supernmy. offr., cls. IV, S. Sttlnts., Nov., 1920; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., "B," June, 1921; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., "A," 5th Dec., 1921.
WILLIAMS, ERNEST HILLAS, B.A., Trinity Coll., Dublin.—B. 1899; cadet, Hong Kong, 1923; passed cadet, Dec., 1925; asst. to sec., for Chinese affrs., 1926.

WILLIAMS, FRANK LESLIE.—B. 1899; ed. Weymouth Coll. and Christ's Coll., Cambridge, (B.A.); 2nd lieut., R.G.A., 1918; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1921; ag. asst. sec. to res., Selangor, June, 1924; attd. to office of sec. for Chinese firs., July, 1924; ag. asst. prot., Chinese, Perak, Nov., 1924; attd. for duty Chinese prot., Ipoh, July, 1925; ag. asst. prot., Chinese, Dec., 1926; ditto, Perak, Apr., 1927; ag. prot., Chinese, Taiping, May, 1927.

WILLIAMS, FRANK STANLEY.—B. 1894; ed. St. Paul's Schl., London; col. audit dept., Feb., 1920; asst. audr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1920; senr. asst. audr., July, 1924; ag. dep. audr., Apr., 1926 to Jan., 1927.
WILLIAMS, G. A., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.C.R.P. (Lond.)—Tempy. commission, R.A.M.C., 1915-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, May, 1921.

WILLIAMS, GILBERT NEVILLE, D.S.O., V.D.—Cape civ. serv., surv. office, 1892; dept. of agr., 1893; prin. cls., 1906; ch. clk., 1909; ch. clk., Union dept. of agr., 1912; ag. under-secr., 1919; confirmed, 1920; served, Bechuanaland Rebellion, 1897 (med. and clasp); S. A. War, 1899-1900 (med. and three clasps); S. A. War, 1899-1900 (med. and three clasps); A.A. and Q.M.G., 3rd divn., E. African Expedy. Force, 1917-19 (D.S.O., 3 times ment. in desps.); sec. for agr., 1st Aug., 1926; del. to Imp. agrl. confce., London, 1927.
WILLIAMS, HON. KENNETH STUART.—B. 1870; ed. Heretaunga Schl. and Christ's Coll., N.Z.; mem., N.Z. parl., since 1920; min. of pub. wks., 1926.

WILLIAMS, MYLES.—Clk. tores. comanr., Bech. Prot., 1902; ch. clk. and registr., 3rd Feb., 1906; ag. govt. sec., Nov., 1908, to May, 1909, and from Apr. to Oct., 1911; acctnt., Bech. Prot., Apr., 1912; passed Cape civ. ser. lower law exam., 1909; passed 2nd grade Sechuna exam., 1912; asst. res. mag., Apr., 1913; ag. res. mag., 3rd July, 1916; ag. asst. comanr., N. Prot., 1915; ag. asst. comanr., S. Prot., 1916; res. mag., 1920; finan. sec. master and regiar. of deeds, 1920.

WILLIAMS, O. G. R.—B. 1886; ed. Lancing Coll. and Oxford; exhibitioner, Hertford Coll., 1906; 1st cls. mods., 1907; 1st cls. Lit. Hum., 1909; Aubrey Moore studentship, 1910; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. in sec.'s off., In. Rev., Oct., 1910; 2nd cls. clk., C.O., Mar., 1911; priv. sec. to Mr. (now Sir Arthur) Steel-Maitland, 4th June, 1916; priv. sec. to Mr. Hewins, 26th Sept., 1917; priv. sec. to Col. Amery, 14th Jan., 1919; 1st cls. clk., 8th Mar., 1919; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; ag. asst. sec., Feb., 1926.
WILLIAMS, PATRICK EDWARD WILLOWS.—B. 1904; ed. Shrewsbury Schl. and Pembroke Coll, Cambridge; B.A.; supt., educn., Tanganyika Territory, 1926.

WILLIAMS, P. MILES.—B. 1890; ed. Pontypridd County Schl. and Ravensburg High Schl., Würtemburg, Germany; R.F.C., 1915-17; disabled; clk., Miny. of Labour, Bristol, Feb., 1918 to May, 1922; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 29th May, 1922; seconded to Empire Marketing Bd., 1926.

WILLIAMS, PHILIP STANHOPE.—B. 1889; ed. at Harrow (head of the schl. 1907-08) and at Caius Coll., Camb. (schol. and Sayer schol.), 1st cls. classical tripos, 1911; cadet, F.M.S., Dec., 1912; ag. 2nd asst. dist. offr., Tapah, Dec., 1913; ag. 2nd asst. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, Feb., 1915; passed cadet, June, 1915; offr., cls. V., Jan., 1916; asst. sec. to res., Perak, Feb., 1917; offr., cls. IV., Dec., 1919; ag. 2nd asst. dist. offr., Larut, June, 1920; ag. temp. as dist. offr., Batang Padang, July-Aug., 1920; asst. dist. offr., Larut, June, 1921; ag. dist. offr., Batang Padang, Dec., 1921; 2nd mag. Penang, Mar., 1925; ag. Br. advr., Perlis, May, 1925.

WILLIAMS, R. GREGSON.—Ed. at Rossall Schl., Caius Coll., Camb.; B.A. 3rd cls. hons., Science Tripos; Royal Schl. of Mines, A.S.R.M., 1st cls. diploma; post-graduate course, Mysore goldfields, India; asst. res., N. Nigeria, 22nd Moh., 1911; ag. inspr. of mines, June, 1911; ag. chief survr., Sept., 1911; contr., mines, Tanganyika Terr., 1926.
WILLIAMS, THOMAS ALFRED.—B. 1875; acctnt. clk., Basutoland, 1906; acctnt., 1913; finan. sec., 1916.

WILLIAMS, WILLIAM JOHN.—B. 1880; ed. Carnarvon County Schl., Univ. Coll., N. Wales and Univ. of Liverpool (engnr. dept.); attd. Portmouth dockyard, 1909; sent to Hong Kong for certification H.M. naval yd., May, 1909; ch. asst. engnr., Shanghai munic. elec. dept., 1911; engnr.-in-ch. and mun., Penang munic. electy. supply and tramways undertakings, 1920; engnr. and mun., Penang Hill rly., Oct., 1923; dir., elect. dept., F.M.S., Jan., 1927.
WILLIAMSON, E.—B. 1884; clk., G.P.O., Transvaal, Mar., 1903; treasy., Transvaal, July,

WILLINGDON, 1ST VISCOUNT (or, 1924); 1st Baron of Ratton (or, 1910); Freeman Freeman-Thomas, G.B.E. (1917); G.C.S.I. (1918); G.C.I.E. (1913).—B. 1866; A.D.C. to Lord Brassey when gov. of Victoria, 1895; M.P., Hastings, 1900-06; Bodmin divn. of Cornwall, 1906-10; jun. lord of treas., 1905-12; J.P.; gov. of Bombay, 1913-19; ditto, Madras, 1919-24; major, Sussex Imperial Yeomanry; lord-in-waiting to His Majesty; gov.r-gen. of the Dominion of Canada, 1926.

WILLIS, HARRY GRAHAM.—B. 1875; ed. Marlborough Coll.; apptd. to N.E. Rhodesia, Oct., 1900; asst. native commr., Jan., 1901; civ. commr., Sept., 1903; native commr., Nov., 1904; ret. owing to ill-health, Oct., 1906; rejoined as clk., mag.'s ct., Livingstone, N.W.R., Dec., 1907; native commr., Oct., 1908; ag. asst. mag., N.R., Sept., 1912-Mar., 1913; asst. mag., Apr., 1913; ag. res. mag., Barotse dist., Sept., 1913-Mar., 1914; ag. mag., Nov., 1923-May, 1924; mag., 31st May, 1924; J.P.

WILLIS, MAURICE A.—B. 1900; ent. M.O.D., G.P.O., May, 1915; apptd., after compet. exam., asst. clk., Apr., 1918; on mil. serv., May, 1918 to Feb., 1919; cler. off., 1st Jan., 1920; transf., P.O. stores dept., Mar., 1920; transf., C.O., May, 1924.

WILLIS, NORMAN EDWARD OMMANNEY.—B. 1872; ed. at Eton; entd. crown agent's office, 1st Jan., 1891; asst. cashier, 4th Jan., 1899; asst. acctnt., 1st July, 1902; acctnt. and dep. chief clk., 9th Jan., 1913; head of gen. dept. and ch. clk., 15th Nov., 1920.

WILLIS, STANLEY SCOTT.—B. 1889; ed. St. Lawrence Coll.; P.A.S.I., 1912; staff survr., Kenya, 1913; milly. survr., 1915-19; staff survr., Tanganyika Territory, 1922; on Anglo-Belgian bdy. comm., 1922-24.

WILLMOFTT, ARTHUR CHARLES.—B. 1880; ed. Lancing Coll. and Aspatria Agric. Coll., Cumberland; asst. survr., Uganda Prot., Mar., 1907; dist. engrnr., P.W.D., May, 1908; ag. asst., D.P.W., Apr.-Nov., 1913; engrnr.-in-charge, Kampala Port Boll. Rwy. constr., Dec., 1913.
WILMOT, FREDERICK CHARLES, M.D., D.P.H., L.R.C.P.—Asts. health offr., dept. of pub. health, Union of S. Africa, 1913.

WILSON, BAMIL MAYOR, M.B., Ch.B. (1901), D.P.H. (1911), M.D. (1911).—B. 1878; ed. Malvern Coll. (Faber Exhib.), and Univ. of Manchester; gov't med. offr., Fiji, 1904; dist. med. offr., Levuka, 1907; dist. med. offr., Ba., 1916; temp. lieut., R.A.M.C., Feb., 1918-19; served with B.E.F. in France; res. med. offr., col. hosp., Suva, M.O.H. and bacteriologist, Fiji, 1922; prin. med. offr., chmn., cent. bd. of health, mem., leg. coun., Br. Hond., 1923; suptg. med. offr., chmn., cent. bd. of health, chmn., quarantine bd., Jamaica, 1925; nominated mem., leg. coun.

WILSON, CAPT. CHARLES.—B. 1889; ed. Foyle Coll., Derry, and Trinity Coll., Dublin (siz., jun. and sen. ex., schol., sen. mod., B.A.); cert B., Dublin Univ. O.T.C.; cadet, F.M.S., 11th Jan., 1913; attld. Chinese prot. and contr. of lab. office, Kuala Lumpur, Aug., 1913 to May, 1914; asst. contr. of lab., Penang, 18th Dec., 1914; passed cadet, 9th Jan., 1915; on milly. serv., 2nd lieut., 6th K.S.L.I., 22nd July, 1915; France, 13th Feb., 1916; offr., cls. V., 7th May, 1916; lieut., 7th July, 1916; capt., 13th Feb., 1919; demod., 16th Mar., 1919; ment. in dep., dep. contr., lab., Seremban, 14th June, 1919; offr., cls. IV., 11th Jan., 1920; ag. sec. to res., N. Sembilan, in addn., during June, 1920; dist. offr., coast, in addn., 1st Sept. to 4th Nov., 1920; ag. dist. offr., Ipoh, 4th Dec., 1920; govt. rice agt., Kinta, in addn., 6th Dec., 1920; 2nd lieut. and adj., Malay Vol. Infy., Perak, 12th Apr., 1921; ag. ch. ast. dist. offr., Kinta, 1st July, 1922; emigrn. agt. in India, 1st Aug., 1922; ag. stamp and probate offr., Singapore, May, 1924; offr., cls. III., Jan., 1925; ag. dep. treas. (B), Singapore, in addn., Mar., 1925; ag. emigrn. commr. for Malaya, Madras, Feb., 1926.
WILSON, C. J.—Medical offr., E.A.P., May, 1911; dep. dir., med. serv., Kenya, Sept., 1923; ag. dir., med. and san'y. servs., 1926.

WILSON, CHARLES LIVINGSTONE.—B. 1874; ed. Queen's Royal Coll., Trinidad; entd. civ. serv., Grenada, as 2nd clk., G.P.O., Aug., 1892; ag. ch. clk., 1893; 2nd clk., coll. sec.'s office, Jan., 1894; off. exec. sec., gen. bd. of health and bd. of educn., 1897-1903; ag. ch. clk. to gov. of Windward Is., 1907-8; ch. clk., govt. office, St. Vincent, clk. of coun., man. of printing sub-dept., and sec., hurricane loan bd., 1907-11; ch. clk., coll. sec.'s office, clk. of coun., and man., printing sub. dept., 1911-19; coll. postmar., Grenada, 1919; a dir. of the pub. offrs. guarantee fund, 1919; sec. of reception comm., visit of H.R.H. the Prince of Wales to Grenada, Sept., 1920.

WILSON, COLIN E. J.—Boarding offr. and clk., Br. Solomon Is., 25th June, 1913; ag. dist. offr., Guadalcanal, 18th Jan., 1915 and 2nd Apr., 1923; ag. dist. offr., Shortlands, 15th Nov., 1916; ag. collr., cust., 7th May, 1918; ag. treas. and collr., cust., 20th Apr., 1919 and 6th Dec., 1924; acctnt. and lat clk., treas. and cust., 11th Oct., 1920; visiting dist. offr., 26th Mar., 1923; ag. dist. offr., E. Solomons, 2nd Apr., 1923; dep. commr. for W. Pacific, 27th July, 1923; dist. offr., Santa Cruz, 10th Dec., 1923; ag. gov't. sec., 6th Mar., 1925.
WILSON, ERNEST PIERRE FRANCOIS.—B. 1876; ed. Amersham Gram. Schl.; clk. acctnt.-gen.'s dept., Admiralty, 1914-1922; cler. offr., C.O., 2nd Oct., 1922.
WILSON, FRED.—B. 1896; ed. Robert Gordon's Coll., Aberdeen and Aberdeen Univ., M.B. Ch.B., 1921; surg., sub-lieut., R.N.V.R., 1915-19; asst. med. supt., Cent. Mental Hosp., F.M.S., Sept., 1924.

WILSON, G. GORDON.—Cadet, S.S., Nov., 1899; ag. 4th mag., Sing., Apr., 1902; passed final exam. in Chinese, Aug., 1902; ag. supt., money order branch and savings bank, Singapore, May, 1903; ag. asst. postmr.-gen., Penang, 2nd Oct., 1903; supt., money order branch and sav. bank, Sing., but cont. to act in Penang; asst. postmr.-gen., Singapore, 15th Feb., 1908; ag. asst. postmr.-gen., Penang, Apr., 1909; ag. asst. supt., govt. monopolies, Penang, Jan., 1911; asst. postmr.-gen., Penang, 1st Jan., 1912, but still ag. asst. supt., govt. monopolies, Penang; ag. supt., govt. monopolies, Singapore, Mar., 1912 to Mar., 1913; ag. postmr.-gen., S. Stiltns., Mar., 1914; promoted to Class III., May, 1915; ag. supt., govt. monopolies, Feb., 1916; offr., cls. II., Jan., 1919.

WILSON, GEORGE FREDERICK.—B. 1886; ed. King Edward's Schl., Bath; apprentice, puh. wks. dept., Cyprus, 1903; clk., ch. sec.'s off., 1904; ag. asst. sec., ch. sec.'s off., Jan. to Jul., 1919 and June to Oct., 1920; collr., cust., Sept., 1919; ag. ch. clk., ch. sec.'s off., Sept. to Dec., 1919; ag. commr. in 1923, 1926 and 1927; ch. collr., cust., and excise, July, 1924; regtr., Br. ships, regr., Cyprus ships, inspr., fisheries; ag. regr., trade marks, ag. regr., companies, ag. regr., patents, July, 1924; mem. leg. coun., 1925; title altered to compt., cust and excise, July, 1927.
WILSON, GEORGE HAROLD SUMNER.—B. 1887; ed. Chigwell Sch., Essex; asst. dist. commr., G. Coast, 18th Aug., 1914; dist. commr., 1st Jan., 1921.
WILSON, G. R. C., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), Cert. of London S.T.M.—Tempy capt., R.A.M.C., May, 1915 to Feb., 1919; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1919.

WILSON, SIR HENRY FRANCIS, K.C.M.G. (1908), K.B.E. (1919); C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1869; ed. Rugby and Trin. Coll., Camb.; foundation scholar, 1878; sen. Bell's Univ. scholar, 1879; Chancellor's medal for English verse, 1880; 6th in 1st class classical tripos, and distinguished in exam. for Chancellor's class, med., 1882; fellow of Trin. Coll., 1884; M.A., 1885; called to the bar (Lincoln's Inn), 1888; sec. to Trinidad judicial inquiry comm., 1892; sent to Malta to collect evidence for the Protestant communities in connection with the Marriage Case, 1893; priv. sec. to the Rt. Hon. J. Chamberlain, M.P., S. of S. for cols., June, 1896; legal asst. in the C.O., 1897; went to S. Africa as legal asst. on high commr.'s staff, Feb., 1900; sec. to administration of Orange River Colony, 1901; col. sec., June, 1902; acted as lieut.-gov., Apr. to Aug., 1903, Dec., 1903, to Aug., 1904, and from Aug. to Dec., 1906; mem. of I.C.C., 1903-1907 (represented O.R.C. on rly. comte.); retired; was sec. to the Royal Colonial Institute.

WILSON, SIR JAMES GLENNY, KT. BACH. (1915).—B. 1849; ed. Sir James Murray's Schol., Hawick, Scotland, Bruce Castle, Tottenham, Edinburgh Instn, and Edinburgh Univ.; M.H.R., N.Z., 1881-97; pres., N.Z. Farmers' Union; pres., N.Z. bd. of agr. since 1914.

WILSON, SIR JEREMIAH, KT. BACH. (1920). C.M.G. (1918).—Entd. Impl. telegraph ser., 22nd May, 1876; transf'd. to Cape telegraph ser., 1880; prin. clk., foreign mails branch, 1893; chief clk., G.P.O., 1898; asst. sec., 1905; sec., 1st Feb., 1908; under sec., under Union adminstr., 31st May, 1910; postm.-gen., Union of S. Africa, 19th Oct., 1910; ret., Dec., 1920.

WILSON, HON. JOHN CARNEGIE DOVE, K.C.' M.A., LL.B.—B. 1865; ed. privately and at Aberdeen and Edinburgh Unvrs., advocate of the Scottish bar; second puisne judge of sup. ct., Natal, Oct., 1904; judge pres. of the sup. ct. of S. Africa (Natal Prov. Div.), Jan., 1911.
WILSON, JOHN WOLSELEY.—B. 1876; entd. civ. ser., Trinidad, 1st Nov., 1899; sup-inspr. of constab., 24th Jan., 1905; inspr., ditto, 1907; now serving as tempy. major in army.

WILSON, CAPT. LOGIE WEIR.—B. 1894; ed. Giggiewick Sch., St. Andrew's Coll., Dublin, Royal Coll. of Science, Dublin, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; B.A. (Dublin); R.A.S.C., Oct., 1914; Royal Irish Rifles, 1917; asst. dist. commr., Sierra Leone, 23rd July, 1919; ag. asst. col. sec., Aug., 1919 to Mar., 1920; ag. commr., Pujehun and Bonthe dists., 1921; dist. commr., 1st Aug., 1926.
WILSON, HON. REGINALD VICTOR.—Elected senator, Commonwealth of Australia, 13th Dec., 1919; hon. min. in Bruce ministry, 1923; min. of markets and immigr., 1925.

WILSON, RONALD ERIC.—B. 1898; H.A.C., 1915; France, Jan.-Sept., 1916; R.M.C., Sandhurst, 1916-17; gazetted to Reg. Forces, (Middlesex Brgt.), Sept., 1917; 1st Bn. Middlesex Regt. (France), Jan., 1918; transfd. to Indian Army, Sept., 1918 (59th Scinde Rifles, F.F.); N.W. Frontier (Waziristan); attd. 82nd Punjabis, 1919; resigned comm. on app't as cadet, F.M.S., Sept., 1921; ag. ast. collr., land rev., Kuala Lumpur, Apr., 1922; ag. supt., cust., Krian, Oct., 1922; ag. 2nd ast. dist. offr., Larut, May, 1923; ag. ast. sec. to res., Perak, Feb., 1924; offr., cls. V, Sept., 1924; dist. offr., Jelebu, Jan., 1925; 4th ast. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Aug., 1926.

WILSON, BRIG.-GEN. SIR SAMUEL HERBERT, K.C.B., (1927), K.C.M.G. (1923), K.B.E. (1921), C.B. (1918), C.M.G. (1914).—B. 1873; entd. Army, R.E., 1893; capt., 1904; major, 1913; bt. lieut.-col., 1916; bt. col., 1917; served in S. Africa, 1899-1900 (Queen's medal, two clasps); European War, 1914-18; Chevalier Legion d'Honneur, 1916; Commandeur de la Couronne (Belgian), 1918; Officier Legion d'Honneur, 1918; French croix de guerre avec palme; Belgian croix de guerre; sec. to overseas defence comte., 1911-14; gen. staff offr., 2nd grade, 1914; gen. staff offr., 1st grade, 1915; brig.-gen. gen. staff, 1916; sec. to overseas defence comte., 19th Nov., 1918; gov. of Trinidad, 7th Nov., 1921; gov., Jamaica, 1st Sept., 1924; assumed govt., 29th Sept., 1924; perm. under sec. of state for the cols., 6th Aug., 1925.

WILTSHIRE, ERIC.—B. 1893; articled pupil to county survr. and architect, Middlesex, 1911-14; served with H.M. Forces, 1914-19; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., Zanzibar, 1920; A.M.I.C.E., Oct., 1924; ag. dist. engrnr., 13th July, 1925 to 11th Jan., 1926; from 15th Feb., 1926 to 14th Dec., 1926 and from 23rd Aug., 1927.

WINGATE, ANDREW FREDERICK.—B. 1880; ed. Blundell's Schl.; on Indian rly., 1912-14; Guayaquil and Quito rly., 1914-19; traffic offr., Sierra Leone govt. rly., 1919-20; asst. traffic supt., Tanganyika Territory cent. rly., 1920.
WINSER, CYRIL LEGH, C.M.G., (1928), M.V.O. (1920).—Priv. sec. to govs. of S. Australia since May, 1915.

WINSTEDT, RICHARD OLAF, C.M.G. (1926), M.A., Oxon., D. Litt., Oxon.—B. 1878; ed. at Magdalen College School and New Coll., Oxford; 2nd cls. mods.; 2nd cls. Lit. Hum., M.A.; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1902; ag. inspr. of schls., Perak; passed in Malay, 1904; in law, Oct., 1904; ag. 2nd ast. dist. offr., Batang Padang, June, 1904; ag. ast. dist. offr., Batang Padang, Feb., 1906; ag. 2nd ast. dist. offr., Tapah, Oct., 1909; ag. ast. dist. offr., Matang, and insp. of fisheries, Jan., 1908; class V., Jan., 1910; ag. ast. dist. offr., Gopeng, Apr., 1910; ag. dist. offr., Kuala Pilah, Sept., 1912; sec. to comte. for Malay studies in addition, Sept., 1913; class IV., confirmed dist. offr., Kuala Pilah, Dec., 1913; federal examiner in Malay in addition, Dec., 1913; promoted to class III., ast. dir. of education, S.S. and F.M.S., June, 1917; ag. chmn., sanitary bd., Kinta, June to Aug., 1919, in addition; cls. II., Nov., 1919; ag. dir., educn., S. Stiltns., and F.M.S., Feb., 1920; fed. examr. in Malay, S. Stiltns., and F.M.S., June, 1920; ag. chmn., comte. for Malay studies, F.M.S., 1920; seconded to act as prin. of Raffles' Coll., Singapore, June, 1921; ag. dir., educn., S.S. and F.M.S. in addn., May, 1923; offr. as sec. to high commr. in addn.; Fed. examr. in Malay in addition, Mar., 1923; dir., educn., S.S. and F.M.S., cls. IA, July, 1924; author of an "English Malay Dictionary" (3 vols.), a "Malay Grammar" (Clarendon Press), "Colloquial Malay," a "Malay Reader" (Clarendon Press), "Malayan Memories," "Shaman, Sawa and Sufi," a study of Malay magic (London), editor and part author of "Malaya" (London, 1922), and author of various pamphlets published by the F.M.S. govt. on Malay subjects; editor of many Malay folk-tales and (with R. J. Wilkinson, C.M.G.) of Pantun Melayu.
WINSTON, V. A.—B. 1903; ag. 3rd clk., post office, Dominica, 1920; asst. clk., P.W.D., Sept., 1920; jnr. audit. clk., Sept., 1924; ag. audit clk., Jan., Apr., 1927.

WISE, KENRICK STANTON.—B. 1881; ed. Dover, Cambridge, and London; M.B., B.S., B.S.C. Lond. Univ.; M.R.C.S., England; L.R.C.P., Lond.; D.P.H.; govt. bacteriologist, Br. Guiana, 1906; govt. med. offr. of health, 1912; surg.-gen. and regisr.-gen., 1914; surg.-gen., Trinidad, 4th Aug., 1919.

WISEMAN, R. A.—B. 1886; ed. Monmouth Gram. Schl. and Oxford; math. demy, Magdalen Coll., 1906-1909; 2nd cl. math. mods., 1906; 2nd cl. hist., 1906; 2nd cl. jurisprudence, 1909 apptd. after compet. exam., clk., sec.'s dept., Admiralty, Mar., 1910; 2nd cl. clk., C.O., Mech., 1911; sec. to overseas prize disposal comtees., Nov., 1914; temporarily served at ministry of shipping, Jan., 1917, to Mar., 1919; sec. to international shipping comtees., Feb., 1917; sec. to inter-allied chartering comtees., May, 1917; priv. sec. to Col. Amery, 1st Apr., 1919; 1st cl. clk., 1st Jan., 1920; prin., 1st Apr., 1920.

WITHYCOMBE, ROBERT, M.B.E. (1918), A.M.I.E.E.—B. 1880; dir., rlyw. and elec. dept., Zanzibar, 12th Dec., 1911; dir., wireless telegraph, 1st Jan., 1914; 3rd cl., Order of the Brilliant Star, Zanzibar.

WITTEENOOM, HON. SIR E. H., K.C.M.G. (1900).—B. 1854; elected M.L.C., W. Australia, 1883; resig. in Dec.; re-elected 1884; resig. 1886; elected, M.L.C., July, 1894; min. for mines, Dec., 1894; agent-gen., July, 1898; resigned, 1901; M.L.C.

WODEHOUSE, Lieut.-Gen. Sir Josceline HENNYAGE, G.C.B. (1913), K.C.B. (1908), C.B. (1889), C.M.G. (1890).—B. 1852; ed. at R.M.A., Woolwich; comanr. in R.A., 1872; served in Zulu war, 1879; Afghan war, 1880; Soudan, 1883-1894; gov. of Eng. frontier, 1887-1894; oomdd. "I" battery, R.H.A., 1894-1898; with Malakand field force (severely wounded), 1898; in action with Egyptian forces at Argin; infantry div. at Tooki; Presidency dist., 1898-1899; Secunderabad, 1900-1; Lahore, 1902; Rawal Pindi, 1903; ag. in oomd. of W. Comd., 1905; Zulu war (medal and clasp); Soudan war (medal, 3 clasps); N.W. Frontier (medal and clasp); 2nd cl. Medjedjie; 2nd cl. Osmanieh; Egyptian bronze star; Order of Palm, French Govt.; gov. of Bermuda, 1907-1908; late G.O.C. Northern Army, India.

WODERHOUSE, P. P. J., C.I.E. (1919).—B. 1877; ed. Eliz. Coll., Guernsey; first clk., regen.'s dept., Hong Kong, 1897-1900; passed in Chinese, 1899; census offr., 1900; asst. supt. of police, 1901; attached Punjab police, 1901-2; passed in Urdu, 1902; J.P., 1902; census offr., 1906; pol. mag., N.T. (Islands), Sept., 1906; passed in Punjabi, 1908; mem. of bd. of examiners, 1907; ag. dep. supt. of pol. and fire brig., 1907; 1908 to 1909, 1911 to 1912; census offr., 1911; dep. supt. of police and fire brigade, 1912; aide-de-camp to govt., 1918 to 1919; ag. capt. supt. of pol., on various occasions, 1918-25.

WODEMAN, GUY STANLEY.—B. 1886; ed. Rossall schl. and Jesus Coll., Camb. (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1909; office asst. to govt. agt., Sabaragamuwa, Oct., 1910; pol. mag., Matale, Feb., 1912; asst. comanr. of excise attached to the office of the excise commr., Apr., 1913; asst. comanr. of excise, Cent. Div., Jan., 1914; asst. govt. agt., Kandy, Apr., 1915; ag. asst. govt. agt., Matale, Aug., 1915; attached to chief censor's office, Sept., 1915; asst. govt. agt., Trincomalee, Apr., 1916; ditto, Colombo, Oct., 1918; ditto, Nuwara Eliya, Dec., 1919; ditto, Kegalla, Dec., 1921; ditto, Matare, Mar., 1923; dep. collr., cust., Apr., 1925; dep. comanr., excise, Nov., 1926; ag. comanr., excise, Sept., 1927.

WOLFE, EDWARD DUDLEY CORSCADEN, C.M.G. (1928).—Tonbridge schl. (1890-94); exhib. in mod. langa., Caius Coll., Camb. (1894); exhib. from Tonbridge schl. (1894); 3rd class mod. langa., tripods. (1896); 1st class Pt. I, and 2nd class Pt. II, Law Special (1897); B.A. (1897); M.A. (1925); cadet, F.M.S., 1898; sent to Canton to study Cantonese (1899); transf. to Hong Kong, 1901; passed cadet, Sept., 1901; sec., P.W.D. enquiry comm., 1901; regisr., land ct., 1901; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1902; ag. asst. regisr.-gen., 1902; regisr., land ct., 1903; ag. pol. mag., N.T., and asst. supt. of pol., 1903-6; seconded to Transvaal govt. serv., 1905; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1907; ag. inspr. of schla., 1907; ag. regisr.-gen., 1907; asst. regisr.-gen., 1907; ag. inspr. of schla., 1907 to 1909; head of sanitary dept., 1909; postmtr.-gen., 1913; ag. col. treas., 1914-16; 1st pol. mag., 1917; ag. col. treas., and custodian of enemy property, 1917 and 1918; ag. capt. supt., pol., 1918; capt. supt. of pol., 1920; ag. col. sec., 1921; attended internat. pol. confece., New York, 1925.

WOLFE, HENRY, M.Sc. (Agric.). Cornell Univ., U.S.A.—B. 1887; tech. asst., dept. of agr., S. Africa, 1916; dep. dir., Tanganyika Territory, 1920; ag. dir., agr., Feb. to Dec., 1921 and Mar. to Nov., 1924; E. Africa campaign 1916-17.

WOLFERTSAN, LITTLETON EDWARD PIPKIN.—Ed. Eton and Clare Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1888; cadet, S.S., Oct., 1889; passed final exam. in Malay, July, 1891; ag. priv. sec. to Sir C. Smith Jan. to Apr., 1894; ag. dist. offr., Bukit Mertajam, Feb. to Mar., and June to Nov., 1892; ag. dist. offr., S.W. dist., Penang, Mar., 1893; 3rd mag., Penang, Apr., 1895; ag. dist. offr., prov. Wellesley, June, 1897; ag. 2nd mag., Singapore Aug., 1897; sheriff and dep. regisr., sup. ct., Singapore, 1898, but still ag. 2nd mag., Singapore; ag. dist. offr., Dindings, Feb., 1900; ag. collr. id. rev., Penang, May, 1900; ag. sec. to H. Cr. for Fed. Malay States, May, 1901; ag. sen. dist. offr. Prov. Wellesley, May, 1902; collector of land rev. and regisr. of deeds, Penang, Aug., 1902; off. assignee, S.S., Sept., 1903; sen. dist. offr., Prov. Wellesley, Feb., 1907; ag. 1st mag., Penang, Apr., 1907; ag. inspr. of prisons, S.S., Apr., 1909; ag. dist. judge and 1st mag., Singapore, Oct., 1909; ag. dist. judge (civil), Singapore, Feb., 1910; ag. dist. judge and 1st mag., Singapore, Apr., 1910; res., Malacca, July, 1910, and Dec., 1916; ag. British adviser, Kedah, June, 1914 to Apr., 1915; offr., cls. IA., Jan., 1919; title of appt. altered to res. coun., Malacca, mem. of execo. coun. and offi. mem. of leg. coun., Jan., 1921.

WOLFF, ERNEST CHARLES HOLFOORD, C.M.G. (1928).—F.M.S. civ. ser.; b. 1875; ed. at Merchiston and Trin. Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1897; cadet, Pahang, F.M.S., Nov., 1897; passed final exam. in Malay, May, 1899; ag. treas., supt. d' posts and tels., and collr. of stamp duties, Pahang, Jan., 1898; ag. ast. dist. offr., Pekan, May, 1899; ag. sec. to res., Negri Sembilan, May, 1901; sec. to ditto, Oct., 1901; ag. ast. sec. to res.-gen., F.M.S., May, 1906; dist. offr., Kuantan, Sept., 1909, but still ag. ast. sec. to res.-gen.; ag. chmn. and sec., sanitary bd., Kinta South, Perak, Jan., 1911; ag. dist. offr., Upper Perak, Apr., 1911; officiating dist. offr., Larut, Jan., 1912; officiating supt. of convict estabt. and inspr. of prisons, F.M.S., Feb., 1915; ag. ast. col. sec. and clk. of couns., S.S., Apr., 1915 to Sept., 1919; offr., cls. Ib., Jan., 1919; prin. ast. col. sec., Jan., 1920; ag. col. sec., S.S., 12th to 27th Feb., 1921; ag. dir., educn., S.S. and F.M.S., June, 1921; dir. educn., S.S. and F.M.S., Dec., 1921; ag. Br. adviser, Kedah, Dec., 1923; Br. res., Negri Sembilan, July, 1924; ag. col. sec., S.S. Apr., 1927.

WOLFE, PHINEAS ERNEST.—B. 1885; ed. Glasgow Academy; clk., land dept., E.A.P., 1909; ag. office supt., 1917; land ast., 1919; regis. of titles, documents and coast land titles, 1920; liquidator of enemy property, July-Nov., 1921.

WOLSELEY-BOURNE, CAPT. JAMES FREDERICK, M.C.—B. 1878; 2nd lieut. Royal Berks. Regt.; lieut., S. African Constab.; served in S. African war, 1899-1902 (Queen's and King's medals); capt., K.A.R.; served in E. Africa, 1914-1918 ("1914-15" Star, War and Victory medals); commanded a column in operations against Aulihan (Somali) tribe, Jubaland, 1917-18 (M.C., African Gen. Serv. medal); ag. ast. comsnr., pol., Kenya, 1927.

WOOD, A. E.—Ed. at Liverpool Coll.; scholar of Univ. Coll., Oxford, 1903; 1st mods.; 2nd lit. hum.: cadet, Hong Kong, 1907; offr. for exam. of male assisted emigrants, 1908; ag. ast. regis-gen., 1908 to 1909; passed cadet, 1st Apr., 1910; ag. ast. regis-gen., Apr.-May, 1910; ag. ast. dist. offr., N.T., 1910 to 1911; 2nd ast. regis-gen., 1911; ag. ast. dist. offr., South dist., New Territories, 1911 to 1912; passed in Hakka, 14th June, 1912; title of ast. regis-gen. altered to 2nd ast. to sec. for Chinese affairs, 1913; ag. 1st ast. to sec. for Chinese affairs, 1913; ag. chief ast. to sec. for Chinese affairs, 1914; ag. dist. offr., 1915; ag. chief ast. to S.C.A., 1916, and 1918 to 1919; passed in Pekingese, June, 1916; sent to Japan to study Japanese, 1916; passed in Japanese, May, 1918; ag. dist. offr., Taipoo, 1919 to 1920; ag. ch. ast. to sec. for Chinese affrs., 1921; ag. dist. offr., Taipoo in admn., 1922; ag. sec. for Chinese affrs., 1923 and 1924; ag. dir., educn., Dec., 1925.

WOOD, BERNARD READER.—B. 1890; ed. Tonbridge Schl., Sid. Suss. Coll. Camb.; M.A., dipl. in forestry; ast. conservator, Indian forest serv., 1914; dep. conservator, 1918; conservator, of forests, Br. Guiana, 1925.

WOOD, FRANCIS ERNEST, M.B., B.C. (Cantab), 1902, B.A. (nat. sci. tripods) 1898.—B. 1877; ed. Downing Coll., Cambridge and St. Bartholomew Hosp., London; London Schl. Trop. Med., 1905; med. offr., Malay States Guides, 1905-20; on active serv. with Aden Field Force, 1915-19; ment. in desps., Apr., 1919; major (local), 1920; sen. med. offr., Perak, 1921; ag. prin. med. offr., F.M.S., Apr., 1922-Mar., 1923.

WOOD, JOHN R.—Ed. City of Lond. schl. and Camb.; foundation scholar of Jesus Coll., 1896; 1st cl. class. tripods, 1899; B.A., 1899; M.A., 1913; called to the bar, 1919; cadet, Hong Kong, 1899; regis. of land ct., 1900; ag. pol. mag. in New Territories, 1902; J.P., 1902; passed cadet, 1902; mem. land ct., 1903-4; ast. land offr. in N. T., Jan., 1905; ag. ast. reg-gen., 1905; mom. of squatters bd., 1906; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1908; dep. regist. and appraiser, sup. ct., 1908; 2nd pol. mag., 1909; ag. dir. of educn., 1911 to 1912; ag. 1st pol. mag., on several occasions; 1st pol. mag., 1920; ag. puisne judge, 1920-22 and in 1925; chmn. Sui An piracy comm., 1922; puisne judge, 1925; ag. ch. just., 1927.

WOOD, LESLIE WILLIAM.—B. 1890; ed. Merchant Taylors' schl. and Queens' coll., Camb.; 1st cl. math. tripods, pt. I.; 2nd cl. math. tripods, pt. 2; B.A., 1912; ast. dist. comsnr., Gold Coast, 18th Aug., 1914; ag. dist. comsnr., 1914-20; ast. col. sec., 1st Jan., 1921; ag. olk. of couns., Feb., 1922.
WOOD, CAPT. REGINALD CECIL MERVYN.—B. 1889; E. African Expedy. Force, 1915-17; seconded, K.A.R., 1918; K.A.R. staff, 1919; adjt., 3rd K.A.R., 1919-22; coy. commdr., 1923; ast. treas., Kenya, 1923.

WOOD, WILLIAM HAROLD.—B. 1896; ed. Abingdon schl.; served with Royal Berkshire Regt., 1915-20; France, 1916-19; Egypt, 1919-20; lieut., July, 1917; ag. capt., Apr., 1918; ment. in desp., 1918; cadet, F.M.S., 15th Apr., 1921; attd. dist. office, Port Dickson, Oct., 1921; ag. ast. dist. offr., Kuala Selangor, May, 1923; ditto, Kuantan, Mar., 1924; ag. supt., lands, Kelantan, Apr., 1925; dist. offr., Pt. Dickson, Apr., 1927.
WOODFORD, HON. W. W.—Elected M.H.A., Harbor Main, Newfoundland, 1889; chmn., bd. of works, 1897; min. of public works, 1898, 1909, and 1918; M.E.C., 1918.

WOODEHOUSE, JOHN LESLIE.—B. 1892; ed. Merchant Taylors Schl.; ast. transport offr., with rank of lieut., Uganda Transport Corps, 27th Nov., 1914; ment. in desps. for service in German E. Africa, 22nd Nov., 1916; ast. dist. comsnr., Uganda, 30th Dec., 1916; 2nd grade adminstr. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 17th June, 1920; dist. offr., 4th June, 1926.

WOODS, PURCY SINCLAIR COMYN.—B. 1879; ed. St. Michael's Schl., Lyme Regis, and Durham Univ.; clk., col. audit branch, exchequer and audit dept., Somerset House, 1st Jan., 1903; ast. auditor, Lagos and S. Nigeria, 29th Apr., 1905; dep. ch. acctnt., G. Coast rly., 30th Jan., 1909; ag. ch. acctnt., 21st Mar. to 26th Sept., 1909; promoted rly. acctnt. on abolition of office of ch. acctnt., 31st Jan., 1910; col. treas., Feb., 1922.

WOODS, WILFRED WENTWORTH, C.M.G. (1926)—B. 1876; B.A. Oxon.; 2nd cl. final hon. schl., mod. hist., 1901; clk., col. audit branch, E. and A. dept., 2nd Sept., 1901; ast. auditor, G. Coast, 22nd Feb., 1902; local auditor, N. Nigeria, 30th Oct., 1904; ast. col. auditor, Ceylon, 8th Feb., 1908; ag. col. auditor on several occasions; col. auditor, Aug., 1915; temp. employed at the War Office, Dec., 1917 to 1919; ag. col. treas., May to Nov., 1920; ag. P.M.G., Nov., 1920 to Apr., 1921; col. treas., Feb., 1922; ag. col. sec., 30th Sept. to 17th Oct., 1926.

WOOLLEY, CHARLES CAMPBELL.—B. 1893; on mily. serv., Aug., 1914; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., May, 1921; attld. Kandy Kach, June, 1921; ag. office astt. to gov't.; agt. N.C. Prov., Sept., 1922; 4th astt. col. sec., Feb., 1924; 3rd astt. col. sec., Mar., 1925; temp. attd. C.O., 1925 to June, 1926; addnl. astt. col. sec., Sept., 1926; 3rd astt. col. sec., Oct., 1926; astt. govt. agt., N'Eliya, May, 1927.

WORKMAN, CHARLES RUFUS MARSHALL, O.B.E. (1927).—B. 1874; ed. Christ Ch., Oxford, M.A.; called to bar, Inner Temple; pol. offr., Ocean Is., 1909; dep. comsnr., Western Pacific, 1911; admstr., Nauru, 1914; ag. res. comsnr., Gilbert and Ellioe Is., May, 1916 to Feb., 1917; ag. res. comsnr., Solomon Is., June, 1917; res. comsnr., Apr., 1919; judl. commr., Feb., 1920; col. sec., Gambia, Sept., 1921; ag. gov. on various occasions, 1921-25.

WORLEY, NEWNHAM ARTHUR.—B. 1892; ed. Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge, B.A., 1913; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1914; asst. contr. of lab., Kampar, July, 1917; asst. dist. offr., Ipoh, May, 1920; asst. prot. of Chinese, Negri Sembilan and ag. dep. contr. of lab., Seremban, Aug., 1920; asst. prot. of Chinese, Perak, Dec., 1921; asst. dir. of educn. (Chinese), F.M.S., June, 1922; ag. asst. dir., educn. (Chinese), S.S. and F.M.S., Jan., 1924; ag. prot., Chinese, Selangor and Pahang in addn., Feb., 1925 and Nov., 1926; asst. dir., educn. (Chinese), F.M.S. and S.S., Jan., 1927; ag. dir., educn., F.M.S. and S.S., Apr., 1927.

WORLEDGE, JOHN LEONARD.—B. 1895; ed. St. Paul's Schol.; active service in France, Sept., 1914 to Sept., 1918; ment. in despatches, Ireland, 1919; asst. audr., Kenya, Apr., 1920; asst. audr. in charge, Zanzibar, May 1920 to Feb., 1921; ag. senr. asst. audr., Kenya, Mar., 1923; senr. asst. audr., Nov., 1925.

WORSLEY, RALPH MARCUS MEABURN, B.A., Oxon.—B. 1887; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., 12th Nov., 1910; attached to Colombo Kachcheri, Dec., 1910; attached to col. sec.'s office, Aug., 1911; extra office asst. to govt. agt., W. Prov., Oct., 1911; asst. land settm. offr., Jan., 1912; office asst. to govt. agt., Central Prov., July, 1914; asst. censor, Dec., 1914; on military duty, Feb., 1915; ag. asst. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Nov., 1919; asst. govt. agt., Trincomalee, Sept., 1921; ag. govt. agt., Prov. of Uva, Aug. to Oct., 1922; asst. attit. offr., Jan., 1924; asst. govt. agt., Hambantota, May, 1924.

WORTHINGTON, ARTHUR FURLBY.—B. 1874; ed. at Tonbridge and Sidney Sussex Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1896; M.A., 1910; cadet, Perak, Nov., 1897; passed cadet, Feb., 1900; ag. asst. dist. offr., Kuala Lipis, June, 1900; asst. dist. offr., Kuala Pilah, Feb., 1902; offr., cls. VI., Jan., 1903; ag. ch. asst. dist. offr., Kinta, June, 1905; offr., cls. V., Sept., 1904; offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1908; offr. dist. offr., Kuantan, Aug., 1910; offr., cls. III., Jan., 1912; dist. offr., Kuala Lipis, July, 1917, Lower Perak, Feb., 1919; offr., cls. II., Jan., 1919; ag. Br. adviser, Kelantan, Mar., 1920; offr. dist. offr., Ulu Selangor, Jan. to June, 1921; Br. adviser, Kelantan, Sept., 1922; ag. dist. offr., Larut, Mar.-Aug., 1922; appt. of Br. adviser, Kelantan raised to "star" July, 1924; on sp. duty, England, Apr., 1924; Br. adviser, Kelantan, May, 1925; ag. Br. res., Pahang, Dec., 1925; Br. res., Pahang, Feb., 1926.

WORTLEY, EDWARD JOCELYN, O.B.E. (1926), M.B.E.—B. 1884; ed. Jamaica Coll.; course in agric. and agric. chem. at Jamaica, govt. lab. and sp. course at the Ontario agric. coll.; fellow of the chem. socy., 1906; jnr. asst. chem., Jamaica, 1902; asst. chem., 1905; lect. in agric. sci., 1906; headmar., govt. farm schl. and stock farm, 1909; comsnr. for Jamaica, Canadian Nat. Exhibn., Sept., 1910, and Sept., 1912; dir. of agr., Bermuda, 1913-20; food comsnr., 1917; hon. sec., internat. comtesse. on potato industry, 1919; visited Canary Is. on behalf of Bermuda govt., 1920; dir. of agr., Nyassaland, Dec., 1920; author of "Companion to Blackie's Tropical Readers," "Poultry Diseases," and "Foods and Food Products of Jamaica."
WRATISLAW, JOHN CYRIL.—B. 1894; ed. Oundle Schol.; asst. dist. comsnr., Sierra Leone, 14th Jan., 1924.

WRIGHT, ANDREW BARKWORTH, M.A., M.C. (1917), Bar (1918).—B. 1895; ed. Haileybury and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; served in European War with Suffolk Regt., 1914-19; major, 1918; asst. sec., ch. sec's. office, Cyprus, 3rd June, 1922; asst. priv. sec. to O.A.G., June to Oct., 1923; ag. priv. sec. to high comsnr., Apr. to June and Sept. to Oct., 1924; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to O.A.G., June to Sept., 1924; ag. ch. asst. sec., regr. of co-op. societies and regr. of co-op. credit societies, Apr. to Oct. 1924; passed exam. in mod. Greek, Aug., 1924; ditto, Turkish, Apr., 1925; ag. ch. asst. sec. for various periods, 1926-27; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to O.A.G., July-Oct., 1926; ag. col. sec., July to Aug., 1927.

WRIGHT, ARTHUR ALAN, B.A.—B. 1887; ed. St. Edmund's Schol., Canterbury; open exhibn., St. John's Coll., Oxford, 1906; lieut. 3rd Bn. Bedfordshire Regt. (S.R.), 1909; 2nd cl. hons. schl., mod. hist., Oxford, 1910; B.A., 1910; cadet, Fiji, 1912; ag. sub-inspr., constab., 1912; ag. clk., ex. coun. and 1st cls. clk., col. sec's. office, 1913; passed cadet, 1914; ag. dist. comsnr., Rewa and prov. comsnr., Naitan, 1915; 2nd grade dist. comsnr., 1915; dist. comsnr., Nadi, 1915; dist. comsnr., Colo East, 1916; on mily. duty, 1917; 2nd lieut., 5th Bn. Rifle Brigade, 1917; on active serv., France, 1917; dist. comsnr., Macuata, 1920; ditto (1st grade), Nadroga and prov. comsnr., Nadroga and Colo West, 1922; ditto, Colo North, 1923; ag. asst. col. sec., 1st Jan., 1927.

WRIGHT, ARTHUR EDGAR.—B. 1880; Fellow of the Surveyors' Institution; ed. at Tiffin's Endowed Schol., Kingston-on-Thames; asst. engrn., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 5th May, 1903; exec. engrn., 1911; ag. assessor in addition, 1915; exec. engrn., 1st grade, 1917; seconded to Weihsiaiwei on special serv., 1918 and 1920; ag. asst. D.P.W., 1923.

WRIGHT, CHARLES HAROLD.—M.A., F.I.C., F.C.S.; B. 1880; scholar, Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; B.A. (Nat. Sc. Tripos), 1902; M.A., 1916; asst. analyst and science master, Trinidad, Apr., 1908; ag. govt. analyst and prof. of chemistry, Trinidad, on two occasions; agric. chemist, Fiji, Mar., 1914; ag. dist. comsnr., Kadavu, July-Aug., 1918; govt. chem., Fiji, Jan., 1920; senr. agric. chem., Nigeria, June, 1923.

WRIGHT, H. PILLEW.—Served in S. Africa war in I.Y. and S.A.C., 1899-1902 (Queen's medal and 3 clasps, King's medal and 2 clasps); staff offr. to O.C., Riet River div., O.R.C.; dist. comsnr., Edenburg and other districts, O.R.C., 1903-1908; asst. dist. comsnr., Uganda Prot., 15th Oct., 1908; dist. comsnr., June, 1914.

WRIGHT, HERBERT ARTHUR.—B. 1883; instr., govt. technical schl., Accra, G. Coast, 10th Apr., 1909; principal, ditto, 28th Sep., 1911; attached to Togoland field force, 16th Aug. to 8th Sept., 1914; in joint charge of detention camp for German prisoners of war, 8th Dec. 1914 to 13th Jan., 1915; asst. censor, 2nd Oct. 1914 to 2nd Feb., 1915, and 2nd Oct., 1915 to 25th Feb., 1916; prov. inspr. of schla., 25th Jul., 1917; ag. senr. inspr. of schls., 29th July to 1st Sept., 1917; asst. dir., educn., 1st Apr., 1926.
WRIGHT, HON. ROBERT ALEXANDER.—Mem., N.Z. parlmt., 1908-11 and since 1914; min. of educn., 1926.

WRIGHT, VICTOR WILLIAM, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1891; served with R. Naval Divn. in Gallipoli and France; wounded, 1914-16; lieut., R.E. and gen. staff, Macedonia, Caucasus, Trans-Caspia, Constantinople, 1916-20; mast., tech. schl., educn. dept., Gold Coast, 1921.

WRIGHT-NOOTH, CAPT. RODNEY GEORGE, M.C. (1916), A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1884; ed. St. Paul's Sch., stud., engrgn. faculty, King's Coll., London, 1904-08; 2nd lieut., R.E., (S.R.), 1909; engrn. on survey and constrn. of Magadi rly. and water wks., 1911-14; R.E., 1914-22; served, Kenya, German E. Africa and Jubaland; severely wounded, twice ment. in desps., M.C.; div. offr., R.E., Londonderry and Enniskillen Divn., Ireland, Apr., 1920 to Apr., 1922; capt., Reg. Army, R. of O., 1922; supt. of pub. wks., Antigua, 4th Aug., 1922; mem., leg. coun., Antigua, 1922; asst., D.P.W., B. Guiana, 1926.
WROUGHTON, JOHN HENRY.—B. 1877; med. offr., Basutoland, 1908.

WUPPERMAN, RUDOLF CARL, M.B., Ch.B., Elin.—B. 1883; entered med. serv., Trinidad, 18th Oct., 1909; asst. res. surg., colonial hosp., Port of Spain, 27th Oct., 1914; res. surg., col. hosp., 8th May, 1923.

WYATT, ALEXANDER HENRY LOUIS.—Ed. Wellington; served in Impl. Yeomanry, S. African War, 1900-01; Queen's Med., 4 clasps; W.O., 1901-04; asst. res., Nyasaland, 1904-14; seconded for serv. with 1st K.A.R., 1914-17; ment. in desps. and awarded M.C.; served in Nyassaland rising, 1915; A.G.S.M. and clasps; dist. polit. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1917; sen. comsnr., Nov., 1922; prov. comsnr., Mar., 1926.

WYATT, ROBT.—B. 1870; Lond. Metro. pol., 1891 to 1895; B. Bond. pol., June, 1895; asst. supt., Feb., 1902; ag. supt., May, 1902, to May, 1903; ag. dist. comsnr., Orange Walk, July to Aug., 1903; ag. dist. comsnr., Coroal, 26th Apr. to 11th July, 1906; ditto, Caye, Feb., 1906; ag. supt. pol., 14th July, 1905; J.P. for Colony; Hythe extra certif., 1899; supt. of pol., 1st Apr., 1906.

WYNNE-JONES, EDWARD IRVINE.—B. 1895; ed. Oxford and Univ. of Wales; cadet, Hong Kong, July, 1920; ag. dist. offr., South, June, 1922; attd. col. secretariat, Nov., 1922; passed final exam., Feb., 1923; dist. offr., North, May, 1923; ditto, South, Dec., 1926.

YATES, GEO. W.—B. 1872; ed. London pub. and high schls.; ent. civ. serv. of Ont. as priv. sec. to various mins., from Feb., 1899 to Oct., 1911; accompanied Hon. F. Cochrane, Ont. min. of lands, forests and mines, to Ottawa on latter accepting portfolio of min. of rlys. and canals in Borden govt.; cont. priv. sec., min. of rlys. and canals until formation of union govt., 1917, when became ch. priv. sec. to Sir Robt. Borden; asst. dep. min. of rlys. and canals, 1920.

YATES, JOSEPH MERVYN ST. JOHN, B.A.—B. 1878; ed. Winchester Coll. and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; called to the bar, Inner Temple, May, 1903; northern circuit, 1903-15; H.M. Forces, 1915-19; regar.-gen., Bahamas, Feb., 1920; ag. stip. magis., Mar., 1920; ag. atty.-gen., Nov., 1920; ag. ch. just., Feb.-Mar., 1921; ag. stip. and circuit magis., Mar., 1921 to Sept., 1922; seconded for serv. as asst. asst. to atty.-gen., Jamaica, Sept., 1922; res. mag., Jamaica, 1923; ag. atty.-gen., Apr., 1923 to Jan., 1924; ag. judge, Kingston ct., Nov., 1924 to Oct., 1925; ag. puisme judge, Oct., 1925; circuit judge, Ashanti and N. Territories, Gold Coast, 1926.

YOUILL, JOSEPH.—Seconded from Impl. serv. to B. E. Africa and Uganda Prots. as 1st grade dist. survr., 1st Jan., 1913 to 17th Dec., 1915; seconded to G. Coast, 31st May, 1916; offr.-in-charge, post and telegraph dept., Togoland, 22nd May, 1918 to 23rd Jan., 1919, and from 12th Aug., 1919.
YOUNG, ALFRED ERNEST, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), L.D.S. (Eng.).—B. 1891; med. offr., Basutoland, 1924.

YOUNG, SIR ALFRED KARNEY, KT. BACH. (1923), K.C., B.A.—Ed. Magdalen Coll., Oxon; called to the bar, In. Tem., May, 1889; 2nd clk., col. sec.'a office, Br. Honduras, Oct., 1890; ag. dist. mag., Toledo dist., Aug., 1891; priv. sec. to Sir A. Maloney, and olk. of councils, 1892; priv. sec. to admnstr. of the govt., 1896; ag. registr.-gen., 1895-7; dist. comsnr., Orange Walk, 1898; registr.-gen., June, 1898; ag. supt. of police, May to Nov., 1899; J.P. for col.; crown prosecutor, legal adviser, and police mag., Seychelles, 23rd Sept., 1903; atty.-gen., B.C. Africa Prot., 17th Mar., 1906; stip. mag., Trinidad, 1908; ch. just., Seychelles, Aug., 1909; adminstd. govt., Apr. to Nov., 1911, and from 16th to 27th Dec., 1912; atty.-gen., Fiji, May, 1914; ag. chief just., Jan., 1916 and from Mar., 1919 to May, 1920; ch. just., Leonard Is., Feb., 1921; ch. just., Fiji, 1923.

YOUNG, CAPT. SIR ARTHUR HENDERSON; G.C.M.G. (1916); K.C.M.G. (1908); K.B.E. (1918), C.M.G. (1897).—B. 1854; late 27th Inniskillings; ed. at Edin. Acad., Rugby, and Sandhurst; local comdt., mil. pol., Kyrenia, Aug., 1878; asst. comsnr., Paphos, Nov., 1878; comsnr., Paphos, Sept., 1879; ditto, Famagusta, Jan., 1882; dir. of survey and prin. forest offr., 1892; mem. legis. coun., 1892; ch. sec., 1894; adminstd. the govt., July to Dec., 1895, Jan. to Apr., 1898; sent on special mission to St. Vincent in connctn. with relief work, Nov., 1902; col. sec., S. Stiltnsts., June, 1906; adminstd. govt., Feb. to May, 1909; ch. sec., F.M.S., 1910; gov., S. Stiltnsts., 8th Aug., 1911; assumed govt. 2nd Sept., 1911; also high comsnr. for Malay States and for Brunei and Brit. Agt. for N. Borneo and Sarawak; ret. 25th Nov., 1919.

YOUNG, HON. SIR FREDRICK WILLIAM, KT. BACH. (1918), LL.B.—M.H.A., S. Australia, 1909; comsnr. of crown lands and immigrn., 1912-14; agt.-gen. for S. Australia, 1915-18; mem. of comte. for stlttn. of ex-service men within the Empire, 1917; M.P. (Co. U.), Swindon, 1918.

YOUNG, HENRY ALFRED, K.C.—Ed. at Derby Schl. and Edin. Univ.; called to the bar (Scotland), July, 1899; asst. crown advocate, E. Africa Prot., 22nd June, 1906; asst. atty.-gen., 1915; res. mag., 1917; stip. mag., B. Guiana, Aug., 1921.

YOUNG, MAJOR HUBERT WINTHROP, C.M.G. (1923), D.S.O. (1918).—B. 1885; ed. Eton and R. Milly. Acad., Woolwich; comand., R. Garrison Arty, 1904; Indian Army, 1908; capt., 1913; major, 1919; asst. censor, Army headqrs., Simla, July, 1914 to Feb., 1915; N.W. Frontier, Feb.-Dec., 1915 (1914-15 Star); asst. polit. offr., Mesopotamia, Dec., 1915 to Aug., 1917; dep. dir. local resources, Mesopotamia, Aug., 1917 to Mar., 1918 (ment. in desps.); gen. staff offr., 2nd grade, Hejaz operations, Mar. to Oct., 1918 (ment. in desps.), D.S.O., 3rd class Order of Nahda); pres., local resources bld., Damascus, Oct., 1918; seconded to F.O., Feb., 1919 to Mar., 1921; seconded to C.O., Middle East Dept. as asst. sec., Mar., 1921 to Dec., 1926; accompanied Mr. Churchill to Cairo. conf., Apr., 1921; visited Palestine and Iraq on sp. serv., Sept., 1921 to Jan., 1922; sp. serv., Lausanne for Turkish peace treaty, Jan., 1922 and at Brussels and Geneva for Iraq frontier dispute on various occasions during 1925 and 1926; ret. from army and apptd. col. sec., Gibraltar, Feb., 1927.

YOUNG, JAMES.—B. 1869; ed. Ayr Acad.; served in Bechuanaland Border Pol., 1890-93; served in Bechuanaland Field Force, Dec., 1896 to Oct., 1897; clk., office of commissary of ordnance C.C., Nov., 1897; Bechuanaland Rebellion, 1897, (med. and clasp); Anglo-Boer War, 1899-1900, (Queen's med. and 2 clasps); served in Bechuanaland Rifles, 1899-1900; acct., mily. adminstr., W. Transvaal, Dec., 1900; clk., mag.'s office, Zeerust, June, 1901; A.R.M., Standerton, Sept., 1902; detached A.R.M. Ventersdorp, Sept., 1903; relieving mag., Transvaal, Nov., 1903; senr. A.R.M. (detached) Krugersdorp, May, 1904; ag. 2nd civ. mag., Johannesburg, Sept., 1905; ag. 3rd ditto, Sept., 1906; A.R.M. (detached), Boksburg, Dec., 1906; A.R.M. (detached), Krugersdorp, Apr., 1907; R.M., Germiston, July, 1909; inspgr. mag., Transvaal, Griqualand West and Bechuanaland, Nov., 1915; civ. mag., Johannesburg, Nov., 1916; mag., Pietersburg, Nov., 1922; mag., Johannesburg, Feb., 1925.
YOUNG, HON. JAMES ALEXANDER.—Mem. N.Z. parl., since 1911; min. of health, 1926.

YOUNG, JAMES ANDERSON, M.C., M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), Certif., L.S.T.M.—B. 1889; ed. St. Andrews and Edinburgh Univs.; late house surg., Derbyshire R. Infirmary; served with R.A.M.C. (T.F.), 1915-19; granted hon. rank of major on resignation; med. offr., Somaliland, Sept., 1919; astt. bacteriologist, Nigeria, 1927.

YOUNG, MARK AITCHISON.—B. 1886; ed. Eton and King's Coll., Camb. (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1909; off. astt. to govt. agt., N. Prov., Aug., 1910; ditto, Kandy, Nov., 1911; ag. astt. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Jan., 1913; 4th astt. col. sec., Nov., 1913; ag. 3rd ditto, Feb., 1914; 2nd ditto and clk. of leg. coun., Sep., 1914; 3rd astt. sec. col., Oct., 1914; on mil. duty, 1918-1919; 3rd astt. col. sec., Jan., 1920; 2nd astt. col. sec., July, 1920; astt. govt. agt., Hambantota, Mar., 1922; prin. astt. col. sec. and clk., exec. coun., Mar., 1923.

YOUNG, MAJOR-GEN. ROBERT, C.B. (1919), C.M.G. (1916), D.S.O. (1915).—Volunteer serv. in N.Z.; left N.Z. with main body, N.Z. Expd. Force with rank of major, 1914; temp. lieut.-col., May, 1915; lieut.-col., Nov., 1915; in command, 1st batt., Canterbury Regt., Nov., 1915 to Aug., 1917; brig.-gen. (temp.), Aug., 1917; in command, 1st N.Z. infy. brig., Dec., 1917 to Feb., 1918; in command, 2nd N.Z. infy. brig., Feb., 1918 to Apr., 1919; comdr., "A" group, N.Z. Expd. Force, Sling Camp, Apr. to July, 1919; col., N.Z. staff corps, Oct., 1919; O.C., Canterbury dist., Nov., 1919 to Jan., 1921; O.C., Southern Command, Feb., 1921 to Dec., 1925; col.-comdt., 1921; major-gen. and comdt., N.Z. mily. forces, Dec., 1925; chevalier of Legion of Honour; six times ment. in desps.

YOUNG, W. A., M.A., B.M., B.CH. (Oxon.), B.Sc. (War.), Lond., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M. & H. (Lond.), cert., Lond. Schl. of Trop. Med. (with distinction, 2nd place), late exhibr., Jesus Coll., Oxford.—War serv., France, 1916-18; olin. asst., children and ear depts., St. Thomas's hosp., 1922; R.M.C., Ealing hosp., 1922-23; East Africa med. serv. (Zanzibar), Sept., 1923; ag. R.S.C., Zanzibar, July, 1927.

YOUNG, SIR WILLIAM DOUGLAS, K.B.E. (1919); C.M.G. (1907).—Ed. at Charterhouse; clk. in govt. sec.'s office, Br. Guiana, Jan., 1877; ch. clk., 1889; ag. priv. sec. on several occasions, 1877-1893, to govrs. of Br. Guiana, Trinidad, and G. Coast; ag. astt. govt. sec., Br. Guiana, 1892-3-4-5; ag. govt. sec., Dec., 1892; ag. govt. agt., N.W. dist., 1895; astt. col. sec., Mauritius, 1895; ag. col. sec., 1896-7-8, 1900-01; admtd. govt., Mauritius, Aug., 1897; comsr., Turks and Caicos Islands, 1901; admtr., Dominica, 19th Apr., 1906; ag. govt., Leeward Is., 1909; admtr., St. Lucia, 1913; ag. govt., Windward Is., May to Dec., 1914; govt., Falkland Is., 28th Dec., 1914; assumed govt., 15th May, 1915; ret., 1920.

ZAMMIT, HON. PROFESSOR THEMISTOCLES, C.M.G. (1911), M.D. (hon.) D.Litt. (Oxon.).—B. 1864; ed. Malta Lyceum; matric., Malta Univ., 1882; grad. in medicine, 1889; govt. analyst, 1891-1920; curator, Valletta Museum, 1903; prof. of chemistry, Malta Univ., 1905-20; mem. Mediterranean fever coman., 1904-07; rector, Malta Univ., July, 1920; mem., exec. and leg. coun., 1920-21; hon. fellow, Royal Anthropological Inst., 1919; local sec. (Malta) of Soc'y. of Antiquaries, 1921; medallist (Mary Kingsley), Liverpool Schl. of Trop. Med.; delegate of Malta govt. at Imp. Educn. Conf., London, June to July, 1923.
ZIAI, MUSTAFA FUAD.—B. 1888; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1922; inspnr., Cyprus mily. pol., Nov., 1906; judge, dist. ct., Apr., 1913; puisne judge, Oct., 1927.
